high.situation: We were given instructions on how to build a simple toolbox out of metal supplied by the workshop. We will be given 7 lessons each are 3 hours long with the help of instructors and demonstrators if necessary. Extra time can be booked via email sent to our inboxes.
high.task_action: We began the toolbox by measuring out the parts shown on the instructions (depicted in mm). After measuring out each part using a ruler we proceeded to cut out the metal parts we measured so we would end up with 2 side panels, 2 front panels and a bottom panel. We would then file the cut metal to make it so that the edges aren't sharp and dangerous. I then bent the metal according to the instructions by measuring a line of where it needs to be bent and bending it by 90 degrees using the clamp. After all the metal pieces are bent properly I then weld them together into the shape of a toolbox. I then measure and cut out the handle with a hollow metal pole. I had to cut out 2 short pieces of solid metal poles to drill and 4mm hole into and cut out the inside so a screw can fit inside. the 2 solid pieces are fit into each end of the hollow pole and then the handle was screwed onto the side panels of the completed toolbox. The final process was to sand blast the toolbox to remove any rust that has built up over the past few weeks and paint the toolbox black which was optional.
high.result: The final result which was our completed tool box was then evaluated by the instructor along with how we abided to the rules of the workshop, ensuring both our safety and other, and our overall efficiency during the whole process. We were each given individual marks out of 90 based on our performance.

high.situation: I needed to present a project demonstration for My ENGG1000 workshop assignment while the pressure to complete another unit's assignment weighed on my mind.
high.task_action: I began to review my code and analyse my understanding of the game to prepare for my project demonstration. I also examined areas that I did not fully comprehend as of yet and began to research each purpose behind a function. I had to keep reminding myself to focus on what was immediately infront of me rather then be haunted by a future that had yet to arrive.
high.result: I managed to present a well formulated project demonstration without cracking under the pressure to be perfect and managed to receive a score I was very happy with. Then I moved on to the assignment for the other unit.

high.situation: In my software engineering class we had to form groups for a competition in which we had to use our interpersonal skills of teamwork and communication to successfully brainstorm how we would create two programs that users could interact with.
high.task_action: Through the use of brainstorming and clear communication between group members, we were able to form two group pairs spreading out skill equally to keep quality of the software high and consistent. We were randomly assigned the topic of skateboard, spiralling and boxer with the goal of creating two programs that would involve a skateboard spiralling and the other involving the boxer. To create the programs effectively and efficiently, consistent and clear verbal and written communication and teamwork was used in the development stages of the programs to eventually deliver the end product.
high.result: The end result was two well-built programs that did what was intended. This was due to the use of interpersonal skills such as teamwork and communication which made the development process and flow of ideas for the program successful.

high.situation:
I had to finish a game variant of rock paper scissors with two extra signs as well as a detailed project report full with an implementation plan and testing plan for each of the modules as well as according to a very specific set of criteria within a limited time span. 

TASK:This project was a very large in size and had a lot of distinct stages;each with emphasis on different kinds of work; and needed to be done in a particular order in order to ensure that each stage got the most efficient amount of time as possible for it so that no stage had too much or too little emphasis on it. Due to this being a large amount of work that had complex stages and steps - In order to most efficiently manage my time I had to break the task up into smaller milestones; each with deadlines that lead up to each other and have the deadlines setup in stages.

ACTION:In order to effectively adhere to these stages - I learned how to utilize tools like the gantt chart and the kanban board to have mindfulness and direction towards the specific tasks that needed to be done in order to effectively ration my focus to make sure that one thing at a time was accomplished properly instead of falling into the multi tasking trap. I created the stages of "Create implementation plan","Create Testing Plan", "Execute implementation plan", "Execute testing plan" and had a kanban board full of tasks for each of the stages i.e define system functionality, create system flowchart, define function functionality etc. Through adhering to these tools; I was able to enter a flow state whenever I worked where I had one big task that I focused on entirely - a task that was recorded on my kanban board for a specific phase - a phase that was recorded on my gantt chart for a specific time.
high.result: The outcome of me breaking up my long term goal into phases and rationing my focus using planning tools was that I ended up finishing my project well on time and to a very high degree of quality in my personal opinion.

high.situation:
It was the 28th of March when I experienced the situation for this reflection about coding in C alone. This was a daunting task as starting Uni meant I had no prior coding experience except the steady rate of my only hands-on coding unit. This wasn’t the only source of my stress, but also; the coding in my programming class was to be done in the language Java, but the assignment of this class was meant to be completed in C. This meant that I had to mentally juggle new information coming from nowhere, meaning that I had become completely lost in the weeks leading up to this day.

Task:

The task on this day was to begin working on a Scissors Paper Rock game with a best of three rounds playing system. I managed to complete the Scissors Paper Rock with only one round and felt extremely proud, but it was implementing a best of three rounds which proved impossible for me to do on my own, I tried this for hours prior to this day but could not do this. It made me feel terrible as I didn’t want to get a low mark simply because it looked like I wasn’t trying. I was trying, but I couldn’t produce the work that proved it.

Action:

When I realized that this task was impossible to do on my own as a result of my lacking coding knowledge and experience, I decided to take the only action I knew how. I put up my hand and asked for help, it was then where after simply talking to a tutor and getting a new perspective on the task when it suddenly clicked, I found my purpose and the exact way to show it. I kept working on it in class and when I felt a milestone reached, I put my hand up again, getting reassurance and more advice.
high.result: After this cycle of receiving help I managed to complete the game and made it fully functioning with a best of 3 rounds and everything I could have wanted it to do, I had received exactly what I needed and wished I asked even earlier. This event made me realize that despite not believing in my abilities in the beginning of the task, I knew that if I asked for help and kept striving for my best my purpose and reason for completing work would shine.

high.situation: Being presented with the large load of content to learn in the Technical Drawing workshop.
high.task_action: Breaking down the fundamentals of each drawing style and their requirements prior to any attempt to ensure I understood their purpose and distinctive qualities allowed me to form my own understanding of the content with every session, before having ample opportunity to practise the process itself.
high.result: The result was incrementally furthering my skillset with every piece completed, and feeling more capable and confident with my abilities.

high.situation: Our software development class in year 12 was split up into groups and given a task, we needed to create a game for our HSC assignment.
high.task_action:
Firstly, we began a brainstorming session to decide what we were going to make. We took many factors into account such as difficulty to program, time to program, and adherence to criteria. Eventually we decided to make a solo Blackjack game, which seemed like our best option and reasonable to make within the time constraint. 

Once we chose the game we were going to make, we devised a plan for the program which had four components. The four people in our group was assigned one of the four components to make, each of which were relatively equal in difficulty and time to program. 

We chose the component that each of us were responsible to program by declaring our weaknesses and strength’s and choosing based on these abilities and lack thereof.
high.result: The result of this group plan was being able to make a sufficient Blackjack game within the given time constraint. Our group assignment was given a 91%.

high.situation:
Multiple Arduino Tasks due for marking in week 7

Task: After an individual brainstorming session I worked out the parameters of the problem being the marks allocated to a task and completability of Arduino tasks due for final review in week 7.

Action: I set out to find which Arduino tasks were most easily completable by my understanding of code and the requirements of the tasks. Then considered that end of week tasks reward the most marks and the final task rewards the highest amount as its the hardest task. Evaluating each remaining task based on (difficulty and mark allocation).
high.result: I had an actionable prioritization of tasks and knew which to focus on to properly score high grades in my class.

high.situation:
To brainstorm and design a tool box out of sheet metal that can hold tools without collapsing.

Task: Brainstorming and design within small group; the key is excellent communication so using mind maps and visual communication is essential. This allows us as engineers to visualise our ideas and further have a greater understanding in the design. We each contributed our own ideas in order to choose the most fitting and functional design for the tool box.
high.result: The result was selecting a design that included the best of our ideas. These ideas were blended together to make a tool box that is most beneficial to the user. Then the design was further developed using prototypes and then the team was able to construct the tool box.

high.situation: Needed to create and deliver a presentation on an emerging technology.
high.task_action: Brainstormed and researched different emerging technologies until deciding to make a presentation on sonic fire extinguishers. Then two weeks were spent researching the emerging technology of sonic fire extinguishers the information was compiled into concise dot points in a PowerPoint presentation.
high.result: The result was being able to deliver an oral presentation on sonic fire extinguishers with an in-depth understanding of how it functions as well as the pros and cons of the technology.

high.situation: We needed to identify and make slight changes to the cut angle of the folds present on the side pieces. This was in order to aid in the overall cohesion and fit of the tool box components.
high.task_action: By forming a small group, we worked together to identify and interoperate provided diagrams, whilst brainstorming easy and time efficient solutions. Through brainstorming and discussions, we were able to collectively agree on a viable solution for the side pieces.
high.result: The result was increasing the angle of the cut, which in turn kept it out of the way of other pieces which ultimately aided in a better fit between all toolbox components.

high.situation: I needed to draw objects and shapes provided with several different methods such as single-point perspective, oblique view, isometric view, multi-view and sectioning on a drawing board table.
high.task_action: The approach to some drawing methods was difficult to grasp, especially when the object of focus is composed of a variety of shapes and sizes. For example, drawing a circle in an isometric view. I thought that it would be best to get used to the methods through the countless failures and to take it slowly regardless of being able to finish the allocated task in class(which could be otherwise done at home).
high.result: The result was that I gained valuable experience with the different methods of technical drawing which boosted my understanding of more complex shapes.

high.situation: We had a group project in high school in which we had to make a presentation and use the limited resources given to us to create a pop up project of the solar system. We had one week to complete it and were in a group of 2. However, I had been very sick/ill that week and my partner wasn’t that enthusiastic or motivated to complete or do the project, however he did say that he will design the pop-up art of the solar system. Long story short even though I was sick and didn’t attend school for that whole week I had done some of the work at home on the information of each planet and the solar system that was going to be included on the pop-up art. However, on the day of the presentation the partner I had been with had done nothing. Luckily for us we were the last to present and the teacher had given us some paper, colours and other equipment to quickly formulate our project, although we had little to 50 minutes to get it done and present it.
high.task_action: We had to be quick and intelligent with our decisions as we had short amount of time on our hands. We had split the task into two sections, my partner worked on the design of the pop up art and myself worked on finishing up on the information of the pop-up art. However, we realised we needed to work on the project (pop-up art) itself as without it we wouldn’t be able to present a project at all. I made a vital decision to help to make the pop-up art as it took longer than expected. The materials the teacher had given us weren’t the best but we had to make do with it. Once we finished the design of the project my partner and I quickly copied down the information I had gathered online on the pop-up art, we had around five minutes to do this, and there was a lot of information to copy down. Somehow, we had completed the entire project just under 50 minutes due to strategic planning and working hard.
high.result: The result was being able to complete the project under time restriction and limited resources having to strategically resource management. Even more surprisingly was how well we worked together due to this adversity, it gave us even more of a reason to complete the project and to do better and that showed in the results/feedback that were given to us a few weeks later in which our project had the second highest mark in the class.

high.situation: We were given the task to construct a tool box made from either steel or aluminium whilst having limited knowledge on the machinery we needed to use.
high.task_action: Thus, we had to research the functionality of each machine needed to give us insight on how we were going to construct our toolbox. Through the research we communicated with each other to gain a better understanding on how we were going to start the construction. During our collaboration we evaluated the most appropriate machinery to use and helped each other use the machines.
high.result: The result was that we were able to construct a tool box using teamwork and communication with each other. It also resulted in time efficiency and accuracy as our tool box were constructed faster and more accurate due to us providing help to each other.

high.situation: A group of people, numbering 7, were tasked with the design and production of a civil model of student housing building.
high.task_action:
*
Planning: We began by determining the scope and size of the model and plan in the site analysis.  

	*
Gathering materials: Once we have a plan in place, gather all the necessary materials. You'll need cardboard, a cutting mat, a craft knife, a ruler, glue, and any other decorative materials you plan to use (such as paint, markers, or stickers).

	*
Cutting the cardboard: basic shapes of our model from the cardboard were cut. 

	*
Assembling the pieces: Bothe glue and tape were used to assemble the model

	*
Adding details: A group member used a 3D printer to print our own extra details, e.g., furniture.
high.result: OUR GROUP COMPLETED OUR BUILDING WITH ACCURATE DIMENSIONS AND TO SCALE.

high.situation: Writing a report on a recently completed project.
high.task_action: Making a to-do list outlining the tasks with higher priority and how long it should realistically take to complete. A Gantt chart is the most effective way to format the time to complete a task, visually showing how long it took and when it took place. Colour coding a Gantt chart helps to signify priority and which tasks should take place first and take priority.
high.result: An easy way to organise tasks and which to focus on first, making the process of writing the report easier and more efficient to complete on time with key components out of the way first.

high.situation: In a crucial must-win game for my football team, our star striker went down with a rather nasty cramp in the early minutes of the second half.
high.task_action: Given we had already made all of our allotted substitutions, it seemed like a no brainer to most of the players on our team that our striker should continue playing with the cramp as we would be better off having eleven players on the field with one injured over having just 10 players to take on our opposition, who were beginning to build momentum and were threatening to take the match away from us. However, we were still quite indecisive in the heat of the moment. Given no one else appeared ready to make would could be such a crucial decision for our season, I took on the initiative and got the team in a huddle to explain to them that it wasn’t worth the risk to aggravate the injury and that I believed in every player that we could still fight back harder and stronger without our striker.
high.result: Subsequently, my initiative and address to the team worked brilliantly and we went on to win the match and progress to the finals, in which I would captain the team.

high.situation:
At my current job, I was being rostered for long shifts across all of my weekdays during a busy period of Uni. I was struggling with managing my time effectively and found myself feeling overwhelmed with the amount of work i had due during a time period with little time to accomplish it effectively. Upon a specific situation upon being asked to stay back a further 4 hours of my shift I approved well knowing I had work due the following day that I hadn't started. 

Task: My personal task was to improve upon my time management skills and find ways to effectively balance my work schedule adequately with the amount of Uni work due.

Action: To address this challenge, I first assessed how I was currently using my time when I wasn't busy. I began by being self aware of my daily activities and whether it was an effective use of my time. I then prioritized my Uni tasks within those periods reliving me of the buildup of stress. Furthermore i adjusted my work availability to cater towards my heavy Uni workload to provide myself more free time outside of work to complete my tasks.
high.result: As a result of my actions, I saw a significant improvement in my time management skills. I was able to complete my Uni project deadlines consistently across the period and deliver work of a quality that approved of. Additionally, I found that I had more time and energy for things outside of Uni and work without feeling like i was wasting my time or that i had things to get back to.

high.situation: During the construction of my toolbox, I faced a series of setbacks that threatened to impede the timely completion of the project. Some of the issues were avoidable, such as a misaligned cut that removed excess material due to my haste in wanting to quickly finish the task. Other obstacles were outside of my control, such as a day of lost progress due to travel complications.
high.task_action: The problems that I had encountered during the construction of the tool box included a misaligned cut which removed excess material, alongside a day of progress due to travel complications. Both of these issues delayed the progress of the task and presented their own unique issues that had to be resolved separately in order to complete the task at hand. The first step I took to resolve the issues at hand was repurposing some of the waste material from earlier in the cutting process to recreate the pieces that were not properly cut. Secondly, to make up for lost time during the recutting of the piece and the day of work missed, I utilized the available extra workshop hours, where I was able to come in on a separate day and work on my project and further progress it by completing any steps that I was unable to complete .
high.result: As a result of these actions, I was able to effectively and efficiently resolve the issues and complete the project within the desired timeframe. This experience highlighted the importance of a balanced approach to task completion, with a focus on both urgency and attention to detail. Moving forward, I plan to incorporate this lesson by taking the time to consistently reflect on the details of the task and to utilize any available additional time wisely to ensure that future projects are completed to the best of my ability.

high.situation:
I needed to remedy mistakes made earlier in the project, while also making sure I complete the project on time.

Task/Actions: Reflected on the mistakes made and brainstormed ideas to solve the problems; carefully picking the best solution for the problem, using tools that would make the fix the easies/quickest while also keeping the overall quality to a standard .
high.result: The result was a finished product that had previous mistakes remedied and fixed while also having any further work completed on time and to the standard acceptable.

high.situation: We needed to design a wooden aircraft.
high.task_action: A group of myself and one 3 other team members decided to split the workload into two separate bits. Myself and one other team member would work on the CAD and physical design of the aircraft, and the two other team members would work on the written report section. Breaking up into small groups allows for better results, as not everyone will have the same take upon the task given. Communication in this step is important to allow the team to collect each persons thoughts and intercede their options. Through this method, several designs of the aircraft were suggested each with their pros and cons stated.
high.result: The result led to a whole group discussion to figure out which design would be best suited and be most successful. In the end, we were able to come out with a final design which the whole team had agreed on.

high.situation: During the HSC I needed to plan out my timetable of study, schoolwork, social life and work outside of school. I needed to effectively prioritise and plan my life so it was balanced, healthy and able to reach my goals.
high.task_action: I was able to achieve this by allocating my time and resources using a schedule. I prioritized my study periods, this included studying for my tests, any schoolwork I had to do and tutoring for subjects that I wasn’t confident in. These study periods would be straight after school and in my free periods for as long as I was capable of. I then prioritized my social life. I allocated my time and resources for this to be on the weekend. During the HSC I would try to stay around my local area when I went out to ensure I didn’t spend too much money as I had least prioritised working. I least prioritized work and making money as I believe it was not as important as focusing on my studies and would’ve reduced the amount of time, I had to do these studies.
high.result: The result of my time and resource management reflected my plan and I was happy with my results.

high.situation:
As a group in year 10 my team had to design a box that was not only stylish but also ergonomic towards our users. Which inevitably failed as a result of poor communication and teamwork.

TASK: At first we all thought that we fitted into a position and planned our roles according to out strengths and weaknesses. For me I was responsible for the design of the watch.

ACTION: Over the next few weeks, problems arose with conflicting ideas as to what our project should do, look like and operate. We had all each come up with elaborate ideas, plans and models that did not take into consideration the plans of others. With little time remaining, we came together as a team, threw away our conceited ideas for the project and really focused on how we could draw ideas from our projects and put them all together.
high.result: As a result we surprisingly finished ahead of all the other teams, who instead of dealing with their teamwork problem just left the project to one person who couldn't handle the work and submitted a less desirable product. By breaking up the tasks once again but this time all under one universal plan we successfully created a watch case that was designed for modern day businessmen and won first place in the class.

high.situation: We have come up with a plan for three different groups to do a specific task each in order to construct a new road.
high.task_action: Once the plan has been finalised and the details have been ironed out it now must be shared with the workers who are going to carry out the plan. The WAY that the plan is shared is vital as by being CLEAR and ASSERTIVE to make sure no group mistakes any details so that when the time comes their part of the plan is carried out without issue. This will be done by making sure the three groups are in their correct group and when their aspect of the plan is delivered it is done with clear pronunciation, along with visual aids to aid incase of a misstep by the presenter. By addressing each group individually no one can get the details mixed up with the other groups.
high.result: The result was a successful presentation were all parties know the exact details o the job they will be undertaking in the coming days.

high.situation:
During technical drawing my resolve was challenged, and as I did not have much prior knowledge I struggled with understanding certain concepts and ideas and successfully implementing them.

Task: My aim was to acquire and learn the skills of technical drawing and its uses to a level where I could proficiently apply and understand it. I also had to overcome my frustration and put extra effort into learning due to my lack of prior knowledge. 

Action: I utilised a number of methods in order to reach my aim. I started with listening to the information provided by the demonstrators, learning and using this to complete the set tasks. Additionally, I would consult with my fellow classmates and the teachers when I required assistance. I also referred to the online resources typically provided in the form of videos to further my understanding.
high.result: I learnt a large amount of knowledge and developed a proficient understanding of technical drawing and its applications. Through the concepts and knowledge I gained, my abilities significantly improved to the point where I could confidently and accurately complete set tasks without needing assistance. Ultimately, I successfully undertook technical drawing and gained valuable skills.

high.situation:
Our newly constructed soccer club had a team that qualified into the cup finals for the first time, although we had already other team players winning theirs before. Our team had strong players from top local clubs, well experienced in their position. Each position had a strong member, however, most of us have never played together and therefore was shown during the start of the competition of our weak chemistry. But game after game just assisting in training we had surprises showing our way which led to us to figure out that we could do better than this and get the best possible chemistry just before our finals, which led us having a deadline into success.

TASK: My main focus which I quickly saw becoming my responsibility as a team captain was to facilitate communication, collaboration and decision making to ensure that each one of used their experience and skills together as a team to the highest potential and build a solid team.

ACTION: Even in days that we were off training I still kept in touch with them to organise our own team sessions, keep up to date of our state in health and build a stronger mindset into just focusing our hunger of winning this cup. I had divided each section of position of players into a mini group session so to just be working together and enforcing their chemistry just in their specific section of position. I encouraged an open minded conversation whilst trading I to hear as many options available and so that everyone’s ideas were considered and valued.
high.result: By the days of intensive hard work and dedication our team had become the strongest in the shortest amount of time that we could have asked for. This has led us into bonding our chemistry extraordinarily to the highest of all such that by the end of the match the hard work has paid off and led into many of us being scouted into better divisions and open up more possibilities in our future.

high.situation: you are the project manager for a new building project and you have a tight deadline to deliver a project report to the client.
high.task_action:
your task is to manage the time and resources of the project to ensure that you meet the deadline and the report by setting 

1. clear and achievable goals.

2. allocate required resources such as personnel, materials and equipment.

3. track time.

4. review the progress.
high.result: As a result of your effective time and resource management, you will be able to deliver the project report to the client on time and within budget. The client will be satisfied with the quality of the product and gives positive feedback, leading to future business opportunities.

high.situation: At the age of 17, I want to become a health provider, working in the NDIS industry or age care services. I had placed a goal in which I wanted to achieve but I understood the responsibilities it would require to take. At the time I was in yr11. I did my research and would often call up the NDIS and understand the process into becoming a registered provider. I had to prioritise a lot of my holidays and weekends understanding the process. Prioritization and focus is a skill that everyone should develop over there course of experience in the workplace. Prioritization is understanding the urgency of the matter and focusing on how to find a solution.
high.task_action:
I had faced a lot of criticism from other Registered providers, they would often make it difficult for me with either providing me with incorrect information or false guidance. Having very limited qualifications, I had signed up to some online course which provide you with qualifications within short periods of time. I was focused in what I wanted to achieve and put a lot of dedication and hours doing research. I had to do a background check, and a few other checks to be deemed safe to work in the industry.  I would stay up late at night prioritising family night doing research and gathering of my documents. With several restless nights I felt like things were getting nowhere. I was under qualified and the qualification in which I provided where not enough. I was focused and dedicated, I didn’t want the opportunity that I had go past me. I came up with a idea of which I would hire staff that which were qualified to work for me.

It came to thought that I can ask for assistance from my uncles and aunties to provide there documents in which I can receive the registration approval. My dad has 11 siblings and all of which work. A few of my uncles work In the hospital and manage the ICU sector, a few of my aunties are teachers and there qualifications are suitable for the registration groups in which I applied for. The process took me over 1 month to gather all the documents. I had to priorities some of my time for my preliminary exam which I had in year 11 and that went on for around 2 and a half weeks.

 

 

Prioritization and focus has become a part of a daily lives, which every decision we make requiring some form decision making. As I grown up, I understood that we have to priorities a lot of things in order to accomplish a dream and a goal. For me a dream was opening up a business, establishing a business wasn’t a easy process and it had required me to priorities a lot of my family time to become a provider. Although a lot of people still mention that I have university  to look out for and I’m still young, then I should have the option of enjoying my time while I’m at it.
high.result:
I believe to overcome challenges we must understand the objectives and focus our goals on what is more important. This is further reinforces by the “Eisenhower Matrix”. This form of prioritisation method is a task management method which I often use to maintain my day to day tasks. It is a form of planning method, you construct your duties into 4 categories and they go as followed, the tasks you do important and need to do first, the second section is for tasks which can be completed after or later on. When the important tasks are completed. Section 3 is tasks you require assistants with, so if someone can give you a hand in or knowledge to completing a task and lastly section 4 is deleting tasks which have been completed.

This process helped me schedule my tasks into the 4 categories, this has enabled me to maximise my time spent on important tasks which are required to be completed leaving the non-urgent tasks to the end. At the end, after a restless 6 months going in and out of meetings, we had finally received the company registration. My family and I agreed that my dad will be the director in the business as he will be running for majority of the time while I complete my study.

During the process I’ve learnt that the process of prioritisation and focusing is a fundamental part of our lives, taking on more responsibilities are we continue moving forward, we are required to often reflect on our progress and what we consider as our priorities. It enables us to create a great work balance between our work life and our family life, the use of “Eisenhower Matrix” allows me personally to reflect on my progress and make adjustments suitable to achieving that goal.

high.situation: IN OUR ENGINEERING WORKSHOPS, PRIORITISATION AND FOCUS PLAYED A VITAL AND CRUCIAL ROLE AS IT HELP US COMPLETE OUR WORKS AND MOVE FORWARD WITH OUR COURSE CONTENT.
high.task_action: IN CLASS, WE HAD TO WORK ON EXERCISES OF THE ARDUINO CIRCUITS, DURING THOSE LESSONS WE ARE TAUGHT TO MANAGE OUR TIME BOTH IN THE WORKSHOPS AND AT HOME REGARDING WHICH TASKS TO COMPLETE AND PRIORITISE. OUR TUTORS HAD COLLABORATIVELY ASSISTED US BY REMINDING US TO USE OUR TIME WISELY FOR THE ARDUINO EXCERCISES AND TO PRIORITISE AREAS THAT WE ARE WEAK IN AND THE ONES THAT ARE YET TO COMPLETE.
high.result: THE RESULT WAS BEING ABLE TO STRENGTHEN OUR UNDERSTANDING OF HOW TO COMPLETE VARIOUS ARDUINO EXERCISES OVER THE PAST FEW WEEKS AND TO ALSO PRIORITISE WHICH CONTENT IS REMAINING TO COMPLETE IN ORDER TO FURTHER BOOST OUR MARKS.

high.situation: While making our model of student sharing accommodation , it was difficult for my team to manage time and complete model within specified period of time because of lack of time and resources.
high.task_action: So, we decided to borrow some of resources from our teachers and meet outside the class when we all were free ,however, it was difficult for us to meet together at one place at one time as all are busy in their chores but leaving our other tasks we met at library and started continuing our model. Moreover , we decided to divide the work between us so, that we can speed up our work. We shared the resources which we bought from teachers for making our model and we spend 6 - 7 hours that day on our model.
high.result: The result was that we completed most of our model on that day and finally we were able to submit our model on last day.

high.situation: Projects within the ENG1000 software design and logic workshop are especially daunting as it is my first-time coding, and I know that to succeed I need to manage my time and resources more efficiently.
high.task_action:
As it is my first year of university, the way I approached assignments and homework in high school will not work out the same with the university, as I know the workload as a student am provided is more challenging and requires more time to understand. I realized that to try to succeed in this workshop, I needed to break my bad habits of poor time management and create new good habits that would serve my purpose.

My attempt at breaking my bad habits involved creating an assignment spreadsheet that listed all the assignments and homework within the workshop and its due dates. It acted as a constant reminder for me that I needed to work on them and helped me plan out which assignments to focus on first. Sleeping earlier and going to the gym early in the morning instead of later in the day, also helped with allowing me more time to do things later in the day, such as reviewing extra material provided such as C programming lecture videos on YouTube.
high.result: Implementing good habits and managing my time and resource resulted in extremely positive outcomes. Compared to the start of the workshop, I noticed I have a better work ethic than I usually would, and I feel better mentally and physically.

high.situation:
In year 12 one of the subjects I was taking was software design and development. Being the only girl in the entire class, I faced several challenges throughout the year. I remember a project I dealt with that required working in a group to create a 3d game, and it was challenging to be taken seriously in the group due to my gender. I often felt as if my opinions or input was disregarded and I wasn’t held at the same level of importance as the other members.

Task: My goal was to establish my credibility and expertise on the project and earn the trust and respect of my group members. I knew I needed to find a way to communicate my knowledge and experience to them effectively.

Action: To achieve this, firstly, I took the time to research and educate myself about the project's details. I made sure I had a deep understanding of the subject to be confident when participating in discussions. I made sure I was always prepared with relevant and insightful contributions. I spoke up confidently and challenged the ideas presented by other group members when necessary. Even when faced with dismissive attitudes; I stayed firm in my solid argument and willingness to learn and participate.
high.result: My actions eventually paid off. The team began to take me seriously as I stated my arguments, appreciated my ideas, and respected my opinions. As time went on, they started to rely on me as a valuable member of the team, seeking my input, and giving full attention to my contributions. With my consistency and hard work, I earned the respect of my class and was shown the same amount of respect as others. In retrospect, my experience showed me that by proving oneself and remaining professional in male-dominated settings, I could thrive and succeed.

high.situation: Due to poor communication and roles allocation our civil modelling project was unfinish. However, several group mates decided to fabricated a story, blaming a car crash for the incompletion of the model.
high.task_action: This completely goes against the Macquarie University student integrity codes of conduct. As such I and another group mates refused to agree on being part of the plan and had notified the Academic leader DR. BANDITA MAINALI on their plan and made up story.
high.result: The Academic leader DR. BANDITA MAINALI understood our situation and had discussed with other members which fortunately resulted in us receiving the mark for the presented model, whereas, the other members will receive a zero for the practical model for breaking the student codes of conduct.

high.situation: we needed to design a building using a very specific criteria.

high.situation:
During my metalworking course, I struggled to manage my time and resources effectively. I had difficulty keeping pace with the assignment demands due to the fast-paced nature of the class, and my lack of precision yielded in cutting the wrong sections of my metal pieces. These mistakes caused setbacks, forcing me to adjust my measurements and slowing my progress, emphasising the importance of precision in time and resource management.

TASK: My task was to contrive an effective method of managing my time and resources while ensuring that the metalworking project was completed on time. I realised I needed to put more time to the project to make up for lost time due to measurement adjustments and lack of commitment.

ACTION: To address the situation, I identified the tasks needed to complete the project and established a schedule that included supplementary classes to increase the project's available time. This approach enabled me to gain insight from my errors and re-establish a focused work pace. I completed simpler tasks first, then advanced to more complex ones, to make optimal use of my time and resources.
high.result: As a result of my efforts, I was able to efficiently manage my time and resources, allowing me to complete the metalworking project within the timeframe specified.  The approach to marking and organising the metal pieces reduced uncertainty, saved time, and facilitated terms of growth. Task prioritisation resulted in an efficient workflow, effectual use of available time and resources, and successful project completion. Overall, my actions led to beneficial outcomes such as increased efficiency, improved scheduling, and the completion of the metalworking project on time.

Allowing myself to complete all the Arduino circuits under the time restraint.
high.result: The result was being able to complete the Arduino circuits using AnalogWrite, PWM, Libraries and Servos under the time constrain, while using the Arduino hardware. I used an online resource Tinkercad to create the circuits online before the workshop began.

high.situation: When constructing my toolbox handle, I encountered the issue of the thread inserts spinning inside the handle, and thus had to develop a solution.
high.task_action: At first I assessed the reason why the inserts kept on spinning. Soon I found out it was due to the fact I did not press them in straight in line with the handle, causing the handle to expand. At first, I evaluated the shape of the handle, which had been deformed. Soon after I tried clamping certain areas of the handle to return it to its original shape, however this failed. As a result, I had to deliver perseverance by reconstructing the handle, the inserts and tapping the threads. Furthermore, I had to deliver urgency as this meant I was taking a step backwards, and may fall behind.
high.result: As a result of my actions, I was able to complete my toolbox on-time to a satisfactory level, due to my perseverance, urgency and flexibility

high.situation: While presenting my project, I discovered that there was an error in my code.

I knew I wouldn't be able to figure out what was wrong with it in the time I had, so I ended up having to accept a lost mark and instead write out a bunch of if/if else statements to get the job done so that I had working code to present.
high.result: The result was having code that worked as intended and met all requirements from the user's end, at the cost of the mark for the inclusion of Extension 2.

This would allow people to focus on what they enjoy and avoid making mistakes. A group chat should be made to allow communication between others in the group for any assistance and to meet up with the team.
high.result: The result was being able to finish the project more efficiently. With the use of teamwork, people in the group were able to do different tasks and stay communicated with the group.

high.situation: We were doing a group assignment that consisted of 3 individuals and the task was to create a design and prototype of a portable speaker that could be used at places like the beach without it being affected by sand and dust particles.

Further we managed out our time frame by using an action plan that consisted of all the necessary requirements in order to build an appropriate speaker meeting all the needs as stated. We split the workload equally as this then allows us to work in a more efficient manner and also allows us to complete the required works before the deadline. We also manage our resources so that we know what will be used and that nothing will be going to waste.

high.situation: Woodwork was one of my electives for my HSC preliminary course and had to submit a project before a certain due date.

In order to ensure I was up to date, I needed to create a schedule to monitor my progress and keep up with all of the steps. Furthermore, I also had to manage my resources in terms of efficiently using the materials to not waste resources and money.
high.result: For my schedule, I used a gantt chart which allowed me to know what my next steps were whilst displaying the tasks I have done as well as how quickly I should have these tasks done. Managing resources was done by keeping scraps and off cuts and are used in areas where it may not be seen so it doesn’t affect the aesthetics of the project, or the off cuts can also be used in areas where it only requires a bit of material.

high.situation: My Civil Modelling Group members and I, had to design a university campus building model with our own designs ideas which were documented on the engineering report. Using Time Management and goal setting software such as TRELLO helps to define the roles of each member, and sets suitable deadlines all members can see. Through Using applications and discussions we were able to effectively distribute tasks early on, and hence time management could be implemented earlier rather than later, additionally utilizing certain software tools for the civil modelling aided greatly in resource management and reduce time to create models and floor plans,
high.result: In the end, we were able to develop a satisfying final project for our model, and were able to minimize moments of procrastination allowing us to not over work or stress due to last minute projects.

high.situation: I was given the task of coding a program to count to 20 in even numbers only.

high.result: Lastly, I was able to complete the task by using the best method.

high.situation:
Time and resource management has become more of an essential element to achieving life goals and meeting deadlines especially in relation to university studies and assessments.

TASK: Overtime, having a strong management plan for your timing and resources has become a vital aspect of everyone's life especially in university. Assessments and tasks are quite congested for some and therefore force people to have solid plans in which quality productive time is spent.

ACTION: Due to these impacting changes, one must adapt to a heavy workload and productively manage their time. Utilising time scheduling software's and other alike programs can effectively alter ones bad habits of time and resources managements. Some may also  improve their time management skills by setting priorities, reducing procrastination, and the like.
high.result: Effectively managing ones time and resources is very effective as it streamlines their workflows and increases productivity. These also improve ones lifestyle and reduce their workload which allows for a more productive day to day.

high.situation: During year 12 as the head of staging in the school entertainment crew/course I was in charge of a team of my peers, delegating specific roles pre-performance and during. I communicated issues live to other departments around the building. During a production wherein there was a trivia segment, one of the contestants was missing.
high.task_action: We had to find the contestant or find a replacement, and since it was a live show we had at most 2 minutes to figure the situation out. As the decision maker for the team I asked my crew if anyone had ideas on how we could solve it efficiently, we discussed for a short time then based on their suggestions, I gave each of them a job to do that would have us ready to send the set on at the right cue. Backstage roles were shuffled around with some people doing two smaller jobs so I could send a small handful out, one to ask around the performers for other sets  if they knew where the contestant was, another to find out if a replacement could be organised among the pool of students we had backstage waiting for their time.
high.result:
The situation and response showed that as a team, even the leader must seek ideas and collaborate with others, because the crew knew what they were each capable of and came up with ways to solve the issue. The contestant was not found, but a volunteer was found by the crew member tasked with finding a replacement, meaning the show could continue as planned. The backstage crew all worked together seamlessly to cover the missing manpower and execute all the moves on time and satisfactorily. 

The show was a success.

high.situation: Due to the limited working time of 6 weeks we had, we were really worried about if we were going to complete the project in time. We also needed to figure out who will take the resources/materials with them.
high.task_action: Before we started to build on our project, we discussed our availabilities throughout the week, in order to find a day to meet up and work on the model. We came to an agreement where we were all free to meet up on Mondays 9am at the library. From then, we started to work together every Monday, so that we would finish the project satisfactorily and by the due date. I opted to take all the resources/materials with me because I travel to the university by car, whereas my group members took public transport. It was really convenient because all the resources/materials we had were really big and hard to hold.
high.result: Our meeting up sessions really helped us get started. It also ensured we were ahead of every other group in our class. By keeping all the resources/materials in my car, allowed me to bring them to the library all the time, which enabled us to work on our project outside of class time.

high.situation: We needed to add space for stairs in our model, however, there were obstructions between other rooms.
high.task_action: As a team, we communicated and collaborated our ideas, which were to rearrange the rooms to make enough space for stairs to be added. This included remeasuring our spaces to match the area of the requirements and extending some places to fit in other areas. Each of our group members proposed a rearrangement, and together we decided which position was best.
high.result: We were able to fit in stairs to get to each level of our student housing accomodation together. This ensured that all of our group members were satisfised with the decision. It also allowed us stay within the requirements for the model making project.

high.situation: I need to be able to complete all of my technical drawings in the allocated time frame
high.task_action: every week, we are given 3 hours to finish each lesson. These 3 hours are very important, because we are given special tools that allow us to draw with consistent angles and lengths. Without these tools, it is almost impossible to draw up to standard, meaning I can't finish these drawings outside of class time. I prioritised which drawings would be harder to do without the special tools provided. for example, Isometric and Oblique drawings had a very high priority, since the need to be drawn at very specific angles, which I can't do with the tools I have at home. Next is the technical drawings, since everything has to be drawn perfectly vertical or horizontal. These have a lower priority because I can do touch-ups and small lines by lining up a small triangle ruler with my page at home. Finally, the lowest priority drawings are perspective drawings. Vertical and horizontal lines can be done as mentioned before, and are much rarer. All of the other lines require no tools except for a basic ruler making them less risky to do at home. After I prioritise my drawings for the day, I allocate no more than 1.5 hours per drawing. If any take longer than that, I'll skip them and try finishing them at home.
high.result: A total of 19 technical drawings completed and submitted before the due date.

high.situation: We were entrusted with evaluating how a multi layer drawing is done with the use of guide lines

In order to tackle this task critically, we must first have a firm grasp of the activities objectives. 
Then, we carried out extensive study to compile data and information from dependable sources (Work layout pdf). 
The  implications on the operations of our drawings were then examined by identifying both the favourable and unfavourable effects of the guide lines. 
Other strategies with and without guidelines were also taken into account, and the advantages and disadvantages of each were assessed as future lessons allowed us to add guidelines to drawings to understand the effects on accuracy.
high.result:
we were able to create a thorough and educated analysis of the use of guide lines for creating multi layered drawings and its purpose.
We were able to pinpoint the main areas of concern and offer doable suggestions to lessen any unfavourable consequences. Our investigation also identified possible avenues for development and innovation that our business could take advantage of.  In the end, our critical thinking strategy enabled us to make a more educated and calculated choice regarding how to draw multi layered drawing with guidelines.

high.situation:
In this first half of the semester, I picked civil modelling and received a design brief for our assessment. My civil modelling class was every Tuesday from 1 pm to 4 pm with the workshop being open from 9 am to 5 pm. Basic materials and equipment were supplied by the demonstrators.

TASK: The task was to create a 1:50 scale model, a report and a presentation within the time span of about six weeks. This work was also to be allocated within large groups, my group having seven people, me included. Expectations from the model include remaining within 50 cm x 60 cm x 52 cm in a 1:50 scale, having exterior and important interior walls, rooms and living spaces that have good sunlight access, reasonable room dimensions, having all rooms from the design brief, and create structurally sound and realistic architecture. The report and presentation must include all applicable subheadings and requirements from the design brief with all members given a chance to speak and contribute their worth to the assessment. This is because as student engineers, we must work together and contribute our own worth to the project at hand.

ACTION: In week two of the semester, I asked my group for their timetables and availabilities to see when everyone was able to meet to work on the project outside of class time. This week was planned by the coordinators to teach us the important content and skills required to tackle the assessment. Additionally, homework was allocated which would serve as the foundation of our models, something I did not know. 

In week three, I handed in a site analysis which ended up becoming more nuanced than previously thought. It was missing a scale and surrounding obstacles such as trees, existing buildings and infrastructure. Moreover, It had to be a physical copy to better connect the site analysis with the planning of the building. This week we were introduced to more skills to help create a concept model and create floor plans, sections and elevations of the building. 

Week four onwards was dedicated to planning and creating the model in time for the week six deadline, which ended up being extended to week seven. The group worked together but was less in sync with each other leading to mistakes. Nevertheless, appropriate floor plans were created so that the foundation, floors and rooftop of the building could be created. A digital floor plan had to be created as opposed to a physical hand-drawn plan in a ditch to gain time for building the model. Individual rooms, floors and walkways were created based on our digital plans but did not have solid plans for windows and doors. 

Once the important details of the model were created -  ie the exterior and outside room interior walls, windows and doors for each room - the report and presentation would begin. The original thought was that the model had to be complete in order to begin the report and thus would be finished alongside the presentation. The model was created using three types of cardboard, representing walls, floors and the ground, a cutting mat and scalpel/cutter, metal rulers and A3 paper to draw and create prototypes. Alternative materials used in the model include cardboard boxes from factories and a 3D printer that I already had to create furniture and little 1:50 scale people.
high.result:
By the assessment turn-in and presentation date, my group was able to develop a successful 1:50 scale model that fit most requirements in the design brief, displaying our knowledge of the site we planned to build on and was only missing small details such as tiny models for the balustrades, stairs and elevators which were all addressed in the presentation. Additionally, an in depth presentation was developed, showing our understanding of the design brief and knowledge and insight into the site, its constraints and surroundings, and how to enhance its positive attributes.

The report on the other hand felt more rushed and could need more focus and time allocated to it. The main factor that it was missing is the physical floor plans which would have helped us plan out the rooms and overall floor better as we were cutting them into segmented modules. Moreover, it was missing physical 1:100 scale sections and elevations, which were already hard to complete due to the unique but complex W shape that our building’s floor plan was in.

The model itself was completed to a satisfactory degree, it would have been better for physical floor plans to be created as they would act as stencils for every wall, window, door and hallway. Moreover, each floor was built just offset from each other leading to gaps and missalignments.

high.situation:
As a software engineering university student, I encountered many situations where critical thinking was required, especially when working on programming projects.

Task: My goal was to improve my critical thinking skills and to be able to apply them in various programming and software development situations.

Action: To achieve this goal, I first focused on understanding what critical thinking is and how it can be applied in programming and software development. I watched multiple videos and tutorials on the topic that helped me gain a better understanding of the subject.

Once I had a good foundation in the theory of critical thinking, I began to apply it to programming and software development situations. I practiced analyzing and evaluating my code from different perspectives, and I worked on developing my reasoning and problem-solving skills. I also collaborated with classmates to practice critical thinking in group programming projects (comp1050), which helped me gain a broader perspective and learn from others.
high.result:
Through my dedication to improving my critical thinking skills, I was able to see a significant improvement in my programming work. I was able to analyze code more effectively, make connections between different functions and modules, and apply creative solutions to programming problems. I also found that I was able to communicate my programming ideas more clearly and persuasively, which was particularly useful in team programming projects and group projects in general.

Overall, I am very happy with the results of my efforts to improve my critical thinking skills in the context of software engineering. Not only did it benefit me academically, but it has also helped me develop skills that will be valuable in my future career as a software engineer.

high.situation: In our workshop, we were tasked with creating an algorithm that was able to more efficiently calculate the winner of a scissors paper rock game.
high.task_action: As we had, up to that point, only worked with hard coding win-outcomes into our programs, I had to use my prior maths knowledge to take my mind away from the coding and towards a theoretical solution. I converted the problem to numbers rather than words, and asked myself what the objective was and what I needed the algorithm to do. By doing this, I was able to use create a new, theoretical solution to the algorithm problem.
high.result: The result of this method of brainstorming the concepts of my code outside of my program allowed me to break down the problem into the main ideas. It ultimatley allowed me to develop a solution to the problem by creating a mathematical algorithm, then converting it into code for the game after this.

high.situation: The situation relating to time and resource management that I experienced was the 2 weeks leading up to my HSC (High School Certificate) final Year 12 exams in 2022 (these exams would determine my final mark and what degree I would qualify for accordingly). During this 2 week period I had my 18th birthday and I had my Year 12 formal party, so it became very difficult to balance studying and celebrating. The stress of the final exams was finally settling in and I was unable to enjoy these 2 major events that a person only gets to experience once. So whilst I wanted to fully enjoy my 18th birthday party and my Year 12 formal, the thought of the final exams was constantly lingering in the back of my mind. Hence, I had to find a way to manage my time such that I didn’t regret not enjoying these events but also not regretting that I didn’t study enough for the exams.
high.task_action:
To ensure that I enjoyed my time during these 2 once in a lifetime events and also did my best in the HSC, I had to implement certain time and resource management strategies. It was through these strategies that I was able to achieve my task of maximising my efficiency whilst studying so that I would be able to spare time to enjoy my 18th birthday party and my formal. 

The very first step I took was to plan ahead, as time itself is also a vital resource. I utilised Google Calendar to break each day up into study sections. Each study section was 2 hours and I would focus upon a single subject. During these study sections I would either go through my flashcards using an app called Anki (which utilises the study technique called spaced repetition) or I would attempt practice exams and reattempt questions I got wrong. By reattempting questions I got wrong I was able to gain maximum benefits out of the practice papers which were limited. 

The next action I took was setting clear priorities. As the dates of each exam was spread out, I would spend more time on the subject that had its test the earliest. Next, I would prioritise topics within each subject whereby I knew I had gaps in my knowledge. This enabled me to not only reinforce what I knew but also ensure that I wasn’t going into my exams without knowing everything that I needed to. 

Another step that I took was to focus on one task at a time. Rather than thinking about each subject as a whole, I made a To Do list on Google Keep which enabled me to break the subject down into parts. This reduced the stress greatly as larger topics were broken down into more mentally digestible smaller topics.

The final step that I took was setting shorter deadlines. For example I would aim to get at least 5 practice exams for a subject, 1 week prior to its exam date. This would ensure I was completing my work earlier than I should have. By doing so I was constantly ahead and on top of what I needed to study.
high.result: The objectives of my actions were to maximise the use of the little time I had to study for my exams so that I would be able to enjoy my 18th birthday party and my Year 12 formal party. The result of my actions was that I was able to fully enjoy the two events with only minimal effects on my exams. I had attempted each test to the best of my ability and I was confident that I was going to receive the mark that I wanted. I hadn’t regretted enjoying the parties or wishing that I spent more time studying for my exams. Moreover I had very little stress during my exam period. When I got my ATAR (final mark) back, I had achieved the goal that I had set. However I was certain that I could’ve done better by not attending the parties, but this didn’t bother me as I knew it would’ve had no extra benefit as I achieved the goal I wanted to. In the end, I had no regrets and I achieved the initial task that I had set out to complete.

high.situation: Product cost fluctuation makes it harder to arrange the balance between worker (supplier), middle man (transport), and final buyer (seller). We are the middle man, and we want to find a solution to guarantee our money because the price may change instantly after we buy from the supplier and the buyer may not offer the price that we want. We need to make a profit to pay for the labor, transportation costs, and future investments. What is the solution?
high.task_action: Making a brainstorm session; forming a discussion group involving everyone in the organization; writing down every suggestion on the whiteboard; encouraging everyone to be creative and contribute. After we make a mind map and identify any related ideas, we select the most feasible ones to turn a profit. The main success came from the fact that there was no hierarchy postulating solutions; everyone was included, so we gathered many ideas.
high.result:
Solution 1: Suppliers prices are defined by the demand of the market. The possible solution to controlling the supplier price is to dominate the supplier market by producing a unique product.

Solution 2: Let's say we pay certain money to the labor and to the trunk for one day; however, our job finished in 2 hours. We paid for 9 hours, but since we do not have a second supplier, our 7 hours money is basically wasted. The solution is to negotiate with other suppliers and convince them to sell their products at a reasonable price.

Solution 3: Finally, we brought the product to the buyer, but the buyer changed the price to a lower price. The buyer was greedy; they wanted to make more profit. Solution is: Instead of begging them to purchase our product, we parked the trunk in front of the potential buyers and held an auction, and the highest bidder earned the right to buy the product. By doing this, we get into a more advantageous position.

Solution 4: Another reason for the disagreement with buyers is that they do not know us because we are new in this market. They are right in that they are not sure about the quality of our product. The solution is to make ourselves known to them by preserving our high quality all the time. In addition to that, we always have those products, so when the demand increases, they will know that they can reach us and we can supply the demand. This trust will get stronger with time.

Solution 5: In the long term, becoming the seller itself by building a platform

Solution 6: Building a logistic center to encourage the small supplier to deliver their products Small suppliers will be compensated with a good price, so they will trust us that we are not taking advantage of them. Trust is the best advertisement, which spreads from ear to ear. It will make us more recognized in the industry.

Solution 7: Exporting the product

high.situation:
As part of a time and resources management course, we were given weekly technical drawing tasks that required learning new skills and completing the assignment within a 3-hour timeframe. The challenge was to optimize time usage and allocate resources efficiently, so we could gain knowledge and improve our skills each week.

Task: The task was to develop an effective time and resource management strategy that would allow us to quickly learn new skills and complete the technical drawing assignments within the given timeframe, while ensuring a high level of quality.

Action: I formed a group with classmates who had similar goals and interests in time and resource management. We began by breaking down each assignment into smaller tasks and estimated the time required for each task. Then, we identified available resources, such as tutorials, guides, and expert advice from our instructor, and allocated them accordingly. We also implemented feedback from our tutor to ensure we where doing it correctly.
high.result: As a result of our diligent time and resource management efforts, we were able to successfully complete each technical drawing assignment within the given 3-hour timeframe while continuously improving our skills. Our group members reported increased confidence in their technical drawing abilities and better time management skills.

high.situation: I needed to present the code that I had written and demonstrated my understanding of the work.
high.task_action: Reviewed and critically analysed the prompts given, including prioritising questions relevant to the assessment; formulated responses appropriate to the prompts and the overall task in order to accurately showcase my knowledge of the integration between code and design as per the assessment criteria; confidently presented to the lead demonstrator and fielded questions in a professional yet critical fashion.
high.result: Portrayed understanding and proficiency in regards to both the required work and extension tasks.

high.situation: We needed to create/ design a part of a code in a programming to enable a robotics machine ( a certain kind) to pick up a brush and dip the brush in paint and write names. These project was undergone as a team and used many types of approaches to be complete as It heavily relied on the fundamental coding being right as what the coding is going to execute is the hand robot is going to use whats coded to be put in motion. The designing of the code consisted  of many types of actions for the robot to move. As a team our goal is that the robot was able to dip its brush (hand) in the paint than using what is coded write down the name of a person when name is  inputed or written on the computer. The aim of the project was that using coding and robotics we will need to be able to help create effective and efficient in more "industrialised" places.
high.task_action: Creating a working code that will be able to give the robot a motion to execute a name written in the computer. Forming a group and analysing the types of codes and language we will be working on to execute the the robot writing a name. We had to analyse which coding language was going to be create an understanding of how we wanted to code to be executed. Than we needed to brainstorm how are we going to get the measurements of each letter outputed to be written and played out in a write way or formate.
high.result: The result was that we were able to create/ design a code that successfully enabled a robot to paint a name that was inputed in the laptop.

high.situation:
During my time in ENG1000, I was part of a group project where we had to complete a building model. The project had a tight deadline, and there were several other assignments due around the same time.

Task: Our team had to prioritise the task and allocate responsibilities effectively to ensure we could submit a high-quality model on time.

Action: To prioritise effectively, we first had to discuss the task and it's respective deadline. We created a detailed project plan with clear timelines, milestones, and deliverables. Each team member was assigned specific responsibilities based on their strengths and availability for example, some focused more on the report while others focused of the physical model making. We also agreed to communicate regularly and provide timely feedback to ensure the project was on track for example we created a group chat and chatted regularly to ensure everyone was prioritising their assigned task.

In terms of focus, we agreed to set aside dedicated blocks of time each week to work on the project for example we met up on weekends, minimise distractions, and avoid multitasking. We also established a clear process for resolving any issues or conflicts that may arise during the project.
high.result: Our prioritisation and focus strategies paid off, and we were able to complete the project on time with high quality and received positive feedback from our tutors. Our team also learned valuable lessons in effective prioritisation and communication, which helps us in future group projects.

high.situation: As individuals we had to make our own toolbox from basic materials using the tools provided
high.task_action: Using the instructions and measurements given to me I formed each piece of steel with basic cuts and refined them done to near perfect pieces ready for the more challenging tools to be used. After each piece had a smooth edge that could not be dangerous I bent, welded and sandblasted the toolbox using the workshop and asking other people for advice such as the teacher and classmates when in need or when I was uncertain.
high.result: The result was having a complete and presentable toolbox that was functional and well-built, I continued after marking to paint the toolbox to make it more appealing and unique.

high.result: The result was a completed toolbox finished in the timeframe of the class.

This meant I needed more time and guidance to complete certain tasks, resulting in me finding the toolbox more challenging than others and also resulted in me falling behind the rest of the class.
high.task_action: Recognising this issue of me falling behind and finding the toolbox challenging, with the risk of not completing my toolbox in time, I tried to take more initiative and be more resourceful. I asked for more assistance from my tutor and the other staff assisting the workshop, instead of getting nothing done whilst feeling lost. I also took advantage of extra time after class to work on my toolbox, and also booked in for a scheduled timeslot to work on my toolbox outside of class.
