There were assignments like technical writing which also added up to the list. I had to come up with a solution to parallely manage multiple tasks at the same time and keep up to the schedule.

 

ACTION: I hung up with a physical deadline calendar in my room and wrote in all the due dates for my units' assignments and tests After learning that rushing to meet deadlines is not the best strategy, I began scheduling recurring reminders on my phone to get started on my assignments one week in advance. I stopped putting things off or procrastinating and started doing it regularly. I also reminded my teammates along with me so that they can tag along the schedule too. I updated our project plan in Miro board regularly with latest updates and changes and made sure everyone was aware of it.
high.result: I started to complete my assignments with 3 or 4 days in hand before the deadline. I could have better sleep which has been a physical concern for a while. I was able to produce higher-quality work on essays and other assignments, leading to better grades.  The unit's former feeling of frustration is no longer there in me. Meanwhile my team is also well progressed towards our final goal.

high.situation: We needed to build and test different parts of our contraptions according to the preliminary design. However, some contraptions wouldn't work due to the materials used or the mechanism failing and many other parameters.
high.task_action: Due to such predicaments we sat down for a meeting and discussed with our team members on how to approach the problems and improve the reliability of each of our contraptions. some of us had to re-try with different materials or entirely redesign that contraption. We wanted our design to be sustainable so we had decided on making use of the stuff we already had around us but due to some materials not delivering the results as we needed, we had to buy some stuff to make it work.
high.result: The result was having successfully working contraptions by the end of the week. Although there was a lot of trial and error involved in the process, with our perseverance and will to make it work, we had all managed to come up with solutions for all our problems.

high.situation: In a team operation focused on creating a Rube Goldberg Machine (RGM), we had a diverse group of members working together and encountered a situation where one team member demonstrated a lack of accountability and reliability throughout the project.
high.task_action:
To address this situation, we had an initial discussion to establish clear expectations and roles within the team. We recognised that accountability was crucial for the success of the project, and each member was assigned specific tasks and responsibilities. 

We had a team member who excelled in pulling the team together and fostering a strong bond, which increased productivity and cohesion. Their efforts contributed to increased productivity and collaboration within the team.

Another team member took on the role of encouraging everyone to contribute their ideas and ensured that all members felt heard and that each member had a chance to express their thoughts. These efforts helped to enhance involvement and collaboration within the team. They made active participation by consistently asking for inputs from each member and encouraging them to elaborate on their ideas. This proactive involvement created an inclusive and supportive team environment.

The third member displayed accountability by taking on specific tasks and responsibilities, breaking down the design process into smaller functions. They analyzed and scrutinized ideas, focusing on the details to ensure each component of the RGM was well-planned and optimized for performance.

However, one team member initially struggled with accountability. They missed multiple meetings and failed to fulfil their commitments on various occasions, and to deliver on commitments and promises they had made. This behaviour created some concerns within the team about their level of dedication and reliability. Despite these challenges and concerns, the team continued to support and encourage this member, giving them opportunities to contribute and holding them accountable for their commitments.
high.result:
Despite the initial challenges due to the lack of accountability from this team member, the team member ultimately managed to turn things around, they ultimately pulled through and delivered their section of the RGM to a very respectable and high standard. 

The result of addressing the accountability issue within the team was a successfully completed RGM project. Our commitment to accountability helped ensure that tasks were completed on time and to the best of our abilities. This experience showcased the importance of accountability within a team and highlighted the significance of fostering a positive and supportive team dynamic to overcome challenges and achieve goals. The final outcome of the RGM showcased the dedication and collaboration of the team, leading to a well-executed and impressive machine.

In summary, the team's accountability played a significant role in the successful completion of the project. It fostered a positive team dynamic, allowed for effective task management, and ultimately led to a high-quality end product.

high.situation: Some of the team members where struggling with the conceptual designs of their RGM.
high.task_action: During out initial brainstorming sessions the team was conducting research and pitching ideas for the final RGM, our plan was to conceptually design the complete RGM and then split it up into smaller sections for each of the team members to build. We encountered an obstacle, in retrospect it was one of our biggest. Part of the the team was struggling with coming up with creative ideas for a few sections. We then came up with a rapid fire brainstorming method, where we would shoot ideas and write them down without a second thought. After we were satisfied with the list of ideas we reviewed them and developed them.
high.result: The brainstorming session resulted into a lot of viable ideas and a few obscure ones but they helped the team unlocked their creativity and helped overcome our greatest obstacle. In addition the process made us re-evaluate our RGM and opened the doors to improvement

high.situation: We needed to prepare an oral presentation to describe our Rube Goldberg Machine.
high.task_action: Undergoing a brainstorming and critical thinking session; we decided which three team members will be the most suitable for the presentation and we divided the contents of the presentation among the presenters. Presentation slides and a speech for each presenter was also made. A lot of practice presentations were done in front of the other teammates and each teammate gave their reviews which were used to refine the presentation. Each part of the machine was described inside the presentation along with accurate descriptions about testing, improvements, precautions, and our learnings so far. The introduction included a innovative sales pitch and our problem definition and the presentation ended with a conclusion that included our final thoughts. The contents of the presentation were selected based on importance and relevance.
high.result: The result was being able to produce a desirable presentation for our Rube Goldberg Machine which was relevant and discussed everything we wanted to about our RGM.

high.situation: We were tasked to present an oral presentation on our preliminary design for our Rube Goldberg Machine.
high.task_action: During our regular team meetings, we engaged in a collaborative brainstorming session. We carefully reviewed the presentation's marking rubric and combined our collective progress for our Rube Goldberg Machine’s design thus far. With a clear understanding of the requirements, we proceeded to deliberate on the essential aspects of design for our Rube Goldberg Machine. these included its key design features, mechanisms, and overall functionality. To structure our presentation effectively, we divided it into three distinct sections: Detailed Machine Description, Limitations and Considerations, and Implementation. We then each prepared speeches and practised in advance to make sure we were within the required time limit and were confident in our delivery.
high.result: As a result, we were able to deliver an oral presentation that was smooth and professional, one which was compelling and informative, on our Rube Goldberg Machine's preliminary design.

high.situation: When constructing our RGM events, we had some difficulty with creative designs, and communicating possible solutions for our individual events.
high.task_action: To overcome this problem, we first consulted with the rest of the team. In a team this should be the first step when you are presented with any problem. Referring to your team for help is useful for building greater teamwork, communication and problem solving skills. This method is not without its flaws, as creative solutions can be difficult to physically develop into a functional product. For certain problems it can also be very difficult to explain what the root cause of the problem is, even with the benefit of communication software like chatrooms and phone calls. Each team member is constructing and conducting tests individually in their own workspaces, and it can be very hard to offer solutions when each team member can't physically see the construction and determine the cause of the problem. After consolidating with the team, if the issue persists, it is also helpful to search resources online for similar problems or alternative methods for solutions.
high.result: The result from communicating solutions with team members is a stronger team. Sharing ideas with other people is a useful way to expand creative thinking skills, and develop more creative solutions to problems. The longer a team works together, they tend to develop similar ways of thinking and generating solutions, which helps in understanding the problem a team member is proposing. It then becomes easier to conceive a solution in a way that the team member will be able to understand and succeed upon. Online resources can help the team member find inspiration to potentially solve their problem or other collective issues the team may have. Online resources (e.g. videos) provide the ability to visualise a similar solution to your given problem. This will make it easier for the team member to solve their problem, developing their problem solving skills further.

high.situation: Our group needed to manage our time and resources to complete milestones for the final RGM.
high.task_action: Employing appropriate project management strategies to increase efficiency of both time and resources invested in the project. Clearly defined the goals and project scope early, and made sure everyone comprehended the project scope through using effective communication. Furthermore, we evaluated project risks such as scope creep and employed mitigation plans to lessen risks posed to the project. To ensure the completion of milestones by the deadline with maximum time and resource efficiency we employed time management strategies such as using the Priority Matrix software (based on the Eisenhower Method).  In addition to Priority Matrix we used a Gantt chart to have a more comprehensive guide to key milestones in this project.
high.result: The result was efficient teamwork where time and resources were managed appropriately, which helped us greatly to avoid project risks and complete project milestones on time.

high.situation: When approaching any engineering task, using data driven decision making is absolutely crucial. Collecting data in engineered structures allows engineers to have a clearer and scientifically supported understanding of their mechanism. By incorporating derived data into your decisions surrounding your product you will be able to produce a higher quality result. Through learning this during the ENGG1050 unit, the quality of our RGM machine was thus called into question when collecting data on the reliability of the machine to complete each individual function. We had many events in the function that originally had a low reliability score when tested 10 times. It was decided that we would need to have a discussion on how to improve these events and use our current data to assist our decisions.
high.task_action: Therefore, by utilising this method we held a team discussion on what areas of the questioned events was causing the weakness by analysing and assessing our current data. We next changed necessary variables in each event until the accuracy met the satisfaction of the entire team. This step was repeated a number of times to ensure the highest result was obtained, such as assessing whether a change in the weight of the ball, reinforcement of elements of the events or a multitude of things being incorporated simultaneously would produce the highest reliability score. When incorporating this tactic into our RGM machine my team and I were able to significantly improve our machine's accuracy and reliability, therefore increasing the overall quality of our product.
high.result: Through my team's shared desire to produce a high quality machine that could be reproducible due to high reliability scores we used our collection of data to effect our decisions of each individual event. This proved to be an incredibly efficient and effective tactic for our RGM. We are all very pleased with the new reliability results of our RGM with each event achieving at least 90% reliability.

high.situation: Throughout the second half of the semester, I have been unable to attend workshops as I had suddenly found employment during the break that required full-time attendance while training.
high.task_action: As my training for work now clashed with the time of the ENGG1050 workshop and my other classes, I had to make the right judgement on how to handle the situation and make trade-offs to achieve balance in my commitment to work and uni. As soon as I was aware of the fact that I had to attend my training full-time during when my classes would run, I had to make sure whether I could really commit to starting that job, as uni is my first priority. Initially, as was communicated to me, attending training meant I'd only have to miss the first two workshops and so I just let my team know of the situation and completed any work that needed to be done. I also stayed in contact with the team and talked to them regularly, especially before and after workshops to get caught up with everything. This way I could attend my work training while also not sabotaging my uni performance. As training finished, I learned that I'd have to do full-time consolidation for a few weeks as well. With the ENGG1050 workshop and my practicals that are hurdles, there was no way I could skip them all. So, after speaking with my manager I was able to obtain a few days off for my other mandatory practicals, and for the ENGG1050 workshop, as it wasn't a hurdle, I decided to continue with ensuring all the required work was being done, especially for the final RGM presentation.
high.result: By communicating effectively with my team and making the appropriate trade offs, I achieved a balance between work and uni. I can confidently say that our final RGM presentation is something our whole team can be proud of.

high.situation:
During our team project, I was assigned the task of constructing a Rube Goldberg machine for our RGM project for ENG1050. Our goal was to create an elaborate, functional machine that would demonstrate various scientific principles in an entertaining way.

Task: My task was to ensure effective collaboration among team members, manage the project timeline, and oversee the design and construction process. It was crucial to maintain a professional and respectful environment where everyone felt valued and motivated to contribute their best. 

Action: I facilitated regular progress meetings to track our advancements, address any challenges, and provide support where needed. I actively listened to team members' suggestions and incorporated their feedback into the design process. I encouraged brainstorming sessions and promoted a culture of collaboration, where every team member felt comfortable expressing their opinions. I also encouraged the team to maintain a clean and organised workspace, promoting professionalism and efficiency which helped our team to work effectively and coherently.
high.result: As a result of my professional behaviour and effective teamwork, we successfully created a Rube Goldberg machine that met all the project requirements. The project not only demonstrated scientific principles but also showcased our team's ability to collaborate and work together in a professional manner.

high.situation: Our team encountered a situation where my friend forgot to submit a weekly assessment, which could have resulted in a significant loss of marks. However, when questioned about it, he took responsibility and honestly admitted that he had accidentally messed up the times.
high.task_action: In response to the situation, we decided to address the issue as a team. We approached my friend with understanding and discussed the importance of accountability and timely submissions. Recognizing the potential impact on our overall grades, we collectively decided to take immediate action.
high.result: By acknowledging his mistake and taking accountability, my friend displayed a commendable level of honesty and integrity. This incident served as a valuable learning experience for the entire team, highlighting the significance of punctuality and responsibility in meeting deadlines. As a team, we quickly emailed our teacher to explain the situation and requested an opportunity to complete the weekly assessment with minimal penalty. This incident not only strengthened our sense of unity but also emphasized the importance of effective communication and support within the team.

high.situation: The team was struggling with communication revolving around team meetings and assignment of the minute taking and writing the reflection.  Due to unforeseen circumstances, the rotation of the roles had been thrown off and uncertainty remained about who was responsible for what role
high.task_action:
Undergoing a brainstorming session; forming a small working group with the problem statement clearly articulated and using the gallery method of brainstorming we devised several viable options.  Subsequently,  we evaluated each of the suggested options and rank them in order of importance, based on the parameters (such as weight, complexity, and manufacturability). 

After intervention from our tutor and a unified team understanding of the improvements needed to be made, the team organised for  two people to be given their respective roles of writing minutes and the reflection with additional time given immediately after the Monday class to address the specific topic of reflection and the quality of the minutes.
high.result: The result was we were granted the leniency to resubmit our previous weeks reflection and improved upon some of our previous marks. We also had a more structured understanding of who was responsible for what role leading into this week.

high.situation: One requirement of ENGG1050 is to hold weekly meetings to work on our RGM machine. This poses a challenge due to the varying schedules of our team members, where only a few members can consistently make it to the zoom meeting. The question arises of how we are going to make this work.
high.task_action:
Keeping every team member up to date and on track with tasks ensures the ongoing development of our project. Consistency and communication is important not only to execute this project, but to do it to a high standard. With this in mind, we knew that we had to work with our unaligned schedules. Some questions that arise include how we would work with our unpredictable schedules. How would we make up for the missing members?

Our solution includes using zoom, where a delegated chairperson would assign each person’s weekly work to be completed. Whatsapp would be used to relay this information, so that the remaining members are informed of their responsibilities for the week.
high.result: This is a trade-off of not having a set time for a live discussion, for our busy schedules. This is made up with the whatsapp group chat where no information is lost, only delayed. This ensures the balance of the work, and creates the expectation for everyone to complete the work despite meetings not being attended by everyone. The scheduling issue for each meting has been resolved and now everyone will be up to date thanks to utilising online resources.

high.situation: The past twelve weeks have consisted primarily of elaborate and fluid teamwork to complete our Rube Goldberg Machine. Having had the ability to work amongst a group of five, it is without doubt that we all come from different backgrounds and life experiences. Different personalities and different ways of thinking can complicate the fluidity of a team. I was faced with the requirement to develop my communication and teamworking skills to enhance my performance within the group; and to produce work that is consistent with my team.
high.task_action: Understanding my strengths and weaknesses was the first step in developing my teamwork and communication skills. I completed a LinkedIn learning course on interpersonal communication to broaden my understanding of forms of communication, and their importance in professional environments. Acknowledging that our group project required collaborative effort to produce an effective and fluid machine, I suggested the use of an external platform such as WhatsApp to maintain communication amongst each other. Through further discussion, we set group rules which would ensure equality and maintain open communication within the group. Additionally, I completed the DISC and OCEAN analysis tests and compared the results amongst my group within the initial weeks of our project. Adopting the skills demonstrated through the LinkedIn course, I suggested we utilise our discovered strengths, whilst also working on our weaknesses, to be able to perform to our best capabilities. Rotating everyone through each role weekly allowed the opportunity to enhance our individual weaknesses.
high.result: By using WhatsApp to communicate throughout the week, we ensured that all our designated tasks were completed in a timely manner. This ensured that project checkpoints adhered to our timeline and reduced the risk of failure to deliver. It allowed us to seek guidance and/or provide support where required. We were kept informed and were able to keep on top of any missed tasks and or need for change. Furthermore, by rotating through a new role each week, I was able to foster an environment of growth in which supported both myself and my team members to utilize our strengths, whilst also working on our weaknesses. Developing and utilising my communication strategies within my team allowed us to collaboratively produce a functioning and fluid Rube Goldberg Machine.

high.situation: In the week 7 STGA I had the opportunity to present along side two team mates to half the ENGG1050 tutor group.

high.result: The team did deliver in week 7, although there was constructive feedback directed at my presentation section. I'll pay closer attention to timing in our next presentation.

TASK:      Our team had to divide the project into various aspects, including market research, product development, pricing strategy, and promotional activities. high.result: Our teamwork and effective communication paid off. Our group delivered a well-coordinated and comprehensive marketing plan during the presentation. high.situation: A recent example of learning in my own life would include learning about prominent methods used within AI.      Task: My task was to teach myself some of the major methods used in AI, to a level where I would understand the methods well enough to implement them from scratch. I wanted to learn a wide variety of methods, ranging from simple methods like linear regression, to more complex models and algorithms, such as using a transformer model, as this would allow me to solve a multitude of different problems.      Action: To start, I decided I wanted to get a high-level overview to guide my next steps, so I searched for what some of the major techniques used in AI were, on Google. I then decided to look further into these specific methods, such as linear regression, or a basic neural network. When looking further into these methods I made sure to collect information from many sources, including sources such as online lectures, lecture slides, forums, YouTube tutorials/lessons, blogs, and various other websites. If any inconsistencies were present between sources, I would take the information that was most common amongst the most credible sources. To test my knowledge, I would apply it to example problems. When my results were not satisfactory, I would continue researching the method until I could get it to work on these problems. To memorise the things I had learned, I would make flashcards in Anki so that I could repeatedly revise these methods using spaced-repetition.
high.result: The result of this endeavour was that I was able to implement many popular methods used in modern AI-based applications and understood them well enough to be able to replicate them from scratch. Most of my implementations are on my personal computer, but I have shared some online, to show others. And I have retained this information through the use of spaced-repetition using Anki.

high.situation: During my tenure at Mercedes-Benz, I encountered a myriad of challenges spanning mechanical, electrical, and software-related issues. While many of these complications were resolved efficiently, there were instances where certain faults—such as malfunctioning engine lights or navigation system errors—would re-emerge despite initial repair. These complex issues ranged from damaged sensors to software corruption or even internal mechanical failure. Such cases required extensive diagnostics and the specialized expertise of senior Diagnostic Technicians (DTs), extending the resolution time considerably.      TASK:      Our team was entrusted with the expeditious and accurate identification and rectification of these multifaceted faults. The collaborative approach involved professionals with varying skill sets pooling their expertise not only to remediate the problem at hand but also to engage in cross-training within the team. The urgency of these tasks was heightened by customer expectations for swift resolutions, often necessitating rapid turn-around times for vehicle return.      ACTION:      We adhered to a standard operating procedure that commenced with onboard diagnostic tests (OBD), followed by an array of both road and in-house workshop evaluations. These rigorous assessments aimed to uncover the root cause of the faults. If a resolution was elusive within the first two hours of diagnostic efforts, the protocol dictated that we escalate the matter to a DT. This senior intervention often proved pivotal in isolating and subsequently rectifying the issue.
high.result: Upon ascertaining the origin of the fault, remedial measures were swiftly implemented. In instances requiring new or replacement parts, a detailed quote was prepared and submitted to the customer for approval. Post-authorization, we would proceed with the necessary repairs and subsequently return the fully functional vehicle to its owner. This collaborative and streamlined approach not only optimized workflow but also significantly enhanced customer satisfaction rates.

high.situation: I was in one of several groups in a Maths competition in which we had to solve questions of varying difficulty for a prize consisting of a basket of sweets.
high.task_action: We as a group selected several questions of varying type and difficulty, and then attempted to solve them separately within two minutes. After the two minutes were up, we put together our answers and determined who was more suited to specific types and difficulties, and split up the work accordingly.
high.result: The result was that we were able to work with increased efficiency, with multiple questions being solved at a time by the person most capable of solving them quickly and correctly.

high.situation: During the tense environment of my 12th-grade final exam, I was positioned next to a copycat girl. Initially, her subtle glances at my paper didn't bother me much, as I  valued concentrating on my own task. After hours, her pestering grew annoying. She kept requesting me to repeat my answers quietly. To protect my academic integrity, I cautioned her to fear the examiner's keen eye because expulsion was guaranteed if he saw us. Though severe, my words didn't discourage her. Then suddenly she boldly slid my paper across the desk into the dangerous no-man's land between us. My heart raced between right and obsessive craving for an easy way out as I watched my hard-earned work teeter towards deceit. My nerve-wracking choice determined my fate and character.      TASK / ACTION :      My tolerance for the girl's flagrant plagiarism on my paper was becoming thin. I asked her calmly but firmly to stop because her behaviour was unfair to me and violated the standards of honesty and academic integrity that should be upheld during tests. With every word, I made an effort to emphasise the importance of honesty, and our academic career should be a reflection of our genuine abilities, not a mere facade based on dishonesty. I spoke about the moral obligation we had to uphold to both the responsibilities we carried as students and the moral duty we owed to ourselves and our education. She kept trying to ignore me, but I was adamant that I would uphold these principles. I came to the realisation that our behaviour in the test room would define who we are as people more so than the grade we obtained. This was more than just an intellectual test; it was also a moral character test. And I was determined to do it while upholding my moral principles.
high.result: My pulse raced as the teacher's attention focused on our desk. Surprisingly audaciously, the girl seated next to me charged that I had taken her paper and placed it in the center. I was called out in front of the entire class as being a dishonest student. The most painful part of my friends' shock was watching them try to share this bogus story with the friend they knew. The humiliation pierced faith with a profound, knife-like wound. However, I refused to back down and said the truth. The trick was eventually exposed by the teacher, and justice was done. Although the humiliation hurt, it strengthened my resolve to be honest and ethical, and my friends gradually came to realise the reality. And that girl had been expelled for the final exam.

high.situation: During my role as an Assistant Instructor at Bangladesh Karate Federation, I faced communication issues within a  team. high.result: * ENHANCED COLLABORATION: We achieved better team understanding, reduced confusion, and increased overall productivity.   	* MEETING DEADLINES: Our project was delivered on time thanks to improved communication.   	* POSITIVE TEAM DYNAMICS: Morale improved, and client satisfaction rose, positively impacting our company's reputation.

high.situation: A project involved constructing a new house, but we faced challenges in coordinating different tasks and managing the construction timeline.

high.result: By breaking down the project into distinct tasks and assigning roles, we streamlined the construction process. Each team member knew their responsibilities, leading to efficient task execution. As a result, the construction stayed on schedule, and the house was completed within the expected time period. This experience highlighted the importance of clear task distribution and effective project management in achieving successful outcomes in construction projects.

high.situation: The engineering team is tasked with designing and developing a machine that can safely and stably lift the POWER TRANSFORMER CORE vertically from a horizontal position. high.situation: As a student in the 12th grade, I was focused on my final examinations, board tests, college entrance exams, and being an active member of the student union.With several studies, extracurricular activities, and college application requirements, it was a vital year with a heavy schedule. ACTION: I started out by clearly defining my academic aims and objectives.After that, I determined which issues and topics needed greater focus and ranked them accordingly.I made a thorough study plan for each subject, allotted time to respond to questions from other students, and made sure my topics were covered completely.I made the decision to stop being distracted by things like excessive social media usage while I was studying.Additionally, I planned regular short breaks during my studies to refuel, kept up a healthy diet, and followed a regular schedule to be physically and mentally fit.
high.result: As a result of these actions,I was able to achieve excellent grades on my final exams, surpass my academic goals, have a smooth college application process, maintain a balance between my academic and extracurricular commitments, and develop time management and focus skills that will help me now and in the future.

high.situation: Oral presentation is the main and most precious step in both group and individual presentations. high.situation: As an engineering student working on a project involving LED control using Arduino, I faced a challenge in creating an efficient and visually appealing LED light display for class task.
high.task_action: My task was to design and implement a dynamic LED light show that could respond to user input and create captivating visual effects. To achieve this, I began by clearly defining the project scope and objectives. I identified that the LED display needed to be interactive, allowing users to change patterns and colors. Then I conducted thorough research on Arduino programming and LED control techniques. During brainstorming sessions, I explored different ideas for the LED patterns and user interaction. I considered options like sound-reactive patterns, color-changing effects, and motion sensing. After narrowing down my ideas, I created a prototype using an Arduino board and a small set of LEDs. This prototype allowed me to test various coding approaches and see how the LED patterns would look in practice. I divided the coding tasks among team members based on their strengths. I focused on developing code for responsive user input and worked on creating visually appealing LED patterns. I continuously tested my code and made iterative improvements. Once I had individual components working, I integrated them into a cohesive system. I invited users to interact with our LED display and collected feedback for further refinement.
high.result: My actions resulted in a successful LED light display for the class task. Users could interact with the LED display by adjusting patterns and colors through a user-friendly interface. The LED patterns we designed were visually stunning and captivating, enhancing the overall appeal of the art installation. The system was responsive to both user input and external factors, such as sound or motion, depending on the chosen mode. Users and viewers of the art installation provided positive feedback, expressing their enjoyment and fascination with the LED display. As engineering student, I gained valuable hands-on experience in Arduino programming, electronics, teamwork, and user-centered design, which was beneficial for my future projects and coursework.      In summary, by following the STAR framework, I successfully tackled the challenge of creating an interactive LED light display, demonstrating effective problem-solving and collaboration skills in an engineering context.

high.situation: In the 9th grade, I encountered a significant challenge in a science fair project where we had to create a working quadcopter from scratch, incorporating various components like motors, sensors, propellers, batteries, and a flight controller.
high.task_action: I worked with classmates to perform a lot of study on these components and their compatibility. high.situation: one of the final exercises of one of the worksheet for the arduino module was that we needed to program a game using a 7 segment display. there would be a top cycle and a bottom cycle and when the two collided and a button was pressed at the same time a led needed to light up. The problem was that the starting segments for the cycles needed to be random, but if you would let the segments just be random you would have a 50 percent change of having a situation where you cannot win the game.
high.task_action: Programming requires you to go through ideas step by step (especially during debugging). As such, i took the approach of beginning with programming a fully deterministic version of the game (same beginning segments always) and slowly adding more randomness while making sure that i didn't introduce the unwinnable situations untill i couldn't add any more randomness.
high.result: The result was that the game was not fully random, since that would result into unwinnable situations. However, I did add as much randomness as I could so that the game was still not deterministic. This resulted into me getting the points for the exercise.

My academic performance increased, and I had more leisure time to relax and pursue my interests. I also discovered that I could better manage my time so that I could participate in extracurricular activities without disregarding my academic obligations or jeopardising my personal life. Overall, these steps resulted in a less stressful and more balanced student life, proving the value of proactive time and resource management.

high.situation: IN WEEK 4 WHEN WE WERE WORKING ON THE IMPLEMENTATION OF A 7 SEGMENT DISPLAY. MY GOAL WAS TO LEARN HOW TO USE 1D AND 2D ARRAYS AND HOW TO LOOP THROUGH THEM TO REACH MY FINAL RESULT.      Task: My task was to write code for the arduino 7 segment display such that it would count up to 15 in hexadecimal. Instead of taking the easy way and just hard coding this project I decided that I would try to learn how to use 1D and 2D arrays. This became an issue as I was mainly proficient in java and although they are quite similar in the structure of the code. The use of built-in constructors was non-existent in c++ and caused quite a hurdle. An example of this is arr.length gives the length of the array in java but nothing exists like that in c++.       This tested not only my Programming Skills but also my problem solving skills as I had to find a way around using built in constructors and use my programming knowledge to create a work around where they were no longer needed. In the end it was a valuable test of my skills for the future so I can look back on my mistakes and not make the same ones again.
high.result: as a result of my programming skills and problem-solving efforts. I successfully created a functioning hexadecimal counter from 1-15 using a 2D array without built in constructors. Although there may be a better way to do what I have done, I found that getting the experience of solving small problems myself allows me to more deeply understand my knowledge of programming and further improve my experiences.      In this situation, my programming/coding skills were essential for writing the code to control the Arduinos 7 segment display, while my problem-solving skills were crucial for addressing various challenges and understanding where I have gone wrong and what I have to fix.

high.situation: Engineering has had a profound and far-reaching impact on both society and the environment. It has played a vital role in shaping the modern world, contributing to both progress and challenges. Here, we will explore the various impacts that engineering has had on society and the environment.
high.task_action: The actions taken by engineers or overall, the impact of engineering on society and the environment, has been clearly shown, as engineers themselves design, construct and maintain the systems, buildings and technologies that enhance societies quality of life.      Infrastructure development, healthcare advancements, energy generation and efficiency, are among a few of the impacts engineering has had on society.      Engineering’s influence on the environment exhibits a diverse range of impacts, covering both favorable and the adverse aspects. Detrimental outcomes of engineering on the environment include pollution, deforestation, and the generation of waste, among other adverse effects. As well as, engineering also yields beneficial impacts, such as the promotion of renewable energy, the development of sustainable infrastructure, and improvements in energy efficiency, are among many positive contributions.       In order to improve connection and facilitate the efficient movement of people and products, infrastructure development engineers have constructed essential infrastructures including roads, bridges, and transportation systems. Medical device, diagnostic, treatment, and outcome improvements have all improved thanks to engineering innovations in healthcare. Engineers have created and improved energy sources like solar, wind, and hydraulic power sources, which have led to the clean and more effective production of energy.       THE NEGATIVE IMPACTS ENGINEERING HAS HAD ON THE ENVIRONMENT:       Industrial operations, transportation, and energy production have had a significant negative impact on the environment, contaminating the air and water supplies, endangering human health, and degrading ecosystems. Some engineering projects call for the development of industrial and urban areas, which has resulted in habitat degradation and deforestation, endangering biodiversity. The majority of engineering projects have all led to the production of solid and hazardous waste, both of which are harmful to human health and ecosystems.      THE POSITIVE IMPACTS ENGINEERING HAS HAD ON THE ENVIRONMENT:      Having developed and promoted alternative energy sources like solar, wind, and hydropower, we as a society are using fewer fossil fuels and emitting fewer greenhouse gas emissions. Engineering has also resulted in significant advancements in the energy efficiency of industries, transportation, and infrastructure, lowering energy consumption and having a less negative impact on the environment. Engineers are always coming up with new and better ways to build a building with sustainability in mind, including green building techniques, efficient transportation methods, and eco-friendly building materials.
high.result: The effects of engineering on society and the environment are quite varies. Through technical advancements, economic expansion, and healthcare innovations, it has improved society’s quality of life. Although, it has also brought forth problems for the environment, such as pollution, climate change, and resource depletion. Engineers are putting more emphasis on sustainable solutions to lessen these negative impacts and creating a more balanced future for society as well as the environment.

high.situation: During the transition from high school to university, I found myself at a pivotal moment in my personal life/journey. high.situation: In my digital media course, the task was to create a multimedia project that would effectively convey a social issue using a combination of images, videos, audio, and text.
high.task_action: Here's how I approached the task using the STAR framework:      	*   SITUATION: The project was multifaceted, demanding the application of critical thinking to navigate its complexity.      	*   TASK: My task was to select a relevant social issue, strategize how to present it using multimedia elements, and ensure that the narrative flowed logically and engagingly.      	*   ACTION: Engaging critical thinking, I meticulously researched and identified a suitable social issue. Forming a team, we outlined a comprehensive approach. Throughout the project, I critically evaluated each media element's alignment with the narrative and purpose. I structured a storyboard that logically connected images, audio, and text to captivate and sustain viewers' attention.      Critical thinking played a crucial role in each decision – from sequencing visuals to harmonizing audio narration. Every choice was underpinned by thoughtful analysis.
high.result: The outcome was a multimedia project that effectively communicated the chosen social issue. The narrative was cohesive, guiding the audience through the issue's intricacies. The selection of visuals, paired with an appropriate mix of audio and text, contributed to the project's impact. The project not only fulfilled the assignment's requirements but also encouraged viewers to engage their critical thinking, stimulating reflection on the social issue's implications.      Furthermore, the process of applying critical thinking strengthened our team's collaboration. Carefully evaluating each media element's relevance sparked insightful discussions, resulting in a unified final product that transcended individual contributions.      Through this experience, I recognized the pivotal role of critical thinking in multimedia creation. As I move forward, the skills I've acquired will empower me to approach future projects with a strategic mindset. The insights gained have equipped me to meticulously plan, thoughtfully curate content, and align each element to deliver a compelling narrative.

high.situation: During my first year of college at Macquarie, the last few weeks of the semester, I had to turn in multiple assignments for different courses. One of the assignments was to write a report on one of the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). Simultaneously, I had other course and programming assignments as well.      TASK: My primary task was to manage my time and resources efficiently to research, draft, and finalize the report without compromising its quality. Additionally, I needed to ensure that my other academic commitments weren't neglected.      ACTION: I began by breaking down the research paper into smaller tasks: topic selection, literature review, drafting, revision, and finalization. I allocated specific blocks of time for each task, ensuring buffer periods for unforeseen challenges. For resources, I prioritize recent articles in Google Scholar that are most relevant to my topic. To keep track of my progress, I write the progress of my report on a calendar, which allows me to set milestones and monitor my progress. For the other courses, I allocated specific hours during the day to make me focus on other courses.
high.result: By effectively allocating my time and using resources judiciously, I submitted the report well before the deadline, receiving a high score of 80%. My structured approach also ensured that my other coursework was completed on time, and I was well-prepared for the end-of-semester assessments.

high.situation: I was working at a company while also pursuing my university degree when a situation arose where I was asked to take on a temporary job position. One of our colleagues had to take an emergency leave, and my manager approached me to cover for her during her absence.       TASK:      The task at hand was to step into this temporary role and ensure the smooth continuation of the department's operations. However, this request presented a significant challenge as my university schedule was already quite hectic. I needed to prioritise my responsibilities effectively and find a way to balance my work and academic commitments.      ACTION:      Assessing My Current Commitments: To start, I took a step back and carefully reviewed my existing commitments, both at work and at university. This involved assessing my course workload, assignment deadlines, and class schedules.      Creating a Planner: To address the challenge of managing my time efficiently, I created a detailed planner. This planner incorporated all my university classes, assignment deadlines, and work hours. It allowed me to visualise and focus on my commitments and identify potential time slots for focused work.      Prioritising Tasks: With the planner in place, I prioritised tasks based on their importance and urgency. I allocated dedicated time slots for studying, attending classes, and fulfilling my work responsibilities. I made sure to accommodate the temporary role without compromising my academic progress.
high.result: The actions I took to prioritise and focus on my commitments yielded several positive outcomes:      -       I was able to effectively cover the temporary position during my colleague's leave without any major disruptions in our department's operations.      -       Despite the added workload, I was able to maintain my academic performance and meet my university commitments, including attending classes and submitting assignments on time.      -       This experience also improved my time management and focus skills and ability to balance multiple responsibilities efficiently.      -       Both my manager and colleagues appreciated my dedication and ability to step up during a challenging time. This experience also strengthened my professional relationships within the company.      In conclusion, by carefully prioritising my tasks, creating a detailed planner, and maintaining open communication, I successfully managed the temporary job position while continuing my university studies, demonstrating my ability to effectively prioritise and focus on critical tasks in a demanding situation.

high.task_action: Initially, I was struggling to complete the assigned work during the allocated workshop time so I began working on the Arduino exercises before class, attempting to complete as many of the tasks as possible without the assistance of the tutors.

high.situation: Our group project was to build a civil model of a residential housing complex for students living on campus. We were entrusted with developing a comprehensive report that outlined the entire process as well as the substantial research undertaken throughout the construction of this civil model.
high.task_action: In order to tackle this project effectively, our group established a set of defined tasks. First, we separated the project into several phases to ensure that everyone understood their roles. We designed a defined timeline, including milestones for research, design, construction, and report compilation.      We began the design process after conducting extensive study. We brainstormed layout concepts together, taking into account elements such as student comfort, space efficiency, and sustainability.      We began building the physical model of the housing complex when the design was completed. We divided this task into smaller groups, with each group in charge of developing a certain building or area. This enabled us to work concurrently, enhancing efficiency and decreasing building time.      Our efforts yielded a very detailed civil model for the student housing complex, as well as a comprehensive report. Every facet of the project was covered in the study, including architectural design, environmental issues, cost analysis, and construction details. Our lecturer and classmates praised our model and report for its depth and expertise.      Furthermore, throughout the project, our collaborative approach promoted a sense of cooperation and enhanced our individual talents in research, design, and project management. This experience not only resulted in a successful project, but it also supplied us with essential skills and expertise for future civil engineering and project development endeavours.

high.situation: Our internship group was tasked with designing an e-commerce website with limited resources.
high.task_action: In our task, we were tasked to create a fully functional e-commerce website by using React. Features such as dynamic design, user experience, and product showcasing were to be implemented along with a fully functional backend host, used to process payments and such. This was done however, by using no external resources or libraries (One major library such as ReactBootstrap) and could only be done only within the language itself. We did this by researching these pre-designed functions commonly used within external libraries, assessing their purpose as well as their efficiency in design.
high.result: Our result was being able to break down the purposes of each pre-designed function and use parts of those pieces to produce a successful website with all the qualities of one which relied on external libraries, therefore satisfying our mentor.

high.situation: During my internship at a leading engineering firm, I was tasked with leading a team of engineers on a critical project to design and implement a sustainable energy solution for a remote community.      Task: The task at hand was complex, involving tight deadlines, budget constraints, and the need to navigate technical challenges in an environmentally sensitive area. My role was to ensure the team's collaboration and to provide effective leadership throughout the project.      Action: To address this situation, I first organized a team meeting to establish clear project goals and roles. I encouraged open communication and a culture of innovation. We conducted extensive research, collaborated with local experts, and conducted on-site assessments to gather data.
high.result: Through our collective efforts, we successfully designed a sustainable energy system that met the community's needs while minimizing environmental impact. The project not only provided a reliable energy source but also improved the community's overall quality of life. It was a significant achievement for both the team and me, demonstrating the power of effective leadership in engineering endeavors.

high.situation: The team comes across issues when their work spaces accidentally overlap, and accidentally create problems for one another.
high.task_action: Gather together, clearly inform the rest of the team of your work, what other areas it could affect (say the graphics team affecting the modelling team), together, the team can decide on a structure that would let everyone do their work, and communicate clearly with the other workers whenever bigger changes are being made.
high.result: The result was a smoother workflow for the entire team, and better communication throughout, letting one's coworkers know of potential problems ahead of time, so it can be worked out ahead of time.

high.situation: We had to collaborate as a team to create a design for student housing that would align with both the site's conditions and environmental considerations.
high.task_action: Our goal was straightforward: design student housing that’s both practical and comfortable for its residents. We divvied up the tasks, and I took charge of the floor plans and material selection. However, this was overall a team effort. I focused on creating floor plans that maximised available space while ensuring plenty of natural light. To keep everyone aligned and encourage fresh perspectives, we held regular team meetings. In these sessions, we shared our progress and threw around some constructive feedback. This collaborative approach helped us fine-tune our individual parts and make sure they fit well within the overall project.
high.result: In the end, we produced a design that met our expectations and also resonated well with the client. While we did overlook some elements like heating, the strength of our project lay in the selection of sustainable materials and the efficiency of the floor plans. Notably, we exceeded the client's environmental efficiency goals by 15%. Encouraged by this, we each took sections of the project to further refine as we prepared for the final presentation.

high.situation: We needed to cohesively design and construct student accommodation located at Macquarie University within a limited three week time period.
high.task_action: Due to the time constraints of only attending one workshop class per week we had to arrange to meet up outside of class periods. We communicated regularly via WhatsApp to organise several days throughout the week to work on the project. Despite the extracurricular activities of team members, there was a strong desire to set aside time to work collaboratively to complete this project.
high.result: The result was effective communication between the group members and contributing equally to the group project.

high.situation: Using an arduino kit and breadboard, we were instructed to code and wire up a circuit to control the spin of a servo using a potentiometer.
high.task_action: When attempting to complete the task, I utilized critical thinking and problem solving skills to approach the problem step by step. To begin I first gathered knowledge and understanding on the various components of the circuit in order to avoid repeatedly stopping what I was doing to go and research, which would not only take up more time in the long run but also break concentration and train of thought. To accomplish this I first read the provided worksheet explaining what a 'library' was in order to understand why we needed servo.h in order for our code, and subsequently our circuit, to function. Afterwards, I examined the example code that was given and further researched what each new/unfamiliar command did.       After I understood what the purpose of each command was, I decided to wire up my circuit first so that I would know exactly how many components I would be working with/had to code for before I began to write my code. If I attempted to write up the code first before I put together the circuit, I knew that if there was an unexpected component that came up, I would potentially have to go back and change other parts of the code I had already written, thus wasting time. Furthermore, when I was creating my circuit, I put focus into using the minimum amount of components necessary in order to avoid cluttering up/over complicating the design and hindering functionality.      Once I had finished the circuit, I created my code using the new commands I had learnt as well as the commands for potentiometers that I had been taught in a prior workshop. Whilst I was creating the code, I ran into some problems where my code had errors and would not work as expected. When this happened, I looked for help from my classmates as well as tutors, adopting an open mind and taking on their constructive feedback and considering alternatives that would better improve the functionality of my code.
high.result: the result was a functioning circuit in which by changing the potentiometer position I could rotate the servo in a direction of my choosing.

high.situation: Firstly, I'd like to note that I currently work as a steelfixer in the construction industry to make a living, even though my actual field of study is software engineering. On one particular occasion, my co-workers and I were tasked with setting up steel protection for a clean water supply pipe in a major suburb in Sydney.
high.task_action: We began by reviewing engineer plan. This entailed ensuring all measurements, such as the concrete's height and width, all fundamental aspects of steelfixing were clarified and comprehended. With all the necessary information in hand, my co-workers and I distributed roles: deciding who would cut the steel, who would handle measurements, and who would be responsible for lifting and transporting the bars.
high.result: Despite the initial plan not being entirely fit to real world conditions, our team proficiently and independently addressed the problem, successfully repairing the clean water supply pipe. After our intervention, we confidently handed over the restored system to the lead engineer for final evaluation.

high.situation: During week 6 Arduino workshops, I was tasked with brainstorming and writing code for a 4-bit binary adder. The project was challenging due to the complex wiring and coding requirements, involving both hardware and software components.
high.task_action: To prepare, I did preliminary research on binary addition and related operators to build a foundational understanding. I listed down unanswered questions to ensure that I arrived in class ready to seek clarification from the tutor team. Once in class, I began the brainstorming process for wiring and coding,  constantly seeking input from classmates and tutors to develop the hardware setup.      I followed a logical sequence of tasks, progressing from foundational research to detailed questions, and from smaller tasks to bigger ones:      -Preliminary Research      -Question List      -Classroom Brainstorm      -Task Prioritization      -Hardware Development      -Coding
high.result: By systematically employing critical thinking throughout the process, I successfully designed the hardware in class and developed the code for the 4-bit binary adder. While I did encounter challenges, particularly in coding and physical interface, these obstacles offered valuable lessons. They taught me how to think logically and problem-solve effectively by leveraging available resources and collaboration. As a result, I was able to optimize my performance, gaining both practical skills and insights into the use of a structured approach to complex problems.

high.situation: poor time management during unavoidable familial/personal issues.      Task: develop strategies for efficiently managing time and resources.      Action:      	*   Assessment: evaluation of daily and weekly schedule, identifying where the conflicts were occurring.      	*   Prioritization: ranking academic tasks, work commitments, and personal time based on their significance and urgency.      	*   Asking for Help: recognizing the importance of clear communication; approaching university help avenues for aid in mental health.      	*   Technology Utilization: leveraging digital tools such as calendar apps and task management software to organize schedules and set reminders for important deadlines, meetings, and study sessions.      	*   Multitasking: better integration of certain activities. For instance, using commute time to review class notes or listen to recorded lectures, making the most of otherwise unproductive moments.

With more time available, it provided a better understanding of unit content after reviewing missed material.

high.situation: In technical drawing class, I found myself in an unfamiliar environment. As someone from a software development background, I was well-versed in coding but had always struggled with artistic skills, especially drawing. The challenge was to learn technical drawing as part of the ENGG1000 criteria.
high.task_action: Faced with the difficulties of not being great at drawing, I recongised the importance of teamwork and communication to overcome my drawing deficiencies. I proactively search for classmates who might excel in technical drawing based on talking to them and proposed forming a small working group with a mixture of skills to tackle weekly tasks. My intention for doing this was to enhance the group technical drawing skills and support one another in grasping fundamental.
high.result: During each session, we would sat near one another, discussing the drawing topics, way to improve and shared constructive feedback. Over time I can see progression with my technical drawing skills as well as those around me.

high.situation: We needed to design Rube Goldberg Machine that leads from one small action to an end result.
high.task_action: Undergoing a few meetings a week with our small team, consulting each other and bouncing ideas of each other to make a clear goal and to understand what we need to do. After coming to a common understanding of the task at hand the next part was coming up with goals, an end goal, close goals and goals that were in the future to see what we could do. Researching and collating all of the information about logistics, finances and feasibility to make a Rube Goldberg Machine that can be made in adherence to the given guildines with feasibility and time management as the most important aspects of the Project.
high.result: We as a group were able to finalise on a conceptual design that we all had come to an agreement to in these few weeks.      We tested and evaluated ideas to make them as feasible and guidline adhering as possible and will be spending future weeks to organise building the machine and tasks have been broken down person to person and week by week to stay within the limited timeframe.

high.situation: In my previous job as a retail sales associate at Myer, I was assigned to lead a cross-functional team for a critical client project during Christmas.      TASK: The task involved coordinating the efforts of staff members from different retail departments, ensuring clear communication and meeting my manager's expectations while adhering to a tight deadline.      ACTION: During the project, a situation arose where one team member consistently showing no friendly service and was not contributing effectively to joint tasks. This was impacting the team's progress and jeopardising the project's success. Instead of criticising the team member immediately, I decided to approach the situation professionally. By scheduling a meeting with the team member and my manager to discuss and understand their challenges, I actively listened to their concerns and acknowledged their perspective. Through providing constructive feedback on the importance of being a reliable team member, we created a plan to provide additional support and clarified expectations. I also offered assistance in prioritising tasks and ensuring a better alignment between their workload and skills. Throughout this process, I maintained a respectful and empathetic attitude, focusing on finding a solution rather than assigning blame.
high.result: As a result of our conversation and the support provided, the team member's performance improved significantly. They started meeting deadlines, actively engaging in team discussions, and contributing effectively. This situation highlighted the importance of approaching PROFESSIONAL BEHAVIOUR with empathy and effective communication to address challenges and maintain a positive team environment.

high.situation: I had myself put in a new situation; this was the 5-minute weekly based oral presentations for my ENGG1050 class. I was faced with a brand-new concept, and as an outcome, I had to try to settle by pushing myself to get out of my comfort zone. The presentations included demonstrating professionalism and providing a debrief of what we had done each week for our project - building a Rube Goldberg machine for our client.      Task: Our main task was to deliver these oral presentations every week, summarizing our progress on the Rube Goldberg machine project. We were expected to showcase professionalism, communicate effectively, and provide a clear overview of our accomplishments and challenges.
high.result: The result was very satisfactory. Week after week, one team member would take the stage to present their progress. Over the course of these weeks, I found myself in the presenter's role several times, and though my presentations were far from perfect, the process was primarily done to start the development of my skills for future endeavours as an engineer. In this context, I felt like it had succeeded.

high.situation: Every week, my engineering team is faced with the task of presenting our progress of our project for ENG 1050.
high.task_action: Before each presentation, we all work together to complete the assigned work. This achieved through clear communication between team members and assigning different roles to each other in order to complete all of the work in the required amount of time. We also take turns with presenting every week. Before the presentation, I prepared by writing down some key points onto a google doc as notes to help keep track of all of the key points of the presentation. This allowed for a concise and clear oral presentation over a zoom call in order to provide the audience of engineers with valuable information.
high.result: The result of planning and communicating well combined with practicing oral presentation has led to the large growth in my presentation skills as this is extremely important of a skill to carry on into a work / team environment in the future.

high.situation: During the first half of semester two in Macquarie University, in ENGG1050 my team every single one of us had to come up with some creative ideas for our RGM project as two components are required for everyone in the group.
high.task_action: We had to discuss amongst the group on what potential ideas we could use for or RGM project. We also had to do some brainstorming on what we are doing, how we are doing to do this, will we be able to complete our project without coming across a big hurdle, etc. When my group was discussing and brainstorming on our possible RGM project, we were able to develop great ideas quickly because we have decided to work backwards from our intention of our RGM project. My group has decided that our ultimate and main idea is to light a candle, so from there we have been able to develop more ideas on what other components we could potentially add to our RGM project.
high.result: Each member of the team had to come up with two components for our RGM project, so individually we have decided to think about ideas on what we would want to do in our RGM project. Then after everyone have an idea on what they want to do in their two components of the RGM project, we would gather back a group and discuss on how to link all the components into one RGM project.

high.situation: Weeks 4-5 was a crucial time during the development of my group’s RGM, these weeks dictated the conceptual and preliminary design of what our result would eventually look like towards the end of the semester. Upon finally reaching the planning phase of the RGM a great sense of perseverance and a willingness to deliver had been brought to light. Being faced with a task which amounted to a culmination of team spirit and effort allowed me to understand that all members of the group would need to try their best to create an RGM which was effective and of the highest quality. The factors I had to consider was knowledge in the form of research, and what skills I currently possessed, these factors accompanied with my urgency to deliver allowed this situation to occur.      Task:      The task that occurred through these two important weeks was the creation of the conceptual and preliminary design of my groups RGM project. There were many factors that contributed to this goal, these included themes, events, and phases. To reach these factors a variety of strategies would have to have been implemented, this included research, brainstorming, and group discussions. Even though the tasks were made apparent I struggled to reach feasible ideas and concepts that the group could use for the RGM project. Whether this be due to overthinking, or insecurities about sharing my ideas to the group it was apparent that my willingness to deliver the best work had become both a strength and weakness at the same time.      Action:      The resolving action based on the struggles of completing the tasks of the RGM ideas was brought about through several events. The main one was the idea of working as a team being made apparent through the other groups weekly presentations, it was clear that they had been functioning fluidly as a team as they got to know each members strength outside of the unit. Having teamwork and each members individuality in my mind allowed me to reach out to my members in the group and finally convey my ideas that I had made. Of course, my ideas weren’t perfect but led to an agreed idea through both research and creative collaboration. Even when we had viable ideas we continued brainstorming because of our perseverance and willingness to deliver the best RGM. Eventually we reached a design that would distribute work evenly among members, a theme of watering a plant, and 4 phases each with specific events.
high.result: Many positive emotions rose from within me as my group and I had finally connected in varying ways, our willingness to deliver an incredible RGM allowed us to come up with our best idea and most reasonable workload for each member. It was the action of reaching out to team members which allowed this result to occur and made it apparent to me that teamwork in this environment and the real world is crucial to becoming an excellent engineer. My perseverance and flexibility allowed me to finally pitch my ideas to the group and allow myself to be connected with them, this event made me truly appreciate my urgency to be my best, but made me understand that this could have been done earlier, rather than needing another group to pave the way forward. Overall, I now understand that complete team unity in a group environment can only be achieved by reaching out and demonstrating a willingness to deliver.

high.situation: Group A7's discussion of the RGM project and the ideas/themes that could possibly structure the final project.
high.task_action: Collectively brainstorming unique themes that could encompass the RGM and how it could differ from more common choices by other groups. Given that the group A7 was only recently formed, the early discussions between members were limited and progress in deciding anything was significantly delayed. However, one or two members were able to take initiative in opening discussions by sharing videos of RGM concepts that interested them, prompting other members to partake as well.
high.result: The result was being able to unanimously agree on a music themed RGM, which members were passionate about completing, thus increasing the likelihood of devoting more effort towards it.

high.situation: Learning effective and proper communication skills to communicate with our team as chairman/group member.
high.task_action: In week one, finding the best communication method as a team started with trial and error as a group discussion about platforms such as Instagram and WhatsApp. We made a WhatsApp group, and then during presentations, we saw other teams using a discord server to communicate effectively. Our group created one, deciding that Discord's ability to hold integrated voice calls for group meetings, and create multiple channels for relevant information, such as deadlines, time for team meetings, weekly roles and assignments, and responsibilities, allowed each member to be clear on their role. When selected as chairman, it is important to ensure each group member understands their responsibilities and tasks for that week.
high.result: The result of creating a discord server through trial and error greatly increased the communication of our group. Allowing each member to access all relevant information for that week and discuss issues/ideas on the relevant channel to the idea. The implementation of the discord server had a slow start, but as group members became more comfortable and familiar with each other ever since creation, the weekly reflection was completed early and submitted on time.
