high.situation: Working as a plumber I received an urgent call on my non working day from a client regarding a burst water pipe.
high.task_action: Immediately I notified the client to turn the residents' water off at the meter. Upon my arrival at the site I saw water leaking from the bathroom wall through the tiles. I was required to determine the location of the leak within the bathroom wall to begin repairs.       First I examined the wall to pinpoint exactly where the water was leaking from. Once located I used a specialised thermal imagery camera to inspect the area for further water damage. This allowed me to see hot water leaking from the pipe in a rough area within the tiled wall.      Next I made the decision to carefully remove the tiles in the surrounding area. Using a jack hammer and chisel I removed the render to reveal two copper pipes. Once properly exposed, I assessed the damage to the copper pipe within the wall. The 15mm copper hot water pipe had split causing the leak. I proceeded to fix the damage pipe by cutting and removing the section of split copper. Once removed I cleaned and prepped the copper within the wall to replace the split section. With my specialised crimping tool I reconnected the piping with copper crimp sockets making the joins water tight and secure. Subsequently the water supply was turned back on to check for leaks ensuring the problem was resolved.
high.result: Through this display of initiative and willingness to make decisions during uncertainty I was able to successfully locate the cause of the issue and provide a carefully considered solution. In doing so there was no further damage to the property. In conjunction providing an efficient response with minimal disruption to the client guaranteed a positive experience for all parties involved.

high.situation: For the engineering team, our task was to enhance the effectiveness of the facility’s manufacturing process for it to take lesser period and incur fewer expenses during production.
high.task_action: The first thing we did was arrange a few meetings and brainstorming sessions with representatives from several departments. The departments included production, design, and quality control, to address this difficulty. We determined throughout these sessions where the manufacturing process needed to be improved most. Other factors we had to overlook were the workflow optimization, material waste reduction, and machine operation simplification. We split up into smaller working groups, with each group tasked with dissecting and suggesting fixes for process elements. With the help of Six Sigma's DMAIC (Define, Measure, Analyze, Improve, Control) approach (Source: Lean Six Sigma Company), we were able to pinpoint inefficiencies and bottlenecks in the process and create action plans to deal with them. Some of the techniques we used to identify improvement opportunities and validate our corrective measures include process mapping, root cause analysis, and statistical process control. We also stayed in touch with stakeholders throughout the project to seek their opinions and ensure that the objectives were achieved.
high.result: Our efforts led to a notable improvement in the manufacturing process's efficiency, resulting in a 20% reduction in production time and a 15% drop in material waste. The company's profitability rose as a consequence of cost savings and greater productivity brought about by the adoption of our suggested solutions. In addition, our cooperative strategy promoted a continuous improvement culture inside the company, where team members actively participated in finding and executing more manufacturing process improvements. All things considered, our project proved how crucial cooperation, problem-solving abilities, and clear communication are to succeeding in engineering tasks and duties.

high.situation: We need to create a program in java language to display an animation of a butterfly landing on a flower.
high.task_action: Forming a team of 4 and brainstorming ideas on the animation and visuals, while also creating a simple program.       ACTIONS: The team finalise on the official visuals of the butterfly and the flower. We assign each other roles and responsibilities to split the work in equal measures. Through various forms of communications including; communicating about the teams timeline and deadlines to complete the task and effectively performing their roles in regards to their part of the project. The team also creates an online platform to discuss the project outside of class hours. Such include a group chat on iMessages or social media platforms. The team encouraged each member to contribute by sharing their ideas and helping with the program. We did encounter some coding problems, however everyone assisted each other to solve the obstacle in the java program.
high.result: Through effective communication, individuals were able to form teams and work together to complete the program assigned. We talked on iMessages when some of us encountered a problem and worked collaboratively together to design a java program that displayed a red butterfly sitting on a yellow flower.

high.situation: Tasked with creating a group presentation with members of the unit. We were randomly picked into small groups, given a topic related to the cyber security unit and asked to create a presentation on said topic to be presented the following week.
high.task_action: As a group, we pulled in our individual insight on the topic, so that as a group we could understand the topic, brainstorm ideas for the presentation and figure out a plan to execute it well according to the marking scheme. With consistent communication we spent time, researching, and implementing our knowledge into the Powerpoint as well as rehearsing outside of the classroom to get are queues and timing right for the oral presentation.
high.result: Through these action, a well structure, articulate and perfectly timed oral presentation was given. Through outside practice every group member was satisfied with each others effort and contibution. Additional results included us making a readable and easy to watch presentation, that was quick and easy for the viewer/marker to read.

high.situation: My group needed to write two processing programs as a part of our project and an interim report about the project, however the due date was really close and one of my group members could not contribute due to technical problems.      TASK: Conducting group meetings for deciding the design of the programs and the way to implement it with proper documentation of the progress of the project in the form of the report. Also, communicating any issues or tasks which should be prioritised to be completed first so our project is finished on time.We had to write the code for two programs which displayed an image of a Blender and an animation of a waving judge in processing.      ACTION:  As the due date was approaching, we acted promptly and divided our group into two pairs to work on the two different programs. We made acceptance criteria and conducted code review for every requirement we needed to accomplish for the project. We pointed out any issues related to the  code and any unfinished tasks that needed to be completed so everybody was on top with their assigned task. At last, we documented the project in the interim report where each member completed one required section of the report which enabled us to complete it quickly.
high.result: The result was fruitful, as we could complete all our tasks on time, and the code passed all the acceptance criteria and code review that were set. We were able to complete both the program and the interim report before the due date. All of our team were satisfied with the final form of our project.

high.situation: due to poor time management and rushed work my project was poorly made, with many pieces of my project having one or more incorrectly measured edges/bends.
high.task_action: With only 2 weeks left before the project was due I had to decide whether to attempt to keep trying make the current parts I had made or to restart entirely. The decision to restart was made with three things in mind:      - Confidence in my ability      - less traffic around the machines      - Time it would take to make adjustments to my current pieces rather than remaking them
high.result: The choice I made was to remake my project from the start, and with the skills I had acquired through the previous attempt I was able to make an improved product in a much shorter amount of time.

high.situation: We were required to design a 4-bit binary adder for Aurdino on TinkerCad. Each LED light represented a bit, and the motherboard would add the bits entered upon the press of a button and show the results in decimal and hexadecimal values—the project aimed to demonstrate the practical application of digital logic and programming concepts in hardware design.
high.task_action: This project was an individual assessment. To achieve this objective, we extensively studied the fundamental principles of binary addition. We then specified the 4-bit binary adder prerequisites, encompassing the input/output setups and operating criteria.       Using the powerful tools of Tinkercad and Arduino IDE, we constructed the circuit architecture by strategically selecting and arranging the essential components within the virtual environment. This included input switches, output display LEDs, and Arduino microcontroller boards. We rigorously simulated the circuit before proceeding with the coding process to ensure its correct operation.   With the Arduino IDE, we meticulously programmed and debugged the code to gain complete control over the binary addition process. We implemented functions to execute binary addition, which included mechanisms to manage carry-over bits and identify issues such as overflow or underflow. The code was optimised for efficiency and accuracy, considering the limited resources of the Arduino microcontroller.
high.result: We achieved the effective execution of a 4-bit binary adder by utilizing Arduino microcontrollers and software-based algorithms. We were able to check the accuracy of the addition process and see the output using virtual LEDs in the Tinkercad simulation, which did a good job of showing how the binary adder worked.      We thoroughly tested the binary adder using a range of input combinations to verify its durability and dependability. The utilization of software-based methods obviated the necessity for tangible logic gates, hence streamlining the circuit design and diminishing the hardware's intricacy.   In addition, we recorded the coding methodologies and algorithms employed in the project to disseminate expertise and perspectives to those interested in digital circuitry design and programming for embedded systems. The Tinkercad simulation functioned as an instructional instrument, offering a practical learning opportunity for students and amateurs investigating Arduino-based projects.   In summary, the project showcased the flexibility and capabilities of Arduino microcontrollers to carry out intricate computational tasks without relying on additional hardware components. The demonstration exhibited the effectiveness of software-based solutions in designing digital circuits and emphasised the possibilities of simulation tools such as Tinkercad in creating and evaluating electronic systems.

high.situation: In the machining class, every student was tasked with manufacturing a toolbox from scratch using various machining techniques and tools. The project was designed to provide practical experience in machining processes and tool usage for the Engineering degree. However, with limited access to machining equipment and a tight deadline for project completion, effective time and resource management were crucial to ensure the successful fabrication of the toolbox.
high.task_action: Recognizing the importance of efficient time and resource management, I strategized a brief timeline to optimize my workflow and maximize productivity. First, I conducted a thorough assessment of the available machining resources, including the equipment, its availability, material inventory, and instructor support. I then identified the critical tasks required for toolbox fabrication, such as material preparation(drawing), machine operations, assembly, and finishing.             To streamline my workflow, I divided the project into manageable phases and assigned specific tasks on specific weeks to ensure that all my tasks were carried out efficiently while following safety protocols and quality standards.             Additionally, I managed to leverage my knowledge and experience to optimize machining techniques and troubleshoot any technical challenges encountered during the fabrication process. I collaborated closely with my instructors and sought their guidance to ensure the successful execution of each machining operation.
high.result: As a result of my strategic approach to time and resource management, I successfully fabricated the toolboxes within the allotted timeframe, meeting all project requirements and quality standards. Despite facing initial challenges and constraints, as well as having several blunders in measuring the Aluminum sheet as it was my first time using a ‘scrape’.             The project not only provided me with valuable hands-on experience in machining processes but also strengthened my communication and collaboration skills. Moreover, the successful completion of the toolbox project instilled a sense of pride and accomplishment in me, validating my efforts and dedication to mastering the art of machining.

high.situation: Needed to create a program which would randomly generate numbers and stop when it reached a prime number.
high.task_action: When solving this problem all my classmates around me were unsure of how to check if a number was a prime number. So we worked together and came up with ideas on how to find the prime numbers. We had to communicate our ideas with one other and help each other find and understand a formula which would calculate if our random numbers were a prime or not.
high.result: Our teamwork and communication resulted in us all having a working formula which we understood and allowed us to complete the question.

high.situation: In my role as a project manager at a Software making project during one of the course at the university, I was tasked with leading team of 5 on a crucial project aimed at developing a new software product. We face a lot of challenges related to internal communication that were impacting our efficiency and project deadlines.      Task: Recognizing the impact of these communication breakdowns, my primary task was to overhaul our existing communication strategy. The goal was to ensure that all team members were fully informed, engaged and aligned with the project goals and timelines. This required a strategic and thoughtful approach to address the specific communication issues we were facing.       Action: I established mandatory weekly meeting where all team members could share updates, discuss challenges and brainstorm solutions collectively. I introduced a project management tool that allowed for real time updates and visibility. I also introduced everyone to the importance of two way communication. I emphasized the importance of clear communication, concise and accessible documentation for every aspect of the project.
high.result: The implementation of this new communication strategy had a profound and positive impact on the project. Team morale  significantly improved as members felt more involved and valued. The clarity brought about by regular meetings and effective use of the project management tools reduced misunderstandings and aligned everyone towards common goals. Consequently, our project met its milestones with improved efficiency and was completed on schedule. The success of this approach has led to its adoption as the standard communication protocol within our department.

high.situation: We did  assignment of creating an innovative transportation system for a fast-expanding urban zone grappling with traffic congestion and air pollution challenges.
high.task_action: Recognizing the significant influence of transportation systems on both society and the environment, we undertook a thorough examination of the current transportation infrastructure and its societal and environmental impacts. We gathered data on traffic patterns, vehicle emissions, public transit usage, and community demographics to understand the existing challenges and identify opportunities for improvement. Embracing the principles of sustainable engineering, we focused on devising a transportation solution aimed at alleviating traffic congestion, reducing carbon emissions, and enhancing accessibility for all residents. To accomplish these goals, we proposed the development of a multimodal transportation network integrating various modes of transportation, such as public transit, bike lanes, pedestrian pathways, and electric vehicle charging facilities. We conducted feasibility studies and environmental impact assessments to assess the practicality and sustainability of our proposed transportation system. Furthermore, we engaged with stakeholders, including local government officials, transportation agencies, environmental organizations, and community members, to gather input and ensure that our solution resonated with the community's needs and values.
high.result: Our endeavors culminated in the successful establishment of a sustainable transportation infrastructure that yielded positive outcomes for both society and the environment. Through the promotion of public transit and alternative modes of transportation such as cycling and walking, we effectively mitigated traffic congestion and diminished air pollution levels within the urban setting. The enhanced accessibility and mobility facilitated by our transportation system particularly benefited marginalized communities, offering them affordable and reliable transportation alternatives. Additionally, the incorporation of electric vehicles and renewable energy sources into our network contributed to a reduction in greenhouse gas emissions, thereby aiding in the mitigation of climate change impacts. In essence, our project exemplified the transformative potential of engineering in addressing societal needs while concurrently preserving the environment for future generations.

high.situation: Throughout the machine workshop portion of this unit, I have seen a number of my colleagues using incorrect methods while operating the guillotine and sheet metal bender, potentially endangering themselves and compromising the quality of their work.
high.task_action: As I understand the importance of teamwork and communication in the workshop, I decided to address the issue directly. I approached the individuals involved calmly and politely, pointing out the safety hazards and inefficiencies of their methods. I explained the correct way of doing things, and  demonstrated the procedure when necessary.
high.result: As a direct result, I helped to foster a system of open communication between myself and my colleagues, where more people felt comfortable to share knowledge and learning with eachother. The reduction in incorrect techniques and practices helped to minimise the risk of injury, and reduced material wastage, increasing the quality of our work.

high.situation: As a beginner coder , I encountered a significant hurdle of understanding how to interpret and write code which demanded strong critical thinking skills. I struggled to grasp the importance of planning before coding and understanding the intricate symbols used in flowcharts.      Task: My task was to develop critical thinking abilities within the context of coding, particularly in problem-solving and task planning. This involved understanding coding concepts, such as loops, and applying them effectively to projects.      Action: To address this challenge, I employed the use of flowcharts. Recognising the importance of visual aids in problem-solving, I utilised flowcharts created with the assistance of the provided website in workshop one. These flowcharts enabled me to break down complex tasks, identify key decision points, and plan efficient coding strategies. Furthermore, I actively got guidance from peers and tutors when i was struggling, recognising the value of collaborative learning. Despite feeling intimidated by classmates with prior coding experience, I remained focused on improving my critical thinking skills, dedicating extra time to mastering fundamental concepts.
high.result: Through my proactive approach, utilisation of flowcharts and learning coding in my free time, I experienced notable improvements in my coding abilities in both this subject and COMP1000. Collaborating with peers provided valuable insights into effective problem-solving strategies and debugging techniques. Despite initial challenges and the disparity in coding experience among classmates, I persevered, gradually developing a stronger grasp of coding principles and enhancing my critical thinking skills.

high.situation: I Needed to complete my ENGG1000 machine workshop project (a steel toolbox) by the deadline in despite of making a mistake while cutting in the second week.
high.task_action: When I incorrectly used the guillotine on my first cut I immediately thought I had made my steel sheet redundant as it had been cut in half, along the wrong line.  I utilised my resource management skills to redraw my pieces onto the now two smaller sheets of steel I had. In doing this I was able to salvage 4 of the 5 required steel panels from my sheets and the other from the scrap bin.  Throughout the rest of the project I was very affective with my time management and planned out every step I needed to complete each week to meat the deadline, in despite of my mistake at the beginning.
high.result: The result was I ended up finishing my toolbox a week prior to  the deadline as I was able to often complete more than what I had planned in weeks prior due to a more affective use of time management.

high.situation: - Background information: I currently work as a casual FOH member focused mainly on bartending at the Macquarie Strike Bowling bar. my responsibilities are as follow, watching and monitoring RSA, and helping other staff with their duties such as cleaning and things like escape rooms, laser tag, and karaoke.        One day were fairly low on business and a small group of lads come in looking for a game or two of bowling, seeing as its not busy we offer them a discount for their first game. As they're playing they purchase one or two standards worth of drinks between two people. As their game continues, our manager for the night stepped into the office to eat and complete end of night tasks so I (bartender at the time) sit at the front monitoring the games as per usual. Shortly after my manager is not seen by the group they start goofing around with how they bowl. Two times they throw a ball down the lane and it hits the pin sweep (this shuts their lane down to prevent more damage) and are told not to do it again. As I walk down again, they do it again.
high.task_action: - My coworker (casual manager but not the one currently managing this shift) and I talk for a moment, and agree that they're doing it on purpose as they are not intoxicated nor handicapped; and shouldn't be allowed to continue as they can cause damage to the machines.        Subsequently, I tell them their game is suspended and were more than happy to talk about compensation for the missing turns. As I walk to the front desk another ball is thrown down in anger; likely due to their "wasted" money.       As such, in a tone that everyone in their group can here I say "If that's how you're going to act, get out, and get out now."       as they walk out and i check their table for the drinks i served earlier, i realize they took them outside the venue (a licensing issue), as such a short pursuit ensues as i find them finishing their drinks not five feet from the door.       I request them back in a civil manner (they're already chugging them) they become hostile, slinging threats and vile language.       I take their cups and request that they never come back as, "guests like y'all ruin everyone's night, not just your own."
high.result: - Company kept their machines in better condition, venue was kept in order/ fun was still able to be had by other guests, drink glasses were recovered that would not have been.       - A small scolding in addition to praise for adhering to a venue rule: No Un-chill behavior.

high.situation: I was in a soccer team and our team has not been playing well the past few games. We need to finish in the top 4 to qualify for the semi-finals. Our team was playing our last game to determine if we made it to the semi-finals or not. We were down 1-0 at half-time and everyone was blaming each other for our team losing.       Task: I was the captain for our team. My responsibilities in that situation was to be emotionally mature and defuse the arguments within our team. I had to play the role of a leader and communicate effectively to my team that we are capable of winning the game. I had to keep the team spirits high by having a positive attitude and convincing our team to argue less and work together.      Action: I made sure I gave everyone a chance to speak and voice their concerns in the changing rooms so it doesn’t affect our performance and teamwork on the pitch. I diffused any problems between players by informing them that we’re all on the same team and we all share a common goal and we shouldn't let petty mistakes or differences get in the way.
high.result: This led us to playing so much better in the 2nd half. We had better teamwork and communication and there were less arguments and our team was playing in cohesion. This led to a better performance and we ended up making a comeback and winning the match 3-1.       Furthermore, our team bonded over the win and we all felt a sense of comradery. This was crucial since it lifted the team spirit and confidence for the upcoming games.

high.situation: Missed technical drawing lessons due to illness and workload, needing to catch up for the final submission task in my engineering workshop.
high.task_action: Contacted the lead demonstrator to explain and ask for help. Together, we planned a catch-up class, extra resources, and guidance.
high.result: Managed to finish the final task within the extended deadline. Despite challenges, adapted and used available help to succeed, learning valuable lessons in overcoming obstacles.

high.situation: During a team project in my psychology class, we were tasked with analyzing a complex case study involving a patient exhibiting symptoms of multiple psychological disorders.
high.task_action: Recognizing the need for critical thinking, we began by dissecting the case study, and identifying key symptoms, behaviours, and possible diagnoses. Each team member was assigned specific sections to research further, ensuring a comprehensive understanding of the case. We then convened to discuss our findings, challenging each other's assumptions and interpretations. We systematically analyzed the information using critical thinking tools such as Socratic questioning and the Ladder of Inference, probing for underlying causes and connections. As we delved deeper, we uncovered nuances that initially escaped our notice, prompting us to revisit our earlier conclusions.
high.result: By employing critical thinking skills, we developed a more nuanced understanding of the case, moving beyond surface-level observations to uncover deeper insights. Our analysis culminated in a comprehensive report that not only identified potential diagnoses but also highlighted the complexity of the patient's condition. Moreover, engaging in critical discourse fostered a deeper appreciation for the complexities inherent in psychological diagnosis and treatment, equipping us with valuable skills for future endeavours.

high.situation: We needed to code and make flowcharts for scissors, paper, rock in our first few practicals. We did not know how to code or much about the syntax as it was our first time.      TASK/ ACTION: To communicate and gather my peers and try and brainstorm on how to tackle flowcharts and code. To correct each other's work and suggest ideas. To make flowcharts and start coding, the worst that could occur would be a simple error message on the code/ a tutor not approving of the flowchart.
high.result: Each person contributed their ideas on how to tackle the flowcharts and attempt the code until the code ran successfully and the flowcharts were approved by tutors.

high.situation: I needed to construct a circuit using the Arduino that would make an LED blink, but have its brightness increase over 50ms, then dim down to off over 50ms (and continue looping until programme was stopped). This required prioritisation and focus for the steps required.
high.task_action: Analysis of the steps needed to get to the end result and planning ahead to see which would allow the task to be finished efficiently. This also involved peer review and asking questions of the tutors to see if my approach was correct. Any corrections to the plan, or the steps themselves, were made before beginning.
high.result: Creating a working Arduino Circuit consists of two main parts: Building the physical circuit and the coding process to make each pin number power the right components on the circuit. In working with my table group, we devised a plan to first prioritise the physical component structure, and then the code. This is because our experience with the code was very low and we needed some method to see if there were parts of the code that were correct by physically seeing if the circuit was doing what we wanted. We did not have the expertise to create an entire code for the task without seeing immediate results along the way, and it was believed that this would be more efficient and effective regardless.

high.situation: the model we were building wasn't coming together properly as the materials weren't bonding with the pva glue the way we had thought they would.
high.task_action: checking what other resources are available to us; considering how to apply them to either aid or make redundant the use of the pva glue. We considered the use of masking tape as a supportive structure to hold the structure together while the pva glue bonded.
high.result: The model came together using a mix of pva glue and masking tape to bond the walls and floors together.

high.situation: I need to code from scratch an extended version of rock, paper, scissors that allows for a player to play against a computer using randomly generated decisions. I need to innovate the methodology in which I conduct the design process.
high.task_action: By breaking down the task into different sections of code that will help me implement different aspects of the game from the logic to the main functionality, I had to innovate ideas in how I would achieve this. By first completing the preliminary design, I thought of new ways to design the code by improving on the logic used. The preliminary design focussed on brute forcing outcomes of the limited amount of possibilities, and with the extended version of with increasingly more outcomes, I innovated from the original idea to create to implement a more robust logic that allows for scalability.
high.result: This ultimately lead to a design that I was satisfied with with regard to code efficiency, intuitiveness, and scalability.

high.situation: In the first couple of weeks at the start of the semester, we were tasked to learn programming in C, which was the first time I'd learned anything about C. However, I've had experience learning Java which helped me tremendously when learning other languages. In week 4, I was meant to write a mini program using cs50, at the time I didn't know how to implement it within the program, as "" didn't work, so the tutor gave me an alternative method in coding in C.
high.task_action: The task involved creating a rock paper scissor game with different conditions per question:      1) Implement a computer-generated player at the start of the game, so when the player picks a computer can generate a randomised hand gesture and then the program can determine the winner.      2) Instead of using Integers to represent the hand gestures, use String values.      After undergoing these questions in class I wasn't able to complete them since I wasn't able to learn and adapt quickly enough before class ended. I was granted the chance to complete the questions by the start of next week's class to get the full marks. During the break, I spent tirelessly learning this new language, even though it wasn't cs50 I was able to create a program to execute a rock paper scissor game following the set conditions per question.      The first question allowed me to learn different ways to randomise integers in C, discover new syntaxes, and find similarities in Java and C, referring to my Java knowledge when designing my program. Such as, If statements and the use of different operators. On the second question, I've had more trouble figuring out how I was able to create a variable holding a String value, as I resorted to using char values instead of using String to hold a single character to represent each hand gesture instead of the entire word. I mentioned this to the tutor and they said it was fine.
high.result: The results of the design of the code were different from what was expected, however, I was able to create a program fulfilling the desired set of conditions specified in each question. For the first question, I added additional features to the game, like when the player enters an invalid input (Integer) they would have to put in the correct input again, this will repeat until the player gets it right and when a tie appears, the game will reset. The same goes for the second question, however, instead of String or Integer values, it's a char value.       In retrospect, I've approached this class thinking it would be a breeze, however having faced adversity at the start of the lesson and further after class, I've realised that learning a new language isn't easy. Even though there are similarities in syntaxes in each language it doesn't mean it can be approached the same way, especially when you barely understand everything from a SINGLE LANGUAGE (java). Coding requires discipline and consistency, staying diligent to the craft even though it can be difficult most of the time.

high.situation: For the machining workshop we had to fabricate a toolbox out of aluminium sheet metal. While fabricating the toolbox I made a mistake doing some of the folds for the bottom panel. The folds were off the folding lines I had scribed on the panel by a few millimetres.
high.task_action: Using a bench vice with soft jaws I slowly worked the folds flat again, making sure to take my time so as to minimise work hardening the aluminium. Once the piece had been flattened most of the way I used an engineer’s hammer to carefully flatten out the remaining high spots. With the piece being flat enough I placed it back into the sheet metal folder and this time around double checked the fold line was properly aligned to the brake’s edge before folding it again.
high.result: The placement of the new fold looked much better than the original one. Instead of having to cut a new piece of material and start from scratch I was able to save this piece and save some time too. although minimal if you looked closely enough you could make out where the original fold was as well as some small cracks caused by the work hardening of the material.

high.situation: In the second week of the Arduino circuits workshop when we were introduced to more complex concepts. I was falling behind on the worksheet as I accidentally forgot to save the code from the previous workshop and didn't realise until I arrived that day.       Task: We were tasked with writing code and designing a circuit for a switch button that turns on an LED.       Action: Since I was behind on the worksheet, I was having trouble with the questions and instead of googling the answer or asking an advisor, I asked some of my peers for help. I chose to ask for help from my peers as I was hoping to make a friend, or at least make acquaintances with people in the workshop so that in the future we could help each other with our studies.
high.result: My peers were not keen on helping me out. They did not say outright that they didn't want to help, however from their body language and tone, and the little assistance they provided, it was quite clear. I asked the demonstrator for help afterwards.

high.situation: Prior to the submission of the final task for the technical drawing workshop, I found myself in the unfortunate situation of needing to complete several exercises that I had left unfinished from my time in class. A predicament exacerbated by the fact that I had joined the unit after week 1 and missed the contents from the first workshop.
high.task_action: In order to complete the necessary tasks, without the luxury of any specialised equipment or help for the unit demonstrators, it was imperative that I utilised the tools I had at my disposal and exercised the necessary resilience and perseverance. Firstly, it was important for me to centre myself using mindfulness techniques such as controlled breathing and meditation in order to cultivate the appropriate mental state that was required to fulfil the assignment. Equally as important to me was to create a conducive environment, I achieved this by clearing my work-space, and fashioning a drawing surface by attaching my paper to a thin slat of wood using bulldog clips. In this scenario, the doctor and nurse would educate the patient about the working mechanism of benzodiazepine.      ACTION: We divided the tasks among ourselves: I took on the role of the doctor, explaining the medical aspects; one teammate played the nurse, assisting me in the demonstration; and the other teammate acted as the patient, asking questions and expressing concerns. We scripted the dialogue carefully, ensuring that the explanation was clear and concise. We also included visual aids and demonstrations to enhance understanding.
high.result: Our video scenario was well-received by our instructor and classmates. The practical approach of simulating a doctor-patient interaction made the concept easy to understand. Due to the clarity of explanation and creative presentation, we received full marks on the assignment. This approach not only helped us achieve academic success but also improved our understanding of the topic.

high.situation: As a student with a background in finance, I decided to also pursue an engineering degree as it felt more hands on and more practical. Having already committed to software engineering, I enrolled in Arduino and Machining as they are also within my interests. The workshop presented a significant challenge for me, as I had limited experience in this field.      TASK:       My task was to complete a series of exercises throughout the workshop, demonstrating my understanding of Arduino concepts and techniques. One particularly challenging exercise required us to create a game using a push-button switch, potentiometer, LCD, and buzzer. The objective was to develop a program where the player would control an arrow on the bottom row of the LCD using the potentiometer and attempt to match its position with a randomly generated arrow on the top row by pressing the button. The game would display the reaction time if successful or sound a buzzer if the arrows were misaligned.      ACTION:       To overcome the challenges posed by the workshop exercises, I took a proactive approach to my learning. I decided to change my study habits by changing my environment. With my bicycle, I would go to my local library every week to free myself of the distractions at home. I dedicated extra time to self-study, reviewing concepts, and experimenting with Arduino components.      When tackling the game exercise, I broke down the problem into manageable chunks and isolated variables to test different solutions. This involved isolating certain functions as much as possible such as the display of only the top arrow to ensure that it was perfectly working and then building from there. I employed a combination of brute force experimentation (playing with variables and hoping) and thoughtful contemplation to understand how different components of the code interacted with each other. I relied primarily on the Arduino IDE documentation for clear explanations of functions and concepts, finding it more reliable than online forums and other sources which made things more confusing.      Throughout the workshop, I maintained a disciplined approach to time management, recognizing the importance of staying focused and avoiding procrastination. I actively participated in workshop sessions, asked questions when needed, and collaborated with fellow participants to share knowledge and insights.
high.result: As a result of my perseverance, resourcefulness, and commitment to learning, I achieved a perfect score of 100% across the Arduino workshop assessments. This accomplishment marked a significant milestone in my personal and academic growth, as it was my first time attaining such a high score in an academic setting.      The Arduino workshop experience helped me further develop valuable problem-solving skills, as I learned to break down complex challenges into manageable components and approach them systematically. I gained a deeper understanding of Arduino programming and hardware, which I can now apply to future projects and endeavours.      This experience reinforced the importance of hard work, self-awareness, and resilience in overcoming challenges. It highlighted the power of education and the joy of assisting others, as I had the opportunity to help struggling peers and contribute to a supportive learning environment.      Overall, the Arduino workshop not only provided me with technical skills but also fostered personal growth, self-discipline, and a mature mindset towards learning. These outcomes will shape my future academic and professional pursuits, as I continue to explore the fascinating world of engineering.

TASK: My responsibilities encompassed all aspects directly related to the product. high.situation: During my engineering project, I worked solo on developing a 4-bit binary adder using Arduino. The project involved using buttons as binary inputs and LEDs to display the results. Later, I integrated an LCD to show the addition results in Decimal and Hexadecimal formats. I encountered a significant issue with the inconsistent toggling of LEDs and inaccurate display outputs on the LCD.
high.task_action: To address this problem, I applied critical thinking and a structured problem-solving approach. First, I carefully analyzed the circuit and code to identify potential causes of the inconsistencies. I tested each component individually to ensure they functioned correctly and reviewed my code for logical errors.      I brainstormed several potential solutions, such as debouncing the buttons, checking faulty connections, and optimizing the code for better performance. After evaluating these solutions based on feasibility and expected effectiveness, I implemented software debouncing for the buttons and rewired the circuit to ensure better connections. I also refined the code to accurately store and display the binary addition results on the LEDs and the LCD.
high.result: Implementing software debouncing and circuit rewiring significantly improved the reliability of the LED toggling and the accuracy of the LCD display. The 4-bit binary adder functioned as expected, with the LEDs correctly displaying the binary addition results and the LCD accurately showing the results in both Decimal and Hexadecimal formats. This successful outcome was demonstrated during my final project presentation, receiving positive feedback from professors. The project was also featured in the department's annual showcase, highlighting my effective use of critical thinking to resolve engineering challenges independently.

high.situation: _      As part of my personal mechanical engineering project for my second skill in the ENG1000 course, I set out to create an aluminum toolbox from scratch. The goal was to build a lightweight yet sturdy toolbox that could hold various tools and equipment securely. Throughout my time in the workshop, I encountered several design and manufacturing challenges that required innovative solutions.      _
high.task_action: _      To tackle these challenges, I followed a structured problem-solving approach:      	*   Problem Identification and Brainstorming:      	* I identified key issues such as material selection, weight distribution, and hinge mechanisms.   	* I formed a small working group with other students at my table, and we offered assistance for difficult measurements and use of machines.      	*   Solution Development and Evaluation:      	* Weight Distribution: To ensure the toolbox was not top-heavy and unstable, I made sure each panel of aluminum was properly measured, filed and evenly cut. These designs were evaluated for their effectiveness in distributing weight evenly.   	* Hinge Mechanisms: The initial hinge design was prone to wear and required frequent maintenance. We brainstormed alternative hinge mechanisms, including concealed hinges and piano hinges. Each design was assessed for durability and ease of installation. We opted for concealed hinges due to their robustness and aesthetic appeal.      _
high.result: _      The structured approach yielded several positive outcomes:      	* Successful Prototype: The final aluminum toolbox prototype met most of the build criteria. It was lightweight, durable, and had a paint finish. The concealed hinges worked well in providing smooth operation and increased longevity.   	* Problem-Solving Skills: The project honed my problem-solving and critical thinking skills. By tackling real-world engineering challenges, I gained valuable experience in material science, mechanical design, and machining.

high.situation: Throughout my career as a Diagnostic Radiographer, I have actively engaged in maintaining my Continuing Professional Development (CPD) requirements. One notable experience was when I presented my first in-service, an oral presentation on the radiographic appearance of paediatric bone development.
high.task_action: I approached this in-service by conducting research on the general bone development cycle, and the ossification centres of the most common areas that we image in paediatric patients. I also explored the radiographic appearance of the ossification centres and their appearance/formation in relation to age, focusing mainly on the elbow, hand/wrist, ankle and foot.      I used academic journals, reviewed case studies, and consulted the radiographer ‘bible’, Radiopedia.org, to collect accurate information that I needed for my presentation. I created a fabulously put together PowerPoint presentation, with images and radiographs (x-ray images) sourced from radiopedia.org or our departments picture archiving and communication system (PACS). I created a speech and practiced my spiel for about a week prior to the actual presentation.
high.result: The in-service was well-received by my colleagues. The inclusion of images and radiographs reinforced the information, aiding in their understanding of the topic.      Interactive components like the brief Q&A session and case studies enhanced their and my own learning experience. It also reinforced my commitment to lifelong learning and sharing knowledge with peers to enhance our collective professional development.      Feedback from the in-service session indicated that apart from being great, I need increased confidence my own presentation skills, and some colleagues suggested I should perform regular in-services on varying topics or case studies to improve said skills.

high.situation: Myself and my group members were tasked with an oral presentation during my second skill of ENGG1000 course. We were expected to be able to present in front of a diverse audience with a variety of ideas, individuals in different courses, and their own special set of expertise. The presentation topic was to create a residence management plan for Macquarie University's students, which recognized the need for a significant upgrade in the living environment for the students themselves. With outdated facilities and a lack of aesthetic appeal, there was a growing concern about student satisfaction and well-being in the residency.
high.task_action: As this was a collaborative effort, I was assigned the task of creating possible designs for the building as well as creating a plan for our group to complete set activities each week in order to make fluid progress. To tackle this task effectively, I first conducted extensive research into modern architectural trends, interior design principles, and student preferences. I also went above and beyond, as I questioned not only my peers but also the other students in my class about what they preferred. In the end, I finally came up with a few ideas myself, but I stuck with a few. This was the step-by-step process.      	* Our team consisted of bright students who brainstormed ideas and developed a cohesive vision for the project.   	* We conducted site visits to assess the current conditions of the student residences and identify areas for improvement.   	* Based on our research and insights gathered, we developed a detailed plan that included architectural modifications, interior design enhancements, and the integration of sustainable features.   	* We prioritized elements such as communal spaces for social interaction, natural lighting, greenery, and ergonomic furniture to enhance the overall living experience.   	* We also proposed implementing technology-enabled solutions to improve safety, convenience, and connectivity within the residence.
high.result: The presentation received enthusiastic feedback from the students in my class, including the teachers. The proposed redesign plan was well-received for its innovative approach and focus on student well-being. As a result, we were able to mostly complete the project; however, due to having fewer individuals on my team, we were not able to complete all physical aspects of the model. However, the basic idea was conveyed.

Through discussions, we exchanged design logic and realized that there are many ways to achieve the same goal and that there is no single “right” solution as long as the results are consistent.

high.situation: As part of a sustainability initiative at our engineering firm, we were tasked with developing a new product that would significantly reduce environmental impact compared to existing alternatives. The goal was to design a water filtration system that could be used in both urban and rural settings, particularly in areas with limited access to clean water.      TASK: My role was to lead the research and development team in creating a sustainable and efficient water filtration system. This involved not only designing the product but also ensuring that the materials and manufacturing processes were environmentally friendly. Additionally, we needed to consider the social implications, making the system affordable and easy to use in developing communities.      ACTION:      	* RESEARCH: Conducted extensive research on existing water filtration technologies to understand their environmental impact and efficiency.   	* BRAINSTORMING: Organized brainstorming sessions with my team to generate multiple design ideas, focusing on sustainability, cost-effectiveness, and ease of use.   	* EVALUATION: Evaluated each proposed design based on criteria such as environmental impact, cost, and usability, and selected the top three for further development.   	* PROTOTYPING: Created prototypes of the top three designs and conducted tests to assess their performance and environmental impact.   	* REFINEMENT: Refined the best-performing prototype based on feedback from field tests and stakeholder consultations.
high.result: * ENVIRONMENTAL IMPACT: We successfully developed a water filtration system that reduced environmental impact by 40% compared to existing products, due to its use of sustainable materials and efficient manufacturing processes.   	* SOCIAL IMPACT: The system was affordable and easy to use, significantly improving access to clean water in underprivileged communities. It was deployed in multiple regions, benefiting over 10,000 households within the first year.   	* COMPANY RECOGNITION: Our project received positive recognition from industry peers and environmental organizations, enhancing our firm’s reputation for sustainable innovation.   	* ONGOING DEVELOPMENT: The project established a framework for future sustainable product development, with lessons learned being applied to other engineering challenges.      By simplifying our approach and focusing on key actions, we achieved our project goals and made a meaningful impact on society and the environment through engineering innovation.

This gives me a deep understanding of the two shapes, allowing me to draw more easily in an isometric view. This experience taught me the importance of integrating past knowledge and metacognition in problem-solving.
high.result: Overall, my illustrations perfectly met all the requirements for standard manufacturing drawings in engineering. My drawings adhered closely to the AS1100 standard, with dimensions for all multi-view drawings rendered in clean and straight lines, effectively demonstrating my integrated drawing knowledge.

high.situation: At Actrol, our team was asked to optimise the stockroom arrangement to make it easier to find parts fast. This was a tiny but significant activity that required team members to collaborate and communicate well.
high.task_action: To address this issue, I formed a small group of coworkers who frequently accessed the stockroom. We had a clear goal: "How can we rearrange the stockroom to improve efficiency in locating parts?"      We began with a brainstorming session in which everyone may openly express their ideas. After gathering a variety of solutions, we reviewed and evaluated them based on the ease of completion, cost, and potential effect on efficiency. We then sorted the proposals to find the most promising ones. During this collaborative process, we settled on a new approach in which each part would be assigned a specific bin placement and the data would be recorded into our stock management system.
high.result: The solution entailed assigning each part to a specific bin location, carefully marking them, and updating the stock management system accordingly. We assigned tasks: some team members moved the parts to their new locations and labelled the bins, while others updated the stock management system with the new bin information. This rearrangement makes it much easier to find parts, resulting in a considerable reduction in time spent searching for goods.

high.situation: During a group experiment, we were tasked with testing the tensile strength of various materials using a universal testing machine. As we began the experiment, we encountered inconsistent results across multiple trials, indicating a potential issue with our testing methodology or sample preparation.
high.task_action: I initiated a meeting with my group members to discuss the discrepancies in our results and to outline a plan to address the underlying problem. We reviewed the experimental procedures, including sample preparation techniques, equipment calibration, and testing protocols to identify potential sources of error. After thorough analysis, we decided to recalibrate the testing machine, standardise our sample preparation methods, and implement a more rigorous data collection process to ensure accuracy and reliability.
high.result: Following the adjustments to our experimental approach, we repeated the tests on the materials, meticulously recording data and maintaining consistency in our procedures. The results of the repeated experiments showed a significant reduction in variability and produced more reliable tensile strength values for each material tested. Our revised methodology not only improved the quality of our experimental data but also deepened our understanding of the importance of meticulous planning and attention to detail in experimental inquiry.

high.situation: As part of the civil modelling course, my group was assigned a project to design a new student accommodation building. TASK: My task was to facilitate the team’s efforts to reach a consensus on the design, ensure that the report and presentation reflected our unified vision, and produce a high-quality model that accurately represented our concept.      ACTION: I organised a brainstorming session to gather ideas and concerns, using a whiteboard to list features and priorities. Facilitating discussions, we used a decision matrix to prioritise design elements, balancing aesthetics, functionality, and sustainability. I coordinated task division based on team members’ strengths and ensured regular check-ins for progress tracking. Once all components were complete, I led a review session to integrate the report, presentation, and model, rehearsing multiple times to ensure a cohesive and smooth delivery.
high.result: Our project culminated in a comprehensive report detailing our design process and rationale, which received high marks for clarity and thoroughness. The accompanying presentation effectively highlighted key aspects of our design, impressing both professors and peers with its visuals and coherent narrative. Additionally, our physical model was lauded for its attention to detail and accuracy, serving as a tangible representation of our design prowess and ability to translate ideas into a physical form.

high.situation: In a programming course focused on honing critical thinking skills, the assignment was to individually create a C language code for a rock-paper-scissors game. The task required not only technical proficiency but also critical analysis and problem-solving abilities to develop an efficient and logically sound solution.
high.task_action: To approach this task with critical thinking, I began by thoroughly understanding the game's rules and requirements, breaking them down into logical components. I critically analyzed the problem, identifying potential challenges and areas where critical decision-making would be essential.      Next, I formulated a plan to implement the game, considering factors such as user input validation, random selection of the computer's choice, and determining the winner based on the chosen options. This involved breaking down the problem into smaller, more manageable tasks and considering multiple potential solutions for each.      Throughout the coding process, I continuously applied critical thinking skills to evaluate the effectiveness and efficiency of my code. I considered factors such as code readability, efficiency, and scalability, making informed decisions to optimize the code structure and improve its overall quality.      When encountering challenges or ambiguities during implementation, I engaged in critical problem-solving, carefully analyzing the problem from different angles and considering alternative approaches. This involved debugging and troubleshooting, as well as seeking input from peers or resources to gain new insights and perspectives.
high.result: By applying critical thinking skills throughout the development process, I successfully created a functional and well-structured C language code for the rock-paper-scissors game. The implemented solution effectively handled user input validation, generated random choices for the computer, and accurately determined the winner based on the game's rules.      Moreover, the experience of developing the code enhanced my critical thinking abilities, particularly in the areas of problem-solving, decision-making, and analytical reasoning. It underscored the importance of approaching programming challenges with a critical mindset, systematically analyzing problems, considering alternative solutions, and continuously evaluating and improving the code. Overall, the assignment served as a valuable exercise in integrating technical proficiency with critical thinking skills in software development

high.situation: In a group project for our Year 12 advanced mathematics course, we were tasked with analyzing real-world financial data related to interest rates and investments to develop an investment strategy.
high.task_action: Our group started by gathering and analysing data on various investment options, including interest rates, market trends, and risk factors. We divided the tasks based on each member's strengths, with one focusing on calculating compound interest and returns, another on researching different investment vehicles, and another on assessing the impact of economic factors on investments. I took on the task of conducting sensitivity analysis to evaluate how changes in interest rates and market conditions would affect our investment strategy.
high.result: Through critical thinking and collaboration, we developed a comprehensive investment strategy that considered various factors such as risk tolerance, time horizon, and expected returns. My sensitivity analysis helped us identify the most effective investment options based on different scenarios, enhancing the robustness of our strategy. Overall, our project demonstrated the application of advanced mathematical concepts to real-world financial decision-making, showcasing our critical thinking skills in a practical context.

high.situation: As a person of color (POC) attending college, I found myself in a challenging situation where I felt that my teacher was not providing adequate support or attention to me compared to other students.      TASK: My task was to navigate this situation while still striving to succeed academically and ensure that my concerns regarding equality and fair treatment were addressed.      ACTION: To address this situation, I took several actions. Firstly, I sought to understand whether my perceptions were accurate by gathering feedback from classmates and peers who might have experienced similar treatment. This helped me validate my concerns and provided me with a support network to lean on.      Next, I approached the teacher to express my concerns in a respectful and constructive manner. I shared specific instances where I felt marginalized or overlooked, providing examples to illustrate my point. I emphasized the importance of equitable treatment for all students and advocated for myself in a firm yet diplomatic manner.      Additionally, I sought out alternative resources and support systems within the college community. This included reaching out to academic advisors, student support services, and affinity groups for POC students. These resources not only provided me with valuable guidance and assistance but also empowered me to advocate for systemic change within the institution.
high.result: As a result of these actions, I was able to make some progress in addressing the inequality I experienced. The teacher became more attentive to my needs and started providing me with the support and assistance I required to succeed academically. Moreover, by speaking up and advocating for myself, I raised awareness about issues of racial inequality within the college community, prompting discussions and initiatives aimed at promoting diversity and inclusivity.      While the situation was challenging, it served as a learning experience that reinforced the importance of advocating for oneself and standing up against injustice. It also highlighted the need for colleges and educational institutions to actively address issues of inequality and discrimination to ensure that all students have equal opportunities to succeed.

high.situation: As part of a group project for my engineering course, we were given the task of designing an aluminium toolbox that encompasses box design, precise measurement, marking, sheet cutting, and bending using specialized equipment. Early in the project, one of our team members frequently missed meetings and failed to complete assigned tasks on time. This behaviour started to impact our progress and the morale of the team.      TASK/ ACTION: As the team leader, I was responsible for addressing this issue professionally and ensuring that the project stayed on track. I needed to find a way to communicate with the team member about their behaviour in a way that doesn't cause conflict and encourages a more cooperative and productive team environment. Therefore, I arranged a private meeting with the team member to discuss their behaviour and understand any underlying issues they might be facing. During the meeting, I tried to be calm and professional, expressing my concerns without placing the blame. I asked if there were any personal or academic challenges they were dealing with and offered my support. Afterwards, I proposed a plan to improve the situation. This included setting clear expectations for attendance and task completion, providing regular updates, and redistributing the workload to better match each team member's strengths and availability. I also suggested implementing a shared project management tool to help everyone stay organized and accountable.
high.result: The team members appreciated the open and supportive approach and explained that they were struggling with balancing multiple commitments. With the revised plan and additional support, they were able to improve their participation and meet deadlines. The overall team dynamic improved significantly, leading to more effective collaboration and increased morale. As a result, we were able to complete the design of the toolbox successfully and submit it on time. The project received positive feedback from our unit convenor for its precise engineering. This experience taught me the importance of addressing professional behaviour issues promptly and constructively.

high.situation: During my final year of college, I was overwhelmed with numerous assignments, projects, and extracurricular activities. Task:       My task was to prioritize my tasks and allocate resources such as time, energy, and focus appropriately to ensure the successful completion of all my responsibilities.      Action:       To address this challenge, I first created a detailed schedule outlining all my deadlines and commitments. Then, I identified the most critical tasks and allocated dedicated time slots for them in my schedule. Additionally, I utilized various time management techniques to enhance my productivity and focus during study sessions. Moreover, I delegated some tasks to my peers when feasible to alleviate my workload and optimize resource utilization.
high.result: As a result of my proactive approach to time and resource management, I was able to complete all my assignments and projects on time with satisfactory outcomes. high.situation: As a student juggling multiple courses, extracurricular activities, and personal commitments, I found myself frequently overwhelmed by the demands on my time and resources. Balancing academic assignments, project deadlines, and social engagements became increasingly challenging, leading to feelings of stress and inefficiency.
high.task_action: Recognizing the need for better time and resource management, I decided to implement the STAR framework to address the situation. Firstly, I assessed my current commitments and obligations, including academic deadlines, extracurricular activities, and personal responsibilities. Then, I identified specific areas where I could improve efficiency and allocate resources more effectively. Next, I took proactive steps to prioritize tasks, setting realistic goals and deadlines for each. I utilized time management tools such as calendars, to-do lists, and productivity apps to organize my schedule and track progress. Additionally, I sought support and guidance from mentors and peers, seeking advice on effective time management strategies and seeking assistance when needed.
high.result: Implementing the STAR framework significantly improved my ability to manage time and resources effectively. By clearly defining tasks and setting priorities, I became more focused and productive, completing assignments and projects in a timely manner. I experienced reduced stress levels and a greater sense of control over my workload. Furthermore, I noticed improvements in my academic performance, as I was able to dedicate more time to studying and understanding course material. Overall, the result of applying the STAR framework was a more balanced and fulfilling student experience, with enhanced productivity and better utilization of resources.

high.situation: We have been learning about best testing practices and communication between managers and our software engineering peers. This involves taking turns in the workshops, being scrum masters, and leading our team towards the goal.      TASK: As the leader of my group (scrum master), I was heard controlling what people did and assigning the tasks to them quickly without delay to ensure the task was completed fast and with high quality. high.situation: A task required us to derive an algorithm capable of determining the winner of a standard game of paper, scissor, rock, spock and lizard. Furthermore, the algorithm must be able to accomodate for an infinite array of possible hand gestures.
high.task_action: To start deriving the algorithm a pattern needed to be conceptualised. This was achieved by thinking of gestures as numerically values such as rock is 0 and paper is 1 and so forth. Then by finding the difference between the human and computer gesture, for example scissors vs paper as 2 - 1 = 1. To better understand the pattern, I organised the data into a table for analysis. In cases where the difference resulted in a negative value I adjusted it by adding the total number of gestures the user chose for that specific game. This process revealed a noticeable pattern: even numbers indicated a human loss while odd numbers indicated a human win. Consequently, the algorithm used modulo and conditional statements to determine the winner of the game.
high.result: The result was an algorithm capable of choosing the winner between human and computer gestures, even in cases involving large sets of gesture (to infinity).

I kept a journal of my progress along the learning process, noting observations, difficulties encountered, and solutions discovered. high.situation: During our ENGG1000 course, we were assigned to work on a series of Arduino projects to build various circuits and understand their functionalities. One specific project involved creating a 4-bit binary adder that would display the result using LEDs and an LCD screen. I had limited prior experience with complex Arduino circuits, and this project required integrating multiple components and ensuring they worked seamlessly together.      TASK: The task was to design, build, and test a 4-bit binary adder using an Arduino board. This involved configuring the hardware setup with buttons for binary inputs, LEDs for displaying the result, and an additional LED for the carry-out. Additionally, we needed to program the Arduino to handle the binary addition and output the result both in binary (via LEDs) and in decimal and hexadecimal (via an LCD).      ACTION:      	* PLANNING AND RESEARCH: I began by reviewing the course materials and additional resources on binary addition and Arduino programming. I also studied the datasheets and documentation for the components I was using.   	* CIRCUIT DESIGN: Using Tinkercad, I designed the circuit layout, ensuring each component was correctly connected. I planned the placement of the buttons, LEDs, and the LCD, and verified that the power and ground connections were correctly routed.   	* PROGRAMMING: I wrote the Arduino code to handle the binary input from the buttons, perform the addition, and display the result. This involved using functions like digitalRead(), digitalWrite(), and lcd.print() .I also included debounce logic to ensure the buttons worked reliably.   	* TESTING AND ITERATION: After building the circuit on a breadboard, I uploaded the code and began testing. Initially, there were issues with the buttons not registering inputs correctly and the LEDs not displaying the expected results. I debugged the code, adjusted the debounce timing, and checked the circuit connections. After several iterations, I achieved a working setup.   	* INTEGRATION WITH LCD: Once the basic functionality was confirmed, I integrated the LCD display. I modified the code to convert the binary result to decimal and hexadecimal and display these values on the LCD. This required understanding and utilizing the LiquidCrystal library functions effectively.
high.result: The result was a fully functional 4-bit binary adder. The LEDs correctly displayed the binary addition results, including the carry-out bit. The LCD accurately showed the decimal and hexadecimal equivalents of the binary sum. This project not only enhanced my understanding of digital circuits and binary arithmetic but also improved my programming skills and ability to troubleshoot and debug complex systems. Additionally, it provided practical experience in integrating multiple hardware components and software logic, which is invaluable for future engineering projects.      Reflecting on this experience, I realized the importance of meticulous planning, iterative testing, and thorough understanding of both the hardware and software aspects of engineering projects. This experimental inquiry approach fostered a deeper appreciation for the systematic process of engineering problem-solving.

high.situation: In my year 12 Investigating Science class there was an assignment to come up with an experimental inquiry, our group did, does the change in the volume of balloons affect the distribution of flour once popped?
high.task_action: This experiment used balloons, flour, a retort stand, and a needle. the experiment was set up with a balloon blown up 10cm, 15cm and 20cm in diameter, a ruler was also used.  Our group hypothesised that the smaller the balloon's volume, the greater the flour distribution because the pressure is inversely proportional to volume according to Boyle’s law.      The experiment was done by filling the different balloon sizes with 100 grams of flour in each, after the flour was in the different-sized balloons the retort stand was placed on a flat and open space with the balloons attached to the stand.  the balloon was popped from underneath the balloon with the needle and measured how far (the furthest spec of flour) it went with a ruler and recorded it. this experiment was repeated several times with all different balloon sizes.       It showed that the smaller the balloon was the further it travelled.
high.result: The results of the experimental inquiry showed the smaller balloon averaged 85 cm of flour being distributed compared to 31 cm from the 20cm in diameter balloon.

high.situation: During the Arduino course, we were tasked with creating a 4-bit binary adder using an Arduino microcontroller. The project required us to integrate multiple components including buttons, LEDs, and an LCD, and to write code that accurately performed binary addition and displayed the results.
high.task_action: To address this, we needed to:      	* UNDERSTAND THE REQUIREMENTS: Break down the project into manageable components: buttons for input, LEDs for output, and an LCD for displaying results.   	* CIRCUIT DESIGN: Design and build the circuit on a breadboard, ensuring all connections were correct.   	* CODE DEVELOPMENT: Write and debug the Arduino code to handle binary input, perform the addition, and display the results.   	* TROUBLESHOOT AND OPTIMISE: Identify and resolve issues with the circuit and code, such as ensuring proper button debouncing and accurate LED displays.      STEPS TAKEN:      	* BRAINSTORMING SESSION: Formed a small working group to clearly articulate the problem and devise a plan.   	* CIRCUIT ASSEMBLY: Assembled the components on a breadboard, ensuring proper connections.   	* CODE WRITING: Wrote the initial code, incorporating functions to read button inputs, perform binary addition, and control the LEDs and LCD.   	* DEBUGGING: Encountered issues such as LEDs not turning on and incorrect display on the LCD. Diagnosed and resolved these by verifying connections, adjusting code logic, and adding debouncing for buttons.
high.result: The result was a functional 4-bit binary adder that accurately performed binary addition and displayed the results on an LCD screen. The LEDs correctly indicated the binary inputs and the carry output. This project enhanced our critical thinking skills by requiring us to analyse problems, devise solutions, and iteratively improve our design and code.      OUTCOMES:      	* Successfully created a working prototype of a 4-bit binary adder.   	* Improved understanding of Arduino programming and electronic circuit design.   	* Developed problem-solving skills through troubleshooting and debugging the circuit and code.   	* Gained experience in working collaboratively to achieve a technical objective.

high.situation: For my engineering project, myself and my team were designated to design a water filtration system for outdoor survival and camping as well as less fortunate community's. high.task_action: In order to identify the cause of the issue, I led  a deep analysis on the design and materials used, as well as consulting multiple faculty's for advice. Next I organized a brainstorming session with my team to identify potential causes using a bubble diagram. We suspected the filter media was the problem. After testing different materials and experimenting and analyzing the results, we ultimately decided to make a change and use activated carbon as it proved to be far superior in removing chlorine, bad tastes, heavy metals and foul odors then any other material we used.
high.result: Due to the switch to activated carbon the water filtration system seemed to be working more effectively and efficiently in removing heavy metals from the water, meeting all standards. Due to this the project was a success and we were able to present a functional filtration system for outdoor survival and camping as well as less fortunate community's.

high.task_action: The fact that this problem is arising and becoming an obvious influence on possibly drowning my marks, I have to step up my game. For the rest of the lessons, I rewatched the machine induction videos to gain my confidence and familiarity in utilising particular machinery I would use in each lesson.
