high.situation: I need to figure out how to turn a binary number into a decimal number.
high.task_action: It was challenging to figure out how to turn the binary number gathered from the Arduino board and turn it into a number to display on the LCD screen. I really wanted to give up and just receive marks based off of what I completed but I decided to persevere because I wanted to deliver a complete assignment. I had to be resourceful so I turned to both my peers and internet research, I learnt that I could use a function to create the logic to turn a binary array into a decimal. Even though defining my own function was out of my comfort zone I decided that I'd be flexible and give it a try.
high.result: I was able to define my own function called "int binaryToDecimal", which when called could take an away and run a formula which would then return an integer that equals the value of the array.

high.situation: In this part of the ENGG unit we were challenged with the task of creating a toolbox out of either steel or aluminium, we had to measure, cut, fold, and connect our pieces to complete this task, and through this, I learnt an extensive amount about time and resources management.
high.task_action: - We began with collecting a large sheet of metal which we chose, in my case I decided to go with steel. in addition, we were given a work booklet outlining the measurements we had to adhere to. I cleared my workspace to have a clear and clean area to conduct my measuring so it was as accurate as possible, and then I was challenged with fitting all the pieces on this large piece of steel sheet, I began to take the measurements of the smaller sheets from the workbook and mark them on the larger sheet to cut out my panels, I decided to draw my smaller sheets in a way where my cutting would be effective as it cuts several pieces in one go.       - After cutting my pieces I obtained the panels which I had to now mark with their corresponding measurements to cut, bend, and drill correctly. with the time I had, I decided to focus don't eh backbone of the toolbox and began to measure and mark the bottom panel which holds everything together, to keep myself organised I put my panels in a pile and marked my name on them so that I wouldn't lose them,. with eh remaining time I marked my bottom panel, but when I saw that others were using the tools and machines that I needed to cut and bend, I decided to ask the teacher to do the handle as no one was doing it and it would save me time from waiting for the others.      - I completed the handle while everyone else was completing their panels which saved me a lot of time, then I began to mark my other panels as the others were cutting and bending their bottom panel, I decided to manage my time and resources in a way which I could complete the cutting and bending of all my panels in one go, this saved me a lot of time and allowed me to cut, bend and weld all my pieces together to create my toolbox.
high.result: Throughout this time and resources management I was able to finish my toolbox 2 weeks before the due date which allowed me to add additional painting which was not required to make it look better, this task taught me why time and resources management is important and can truly affect the outcome of a project.

This is one of the experiences which proved to me how important critical thinking is to real problem solving and event management, which I am doing use of in my mechanical engineering studies at the moment.

high.situation: As the Director of a plumbing company, I was responsible for overseeing the installation of new plumbing for a newly constructed duplex in Kensington. high.result: Our team successfully completed the plumbing installation for the duplex within the timeframe, without any excess materials leftover. Our adherence to the plan ensured effective time management whilst upholding quality standards. This experience showed the significance of strategic planning and cohesive teamwork in achieving success when meeting a deadline.

As a result, I successfully met the project requirements and received positive feedback on the clarity and precision of my drawings. This experience underscored the importance of critical thinking in engineering tasks, emphasizing the need to approach challenges methodically and continuously refine one's work to achieve optimal results.

high.situation: During a critical project at work, our team was facing significant delays due to a lack of communication and coordination among team members. This was affecting our deadlines and causing frustration among the team. As the project lead, I recognized the need for a change in our professional behavior to improve efficiency and team morale.
high.task_action: My task was to foster a more professional and collaborative work environment to get the project back on track. First, I organized a team meeting to discuss the issues and listen to everyone’s concerns. Then, I established clear communication channels, including daily stand-up meetings and a shared online workspace where team members could update their progress and share information. I also introduced a code of conduct emphasizing respect, punctuality, and accountability. Additionally, I assigned specific roles and responsibilities to each team member to ensure clarity and ownership of tasks.
high.result: The changes led to a significant improvement in our project workflow. The daily meetings ensured everyone was on the same page and could address issues promptly. The shared workspace improved transparency and coordination, allowing us to track progress more effectively. The code of conduct helped in maintaining a professional atmosphere, reducing conflicts and improving team morale. As a result, we were able to catch up on our deadlines and complete the project successfully, with the team reporting higher satisfaction and a stronger sense of collaboration.

high.situation: During one of my engineering units, I was working on a project involving gesture recognition using a C program. high.task_action: I started by diving deep into the existing code to understand how the current gestures were being recognized. It wasn't easy at first, but I took my time to carefully analyze each part. Then, I researched various algorithms that could help in recognizing new gestures more effectively. With this knowledge, I began writing new functions and tweaking the old ones to incorporate the additional gestures. Testing was a crucial part of this process; I had to ensure that the new gestures worked seamlessly with the existing ones. There were times when things didn't work as expected, but I kept refining the code until it was just right.
high.result: In the end, my efforts paid off. The program could now recognize a variety of new gestures, making it much more versatile and user-friendly. My instructor appreciated the enhancements, noting how the new gestures added significant value to the project. This experience not only improved my coding skills but also taught me a lot about critical thinking and problem-solving.

high.situation: We recently encountered a customer who operated a manufacturing facility experiencing issues with its wastewater treatment system. The plant had an outdated and underperforming wastewater treatment system. This was causing regulatory and environmental problems due to its ineffective treatment capabilities.
high.task_action: Our challenge was to design and implement a more sustainable wastewater treatment system to meet regulatory requirements and minimize the company’s footprint. We assembled a diverse team of water management, chemical processing, and environmental engineering experts to tackle this complex problem.       First, we conducted a comprehensive review of the existing business wastewater treatment system to identify key areas that must be addressed. Then we examined innovative technology and best practices in the wastewater treatment industry. We focused on methods that require minimal energy, chemicals, and waste.       Based on our assessments, we recommended a multi-pronged approach that included investing in the company’s treatment plants to incorporate state-of-the-art equipment such as membrane bioreactors and advanced oxidation systems. The company was also recommended to implement recycling programs. Other process improvements were generated to enhance resource recovery and reduce waste.        In the implementation stage, we worked closely with the company’s technical staff to ensure the proper setup and operation of the new systems and processes. Training and support were provided to help the staff adapt to the changes and to improve the systems’ performance over time. All these measures led to a significant reduction in the company’s waste footprint and the diversion of substantial amounts of recyclable materials
high.result: Our sustainable wastewater treatment solution significantly benefits to both the company and the environment. The company saved water, electricity and chemicals, leading to increased production efficiency and lower operating costs. Furthermore, the new treatment plant enabled a considerable reduction of pollutants discharged into nature which helped preserve the nearby natural ecosystems and fulfilled legal regulations. In summary, our solution addressed pressing environmental concerns and contributed to corporate sustainability. This real-life example demonstrated to the students how engineering can work towards creating a better society and environment.

high.situation: During a software development project, our team encountered a critical bug in the code that was causing the application to crash unexpectedly.
high.task_action: As the matter was urgent and delaying our project, we decided to call for a team meeting to discuss the issue and find a solution to this . In the meeting, we identified the bug's impact on the application's functionality and the consequences to be faced if not resolved soon. We divided into smaller groups based on each others best field and started debugging and fixing the issue, one group focused on isolating the root cause of the bug, while another group started drafting a temporary workaround to mitigate the impact on users. As the team leader, I ensured that there must be clear communication channels established and everyone should post their progress regularly in the group chat, and everyone understood their responsibilities. We utilized tools like Telerik Fiddler, Eclipse Debugging Tools, and shared code repositories on GitHub.
high.result: Through collaborative efforts, we successfully identified and resolved the bug within the time limitations. The temporary workaround allowed us to deploy a quick fix to minimize disturbance to users while the solution was being developed. Additionally, the incident served as a learning opportunity for the team, prompting discussions on code review processes and the importance of testing protocols to prevent similar issues in the future. As a result of our effective teamwork and communication, we strengthened our problem-solving skills and improved the overall quality of Teamwork.

high.situation: For our group project in COMP1300, we were asked to prepare a presentation on Data Breaches. high.situation: In my childhood, I was attending a school where most of the students belonged to the same cultural and most of them were from well-off families. This deprived the students a chance of experiencing and embracing the differences in cultures and identities as well as embracing different views. Therefore, there were alternative cases of exclusion with other people and prejudices, as well as using stereotypical judgments.      Task: Being an advocate of equity and diversity right from the tenure of early learning, my role was to encourage people from all backgrounds to integrate into the school environment and to embrace them as equals. This entails elimination of prejudice, appreciation of diversity, and encouraging others to do the same within institutions and social settings.      Action: To break the cycle of discriminations, I ensured as a student I took positive measures in matters relating to equity and diversity in the school. To begin with, thank you for being yourself: I asked classmates and teachers about the need to accept people as they are and honor their uniqueness. Such discussions assisted in making individuals in the school more aware of other people’s biases and stereotyping.      Then, I arranged cross-cultural festivities in which stagers could brought and presented their culture, language, and traditions to others. These events also gave the chance to students for directly coming to know other cultures and fostering positive attitudes towards their classmates who belong to different cultural groups.      I also collaborated in social activities available in school that are related to cultural diversity or the equal opportunity committee for example. These activities enabled me to engage with students and faculty with a goal to design and plan informational seminars, lectures, and promotional campaigns to address concerns about equality and cultural differences.      Moreover, they finally engaged in active confrontation of discrimination and prejudice in terms of behavior and language both in school and among friends, and argued against prejudices in rules and regulations that concerned school. I encouraged equality and diversity on school with regards to the multiple facets that define the students.
high.result: Due to these actions, there was an appreciation of the school diversity as well as the initiatives taken towards equity amongst the school community. As for the changes, there was a remarkable shift in attitudes, perceptions, as well as policies regarding multiculturalism, multilingualism, as well as diverse ethnicities of students and co-workers.      We also observed notification, in the form of less exclusion and discrimination among students as they learned to be cautious of the things they say about their fellow student. Students from all different backgrounds were able to feel as though they would be accepted at the school, which was implemented as a part of The school culture changed, making it more accepting for students from all different backgrounds.      Additionally, the change that I contributed to sustaining the dialogue for equity and diversity within the school community in order to make them values become an importance feature for the generations of students to uphold. Combined, these actions allowed for the formation of a more fair environment for all students in this school, hence an increased probability of all these students performing well.

high.situation: I received an individual Arduino assignment. Even though the given task appeared quite simple at first glance, some of them, such as comprehending what a particular component of the circuit was designed for or identifying a problem with the connection, were challenging.      If I remain unfocused, I would be at the mercy of the situations and thus be confined to one point without making any significant progress.      TASK:       In order to accomplish the task to effectively handle the unknowns and then work through building the circuit systematically.      ACTION:      	* Identify the Unknowns: For this task, I went through the instructions carefully and pointed out problematic aspects.      It could be the single function of a particular part or confusing connection diagrams and wiring details.Prioritize Learning: I developed my checklist in terms of the issues which seemed to be worth knowing more if I have to continue.Focused Research: I ensured that I looked at the prioritized unknowns with much more energy and concentration. This included browsing tutorials, or even relevant coursework in relation to a specific component or troubleshooting method.Seeking Help: When this proved insufficient, I did not shy away from asking my teacher for clarification during a teacher’s office hour or during a tutorial session.
high.result: Through prioritizing of my learning, I tried to clock my time in solving the unknowns first, which helped me to overcome the initial difficulties. With this knowledge in my hand, I could proceed to construct the circuit gradually and could resolve minor problems if they emerged.

Additionally, the team made deadlines for each of the tasks regarding the creation of the program and splitting the work within the group members to ensure time efficiency and greater focus. The developers focused on creating program while the game logic workers developed ideas of additional hand gestures and how they will align with one another in the form of rock paper and scissors.
high.result: Through creating a scrum board, deadlines and assigning roles to each member, my team were able to prioritise the assignment and equally contribute to the development of the program in the C language. This ensured we were focused and on track with all the necessary tasks to complete the assignment before the due date. The skill of being able to stay on focus and keep your priorities above your'e wants of distractions eliminates time wasting and benefits you with any task in life.

high.situation: I was behind on my tool box as a result of being ill and not being able to come into class

high.situation: For our ENGG1000 project, we were tasked with developing a Rock Paper Scissors Lizard Spock game using C. This project required us to incorporate all five gestures—rock, paper, scissors, lizard, and Spock—and adhere to specific game rules, necessitating careful planning and implementation. One of the significant challenges we faced was defining a robust solution that would handle user inputs, determine game outcomes accurately, and ensure a seamless user experience.
high.task_action: Initially, we conducted a brainstorming session to understand the project requirements and constraints. This session involved discussing potential challenges and necessary features. We realised that a structured approach was essential to methodically addressing each aspect of the project. So, I suggested using the STAR framework to guide our strategy, ensuring we tackle the situation, task, actions, and results.      Our first primary task was to design the game's logic. To achieve this, I led a subgroup to draft a flowchart that visually represented the decision-making process for each gesture and how the game would determine the winner. This flowchart was instrumental in helping us understand the intricate relationships between the gestures. To simplify the decision process, we created a lookup table that mapped out which gesture beats another, reducing the complexity of nested conditionals in our code.      Next, we divided the coding tasks among the team members, assigning specific functions to individuals based on their strengths. For example, one member focused on user input validation while another worked on the game loop and scoring mechanism. I was responsible for integrating the different functions and ensuring they worked cohesively. This involved writing test cases for each function and debugging any issues during integration.      We implemented a simple text-based interface for the user interface to keep the focus on functionality. I designed the prompts and messages to ensure clarity and ease of use. After the initial development phase, we conducted extensive testing individually and as a group to identify and fix bugs. This included testing edge cases and unexpected inputs to ensure the program's robustness.
high.result: Our collective efforts culminated in the creation of a fully functional Rock, Paper, Scissors, Lizard, and Spock game that not only met all the project requirements but also showcased our team's prowess. The game logic was clear and efficient, allowing for smooth gameplay. We handled user inputs gracefully and determined game outcomes accurately, enhancing the overall user experience.      This project yielded several positive outcomes. We not only completed the project within the given timeline but also presented a well-documented and thoroughly tested program. Our clear division of tasks and regular communication ensured effective teamwork, allowing us to collectively learn from each other's strengths and overcome challenges. Our professor commended our structured approach, particularly the effective use of the STAR framework, which bolstered our confidence in our problem-solving abilities.      This experience served as a significant milestone in my personal and professional growth. It reinforced my technical skills in C programming and enhanced my ability to work collaboratively in a team, manage a project from conception to completion, and apply structured problem-solving techniques in real-world scenarios. The project's success has inspired me to take on more challenging projects in the future.

high.situation: The start of the third lesson of Technical Drawing I began to get more comfortable in this field of engineering unlike the first lesson where my drawing skills weren't so good, along with the measurement side of things.
high.task_action: The third lesson required us to create a multi-view drawing of the following objects in the exercise. Multi-view refers to drawing the object in a 2D display from every angle (Top, Side, and Bottom). It required us to be attentive and precise with the measurements based on the following objects. These would include the diameters, corners, sides, and whether I need to adjust the size of the measurement to create a larger depiction of the drawing from each multi-view angle.       So, I started with the top view on the top-left side of the paper and when I was finished I drew lines down the paper so when I created the front view the measurements were aligned. I did the same for the bottom view on the right side of the page when I finished the front view.
high.result: Took a lot of trial and error to be precise with the measurements, however, I was able to recreate multi-view angles on the following object in the exercise.

high.situation: As a member of an engineering student organization, I was tasked with advocating for the implementation of sustainable practices within our campus facilities.
high.task_action: To address this task, I formed a small working group consisting of passionate individuals who shared the same goal of promoting sustainability. We began by conducting research to identify areas where sustainable practices could be implemented effectively. This involved studying energy consumption patterns, waste management systems, and potential renewable energy sources.      Once we had a clear understanding of the current situation, we developed a comprehensive proposal outlining our recommendations for sustainable initiatives. This included specific actions such as installing energy-efficient lighting, implementing recycling programs, and exploring the feasibility of solar panels on campus buildings.      To advocate for these initiatives, we organized meetings with key stakeholders, including university administrators, facility managers, and student representatives. During these meetings, we presented our proposal and highlighted the benefits of adopting sustainable practices, such as reduced energy costs, improved environmental impact, and enhanced campus reputation.      We also utilized various communication channels to raise awareness among the student body. This involved creating informative posters, organizing awareness campaigns, and hosting workshops to educate students about the importance of sustainability.
high.result: As a result of our advocacy efforts, the university administration recognized the value of implementing sustainable practices and agreed to take action. They allocated funds for the installation of energy-efficient lighting in several campus buildings and initiated a pilot program for recycling. Additionally, they formed a sustainability committee to review and implement further recommendations from our proposal.      The outcome of our actions was a significant reduction in energy consumption, increased recycling rates, and a greater sense of environmental responsibility among the campus community. Our advocacy efforts not only had a positive impact on the immediate campus environment but also fostered a culture of sustainability that extended beyond our organization.

TASK: My task was to ensure that our team meetings were more productive and that everyone was clear on their responsibilities. Before each meeting, I prepared an agenda with specific topics to cover and allocated time slots for each. During the meetings, I took on the role of facilitator, ensuring we stayed on topic and that everyone had a chance to speak. high.situation: Having assembled and repaired bicycles before, I joined the machining workshop to gain insight into custom fabrication, as it aligned with my interests in cycling and would help me understand how machined parts are created. The machining workshop posed a considerable challenge for me due to my lack of prior experience with such machines.      TASK:   My task was to assemble a toolbox from one sheet of steel. Throughout the weeks we would progress from manually operated tools & machines to electrically powered machines as the complexity of assembly increased.      One particular difficulty I encountered was attempting to drill a hole in the centre of the circular faces of an aluminium cylinder WITHOUT the use of a lathe. Had we been allowed to use the lathe every student's piece would have been consistently centered.   I understood that this would only be a simple task given the correct tools and procedures in which I was unsure of at the time.   So far I had only been learning the machines from other students by asking if I could watch them and also helping them in return such as operating the foot operated guillotine together for cleaner cuts.   This was at the stage in which the workshop was transitioning to electrically powered machines where I was unsure as to the procedure for the resources and machines we were able to utilise.      ACTION:   I had reached a certain point in the workshop where I was ahead of the other students so I took the initiative to approach one of the tutors for help.   Kosta was highly approachable and immediately went into action.   He went on to teach me an approximation technique with a ruler and a scribe.      Once the centre was found we then went on towards the drill press. One such useful insight he had was to use cutting fluid. Had I taken the initiative myself to use the drill press I would have not have thought of using cutting fluid thereby damaging parts.      Throughout the workshop, I recognized the importance of communication in gathering the best information to maintain the quality of my work. Drawing from my experience in the ENGG100 Arduino workshop, I developed the tenacity to ask for help when needed, driven by my desire to maintain a high standard of workmanship.
high.result: Under Kosta's guidance, we successfully created a perfectly centered hole in the aluminum cylinder. Inspired by this result, I shared the approximation technique and drill press procedure with other students who were facing similar challenges, promoting a culture of knowledge-sharing and collaboration within the workshop.      No two toolboxes are the same in this ENGG100 machining workshop and is unique to every individual.   The handiwork of students were diverse and had real world tangibility in which I believe was the reason as to why this machining workshop had such a socially engaging atmosphere.      Despite not achieving a perfect score, I earned a high distinction for my toolbox, demonstrating the value of perseverance, communication, and teamwork.   I believe machining was the most difficult as it depended on the true handiwork of students unlike the other workshops where the solutions were very straightforward and not tangibly real world.      Moving forward, I plan to apply the lessons learned from this workshop to future projects, recognizing the power of effective communication and teamwork in achieving shared goals. This experience has not only enhanced my technical skills but has also shaped my approach to working with others, fostering a mindset of continuous learning, adaptability, and mutual support.

high.situation: DURING MY TENURE AS A TEAM LEADER AT A MARKETING AGENCY, I ENCOUNTERED A CHALLENGE WITH PROFESSIONAL BEHAVIOUR AMONG TEAM MEMBERS. INSTANCES OF UNPROFESSIONAL CONDUCT, SUCH AS INAPPROPRIATE LANGUAGE IN MEETINGS, LACK OF PUNCTUALITY, AND DISRESPECTFUL INTERACTIONS, WERE BECOMING INCREASINGLY FREQUENT. THESE BEHAVIOURS NOT ONLY DISRUPTED TEAM DYNAMICS BUT ALSO RISKED DAMAGING OUR CLIENT RELATIONSHIPS AND OVERALL PRODUCTIVITY.      TASK:MY TASK WAS TO ADDRESS THE ISSUE OF UNPROFESSIONAL BEHAVIOUR WITHIN THE TEAM AND CULTIVATE A WORKPLACE ENVIRONMENT CHARACTERIZED BY RESPECT, ACCOUNTABILITY, AND PROFESSIONALISM.      ACTION:      To address unprofessional behaviour within the team, I implemented several key strategies. First, I communicated clear expectations regarding professional behaviour during team discussions. Then, I created and enforced specific rules to guide behaviour and conducted regular check-ins to ensure compliance. Additionally, I organized training sessions focused on improving communication and time management skills for team members. I also introduced a rewards system to incentivize positive behaviour and reinforce adherence to professional standards. Lastly, I fostered an environment where team members felt comfortable expressing their thoughts and feelings openly.
high.result: These actions led to significant positive outcomes. Firstly, team members began treating each other with more respect and cooperation, creating a more positive work environment. Secondly, clients noticed the positive change in team dynamics and expressed higher satisfaction with our work. Thirdly, with fewer conflicts and better collaboration, the team's productivity and efficiency improved, resulting in timely completion of tasks. Additionally, team members developed their professional skills and felt a sense of personal accomplishment and growth. Lastly, these changes became ingrained in the team's culture, leading to long-term benefits and continued professional development.

high.situation: Design and build an engine management wiring harness including implementation of all required sensors and modules for use in an off road vehicle.
high.task_action: The design and implementation requires the use of a number of sensors and modules to not only allow for correct and efficient operation of the engine, but also monitor engine vitals, provide feedback and then allow for real time adjustment of engine running parameters based on the feedback data and current vehicle load positions. This also incudes data acquired from external sensors measuring a range of environmental factors. This required us to evaluate the necessary data requirements to allow for not only basic operation of the engine, but adequate operation in elevated/high stress environments for extended periods of time. Wire material, coating material, and thickness's were all evaluated based on current requirements and cross referenced with current state of the art technologies available. This was also true for sensor selection with the added requirement of ease of replacement during operation as well as ease of procurement should multiple failures occur.
high.result: The result was being able to identify, in entirety, the necessary data acquisition requirements for the engine management system to allow for not only basic operation, but operation at consistently elevated load levels over a wide range of external environmental factors.

high.situation: We needed to brainstorm a design mechanism for the RGM that has to be presented by our team.
high.task_action: Undergoing a brainstorming session; forming a small working group with the problem statement clearly articulated and using the gallery method of brainstorming we devised several viable options.  Subsequently,  we evaluated each of the suggested options and rank them in order of importance.
high.result: The result was being able to select a design solution from the list of quite a number of solutions generated in the brainstorming session. We broke down the required tasks for each member to further work on it.

high.situation: Our team was in the critical stages of designing a Rube Goldberg machine for an upcoming competition. The design process required innovative thinking and meticulous planning to ensure each component worked seamlessly to accomplish a simple task in the most complex way possible. However, just as we were about to finalize our design, I faced an urgent family emergency that required me to travel overseas unexpectedly. This situation left the team without one of its key members during a pivotal phase of the project.      TASK:       Unfortunately in the midst of everything I had a family emergency requiring me to go overseas. This completely caught us of guard however after critically analyzing the task for the team was to continue refining and building the Rube Goldberg machine, ensuring that the project remained on track for the competition deadline. The challenge was to maintain the momentum of critical thinking and problem-solving without direct input from all team members. The team had to find a way to collaborate effectively, share ideas, and make decisions in a decentralized manner, ensuring the continuity of our project under unexpected circumstances.      ACTION:       In response to this challenge, the team employed several strategies to foster critical thinking and collaboration. First, we established a virtual workspace using online collaboration tools such as Miro and Google docs which allowed for seamless communication and file sharing. We scheduled weekly video calls and a WhatsApp group chat to discuss progress, brainstorm solutions to design challenges, and make collective decisions. During these meetings, each team member was encouraged to contribute ideas and feedback, ensuring that multiple perspectives were considered in every decision. To compensate for my absence, roles were temporarily re-assigned, with specific individuals taking on additional responsibilities to oversee critical aspects of the design process. The team also adopted an agile approach to problem-solving, breaking down tasks into smaller, manageable parts and using rapid prototyping to test and refine ideas quickly.
high.result: Despite the unexpectancy of me going overseas and the team working without one of its key members, the team successfully maintained its critical thinking and problem-solving momentum. The use of digital collaboration tools enabled effective communication and idea-sharing, ensuring that the project stayed on course. Our agile approach to design and problem-solving allowed us to quickly adapt and refine our Rube Goldberg machine, ultimately leading to the successful completion of a fully functional and creatively complex device. The team's ability to think critically and collaborate effectively in my absence not only ensured that we met our project deadline but also strengthened our collective problem-solving skills and resilience. This experience underscored the importance of flexibility, communication, and a strong team dynamic in overcoming unexpected challenges.

high.situation: While I was enrolled in the Physics Design course, I was required to attend laboratory work. I actively participated in an experiment aimed at Visualising Electric and Magnetic Fields-on liquid crystals. It was a paired lab task. The experiment was designed to show how electric and magnetic fields affect the alignment and characteristics of liquid crystals, giving participants a concrete means to witness these fields' impacts. The lab was furnished with powerful magnets and elaborate arrangements of fine glassware holding the liquid crystals.      TASK / ACTION :      I was completely immersed in the difficult process of assembling the equipment and preparing the liquid crystal samples.In the middle of the experiment, while I was adjusting the electrodes for applying a magnetic field to the liquid crystals, my gloved hand slipped, leading to a sudden increase in voltage and an insignificant explosion of a nearby capacitor. The fragments of glass from the protective casing broke in every possible direction, and one of them injured my arm. I rushed for the main power switch with my undamaged hand and quickly turned off all of the electrical devices to remove any potential threat from electrical hazards. Hearing the disturbance, my lab partner—who had been working on the opposite side of the lab—hurried over. He was trying not to freak out when he saw how hurt I was and how the glass was all over the place. My main worry was safety, not only for myself but also for my lab partner and anyone else nearby.
high.result: An absence from lab work was required to allow for healing after the injury, which required several stitches. Our laboratory's safety procedures and the way our experiments are set up have been thoroughly reviewed in response to this incident. In order to conduct research with potentially hazardous materials and handle electrical components safely, we upgraded the safety measures. The experience also reinforced the significance of paying close attention to details and being mindful of safety when performing physics experiments, particularly when working with intricate setups like those involving electric and magnetic fields on liquid crystals. Even with that hampered the experience made me even more determined to pursue experimental physics with an even greater emphasis on accuracy and safety.

high.situation: Every week, our team must present to our tutor what we have completed so far in the week as a team. This mostly details an oral presentation of our team's Miro board contributions to the current week's tasks. This is done through a Zoom meeting, where the presenter shares their Miro board to the common room. This includes the group tutor and the other team's under that Tutor's charge.
high.task_action: We usually aim to finish our group discussions roughly 30 minutes before we are asked to return to the common room (where are asked to present). Usually I, along with the team, discuss the various ideas and options we have presented in our Miro board before deciding what to add/ remove from our board before presenting. We also go back and forth on the ideas on our board that truly reflect the spirit of the team, and whether or not we are confident in presenting this information to our tutor and the other groups. With this extra 30 minutes or so of time, I try to familiarise myself with the board's content and any diagrams/ infographics the team has come up with so that I may be able to better explain and preview it to the tutor and other groups.      Once I am confident that I understand the content of the board well enough, I go ahead and structure it in a way that is easy for the other teams to follow along. This usually includes adding some sort of structure to annotations, adding colour and pictures to any boring words and having the Miro board appear presentable and clean before I give my oral presentation to the class.      Upon presenting our team's board to the tutor, I try my best to talk slowly and clearly into my microphone so that everyone is able to keep up with my words and with the content being displayed. When talking, I also scroll across the Miro board to reference the content I'm explaining so that the audience is not forced to look for it. Due to bandwidth limitation in Zoom, the share screen stream is usually blurry. To compensate for this, I zoom into the Miro board so that it is legible enough for everyone to view. I also keep track of the time on my phone so that I know when to stop presenting and to wrap up before time is over.
high.result: Following these guidelines when it comes to presentation, I'm confident that our oral presentations every week are one of the best among our tutor's group. Taking that extra half an hour or so of time to properly go over the Miro board content and organizing it really helps me out during presentations.

high.situation: I had decided to include a Galton board within my section of the Rube Goldberg machine, however I did not have one on hand and had no prior experience with constructing one from scratch.      Task: I was to build a Galton board from scratch using supplies I had lying around the house as to maximise sustainability of the project and avoid trips to the shops and unnecessarily buying materials. I needed to learn how to make a Galton board, and apply my learnings to construct a working Galton board.      Action: I took to google image search to take inspiration from a range of Galton board designs available on the internet, taking note of what materials were used, what kind of ball was passed through them, how large the pins in the board were etc. After the initial research phase, I decided with another group member for the sake of continuity I would use a ping pong ball and I took to the garage to find a scrap piece of wood that could act as the main basis of the construction of my Galton board as well as some pin-like objects that I could use in the construction of my board. I settled upon some nails and initially used a pencil and measurements of the ping pong balls diameter to mark out where I would hammer the nails into the board, I then tested the board with my pre measured points, however due to the thickness of the nails and my inaccuracy with a hammer, the gaps were far too small for the ball to fit through. I spent a day in the garage with an extra piece of scrap wood experimenting with different nail distances until I was able to test run the ball through the Galton board smoothly, allowing it to pass each row of nails either between two nails, or between one nail and the edge of the board.
high.result: As a result of my research and development, I was able to go from not having a Galton board and not knowing anything about how to construct one, to having a perfectly working Galton board, made out of recycled materials, ready for use in my section of the Rube Goldberg Machine.

high.situation: During weeks 2 through to 5 we discussed ideas, themes as well as our design concept for our Rube Goldberg Machine to which the team came up with a creative solution to the given task, demonstrating our ability to think beyond the box. In order to ensure that everyone completes the same amount of work and that no one is left with an excessive workload, the assignment should also be broken down for each member.      PERSONAL FEELING/REFLECTION: As I think back on our experience working on the Rube Goldberg Machine design process, I'm proud of the teamwork that our members displayed. Through various means, such as ideation and equitable delegating of tasks, we have nurtured a spirit of fairness and unity. I'm eager to see our concepts come to life and our group's goal realized as we move into the prototyping stage. Notwithstanding the difficulties that lie ahead, I have faith that we can work together to overcome them by relying on our common commitment and encouragement. Along with being a machine, this project has also been a voyage of development, creativity, and teamwork, which I am thankful to have shared with such a creative and dedicated group of people.       LEARNING: We have gained an understanding of the value of effective teamwork and collaborative innovation from our experience designing the Rube Goldberg Machine. We've developed a feeling of justice and cohesion among our team members by doing in-depth brainstorming sessions, clearly defining roles and responsibilities, and using tools like Miro for communication and planning. We know that the prototyping phase will present obstacles, but we also know that we can work together to solve them. In addition to imparting useful technical skills, this project has taught us the value of cooperation and a common vision in accomplishing our objectives.

high.situation: Upon enrolling in ENGG1050 I discovered that the entire unit was to be conducted online. Lectures, tutorials, individual and group work; all to take place in an online environment. The unit runs this way to help us (the students) prepare to navigate the professional world, which is increasingly turning to online forums for communication. This means that there has been no face to face interaction with staff or students. As a result, we have had to employ a variety of strategies to facilitate communication between peers, particularly as this unit revolves around a group initiative to design and construct a Rube Goldberg Machine (RGM).      TASK:      The task at hand has been to implement various streams of communication between myself and my group. The type of communication has to be professional and readily available to each individual to facilitate quick and easy transmission of information and ideas. We must ensure that all group members are on the same page at all times so that everyone is aware of their roles and responsibilities.      ACTION:   To communicate effectively amongst the group we have utilised many different online applications. These include Zoom, WhatsApp, Google Docs, and Miro. Each team meeting is held over Zoom and the agenda and minutes for the meetings are collaboratively created and shared via Google Docs. We organise the times for our meetings and pose questions/answers to each other outside of meeting times through a WhatsApp group chat. Miro has been used to create our team charter and timeline, to brainstorm, and to upload diagrams and sketches of our design solutions.
high.result: As a result of using numerous communication streams, my group has been able to successfully share our thoughts and ideas in a professional and constructive manner to progress the creation of our RGM. Zoom facilitates real time conversations, which are crucial in addressing and overcoming issues. For example, we ran into a problem regarding how each section of the machine would fit together. Using Zoom, we were able to conduct a quick survey of common materials each team member had access to in order to create the illusion of a seamless RGM. Creating and circulating our agendas and minutes via Google Docs has allowed group members to stay up to date with the team’s progress and to be aware of their responsibilities, even when unable to attend team meetings. WhatsApp has been crucial for sharing videos of the advancements made to the RGM. This enables the group to visualise how the RGM is going to look. It is very important to know how the section before your own looks, in order to match the lighting, background, and camera angle, and as a result, create a smooth transition. Finally, using Miro has allowed our group to have a visual record of the progression of our ideas. We are able to upload evolving designs and annotate each other’s work, demonstrating how Miro has been a highly useful mode of communication.

With the six of us in a group we began brainstorming materials, theme ideas and actions required for our project. We decided on an RGM theme which revolved around a student that had to wake up early to study but then got up late. Tools such as Miro for brainstorming and design, and Discord for weekly meetings were utilised to help us achieve this.
high.task_action: We started by brainstorming ideas together during our tutorial workshops and during our weekly group meetings, ensuring everyone had a chance to contribute. Individually, we listed materials we had lying around our house and brainstormed on ideas, themes, and actions we could perform linking each other’s part together. Given the constraints of not being able to meet face-to-face, we utilised the online whiteboard platform, Miro, to facilitate our brainstorming sessions and visually map out our preliminary design. We also utilised our weekly Zoom class tutorials and Discord meetings to ensure effective communication. Additionally, we established a structured approach to organising our roles and responsibilities using templates within Miro, such as a role rotation calendar, a timeline, and a notice board.
high.result: At this stage, we’ve reached the preliminary design phase. We have successfully brainstormed and evaluated our ideas for each of our video sections and selected an appropriate theme idea. Each team member has been allocated specific roles and tasks for the upcoming weeks based on our established role rotation calendar. This ensures an organised approach to our RGM progress, facilitating effective teamwork and task management.

Furthermore, given the time available to this member due to their inactivity with our group and in class sessions, they should be able to complete this simple task to an extremely high standard.

high.result: Through the implementation of team-based work, I have been able to develop my interpersonal skills. high.situation: As a science student with no background in engineering, I lacked experience in planning, designing, and carrying out practical tasks, especially online. I decided to take ENGG1050 as my flexible unit which was an online unit in order to close this gap. As part of this course, we had to design a Rube Goldberg Machine (RGM) in groups. Hence, It was essential to have the preliminary design completed before week 6 in order to be able to have our design ready for the group presentation assessment.
high.task_action: Throughout weeks of 1-6, my team has consistently worked and communicated thoroughly as to what we aim to achieve together and how our RGM would look like by the end of the semester. Also, as responsible team members, what our individual roles are each week to work on building better skills in this unit. Initially, it was a little difficult in building ideas and understanding our goal in 1-2. However, through effective communication we were able to understand each other and understand what we needed/what research was required to build our ideas in terms of progress.              My team and I gradually worked together and fulfilled weekly tasks by time management. This was done as each week at the start of the lesson everyone would pick their tasks to meet the agenda/weekly tasks. For example, one week, I would work on the agenda minutes, while one of my team members would choose to present orally in that week, and another team member would write out the reflection and the everyone would contribute to work on a plan ahead to our RGM design Since we have set this goal, as to each week we rotate our roles everyone will have the chance to understand what is happening in the meeting and everyone is on the same page. In order to learn as much out of the unit, I took any opportunities/tasks available during the group work to build my skills and challenged myself to complete tasks on time. At the end of each lesson as a team we provide feedback and then approve the agenda/minute and reflection before submission. This tactic allows us and especially myself to gain better grades, therefore we all agreed to follow the same procedure each lesson. We have been receiving good grades so far and have been giving our weekly goals successfully through the help of the Miro website. Our team was able to concurrently and effectively work on a brainstorm without us meeting in person. Additionally, we had a place that any team member could independently access at any time, saved our progress on the cloud, and allowed us to access at any time.             Our group also made timeliness a priority, embracing the maxim "pull up your weight, don't be late." Our ability to fulfil deadlines and goals was facilitated by our dedication to punctuality, which also strengthened our accountability.             RESULTS: Now that our initial RGM design has been built, we have a clearer idea of how important it is to assess needs and set weekly goals that are doable. Without our team's efficient cooperation, which stayed dependable and concentrated on our weekly goals, this accomplishment would not have been feasible.              Additionally, by utilising online learning tools like Zoom and Miro, which significantly improved our capacity for cooperation and communication, we were able to accomplish our weekly objectives. These platforms made efficiency and convenience possible at a level that would not have been possible otherwise.             Personally, I've discovered that it's best to start with simple objectives and progressively take on bigger tasks every week. I was able to clearly grasp the weekly needs and the objectives I needed to set by gathering the bravery to present and finish weekly chores on time. Setting goals allowed all team members to participate equally and was crucial in forming our initial design.In addition, our group created a culture where proposals were welcomed and received constructive criticism in addition to real interest in them. My capacity to work well in a team and to communicate effectively was enhanced by this experience.

high.situation: Working on presenting our group's preliminary design

In order to achieve this, we separated the whole task into smaller roles and gave each person a role. For example, one person would be in charge of infographics and making sure the presentation was ledgible and clear. Another person was in charge of writing up the parts of the presentation for our materials and where we could get them from. Another member took on the role of verbally presenting the presentation to the rest of the 1050 cohort.       We attempted to fairly and evenly distribute the tasks and played to the strengths of each group member. We asked who among us was the most confident in terms of presenting, who had the best eye for making the presentation look professional, and who could put our ideas to words in the most eloquent and clear manner. Using this method, we believe that we made the 'best' presentation we possibly could have as each role was taken on by the person who we, collectively as a group, thought had the most fitting skillset for said role.
high.result: we created an effective and well executed presentation on our preliminary design that was met with positive feedback from both tutor and fellow peers.

high.situation: First of all, I want to share a quote from one of my favourite books, "The Creative Act: A Way of Being" by Rick Rubin. He says, "The creative process is a dance between chaos and order, between intuition and intellect". With that in mind, I entered a DJ competition in Sydney last year. I didn't win, but I got a lot of positive feedback from the judges and other competitors. They noticed my work stood out because we were all supposed to show off our skills on DJ decks and mix music, but I took a different route. Instead of playing the usual tunes people expect, I decided to blend Mongolian sounds with electronic music, which turned out pretty cool. Now, some might think this has nothing to do with my engineering classes. But to me, DJing is a form of art, just like music and engineering are. They're all about creating something new and expressive.
high.task_action: So, the idea might be simple at first, something maybe a lot of us have thought about "mixing traditional folk songs with modern music". But actually bringing that idea to life, making it real, that was the real challenge for me. Like Rick Rubin points out in his book, "Creativity is like a dance between chaos and order". It's something we all understand, but I'm not saying it's impossible, actually making it happen is not that easy. So, what I did was listen to hundreds of Mongolian traditional songs and sounds that could blend with electronic music. Then came the real test: blending them together using DJ controllers, which was pretty good and unique according to my judges and competitors. DJ decks can get pretty complex with all the different controls and options, kind of like engineers working on big projects like bridges, etc. Sure, building a bridge is a much bigger task, but what I want to say is both rely on creativity.
high.result: So, what I'm getting at is this creativity is all around us. We can't live without creativity or imagination. We need them to thrive and to keep improving things, whether it's in music, engineering, or anything else. Sometimes, what might seem small or insignificant to you could actually be something totally new and groundbreaking for the world around us.

high.situation: While I was enrolled in the Introduction to Engineering course, I had to go to weekly workshops that were aimed to provide us with practical experience regarding various engineering perspectives. I actively participated in a project making aimed at Civil Modelling. The project was all about utilising paper and hard boards to create a model building. Five groups, each with five individuals, were formed out of our class. Our team was excited to embark on creating an incredible model. Our ability to apply academic information in a real-world situation and develop our problem-solving and teamwork abilities was enhanced by the workshops, which served as both an instructional and practical platform.       TASK/ ACTION:       Considering each person's strengths, our group divided everyone into distinct positions. However, Nay, one of the members, was tasked with cutting the materials and making sure that every component was prepared for assembly. He appeared uninterested right away and frequently showed up late for our scheduled meetings. Notwithstanding our endeavours to engage him, he displayed no enthusiasm in making a contribution to the project. The pressure escalated as the deadline drew near. He showed up for one of our last workshops clearly agitated and disoriented. He ignored our attempts to communicate and spent the most of the time on his phone rather than concentrating on the assignment that was assigned to him. He reacted dismissively when we reminded him of his duties, stating, "It is merely a workshop, thus it is not really important." When he hurriedly cut numerous pieces of hardboard without accurately measuring them out of aggravation, everything went south. This thoughtless move produced components that were not suitable for our model and were uneven. "Utilise what you have; it suffices,” he shrugged when we challenged him about it.
high.result: Nay's incompetent actions had serious and fast repercussions. We had a little window of time before the presentation, so we had to quickly get new supplies and accurately recut the parts. For the remainder of the group, this resulted in needless stress and additional work hours. Though we did our best, the finished model was not as well-polished or solid structurally as we had hoped. The tutors identified our model's shortcomings throughout the presentation, pointing out its uneven cuts and lack of coherence. In comparison to other groups, we got lesser grades, and this had a noticeable negative impact on our group's morale. The event made clear how important it is for each team member to contribute and how unprofessional behaviour can derail the efforts of the entire group. Our initial joy quickly gave way to annoyance upon realising that weeks' worth of labour could be lost due to the carelessness of one person. Our team's trust and collaboration were damaged, which emphasises how important it is for everyone involved in a joint endeavour to take responsibility and work hard.

high.situation: The innovation process aims to develop high-efficiency power transformers for battery grid substations. These transformers will charge batteries during off-peak periods and supply them back to the grid during peak hours.
high.task_action: These substations require transformers that can handle the unique demands of charging and discharging large battery banks, with minimal energy loss and high reliability.       So, we created a team for this project and brainstormed some ideas that could help us determine the customer requirements and some key considerations. Finally, we came up with our Preliminary design. The team also discussed risks to the on-time delivery of the current project and some ideas as to how we will mitigate these risks.       RESULTS: As a result, we considered some key factors such as,      	* Efficiency   	* Reliability      	* Cost-Effectiveness      	* Thermal Management (cooling systems)      	* Materials and Components       After considering all the factors, the team developed power transformers tailored for battery energy storage systems (BESS) integrated into grid substations, focusing on improving efficiency, reliability, and cost-effectiveness. The team decided on the final design, which can be submitted to the customer for approval. The company also guarantee that the ultimate design complies with all crucial safety and performance standards.

RESULTS:      As our Final RGM design nears completion, the importance of assessing needs and setting achievable weekly goals becomes increasingly apparent. high.situation: We had a project in engineering class where we had to design and construct a Rube Goldberg machine. The idea was to build a multi-step, intricate machine that could execute a simple task in a sophisticated manner. My group decided to construct a machine that waters the plant.      TASK:   The assignment involved coming up with ideas, designing, building, and demonstrating a working Rube Goldberg machine that complied with project specifications. We had to make sure that the machine was creative, dependable, and interconnected at every stage. We also had to explain the physics behind each step in our design process and document the entire process.      ACTION:   To achieve this, my team and I took the following actions:      	*   BRAINSTORMING AND PLANNING: To generate ideas for the machine, we began with a brainstorming session. We discussed the viability of various concepts that we drew using the gallery method. We came up with a ten-step plan that included pulleys, rolling balls, levers, and domino effects.      	*   DESIGN AND PROTOTYPING: Each team member was given a specific part to design and prototype after I divided the machine into sections. First models of each step were made with cardboard, string, marbles, and other materials. To ensure seamless transitions between steps, we regularly convened to test how each component would interface with the next.      	*   CONSTRUCTION AND TESTING: Once our prototypes were improved, we put the entire machine together. Precise alignment and adjustments were necessary to guarantee dependability. To find and address areas of failure, we conducted a number of tests and made adjustments to increase consistency.      	*   DOCUMENTATION: We kept records of our designs, difficulties, and solutions all along the way. For every stage, we drew intricate diagrams and wrote explanations that emphasized the pertinent physics concepts, like momentum, energy transfer, and gravity.      	*   PRESENTATION: Finally, we prepared a presentation to demonstrate our machine to the class. We explained each step, the design choices we made, and the physics concepts illustrated by the machine.
high.result: The intricate and entertaining task of pouring a cup of tea was successfully completed by our Rube Goldberg machine. The project was well accepted, and our professor and peers were impressed by the ingenuity and complexity of our machine. We received great marks for our comprehensive documentation and lucid presentation. Our ability to work as a team, solve problems, and apply engineering concepts practically all improved as a result of this project.

high.situation: In Week 3 of our Rube Goldberg machine project, we reached a point where we needed to decide on a theme and define what our PROBLEM STATEMENT for our machine was actually going to be. Choosing the right theme/problem statement was crucial because it would shape everything we did afterwards, from the design to the final build. Everyone in the team had different ideas about what we should do, and each idea came with its own challenges and unknowns.      TASK: As one of the active members during the project planning phase, I took on the role of helping our team decide on a theme for our Rube Goldberg machine. This task meant dealing with a lot of different opinions and trying to find a common ground that worked for everyone. Our project had specific goals and limitations we needed to consider.      ACTION: Understanding the importance of making a well-informed decision, I set up an extra meeting to help us get past our deadlock. At the meeting, I laid out a SWOT analysis for each theme idea, looking at the strengths, weaknesses, opportunities, and threats. I had prepared this by asking everyone for their input beforehand. This approach gave us a clear framework to discuss each option. I encouraged everyone to share their thoughts and stressed the importance of choosing a theme that was not only creative but also practical, considering what we could actually achieve with the resources and time we had.
high.result: After a lot of discussion and a clear look at the pros and cons of each theme, we all agreed on one that was both creative and doable within our limits and the time we had. This decision brought us all together with a clear direction, really shaping how we planned and carried out the rest of our project. Everyone valued the organized way we made our decision, which not only solved the issue at hand but also made us stronger as a team for future projects. This whole experience really highlighted how important it is to step up and make decisions, even when things aren’t certain, and it helped me grow a lot in leading and bringing people together.
