high.result: despite the time constraints, I managed to finish my aluminum toolbox one week before the due date. If it wasn't for time management, I would not have completed the toolbox that early. it is important to manage time in order to meet project deadlines and improve task productivity.

high.situation: I am completing this unit in a timezone that opposes Australia's timezone and in a country where finding resources to build the RGM is somewhat difficult. Our team has to meet up weekly, and finding a time that suits my partners' time is not easy given the timezone circumstances.
high.task_action: We were able to select a time when it is not too early in Beirut and not too late in Sydney(11 am and 5 pm respectively). This way every group member can be equally productive and contribute to the completion of our project. Moreover, we decided to use resources which can be commonly found in our homes and in both countries, while keeping our RGM interesting.
high.result: The results were satisfactory and we were able to meet and create a conceptual design for our RGM using the resources available.

high.situation: We needed to have accountability in our team operations to form a successful team
high.task_action: Accountability in our group: We discussed what elements are required to have a successful group and effective project management and one of the items we landed on was accountability. Accountability is given out through the delegation of tasks, each week we have a different person presenting and doing minutes to ensure a fair share of accountability. In the RGM everyone is accountable for their actions within their videos, and the transitioning from person to person.
high.result: This has resulted in fair sharing of the workload, which has kept the group members happy as no one feels as if they are doing all the work.

high.situation: We had to design and successfully implement an RGM. Although the design process was simple to some extent and only challenged our creative thinking, the implementation turned out to be difficult as it required a lot of time, supplies, and effort with often minimal reliability. For instance, adding mentos to a coke bottle tied to a skateboard did not give it enough velocity to move on a horizontal plane and getting a dominoes effect to open a box is difficult.
high.task_action: For the above reasons and more, our team had to re-evaluate or redesign numerous parts of the RGM during meetings outside the scheduled workshops based on the reliability which deduced from the performed tests. Our goal was to make the RGM completely functional, all while maintaining creativity. For example, we decided to add a metal pipe and balance it on a stool, while attaching its head to the box, once the dominoes effect reached the edge of the pipe it changed balance and opened the box.
high.result: After replacing the non-functional parts of the RGM with more realistic and well-tested parts, the RGM seemed to perform the required task in a reliable and creative manner.

high.situation: WITHIN MY WORKPLACE WE HAD MANY LARGE DELIVERIES THAT HAD TO BE TRANSPORTED TO THE STORE HOWEVER DUE TO THE SMALL SIZE OF THE WORKSHOP AND NOT HAVING A DELIVERY DOCK WE HAD TO FIND AN EFFECTIVE METHOD OF MOVING ITEMS.
high.task_action: As a workplace team we had to undergo brainstorming sessions in order to figure out how to deal with our situation. We used brainstorming ideas and white boards to collect our ideas and methods and devise options that we could possibly use to transport our goods into the store as the goods were mainly oversized and wouldn’t fit through a conventional door. We came to evaluate every viable option that was supplied and then choose which ones we thought could work and those which would not work to devise our best options.
high.result: The result was then being able to have many different options that we could choose from that would all have the same end goal in mind and therefore allow us to move our large goods into the store.

high.situation: Shortly after the commencement of session two I was approached by an organisation to partake in an internship that relates to my studies. Seeing it as a great opportunity to increase my knowledge and experience I accepted the offer whilst understanding the workload I would be taking on. This would require careful planning and thought from week to week, making sure I was able to complete what I needed to get done. My attitude towards work would need to remain professional and enthusiastic as I took on more responsibility and my approach to learning must adapt accordingly if I am to stay on top of my university studies.
high.task_action:
Developing an understanding of my role and responsibilities at work was difficult at first as I was not provided with much information outlining the scope of my position. This left me to investigate my role further by asking many questions and developing a proactive attitude towards this issue. Thankfully by asking those questions I was able to quickly build relationships within my team that aided me in learning the functions of my role. Once I had an understanding of this it allowed me to begin building a plan and weekly schedule around my work and studies. 

My approach to learning this session has been refined as I adapted to the added pressure of work. Where I would once have the entire week devoted to studies I now must share this time, which has resulted in me transitioning from a quantity based approach to shorter quality study sessions. This improvement is an ongoing learning experience as I develop a stronger attitude towards university and no longer allow distractions to interrupt my studying.  

I have structured my week into two parts where I work from Monday to Thursday which leaves Friday to Sunday to complete any university tasks and study. Throughout the week I put thought into what needs to be done during those three study days and write a to-do list to complete before the following week. I have created a numbering system for each week's list which takes into account the importance, difficulty and urgency of each item which I then use to allocate appropriate times to each.
high.result: The results of the development in my attitude and learning techniques have helped me greatly as I have not missed a task or deadline thus far and have been satisfied with the marks I have achieved. Along with this, I believe that I am performing in my internship and learning about the industry. Many of the soft skills I am developing from work have also helped me in my studies.

high.situation: Our team for the semester was newly formed and we had yet to get to know one another or really engage in extended discussion beyond course material. I was the spokesperson for the week so I felt it was a good opportunity as the de-facto leader to have each of us talk about ourselves for a little bit - beyond name, major and a 'fun fact'.
high.task_action: I volunteered to go first, as I knew this could be somewhat awkward or embarrassing for people when with a group of strangers. I felt going first would set a good example and create a positive expectation for everyone, since admitting a weakness in particular can still have a stigma attached to it - making it hard to do outside of a comfortable social environment. Once I got the ball rolling, it was fairly smooth as each member discussed their contribution to the Strength/Weakness section we had added to our Miro charter. Whilst much of the attributes we discussed were common, it was good to get to know each other on a more personal level and have the team dynamic start to feel a little more natural. Following on from that, there was a noticeable increase in natural conversation between team members, and not just about the course work - which was a good indication that the team building exercises had yielded some results.
high.result: This activity resulted in a more pleasant atmosphere among the team, and has led to members more actively contributing to discussions as they feel they can speak without being judged or spoken down to. I believe those early efforts to conduct team building exercises has positively impacted our group, and that we would not work as well as we do had we not done team building exercises early.

high.situation: In my first year at Macquarie University, I took Engineering Design (ENGG1050). The objective of this course was to teach the necessary team-working, and organisation skills needed in a modern online engineering environment. This was facilitated by forming a team and working to create a Rube Goldberg Machine building and utilising a multitude of relevant skills along the way. During Week 5, my team needed to complete our conceptual design for our RGM with a theme of sustainability and an end goal of watering a plant.
high.task_action: I discussed the presentation of our conceptual design at week 5’s meeting and deconstructed the parts of the conceptual design into manageable parts. Each section of the 7 sections of the RGM required at least 2 action events. By this point, our group had already brainstormed a variety of ideas for each of our sections but no choice was committed to. I decided to set a goal for the team, to have decided on the two action items for their section before our week 6 meeting.
high.result: As a result of this goal we were able to start work on creating a visual representation of our conceptual design on Miro aiding in our ability to present to the tutor group. This also increased team engagement as we were able to monitor each other's work and review everyone's section.

high.situation: During Week 5, the group had the task of creating the preliminary flow of the Rude Goldberg Machine. This encompassed group teamwork and analysis of how the machine will work effectively.
high.task_action: With our goal for the week set, we needed to have key focus areas of the project that will impact the end product significantly. After reflecting on last week’s work, we focused on making sure the RGM would flow through our individual projects smoothly. Also, we needed to understand the degree of difficulty of each action in our RGM. We completed a WHS risk assessment that covered the main risks and hazards associated with all our multiple actions as well as the difficulty of each scaling them from most to least challenging, focusing our attention more on the harder actions to prototype and test.
high.result:
The result was a clear first Miro design of our RGM. It will allow us to find weaknesses in our design and alternative actions for each person.

PERSONAL FEELING/REFLECTION: As we accomplished the major goal for the week well, it took a lot of pressure off the group as this visual communication board helped a lot to lay out all our ideas that we had during the previous weeks and onto a central flowchart.

LEARNING: As a group, we had no issues with communicating our ideas, but I found organising and planning these ideas out to be a very beneficial step in the process where after our brainstorm everyone found the task less complex once jotting out the proposed design. Through this experience, I have found the attitude and willingness for progression in the group work as well as building my manner of Professionalism with writing agenda, minutes, reflections and presenting presentations in a professional routine.

high.situation: We needed to create as new solution concept after deciding that our previous solution or cracking on egg and cooking it was too unrealistic.
high.task_action: Organised a team meeting to specifically address and create a new solution concept. After each member suggested a new solution concept, we collectively identified the strengths and weaknesses of each solution.
high.result: As a result of this meeting, it was decided that the new solution concept would involving boiling an egg rather than frying it. This changed reduced the number of complex and unreliable processes in the solution.

high.situation: Decide on the function our Rube Goldberg Machine (RGM) was to perform.
high.task_action: After several team meetings over the Week 4, we decided that frying an egg would be the ultimate goal of our RGM as we had a kitchen themed name so it would only be appropriate if we had a kitchen themed function. However, upon further brainstorming and planning unique events, the more complex we realised this goal was for us. So in our final team meeting before the Week 5 finalisation of our RGM the idea was presented that this function was too hard and that we should consider another function. This function ended up being a thread attached to an egg being burned so it is dropped into a boiling pot of water to boil the egg.
high.result: This resulted in a revamp of our MIRO board in order to incorporate this new final function and making our overall project simpler by unique events being easier to decide and splitting each event to different team members efficiently and effectively.

high.situation: During weeks 1-5 of the Engineering Design course, we have done many engineering teamwork exercises leading up to our overall project, the Rube Goldberg Machine (RGM).
high.task_action: As sharing my ideas is a big part of my personal and professional skills, I have come up with many helpful ideas for the RGM. I have also improved my teamwork when providing risks for the project, objects and materials and swapping between roles each week. I also planned my individual part for the RGM project so that I am prepared to submit what is needed when the project is being completed.
high.result: The result of this teamwork and my contribution of ideas has led to an improvement in my personal and professional skills. The design for our RGM is now also complete and the group is nearly ready to start building the whole RGM project.

high.situation: We need to brainstorm design ideas for our RGM machine.
high.task_action: We first try to decide what our theme should be. To help our enviorment health, we decided to use recyclble material for our machine. The purpose of the RGM machine is to help engineers to get the vitamins that they need for creative and critical thinking, so we decided to set our end goal to have a borraca tablet drink to help out our engineers. After these two objective are solved we moved on to brainstorming simple systems for our machine. We came out with 12 parts with each group member responsible with two sections.
high.result: We took the systems that we brainstormed for our simple machines and connected to make bigger parts in our parts. For example we used a lot slopes on our machine to help us move the marble and berroca. The materials used are also listed under each person part so we could understand if we have repeated too much. However these systems are not put in to test so we will need to test them and make sure they work.

high.situation:
While the aforementioned skill can be applied everywhere it was best exemplified when planning, allocating and completing numerous tasks in our shortened week 6 workshop.

TASK: The organised agenda for the week required that we plan our graded presentation, complete our Work, Health, & Safety documents, write our group reflection, and finalise the detailed design aspects of our Rube Goldberg Machine. While normally we would have three hours to complete the workshop, our allocated time was reduced to two due to longer weekly presentations as well as the morning address from the tutors. Furthermore, we were also a group member down due to illness. 

ACTION: As organised at the beginning of this semester, our group participated in a rotating roster for the overarching roles needed to cohesively function on a week-week basis. This resulted in me taking the lead as Chairperson this week (Team Leader). Thus to complete the required workload I allocated tasks based upon members' strengths as identified by myself and then agreed upon by the group. For example, one member was better at ideation for presentation while another was better at thinking critically in regards to risks and their mitigation.
high.result: By effectively evaluating group members' strengths and weaknesses individually, and as a group, we were able to meet the deadline for the workload, and thus, stay on target for our project timeline. When all tasks were completed we also shared our ideas and findings with each other in an informal presentation format so that everyone was on the same page moving forward.

high.situation: In order to create an RGM machine with a team, on week-by-week basis, we are to progress the project in design steps and then begin building our own parts and finally compile a video.
high.task_action: Every week we go through a session, planning out our own RGM in stages, with these final weeks a result of the efforts we put out throughout the unit. As such we were focused on building our designs, aiming to have our RGM parts to have high reproducibility with quality. To be productive we discussed with others on our usage of certain materials, especially if they didn’t work, having to come up with another reasonable solution through open discussion. Furthermore, we set deadlines to have our individual parts ready for our editor to compose the video.
high.result: The result was being able to build our RGM events promptly by adapting to any unexpected problems such as materials not being sturdy enough or a whole part not working. Since we set deadlines, we were able to allow some tolerance for any delays caused by this. Hence, we effectively managed our time and resources with rational judgements.

high.situation: I had to create a two-part machine for our groups final Ruben Goldberg Machine.
high.task_action: My idea was to create a volcano explosion using simple chemical reactions of vinegar and bi-carb soda, using the run-off as a counterweight to trigger a pulley system connected to a fan, turning it on. The fan would cause a marble to roll across a desk and down a ramp.
high.result: I was successfully able to create and film the machine from start to finish. It was then ready to be merged with the other components of the teams Ruben Goldberg machine.

high.situation:
Mid semester break return 

Task:

Returning to full time university work loads and ensuring the team and myself are motivated and delivering results. This came with both personal and team problems as we all struggled a little with returning to work. We had to ensure all of the team held each other accountable to our deadline and meeting times. Multiple team members found it difficult to re-engage with the task and this was only countered by stringent performance managing these team members in order for them to regain motivation Personally I found it difficult to re-engage however I took the professional route to ensure I didn't let my team down and forced myself to work hard.
high.result: We managed to all come together to diligently work on the RGM and as of week 11 we have a fully functioning and 90% recorded RGM. The team also demonstrated brilliant resilience to come together after some deep divides developed due to the effort individual members where putting into the project.

high.situation: Our group had to find a way to implement our ideas to actual working events reliably.
high.task_action: For us to make our events a reality we organised to meet up at uni allocating each person to bring the needed materials and we worked on each event brainstorming ideas to make the machine more efficient and reliable.
high.result: because of getting together we didn’t have to buy any materials as at least one member had an item we needed. Another benefit was together we solved our reliability problems much faster and by the end of the day managed to improve our machine significantly.

high.situation: During the mid-semester break our group, individually, gathered all the necessary household materials and equipment to construct their respective modularized events of the Rube Goldberg Machine (RGM). Adjustments and testing were also conducted to assess the level of reliability of the individual sections and hence, the overall system reliability. A video of the successful operation of the RGM was recorded.
high.task_action: Using the preliminary design concepts formulated in the previous group meetings, we each identified the relevant materials required for each of our two events. Next, we gathered all the necessary materials and tools to build RGM sections. Also, as one of the constraints is sustainability aspect, this meant that if we did not have the materials or equipment at home, we would look for alternatives by substituting it with a household item. Evidently, parts of the preliminary design were slightly changed to adequately meet the sustainability constraint. Once each section was constructed, each member would perform 20 test runs and record the success rate. Next, the source of errors was identified for each segment, and suggested improvements were amended into the system. Then, another 20 test runs were performed, and the improved success rate was documented. Additionally, each member recorded the successful operation of their sections of the RGM.
high.result: By the end of the mid-semester break we had 5 videos from each member consisting of two events from the RGM. Also, we presented the initial success rate and section reliability, which was used to determine the overall system reliability. We each presented the sources of error, the suggested improvement strategies and the resulting success rate and section reliability of each person to the rest of the group. As a resulted, the improved reliability of the entire machine was determined.  Further, the 5 separate videos were merged into one to present a seamless operating RGM.

high.situation:
We needed to complete our final RGM video by the end of Week 12.

TASK / ACTION: In one of our pre-workshop meetings, we determined that everyone had a load of assignments due in weeks 12 and 13. Thus, we had to set a goal due date that is at least a week earlier than the official due date for the final RGM video. By discussion, the goal due date to finish the final video was decided to be by the end of Week 10. As a part of that goal, we also had to take into account that the video editor needs at least 2 days to edit the videos. Thus, we broke down the goal due date, to two goal due dates. The first goal due date was to record our individual RGM components and have them uploaded into our SharePoint by Friday on week 10. The second goal due date was for the video editor to finish combining and editing all of our videos to produce the final RGM video by the end of week 10.
high.result: By choosing appropriate goal due dates, we were able to successfully finish our final RGM video by the end of Week 10. Which gave us more time to work on other units' assignments. This also gave us an extended period of time to work on our week 13 presentation.

high.situation: We needed to ensure components of the project were completed before the weekly zoom meeting, allowing the best presentation in each meeting to be achieved.
high.task_action: A Gantt chart was made and was accessible to all team members to effectively portray the timing and deadlines of tasks that needed to be completed. However, the Gantt chart was lacking in sophistication and detail and only provided very brief descriptions of the tasks. Due to the lack of detail and minor communication between team members, the weekly meeting would arrive and our team would be ill-equipped to provide an extensive and in-depth presentation that was due that day. To resolve this issue, more frequent check-ins with teammates on discord were had, and extra detail was added to the Gantt chart and the Miro board to ensure every teammate knew exactly what needed to be done by what deadline.
high.result: The result was being able to effectively manage tasks and time without needing to rush, and have full control of the task to complete it effectively by the deadline.

high.situation: for the future I need to have developed a decently advanced level of critical thinking to be able to solve/adapt to situations to solve problems.

high.situation: I needed a modular solution to scissors-paper-rock that involved changing element variations, element counts, player types and supporting new types in the future.
high.task_action: After doing some thinking. This should be done in a way that allows for a one program fits all solution. That is, I would be able to compile each element variation, count and player types with one file without having to handle multiple different files. The reason for this is that multiple files are ultimately harder to handle. A bug was found within the common shared program. Then each file will need to be updated and changed to fix the bug according. After brainstorming possible solutions. The easiest to implement and most responsive were using preprocessing macros to selectively choose which program to compile at run time.
high.result: This, along with mathematical concepts and pointers were combined to make a highly modular scissors-paper-rock program that is capable of supporting element variations, element counts, player types and easily scalable to new types in the future. This required background knowledge and further research on my part however.

high.situation: AS I'M STARTING MY ENGINEERING DEGREE, I WANTED TO STAY ON TRACK AND ABSORB ALL THE INFORMATION THAT WAS GIVEN TO ME. THUS TEACHING ME THE IMPORTANCE OF STAYING ON TRACK WHILE STUDYING.

high.situation:
Beginning of this unit, having a group assigned and being told we will be conducting an online class every week. During this time, we will not only present our previous week’s work but plan and create a new presentation for the following week. My attitude and thought process after hearing this was negative. This was because the previous Engineering core unit was in person and a hands-on workshop, where our marks were reliant on ourselves of what we learnt. Whereas this unit is relying on others to contribute for better marks.

 

Task:

During one of the earlier weeks of the unit we were tasked to brainstorm ideas for a Rube Goldberg Machine. During the main group meeting there was a clear ‘leader’ in our group, who tried to allocate roles/ plan of action. I was more than happy to listen and contribute, but it became clear the other group members were not so keen. This was even more so prominent when it came to our team meeting later in the week, where we were to complete and submit an agenda and minutes of the meeting. After this, my thoughts and attitudes towards this unit were still negative.

 

Action:

Within the meetings I tried my best to communicate positively with my group members to get better collaboration. This did work to get some people talking and becoming comfortable with the group. I carried this through to our agenda, minutes, and weekly reflection, where I would quickly jot down any information I thought to be relevant, then ask group members if they can check my grammar or if they want to add anything else. Overall, I tried to bring across a positive mindset and attitude to my group members even though I personally had a negative attitude towards the unit.
high.result: Me bringing this ‘positivity’ to the group even though my actual attitude to the tasks were not positive, created a place where my other group members felt comfortable to contribute. Which then in return made my attitude towards the unit positive as now our whole group was working together and getting things done efficiently. This comes back to the final word in the topic, ‘learning’. For which, I learnt that even though I may have a negative attitude towards something, by spreading positivity despite what I feel, will in return make not only everyone else positive but yourself.

high.situation: I needed to ensure the reliability of the actions in my mechanisms for the Rube Golberg Machine to ensure that the designs of the components are of appropriate quality allowing for correct and consistent function allowing for easier recording.
high.task_action: I implemented an agile development approach to the designs of components I was to produce in order to allow for incremental improvement of the design hopefully ensuring higher reliability and consistency. Implementing this approach to design I created one initial prototype for each component then continually improved both by changing one factor/part at a time to increase reliability until a satisfactory level of reproducibility was achieved. This approach allowed for rapid improvements to the reliability of the components making the design process more efficient and effective.
high.result: The result was 2 completed and reliable components that could both reproduce the intended action sequence of the section 9/10 times as opposed to the original 2/10 times & 3/10 times of the initial designs.

high.situation: During the start of this semester , I realized that the workload has significantly increased due to major project deadlines and exams across the different units. I found myself juggling deadlines, complex assignments and studying for exam simultaneously. It was difficult to maintain focus and prioritize different tasks effectively.       Task : The major task is to maintain prioritization and focus on different works and units and to reclaim control and achieve higher academic goals.       Action : I identified various approached which could help me for the overwhelming situation.        	*   Prioritization Matrix : I created a prioritization matrix which related to two major components of tasks which are Urgency and importance criteria. The tasks which were urgent and important were placed under high priority work whereas less urgent but important tasks were scheduled strategically. The task urgency and importance were changed according to the deadlines.       	*   Detailed Schedule : A detailed weekly and monthly schedule is made which allows me to include lectures, project work and breaks. This helped me with time management and also helped me to identify the potential gaps where additional work can be done.       	*   Focused Techniques: I experimented with different focus techniques like Pomodoro technique (working in focused 30 min interval with short breaks) and meditation to help me concentrate with my work.
high.result: The above mentioned strategies were consistently used to achieve better results and less stressful environment. The prioritization matrix and schedule dramatically increased my time management efficiency. Utilizing focus techniques improved my concentration during the study sessions, it also helped me with understanding the contents in each unit. Ultimately, by prioritizing and focusing effectively, I was able to meet all deadlines efficiently and deliver high-quality work across all the units. I further pledge to consistently put efforts to achieve great results by the end of the semester.

high.situation: I encountered a significant pain point related to the inconsistency in task display on the learning platform used for course management. This issue was negatively impacting both instructors and students, leading to confusion, missed deadlines, and decreased efficiency in academic activities.
high.task_action: To address this pain point, I took the initiative to devise potential solutions independently. Recognising the importance of offering suggestions to improve the learning platform, my task was to brainstorm and propose measures to enhance the consistency of task display.      Firstly, I conducted research to understand the root causes of the problem and identify potential solutions. I reviewed literature on user experience design, online learning platforms, and best practices in course management systems to inform my approach.      Based on my research, I generated a list of potential solutions to improve task display consistency on the learning platform. These included:      1. Developing standardised templates for course instructors to use when creating tasks, ensuring uniformity in presentation across all courses.      2. Implementing automated checks and validations to identify and rectify inconsistencies in task display, such as missing deadlines or incomplete instructions.      3. Introducing a feedback mechanism for students to report issues with task display, enabling quick resolution by course administrators.      4. Providing training sessions and resources for faculty members on effective course design practices and utilising the learning platform's features to ensure consistent task display.      I organised my suggestions in a structured manner, outlining the benefits and feasibility of each proposed solution. I also considered potential challenges and limitations, as well as the resources and support required for implementation.
high.result: By independently suggesting potential solutions to improve task display consistency on the learning platform, I contributed to raising awareness of the issue and initiating discussions on possible remedies. While the solutions were not implemented, my suggestions could serve as a starting point for further exploration and action by relevant stakeholders. Additionally, my proactive approach demonstrated initiative and problem-solving skills, highlighting my commitment to enhancing the learning experience for both instructors and students.

high.situation: In my previous course, ENGG2000/3000, students were allocated to a specific group within a team depending on their specialization (three groups: Structures, Motions, and Communications). As a mechanical student, I was placed in the ‘Structures’ group with four other civil/mechanical students. In my structures group, there were two students in their third year, and I was in my second year. The roles and responsibilities of each group were unique and specific to their discipline. The ‘Structures’ group was responsible for cube structure design, selection of material, and finally, manufacturing and assembly processes. The ‘Communications’ group was responsible for the software elements such as programming and debugging. The ‘Motions’ group was responsible for the motor plus sensor selection and electrical connections (wiring). Progress videos and pictures of the marble cube were compiled in a final video presentation.
high.task_action: As the structures group we brainstormed, and the finalized design concepts formulated for the structural element of the marble cube. Further, our group identified the relevant materials required for each relevant subsystem. Next, we gathered all the necessary materials and tools to build each section. Also, as one of the requirements is materials from certain suppliers only, this meant that if we did not have the materials or equipment available at these suppliers, we would look for alternatives by substituting them with something similar. Parts of the finalized design were slightly changed to adequately meet the supplier's requirements. Once each section was constructed, the group would perform 20 test runs and record the success rate. Next, the source of errors was identified for each subsystem and suggested improvements were amended to the system. Improvements or adjustments were made with the assistance of the other two groups in the team. Then, another 20 test runs were performed, and the improved success rate was documented. Additionally, a collection of videos and pictures were taken to document progress.      Simultaneously, both other groups (communications and motions) were in frequent communication with our group, using our feedback, to make their selection of motors or sensors and their relevant programming. Whenever any changes big or small need to be made to the speed of the motors or the rate of the sensors, one member from the other group would make the necessary adjustments. Also, any other issue such as an increase or decrease in power output or dimension of the motor or sensor would be amended with the help of the other groups in the team. This streamlined the process as roadblocks or delays were attended to promptly.
high.result: By the end of the semester, we had developed a fully functioning marvellous marble machine that performed all the requirements outlined by our client. Also, the initial success rate and section reliability were improved and adjusted as necessary with the assistance of the other two groups. Immediate communication with the other groups when any issue presented itself allowed for efficient reliability improvements of the marble cube. Further, the progress videos and pictures were edited into the video presentation.

The challenge of balancing a full-time job with rigorous studies was significant, pushing me to skillfully manage conflicting demands and effectively organize my time.
