high.situation: From my work experience I have gain insight into the importance of communcation in effectively managing projects. During the construction of an airport terminal building, unexpected soil conditions were encounterded at the excavation site, posing a risk to the stability of the foundation.
high.task_action: As a cadet engineer on the project, my task was to help address the issue of unstable soil conditions and aid in the development of a solution that would ensure the structural integrity of the building. I coordinated a meeting with representatives from the structural design team, geotechnical engineers, and project management to discuss the situation.       During the meeting, we analyzed the soil reports and conducted a thorough assessment of the potential impacts on the foundation design. We brainstormed various solutions, including implementing soil stabilization techniques, adjusting the foundation design, or relocating critical structural elements.       After a comprehensive evaluation, we decided to proceed with a combination of soil stabilization methods and modifications to the foundation design. This involved injecting grout into the unstable soil to improve its load-bearing capacity and incorporating additional reinforcement into the foundation structure.
high.result: The implementation of the selected solution successfully mitigated the risk posed by the unstable soil conditions, ensuring the structural stability of the building. Despite the unforeseen challenge, the project remained on schedule, and there were no compromises on quality or safety standards. The collaborative approach taken to address the issue fostered teamwork and communication among the project team, ultimately leading to a successful resolution and the continued progress of the construction project.

high.situation: In my software engineering course (COMP4060), my team and I were tasked with creating and analysing a real-life scenario that highlighted ethical challenges in software development, particularly those involving user privacy and data protection. Our hypothetical situation involves a corporation that created an application that unintentionally acquired more user data than disclosed, including sensitive personal information, without the users' explicit agreement. This circumstance reflected real-world difficulties, highlighting the intricacies of ethical decision-making in the technology business.      TASK:      Our goal was to provide a solution that addressed the ethical, technical, and social responsibilities involved in the case. We wanted not only to address the existing situation but also to prevent future instances from happening. The solution needed adherence to ethical standards, regulatory compliance, and data protection best practices.      ACTION:      We began our strategy by performing an extensive study on data privacy legislation, ethical software development principles, and industry standards for user data security alongside case studies related to the topic of utilising/trading customers’ data in illegal ways. Some of those included the recent allegations of OpenAI using illegally collected data to train their AI and Facebook’s history of illegally collecting data without its user’s consent. Based on our results, we started a brainstorming session for 20 minutes aimed at building a multidimensional solution to the problem. Our proposed solution comprised technical measures to protect user data that range from better sourcing analytics and providing ethical training to the development team, to a transparent communication approach to regain consumer trust such as publicising how and what data is collected and used and making public statements regularly regarding the company’s customer security and data privacy, and an internal audit of data-gathering processes. In addition, we proposed that employees receive regular ethical training in order to establish a culture of responsibility and honesty.
high.result: The initiative resulted in an extensive strategy that not only addressed the issue of unethical data collection but also laid the groundwork for long-term ethical procedures within the organisation. While our method was praised for its thoroughness and practicality, the process caused me to think hard about my ideals and obligations as a prospective software developer. I understood the vital need to incorporate ethical considerations into all stages of software development, from design to deployment. It strengthened my opinion that transparency, user permission, and personal data protection are non-negotiable values in technology. Furthermore, the experiment demonstrated the importance of ongoing ethical education and a strong ethical culture in preventing wrongdoing. This realisation has reaffirmed my dedication to prioritising ethical standards in my professional trajectory over profits or other means, ensuring that my activities benefit the tech industry and society as a whole.

high.situation: When I worked for Sydney Water as an undergraduate mechanical engineering student, I came across a situation where the company was having trouble making the most of its water treatment operations in order to satisfy growing demand while maintaining sustainability and effectiveness. The increasing demands of the population and environmental concerns put pressure on the water treatment plant to increase its capacity without sacrificing quality requirements.      TASK:      My task was to analyze the existing water treatment processes, identify areas for improvement, and propose feasible solutions to address the evolving needs. This involved understanding the intricate details of the plant's operations, assessing resource utilization, and evaluating the effectiveness of current technologies in meeting demand.      ACTION:      To address this task, I used a methodical manner. First, I thoroughly investigated industry best practices and cutting edge water treatment technologies. After that, I worked closely with the engineers and operators of the plant to have an understanding of the operational difficulties and limitations they encountered on a daily basis. I pinpointed important areas where improvements might be made to optimise resource utilisation and expedite procedures through active communication and data analysis.      Utilizing my engineering knowledge and problem-solving skills, I proposed several innovative solutions tailored to the specific needs of the plant. These included implementing advanced filtration systems, optimizing chemical dosing procedures, and integrating automation technologies to enhance operational efficiency and minimize environmental impact.
high.result: The implementation of these solutions yielded significant positive results. There was a noticeable increase in the plant's overall efficiency, with reduced energy consumption and waste generation. The improved water treatment processes enabled Sydney Water to meet the escalating demand for clean water while maintaining compliance with regulatory standards. Additionally, the enhanced sustainability practices garnered positive feedback from stakeholders and contributed to the company's reputation as a responsible environmental steward. Overall, the successful outcome of this project demonstrated my ability to understand organizational needs, set strategic goals, and take proactive action to achieve tangible results.

The marble had to navigate a series of obstacles and mechanisms within the cube's internal space before exiting. This project emphasized teamwork, creative problem-solving, and adhering to design constraints.      TASK:      As the group leader, my primary task was to guide the design and construction process while ensuring collaboration among my team members. This involved:      	* FACILITATING BRAINSTORMING: I led brainstorming sessions to generate ideas for the marble's path, obstacles, and mechanisms. I encouraged participation from all group members and ensured all ideas were considered.   	* DEFINING ROLES & RESPONSIBILITIES: Based on individual skillsets and interests, I delegated tasks for designing specific components, acquiring materials, and assembling the final structure.   	* PROMOTING COMMUNICATION: I scheduled regular progress meetings to discuss challenges, evaluate design choices, and ensure everyone was on the same page.   	* CONFLICT RESOLUTION: When disagreements arose regarding design elements or construction methods, I facilitated discussions to find solutions that met everyone's needs and the project's overall goals.
high.result: * FUNCTIONAL MARBLE MACHINE: Through collaborative effort, we successfully designed and built a functioning marble machine cube that met all project requirements. The marble traveled through the planned route within the cube, overcoming obstacles and exiting from the designated point.   	* IMPROVED TEAM DYNAMICS: The project fostered a sense of teamwork within the group. We learned to communicate effectively, share responsibilities, and resolve conflicts constructively.   	* ENHANCED LEADERSHIP SKILLS: By leading the brainstorming, delegating tasks, and facilitating communication, I developed leadership skills that proved valuable for future team endeavors.      This experience in ENG2050 provided valuable insights into the importance of collaboration and clear communication in leading successful engineering projects.

high.situation: During week two, in a different unit, my group was tasked with conceptualizing a design for a precast concrete bridge.
high.task_action: Each member had one week to brainstorm and come up with a design for the bridge, the following week in class everyone would take turns presenting their idea; brief description, and the advantages and disadvantages. After listening to each option, the group would provide their opinion on the best option.
high.result: This resulted in the group choosing a conceptual design that can now advance to the detailed stage. Tasks can be assigned to members and a project schedule can be created with the relevant deadlines.      PERSONAL FEELING/REFLECTION: The first part was simple in theory; conduct research and provide a design that can be viable for the project. However, due to being stuck with members who do not share the same work ethic or discipline, this task did not get completed. Initially, I was frustrated and angry with the group’s lack of work, but I wasn’t surprised since this is a reoccurring behavior from them. Since I was the only one who researched and came up with a design, we proceeded with my design, and I have been leading this endeavor.      LEARNING: When I am in a group with people who lack the motivation and vision to conduct the project, I must take charge as a leader and provide them with this needed direction. This will in turn help me feel happier and excited about the project as opposed to anger, irritation, and dread. Leadership has never been my strong suit, but it is a necessary skill to learn if I am to thrive in the engineering industry.

high.situation: Developing a new marketing campaign aimed at a varied population was the team's duty in my prior employment. It was clear early on in the project that there was a lack of diversity in our team, which could have limited our comprehension of and capacity to interact with the target audience. This circumstance made us question the campaign's content's inclusion and cultural sensitivity.      Task: Seeing the divide, I suggested that our team broaden its vision by embracing a greater range of cultural experiences and thoughts. Making sure our marketing approach avoided stereotyping and cultural insensitivity while connecting successfully with our broad audience was the goal.      In response, I spearheaded the establishment of a varied advisory council inside the organisation, which included staff members from several divisions who contributed a range of cultural backgrounds and work experiences. I set up workshops so the advisory panel could provide input on the ideas, message, and graphic assets for the campaign. I promoted candid conversations and made sure that every criticism was taken into account and included into the campaign creation process.
high.result: Our campaign's strategy has significantly improved as a result of the advisory group's engagement. Before the campaign started, we were able to detect and address a number of possible problems with inclusion and cultural stereotypes. In comparison to earlier initiatives, the final campaign's sensitivity and inclusion drew good comments, which raised engagement rates within the targeted groups by 20%. Regular diversity seminars and training sessions were started as a result of this approach, which also promoted a stronger feeling of community and knowledge of diversity and inclusion concerns within our team and the larger organisation.

high.situation: In my engineering class, we were tasked with designing a marble machine project that not only demonstrated mechanical principles but also adhered to ethical guidelines and social responsibility standards. The challenge was to incorporate sustainable materials and ensure that the design process and final product reflected our commitment to environmental conservation.      TASK: As the project lead, my task was to guide the team in conceptualizing and implementing a design that met our academic requirements while also prioritizing sustainability and ethical considerations. This involved selecting environmentally friendly materials, minimizing waste, and designing a machine that could be easily recycled or repurposed at the end of its lifecycle.      ACTION: To achieve our goals, I initiated a series of team meetings to discuss our approach to sustainability and ethics in our project. We conducted research on sustainable materials and decided to use recycled metals and biodegradable plastics. Throughout the design process, I emphasized the importance of transparency, encouraging team members to openly discuss any challenges or ethical dilemmas they encountered. We also engaged with our professors and industry professionals to seek advice on how to enhance the social responsibility aspect of our project. By maintaining open lines of communication and fostering a collaborative environment, we were able to navigate the complexities of integrating ethics and sustainability into our engineering project.
high.result: The outcome of our efforts was a marble machine that not only met the project's educational objectives but also exemplified our commitment to ethical engineering practices and social responsibility. Our project was recognized by our professors for its innovative use of sustainable materials and its thoughtful approach to environmental impact. Furthermore, we were invited to present our project at a university showcase event, where we highlighted the importance of ethics and sustainability in engineering. This experience not only reinforced my understanding of the crucial role that ethics and social responsibility play in engineering but also demonstrated the positive impact that thoughtful consideration of these aspects can have on a project's success.

high.situation: During a group project, our team was tasked with developing a new software application intended to optimize energy consumption in commercial buildings. The software's algorithm was complex, and we had a tight deadline. Midway through the project, we discovered a way to significantly cut corners in the testing phase that would save time but could compromise the reliability of our results. This situation presented a dilemma between maintaining integrity in our work and meeting our project deadline.      TASK: As the team leader, my task was to guide the team in making a decision that aligned with our ethical standards, without compromising the project's success. The integrity of our work and the potential impact of our software on energy savings were at stake.      ACTION: I initiated an emergency team meeting to discuss the discovered shortcut and its potential implications. I emphasized the importance of ethics, integrity, and social responsibility in engineering work, especially when creating solutions with real-world applications. I proposed an alternative plan to reorganize our workflow, allowing us to adhere to the testing phase's integrity without cutting corners. This involved redistributing tasks, extending work hours, and seeking feedback from our professors to optimize our approach without sacrificing quality.
high.result: The team unanimously agreed to the alternative plan. The reorganized workflow and extended hours were challenging, but it enabled us to maintain the integrity of our testing phase. Our final presentation highlighted not only the technical achievements of our software but also our commitment to ethical standards in engineering. The project received commendation from our professors for both its innovation and ethical approach. Our software demonstrated promising results in simulations, attracting interest from a local startup looking to implement sustainable technologies. This experience reinforced the value of ethics, integrity, and social responsibility in professional practice, shaping my approach to engineering challenges.

high.situation: At university I had an in-class group project that required us to effectively communicate to each other and collaborate among team members for a semester-long assignment.
high.task_action: Recognizing the importance of clear communication and efficient coordination within our group, we embarked on defining a communication structure that would streamline information flow and enhance collaboration. Firstly, we held a group meeting to establish a shared understanding of the project objectives and the importance of effective communication. We articulated the task of developing a communication structure to ensure everyone understood their roles, responsibilities, and expectations.      Next, we conducted a needs assessment to identify the communication requirements of our group. This involved gathering feedback from each team member regarding their preferred communication methods, frequency of updates, and preferred channels for sharing information. We also considered the nature of the project tasks, deadlines, and potential challenges that could arise during the course of our collaboration.
high.result: The implementation of our communication structure resulted in improved coordination, clarity, and efficiency within our group. By establishing clear channels and protocols for communication, we reduced the likelihood of misunderstandings and delays in task completion. Team members reported feeling more informed and engaged, leading to increased productivity and collaboration.      Overall, the successful implementation of our communication structure facilitated seamless collaboration and contributed to the overall success of our group project. It also fostered a positive team dynamic and enhanced our communication skills, which are valuable assets for future collaborative endeavours.

mid.situation: In the ELEC3024 Final Project, my team and I were tasked with designing and testing a DC motor system for an elevator, focusing on creating a closed-loop speed control system using MATLAB, Simulink, and an Arduino Uno. Early in the project, we encountered significant technical challenges, including issues with the motor's response to control signals and differing opinions on troubleshooting approaches.      TASK: My role, amongst other things, was to maintain a positive attitude and foster an open, collaborative environment to ensure we could overcome these technical challenges and successfully complete the project. This required effective communication, a willingness to consider diverse perspectives, and a commitment to continuous learning.
mid.task_action: I initiated regular team meetings to discuss our progress, challenges, and potential solutions. In our meetings, team members were encouraged to share their thoughts and concerns openly. I tried to actively listen so that my colleagues opinions were heard and respected. Some disagreements arose but were dealt with by finding common ground and integrating the best ideas from each perspective.      To enhance our collective understanding, I suggested that each team member tackle a specific problem relating to our project. For example, one member focused on PID controller tuning techniques, while another investigated hardware compatibility issues. We then reconvened to share our findings and apply the new knowledge to our project.      I also adopted a growth mindset by trying to view each challenge as a learning opportunity and tried to convince my team that every obstacle we faced was a chance to develop our skills and knowledge further.
mid.result: Our collaborative and open approach led to a significant improvement in the motor control system. By integrating diverse perspectives and applying new knowledge, we successfully tuned the PID controller and resolved the hardware issues. Our final project demonstration showcased a fully functional elevator system with precise speed control. The project received positive feedback from our tutor, reflecting our collective learning and adaptability.

mid.situation: Our team's assignment at XYZ Corporation was to create a new software feature that would improve our primary product's user experience. Due to recent internal changes and a new team makeup, there were doubts over the team's capacity to deliver the project by the deadline.
mid.task_action: In order to fulfill the project deadline, it was my duty as the team leader to make sure everyone remained focused, used their time wisely, and worked well together. To accomplish our goal, I had to facilitate effective communication, bring the team together, and make the most of everyone's skills.      For action, I took the following steps to address the situation:      1) KICKOFF MEETING: I called a preliminary meeting to lay out the goals of the project, the schedule, and the roles of each participant. This made sure that everyone was aware of the main objective and their individual roles.      2) ESTABLISHING COMMUNICATION CHANNELS: I established frequent check-ins and set up a shared workspace using Slack and Asana, two project management apps, to promote productive cooperation. This made it possible to promptly resolve any problems that emerged and kept everyone informed about progress.      3) DELEGATING TASKS: I deliberately assigned assignments to team members based on their areas of experience and strengths. I matched people with varying levels of experience to facilitate mentorship and guarantee high-quality work.      4) MOTIVATING AND SUPPORTING THE TEAM: To maintain a positive attitude, I made it a point to acknowledge and celebrate tiny victories. In addition, I offered assistance by being accessible to address concerns, settle disputes, and supply the tools required to do the job.       5) MONITORING PROGRESS: I kept a close eye on the project's advancement in relation to the schedule, conducting weekly review meetings to evaluate our progress and make any necessary plan adjustments to stay on course.      RESULT      1) TIMELY COMPLETION: We successfully finished the project two days before the scheduled completion date, giving us more time for quality control and testing.      2) IMPROVE TIME COHESION: The group experienced an increase in cohesion and cooperation, as well as better communication and a stronger sense of mission.      3) HIGH-QUALITY OUTPUT: Both internal stakeholders and end users gave the software feature positive comments when it was installed flawlessly.      4) ENHANCED LEADERSHIP SKILL: My leadership abilities were greatly improved by this experience, especially in terms of controlling team dynamics, assigning work efficiently, and creating a supportive and productive work atmosphere.

mid.situation: As part of my engineering program, I was elected to lead a team of students in a semester-long capstone project. Our task was to design and build a prototype of a motor control system for an electric vehicle. Midway through the project, we faced significant challenges with our initial design, which resulted in unstable motor operation and erratic speed control. The team was frustrated, and morale was low due to the setbacks.
mid.task_action: Recognizing the urgency, I took immediate steps to address the issues. First, I convened an emergency meeting to reassess our design and pinpoint the root causes of the instability. During the meeting, I encouraged open communication and brainstorming, ensuring every team member's input was valued. We identified key areas needing redesign, particularly the motor driver circuit and the control algorithm.      Next, I divided the team into smaller groups, assigning specific tasks based on individual strengths. One group focused on troubleshooting and redesigning the motor driver circuit, while another worked on refining the control algorithm for more stable operation. I also reached out to a faculty advisor for expert advice and scheduled additional lab sessions to give us more time to work on the project.      Throughout this period, I maintained a clear timeline with regular progress check-ins to keep the team on track. I also made sure to celebrate small victories along the way to boost morale and keep everyone motivated.
mid.result: By effectively reorganizing our efforts and leveraging team strengths, we were able to overcome the initial design flaws. The redesigned motor driver circuit provided stable and reliable operation, and our improved control algorithm ensured smooth and precise speed control. We successfully completed the motor control system prototype on schedule and presented it at the end-of-semester showcase. The project was well-received by faculty and peers, earning praise for its innovative approach and functionality. This experience not only enhanced our technical skills but also strengthened our ability to work collaboratively under pressure.

mid.situation: In my previous role as a project leader, we were working on a small project with a tight deadline. The team consisted of student members from different engineering disciplines. This allowed for the communication of issues, where each member could find their own way of solving the problem, to meet project requirements. We also had a number of deadlines for reports which required cross-checking between members.
mid.task_action: We recognised that there was a need to improve our communication processes in ensuring deadlines were met. I took the initiative to address the problem. My task was to communicate between members and to gather results and designs from each member during the meeting, and propose one streamlined design which would ensure everyone was on the same page. I started by organising a meeting with all members to discuss the importance of communication and gathering feedback from amongst each other. Some of the strategies that I implemented included:      	*   A CENTRAL COMMUNICATION TOOL: I researched and selected Slack as our centralized communication platform, which allowed for real-time messaging, file sharing, and integrations with other project management tools we were using.      	*   SET CLEAR COMMUNICATION PROTOCOLS: I developed and shared a set of communication protocols, including guidelines on how and when to use different channels (e.g., Slack for daily updates, discord for regular communication, and video calls for urgent discussions).      	*   REGULAR CHECK-INS AND UPDATES: I initiated a weekly meeting to ensure everyone was aligned on their tasks and to address any immediate concerns. I also scheduled status updates within tasks so all members could be aware of where the project was, and where it still needed work.
mid.result: The result of these actions was a significant improvement in team communication and project efficiency. This came in the form of enhanced clarity and collaboration within the team, where tools such as Discord and Slack aided our team in communicating clearly, which also led to the reduction of misunderstandings. This also allowed for an increase in information sharing amongst all the members. Team members could easily refer back to discussions and documents, which reduced duplication of efforts. Regular check-ins and updates ensured that everyone was aware of their responsibilities and deadlines, leading to a higher level of accountability and on-time task completion. With the improved communication processes, we were able to complete the project on time. This experience gave us a real understanding of how to manage deadlines in the future.       Overall, by addressing the communication challenges head-on and implementing effective solutions, we were able to enhance our team's productivity and achieve our project goals successfully.

mid.situation: During a recent transportation engineering design project, our team encountered a pivotal decision point regarding the pathing of a new road. Two distinct design options emerged, each following a different route and necessitating unique construction processes tailored to the respective topographies.      TASK: The task at hand was to critically evaluate the merits and drawbacks of each proposed path, considering factors such as construction feasibility, environmental impact, cost-effectiveness, and long-term sustainability. Ultimately, we needed to reach a consensus on the optimal solution that would best serve the client’s needs while minimising potential negative consequences.      ACTION: To address this challenge, I facilitated an in-depth discussion among the team members, encouraging open dialogue and active listening. We meticulously analysed the technical specifications, environmental assessments, and cost projections for both options. Additionally, we sought input from relevant pre-existing roads, transport engineering tutors, and other teams undergoing the same task. Throughout this process, I strived to maintain an impartial stance, acknowledging the validity of both perspectives while objectively weighing the trade-offs associated with each choice. I encouraged team members to articulate their concerns and rationales, fostering an environment of mutual respect and collaboration.
mid.result: After extensive deliberation and careful consideration of all factors, our team reached a balanced resolution. We opted for a hybrid approach that incorporated elements from both design proposals. This solution optimised the route's efficiency while minimising environmental disruption and adhering to budgetary constraints. The chosen path struck a harmonious balance between practical considerations and environmental stewardship, leveraging the strengths of each initial proposal while mitigating potential drawbacks. By embracing a collaborative and open-minded approach, we arrived at a solution that garnered broad support from all team members. Through this experience, I learned the importance of effective communication, active listening, and the ability to navigate complex trade-offs. Embracing diverse perspectives and fostering an environment of mutual respect were crucial in reaching a well-rounded and sustainable resolution. This project reinforced the value of sound judgment, balanced decision-making, and the pursuit of solutions that harmonise multiple priorities for the greater good of the client.

mid.situation: For our group presentation this semester in ENGG3050, we had to decide who was going to present which topic about our start-up company.
mid.task_action: We sat down as a group and began brainstorming. the discussion was going nowhere so i took the initiative and led this discussion. i asked each individual in the group on what topic they would be most comfortable with. a few of the group members were hesitant but they eventually decided what topics they wanted to do. a few of the members had selected the same topic so i flipped a coin for them to make it fair on who does that specific topic. after everyone was assigned a topic i chose the last remaining topic.
mid.result: The result was that we were able to assign each member in our group a topic to present that they were most comfortable with. i designated myself as team leader and helped assign the roles of each member.

mid.situation: During the recent ENGG3050 in-class presentation assignment, my group was tasked with researching a startup. I chose to focus on the funding process and general business case of our selected startup, Tesla. This project required us to analyse various aspects of Tesla's funding journey and business strategy and present our findings in a concise and engaging manner.      Task:      My specific task was to identify and articulate the various stages of Tesla's funding process and the key business strategies that contributed to its success. This involved detailed research into Tesla's funding rounds, understanding the company's sales pitches, and identifying the key value propositions (KVPs) that secured their financing. The goal was to present this information clearly and compellingly to our classmates.      Action:      To tackle this task, I employed creative thinking to identify unique angles and insights that would make our presentation stand out. First, I conducted thorough research, gathering data from various sources such as financial reports and industry analyses. I then brainstormed innovative ways to present this data, using visual aids like infographics and timelines to make complex information more accessible. Additionally, I collaborated with my group members to ensure that our presentation had a cohesive narrative and flow.
mid.result: The outcome of our efforts was a highly engaging and informative presentation. Our creative approach in presenting Tesla's funding journey and business case was well-received by both our peers and the instructor. ( In my opinion) We received positive feedback for our use of visual aids and the clarity of our analysis. This not only enhanced our presentation but also demonstrated the effectiveness of creative thinking in solving complex problems and communicating ideas effectively.

mid.situation: In a recent group project for my Distributed Systems class, we were tasked with designing and implementing a client-side simulator for job scheduling. Our team had conflicting ideas on which scheduling algorithm to prioritize: the First Capable (FC) scheduler or a custom scheduler that we believed could outperform FC.      Task: The objective was to reach a consensus on the scheduling algorithm to implement, ensuring that our solution would not only meet the project requirements but also be feasible within our tight deadline. It was crucial to resolve this conflict quickly to start the implementation phase without delays.      Action: I initiated a negotiation and compromise session with my team. First, I clearly articulated the problem: we needed to choose a scheduling algorithm that balanced performance and complexity. I then proposed a structured approach to resolve the conflict:      1. Presentation of Ideas: Each member presented their preferred algorithm, detailing the pros and cons based on parameters like efficiency, implementation complexity, and expected performance.      2. Evaluation Criteria: We collectively established evaluation criteria, focusing on feasibility, potential for performance improvement, and alignment with project requirements.      3. Compromise Proposal: To find common ground, I suggested a hybrid approach. We would implement the FC scheduler first to ensure we had a working solution. Simultaneously, we would design and test the custom scheduler, comparing its performance against FC.
mid.result: This approach led to a collaborative and productive workplace. By agreeing to first install the FC scheduler, we assured that we satisfied the project's baseline criteria. Parallel work on the custom scheduler enabled us to innovate and maybe outperform expectations. The end result was a dual implementation in which we effectively proved the efficacy of both schedulers. After extensive testing, the custom scheduler demonstrated increased performance, as mentioned in our final report, giving us top ratings for functionality and creativity.      Our ability to successfully negotiate, compromise, and overcome disputes led to successful project delivery, demonstrating our collaboration and problem-solving abilities.

mid.situation: During my final year at university, I was part of a group project aimed at designing a gearbox. Our group consisted of five members, and we had to present our design within a strict deadline. About two weeks before the presentation, one of our key members, who was responsible for the design using Creo Parametric 9.0, became unresponsive. This situation left us in a difficult position as the rest of us were not as proficient with the software, and we needed the completed design to finalize our presentation slides.      TASK: As the team leader, it was my responsibility to ensure that the project was completed on time and to manage the team's workflow. I needed to find a way to either get the design completed or adjust our presentation to still deliver a coherent and professional project.      ACTION: First, I reached out to the unresponsive team member multiple times through various communication channels to understand the reason for their absence and to encourage them to contribute. When I received no response, I called for an emergency meeting with the rest of the team to discuss our options. We divided the remaining tasks among ourselves and decided to seek help from other students who were proficient with Creo. I also approached our unit convenor to explain the situation and request an extension, which was unfortunately denied. Despite this setback, I coordinated with the team to work extra hours, taking shifts to complete the design. Additionally, I contacted a friend who was an expert in Creo to guide us through the complex parts of the design.
mid.result: Although we were under significant stress, we managed to complete the gearbox design just in time for the presentation. The final product was not as polished as we had initially planned, but it was functional and met the project requirements. Our presentation was well-received, and we were commended for our problem-solving skills and teamwork under pressure. This experience taught me valuable lessons in crisis management, delegation, and the importance of a strong support network.

mid.situation: During the past few weeks we have been deciding on the scale of complexity for this project and how we can effectively balance the workload together.
mid.task_action: Our team brainstormed together potential endings/ideas of different complexities to be included in our RGM. Instead, as a team, we decided to play towards our individual abilities/interests and have a competent video editor on the team transition them together. This was done as we had an international student and it was difficult to arrange a meeting time where we can bring all our machines together and film.
mid.result: As a result of our team discussions and individual skillsets we have outlined a great plan for our RGM together with schematics and how they'll link together.

mid.situation: We were required to design and record our RGM components.

As as a team from time to time would message one another to see how the progress is going and update on our progress in our communication platform to make sure everything is going well and have no situations occurring. But a problem occurred as a team member did not reply nor communicate with the entire team for weeks which caused an issue of our team dynamic and our schedule. mid.situation:
Produce a RGM project along with other classmates, sticking to the unit's demands and schedule. 

Task: Each team member has to create, analyze and record 4 steps of a RGM, while sticking to a specific theme.

Action: To optimize our time, instead of having a fixed hour and date to work on the project, each of us would work on their free time. It was getting hard to stick to the meetings because each of us had very different time schedules, therefore we could manage to work separately on the tasks we had to contribute. This allowed more flexibility when working for the unit.

Because we lost two group members, we also effectively reorganized the structure of our initial project. We all had to add at least one extra step, and we looked for simpler objects to work with.
mid.result:
Because we restructured the project, changing the selection of objects, the theme of our project was compromised. We removed the use of cooking ingredients, and although this minimized the budget, it made it difficult to portray the “cake baking” theme. However, we decided to stick to the initial theme and we are still going to present a decorated cake for the final steps, we only changed the previous steps.

We faced unexpected challenges and this obviously affected us, because we were all quite busy.

Learning: We were able to adapt to uncertain situations and didn’t let the stress of losing two members fully affect us. We communicated and discussed what was the simpler and more effective option to continue with, and we all did extra work to fill the gap of members we had.

The source of communication we initially chose, discord, was highly effective and we will consider using it on future projects.

mid.situation: TEAM MEETING OCCURRED TO FURTHER DISCUSS AND DEVELOP THE RUBE GOLDBERG MODEL DESIGN. WE FURTHER DEVELOPED MANY TEAM WORKING SKILLS. THE INDIVIDUAL SKILL I CONCENTRATED ON WAS COMMUNICATION.
mid.task_action:

mid.situation: As part of a recent group project as part of my engineering course, we were given the responsibility of creating a long-lasting water filtration system for a rural hamlet that was experiencing serious water contamination problems. The lack of access to potable water in the neighbourhood caused health issues for the locals. To guarantee the project's solution was practical, economical, and ecologically friendly, a great deal of study, creative thinking, and testing of several filtration techniques were needed. The pressure to provide a workable solution quickly increased due to the urgency of the issue.      TASK:   As a member of the group, it was my responsibility to evaluate the various filtering techniques and determine which one would work best for the environment, the cost, the ease of implementation, and the effectiveness of removing impurities. It was my duty to make sure the selected approach would be doable for the community to maintain and run with little outside assistance. It was difficult to strike a compromise that would appease the community, project funders, and our academic supervisors while weighing these considerations.      ACTION:      I utilised a lot of critical thinking techniques to complete the work. I began by compiling information on different filtration techniques, including membrane filtration, sand filtration, and activated carbon filtration. I studied case studies of related initiatives, spoke with experts, and thoroughly reviewed the literature. I weighed the benefits and drawbacks of each approach and compared the results with the requirements of our project. I built a comparison matrix that emphasised important elements like cost, effectiveness, maintenance needs, and environmental impact in order to guarantee a thorough examination.      After sharing my findings with my team, I led a series of discussions and encouraged them to challenge preconceived notions and pose questions. We were able to pinpoint possible blind spots and improve our understanding of the viability of each strategy thanks to this cooperative approach. To examine any potential obstacles and techniques for mitigating them, we also carried out a risk assessment for each alternative. Through this procedure, we were able to agree on the best filtration technique.
mid.result: The result of my critical analysis and the team's collaborative effort was the selection of a dual-stage filtration system combining sand and activated carbon filters. This solution was not only effective in removing a wide range of contaminants but also affordable and easy to maintain. Our project was well-received by our professor and peers, and the rural community implemented our design successfully, significantly improving their access to clean water. The positive feedback from the community and the tangible improvement in their health outcomes validated our decision. Additionally, the project earned us recognition at a university-level competition, highlighting the practical impact of our work.

mid.situation: During the course of ENGG2050, the cohort was tasked to design an IoT tea maker. This inherently came with a multitude of differing challenges that my team and I had worked through. A significant and recurring issue that would routinely arise was agreeing on particular aspects of the IoT appliance. One situation I could recall quite clearly was regarding the choice of materials for the water heating element. The challenge was to find materials that were efficient, safe, appealing and cost effective.      Task:      The specific task was to select the material that was most feasible for the IoT appliance to not only heat the water up in a timely manner, but also maintain the temperature for the intended time, whilst also ensuring the device was constructed in a way that's safe and durable.      Action:      In the SGTA that this task arose, we instantly took part in a brainstorming session, followed immediately by an in-depth discussion, in order to explore each other's ideas on views on various materials, such as: copper, stainless steel, glass, plastic and more. Each team member (3 including me) had individually researched potential materials with a large focal point on aspects such as cost, user-safety, thermal conductivity and whether or not it would’ve been feasible to implement for our current design. After we all researched, we came back together to share our findings and debate the merits of each option we had scavenged, and then utilised a systematic and structured format to ensure everyone had a clear and uninterrupted opportunity to convey their findings. After this we attempted to utilise a somewhat democratic-style approach with a voting aspect in the hope to come to an agreement with relative ease. However, we unfortunately could not come to an agreement - and decided to reschedule an online meeting over a social media platform ‘discord’, 3 days after our SGTA. This was planned in order to have a few days to think of alternative solutions, as well as weigh the pros and cons for each material we had previously discussed. 10 minutes into the scheduled online meeting, we were able to all be on the same page for which materials we thought would be the most effective.
mid.result: The result of these negotiations and discussions was the selection of a high-grade stainless steel heating element. We had found that this particular material would provide the best balance of heat-efficiency, cost and long term durability. However, we would have really liked a translucent material there if it was more feasible (mostly for visual appeal, and ease to see water level). This decision did not only satisfy the IoT tea making device’s technical and cost requirements we had laid out prior, but had also taught us a valuable lesson in collaborative decision-making and compromise, without realising it in the moment. The team’s ability to negotiate and resolve the conflicts surrounding material choice had directly contributed to the successful design and functionality of our IoT device. Furthermore, the experience had indirectly enhanced our problem-solving skills and understanding of the vital role that material properties play in both product performance and user experience. This helps ensure that we will be better prepared for future projects and professional challenges regarding the topic of negotiation, compromise and conflict resolution.

mid.situation: I NEEDED TO COOPERATE WITH MY COWORKERS AND COMMUNICATE WITH CUSTOMERS TO SAFELY RUN A WORKSITE WHICH HAS CONSTANT TRAFFIC FLOW. AT KIMBRIKI ENVIRONMENTAL ENTERPRISES, I ALONG WITH MY COWORKERS CONTROL THE WASTE DISPOSAL OF ALL VEHICLES. WITH 1200 VEHICLES ON OUR SITE OVER THE COURSE OF THE 10 HOUR SHIFT THERE IS A CRUCIAL ROLE IN THE COMMUNICATION WITHIN THE TRAFFIC CONTROL ASPECT TO KEEP BOTH WORKERS AND CUSTOMERS SAFE. COMMUNICATION IS VITAL IN HAVING A SAFE WORKPLACE. COMMUNICATION IS CONSISTENT IN ALL WORKPLACES, WHETHER IN AN OFFICE OR ON A WORKSITE. CLEAR, SHORT AND UNIVERSAL COMMUNICATION ALLOWS FOR MESSAGES TO BE PHYSICALLY OR VERBALLY CONVEYED WITH EASE WHETHER THEY ARE INSTRUCTIONS OR WARNINGS.
mid.task_action: Implementing meetings and training allow employees to be on the same page and create a minimum skill level. Having employees on the same page is one of the most important aspects in regards to communication because these workers will have similar unified verbal communications or sign language. Dealing with vehicle traffic flow requires my coworkers and I to have simple sign languages like a straight arm with palm up facing traffic for a ‘STOP’ or an extended arm with a pushing motion to the ground to communicate ‘SLOW DOWN’ to customers to maximise understanding and decrease the risk of injury or damage.       Furthermore, messages between coworkers with short concise phrases over a two way radio system allow for there to be little confusion and increased radio silence. This is particularly important incase of an emergency. For example, a coworker can quickly inform the site coordinator that there has been a dumping of hazardous waste like unwrapped asbestos or chemicals because there is not unnecessary noise on the radios. In doing so customers can be properly protected, and the waste can be cleaned up efficiently.
mid.result: The result was being able run a worksite which has clear communications between coworkers and customers, which leads to a heavily reduced risk level for a high-risk workplace. Workplaces with high levels of effective communications create a nearly risk free environment.

As a result, our group project received a high score. The act of motivating everyone, coupled with a serious work ethic, created an efficient process. Team members felt respected and appreciated, which increased overall productivity and job satisfaction.

mid.situation: At the beginning of this semester, the engineering students in this unit were presented with a major project. This project was to design an internet operated tea maker. Creating new solution concepts, however, proved an obstacle in a few areas.
mid.task_action: To overcome this, an emphasis was provided on brainstorming. This should always be a step in an engineering project as it allows for flexibility of ideas before a more finalised solution is presented. In this project, brainstorming was conducted to determine solutions for the implementation of both iced and hot tea being made in the same unit. To ensure effective brainstorming was conducted, all the requirements for the project were understood. This included the initial communication with the customer, where all the criteria they wanted out of the tea maker were listed, but also the technical requirements of the tea maker. In the context of the different teas for the tea maker to produce, the requirements were for the iced tea to be prepared with ice, but also the preparation of hot tea. This would include having the device maintain a temperature of beneath 0 degrees Celsius to freeze water into ice, and also maintain a temperature of 100 Celsius to boil water for the hot tea. With these requirements understood, work can be done to create solutions to the project. However, after brainstorming solutions to the project, only a few options were presented. It was difficult to create new solutions that weren’t too similar to previous ones. By the conclusion of the brainstorming phase, only a few solutions were presented, with definite similarities between them.
mid.result: As a result, creating new solution concepts did propose potential solutions through the brainstorming phase. This means the exercise was successful and positively affected the contribution to the completion of the project. Creating new solution concepts does help to obtain a rough estimate of what the final solution should accomplish. The brainstormed ideas can then be based around this estimate to ensure consistency in designs. This will help keep the designing of the solution on the consistent path to success, without too many outlier brainstormed ideas to consider. However the lack of ideas did limit the creativity of the final solution to the project as there is little variation between the different ways to accomplish the solution.

mid.situation: In our tech start-up, we identified a growing trend in smart home appliances and saw an opportunity to innovate by creating an internet based tea maker. The goal was to provide tea enthusiasts with a device that could be controlled remotely, offer personalised brewing settings, and integrate seamlessly with smart home ecosystems.      TASK: As the lead designer on the project, my task was to conceptualise and design the tea maker. This involved not only the physical design but also the user interface and the connectivity features. Critical thinking was essential in addressing potential challenges, understanding user needs, and ensuring the product's feasibility and functionality.      ACTION:      	*   RESEARCH AND ANALYSIS:      	* Conducted thorough market research to understand the preferences and pain points of tea drinkers.   	* Analysed existing smart kitchen appliances to identify successful features and common pitfalls.      	*   USER CENTRED DESIGN:      	* Employed critical thinking to empathise with users and anticipate their needs. For example, recognising that different types of tea require different brewing temperatures and times led to the inclusion of customisable settings.   	* Created user personas and scenarios to guide the design process, ensuring the tea maker would be intuitive and user friendly.      	*   TECHNICAL FEASIBILITY:      	* Collaborated with engineers to assess the technical requirements for Wi-Fi connectivity and app integration.   	* Used critical thinking to evaluate the best methods for real-time communication between the tea maker and the user’s smartphone, ensuring reliability and security.      	*   PROTOTYPING AND TESTING:      	* Developed prototypes and conducted usability tests to gather feedback and identify areas for improvement.   	* Applied critical thinking to interpret test results, making iterative design changes that balanced user feedback with technical constraints.      	*   PROBLEM-SOLVING:      	* Encountered challenges such as ensuring the device's safety, minimising energy consumption, and maintaining a sleek design. Used critical thinking to devise innovative solutions, such as integrating an auto shut off feature and using energy-efficient components.
mid.result: The critical thinking applied throughout the design process resulted in a highly functional and user-friendly internet-connected tea maker. Key achievements included:      	* Positive user feedback on the convenience and customisation options.   	* Successful integration with popular smart home systems, enhancing user experience.   	* Recognition in industry reviews for innovation and design, leading to strong market interest and initial sales.      By leveraging critical thinking, we were able to create a product that not only met but exceeded user expectations, positioning our company as a leader in smart kitchen technology.

mid.situation: During this semester of ENGG2050 my team and I were set the task of designing a tea maker that would fit certain criteria set by Rex, some of these included the need for it be activatable remotely, to dispense 8 cups of tea a day, to be able to store milk and butter. Given that this was a team project the ability to think creatively and communicate new possibilities between group members was essential as everyone had a slightly different approach to tackling problems and coming up with solutions to solve them.                    T – TASK:      The biggest problems/challenges that arose throughout this project was the clash of ideas and ordering of priorities for the machine’s specifications. Especially with the plethora of requirements, various group members have extremely different views on what they believed to be top priority and what could be left out. The task that I set for myself was to try and mitigate or minimise differences in views by creating some common understandings of concepts and ideas to create the best machine possible.             A – ACTION:      One of the biggest disagreements originally was how we would store the various liquids and the method of heating that we should adopt, with different members wanting to prioritise storage environment over capacity and heating solutions through induction or chemical heating. By taking in the information given by my teammates I set out to take a creative approach on the issues at hand and asked for them to communicate why they had prioritised certain solutions over others. One of the major concerns was that heating units/coils would be large and bulky taking up too much space and adding weight, this paired with the problem of storage of cool items. Thinking creatively on the idea and showcasing adaptability I came up with the idea of using a thermoelectric Peltier module between the cold milk and sustained heated water source. It acted as a wall between the two and sucked the heat/energy from one sided and deposited/released it on the other, similar to how the walls of a chest freezer works solving both the heating and storage problems by allowing for the two varying temperatures to exist near each other. This solution was born purely out of the communication of varying possibilities and applying a creative solution that the group would be happy with.                    R –
mid.result: The suggestion of the Peltier module using creative thinking due to communicating possibilities allowed for the group to agree on the idea and made everybody happy and positively impacted the overall situation.                                  DEAL FRAMEWORK:                     D – DESCRIBE:      The skill that I developed through the scenario of designing a tea maker was ‘Thinking Creatively and Communicating New Possibilities’. I believe that this skill is essential in any work environment and even more so in a team setting where ideas and solutions can sometimes be conflicting. In the given context of the tea maker design project, when brainstorming ideas communicating ideas requires a creative side to it as it is purely theoretical for the time being, and communicating the ideas effectively is essential within the group setting.                    E – EXAMINE:      My strength in the area of the soft skill that I was developing was my ability to think creatively, taking an assessment of a problem and trying to approach it from a different direction in order to try and satisfy various parties within the team. A challenge I did face however was communicating my idea effectively, especially within the team environment, trying to introduce an idea that would potentially please multiple parties but potentially not all. This paired with the emotional aspect of presenting a new ‘creative’ idea to the group as we were all still becoming acquainted with one another. Another challenge was presenting an idea that opposed someone else in the group, not wanting to step on anyone’s toes and feeling nervous were key aspects of challenge for me.             A – ACT:      Ways to further develop the soft skill of creative thinking and effective communication of possibilities would be to work in more team centred environments. This would allow me to become more comfortable when voicing ideas or solutions even if it may contradict another party in some form.             L – LEARN:      Since I have recently moved into a corporate team centric role for work, it is good development practice of soft skills within a more forgiving environment, especially when expressing ideas has no direct impact to the real world it allows for more free expression and building of confidence. I now understand the importance of developing soft skills such as this and am excited to apply it within a professional setting.

mid.situation: Our design unit was tasked with creating a prototype for a new tea maker. We were a group of four, each with different preferences and priorities. I valued aesthetics and user experience, while others were prioritising functionality, sustainability, or cost-effectiveness.   Task: The challenge was to combine our individual ideas into a cohesive design. We started by brainstorming features, throwing everything out there – from sleek, touch-screen interfaces to built-in milk frothers.   Action: As expected, conflicts arose. However, I was not expecting the amount of conflict that came from some ideas being unheard. One member insisted on a minimalist design, while another wanted a multi-function machine with pre-programmed settings. To navigate these differences, we employed several tactics.      	* Active listening: We focused on understanding everyone's perspective, asking clarifying questions and acknowledging concerns.      	* Idea prioritization: We used a voting system to identify the most liked features. From there, we discussed compromises, like incorporating a simple interface with pre-set options to appease both functionality and aesthetics desires of the customer.      	* Focus on the user: We kept reminding ourselves of the target audience – tea enthusiasts who wanted a convenient and reliable brewing experience. This shared goal helped us find common ground.
mid.result: Through negotiation and compromise, we arrived at a final design that balanced aesthetics with functionality. Our tea maker featured a sleek, user-friendly interface with basic brewing options and a sustainable, energy-efficient design. We all felt proud of the final product, which was a testament to our ability to collaborate effectively.   This project taught me valuable lessons in teamwork. Firstly, effective communication is key. By truly listening to each other, we were able to understand and respect each other's viewpoints. Secondly, compromise is essential, particularly for myself. Rigidly clinging to my own ideas prevents progress for the whole group. Finally, focusing on a common goal – in this case, creating a great tea maker – helped us overcome differences and achieve a successful outcome.

mid.situation: DURING A TEAM PROJECT IN MY CONTROL SYSTEMS ENGINEERING COURSE, OUR GOAL WAS TO DESIGN A MOTOR CONTROL SYSTEM TO PRECISELY MANAGE THE SPEED OF A DC MOTOR FOR AN ELEVATOR SYSTEM. THIS PROJECT REQUIRED THE UTILIZATION OF KNOWLEDGE IN DESIGN.       Task:      I was responsible for designing and validating the transfer function of the motor control system. This task involved deriving the mathematical model representing the motor's dynamics and ensuring that our system's performance aligned with the motor's datasheet specifications.      Action:       Utilizing my knowledge in design and control systems, I derived the form of the transfer function using Newton’s 2nd Law, Kirchoff’s Law and the Laplace Transform. I then used the motor’s specifications and inputted them into the transfer function form to derive the transfer function to describe the motor.       To validate the function, I used a 6V step response on MATLAB and simulated the output. The output was 180 RPM which was exactly what the datasheet showed.
mid.result: As a result, we exceeded our targets by achieving a highly accurate transfer function that closely matched the datasheet specifications. The outcome was a positive impact on our project's success, as we now had an accurate transfer function to work with.

mid.situation: I faced several moral conundrums and social responsibility issues in the engineering sector as a student. By taking part in conversations and debates on ethical problems in engineering, I helped my peers and myself develop critical thinking and awareness.
mid.result: I developed a solid foundation in ethics, integrity, and social responsibility by making a conscious effort to give these areas top priority. This gave me the ability to approach engineering problems with a stronger sense of moral responsibility and a dedication to preserving the environment and society's well-being. I discovered that incorporating these ideas into my work improved the calibre of my contributions and helped me feel fulfilled in my profession as an engineer.

mid.situation: I was put in charge of a team of analysts at work for a mining task where the clients expectation was too high.
mid.task_action: We were tasked with cleaning up the spend in areas surrounding certain shopping centers where the client wanted a 10km radius for metro areas and 50km radius for rural areas. After completing some minor centers in the metro area it was visible that 10km would not work in larger cities as the demand was just too high to mine all spend (e.g. finding and assigning all the store within the Sydney CBD). I went back to the client lead with this concern from the team to negotiate a potential better solution that the client would still be happy with.
mid.result: After the negotiations the client agreed to decrease to a 5km radium for minor metro areas and to 1 block for cbd type areas whilst also only focusing on major brands within those areas. This enabled the team to deliver our piece of work on time and for the client to be happy with the result leading to us being able to retain them as a client.

mid.situation: During the ETHGlobal Hackathon in Sydney, April 2024, I led a team of five participants in developing a decentralised finance (DeFi) application. Our project aimed to create a platform for automated cryptocurrency trading strategies using smart contracts on the Ethereum blockchain. The team included individuals with various backgrounds and experience levels in blockchain technology, which initially led to some misalignment in understanding the project scope and technical requirements.      TASK: As the team leader, my primary task was to align the team’s goals, establish clear roles and responsibilities, and ensure effective collaboration to meet our project objectives within the 48-hour timeframe of the hackathon. This involved managing diverse skill sets, resolving conflicts, and keeping the team motivated and focused.      ACTION: To address these challenges, I began by organising an initial meeting to discuss each member’s skills, experience, and expectations for the project. I then created a detailed project plan with defined milestones and individual responsibilities. To foster efficient collaboration, I set up a shared workspace using GitHub for code management and Discord for communication.      When disagreements arose, such as differing opinions on the smart contract design, I facilitated discussions to ensure all viewpoints were considered and led the team towards a consensus. Additionally, I provided guidance and resources to team members who were less familiar with specific blockchain concepts and technologies. Regular check-ins were held to track progress, address any issues promptly, and adjust the plan as needed.
mid.result: As a result of these actions, the team became more cohesive and productive. We successfully developed and deployed a functional prototype of our DeFi application by the end of the hackathon. Our project was well-received by the judges, earning us a special mention for innovation and teamwork. This experience significantly enhanced my leadership skills and demonstrated the importance of effective communication, conflict resolution, and mentorship in a fast-paced, high-pressure environment.

mid.situation: During my third-year unit, CIVL3301 (Design of Concrete Structures), my group was tasked with designing a car park structure on campus. This project required us to plan and virtually design a structurally sound car park that adhered to relevant Australian Standards (AS). We faced significant challenges, including identifying the appropriate standards to follow, and uncertainties regarding the minimum and appropriate measurements for car spaces, parking spaces, turning radii, and the design of pedestrian access points such as lift shafts, stairs, and ramps.      TASK AND ACTION:      As the former designated team leader had unenrolled from the unit by the census date, I was then put in charge of ensuring that our group overcame these uncertainties and delivered an updated, comprehensive and compliant design report. My primary goal was to organise the team, delegate tasks effectively, and ensure that all aspects of the project were addressed, adhering to the necessary standards and constraints.      To tackle these challenges, I took several key actions:      	* ORGANISED REGULAR MEETINGS: I scheduled a series of focused meetings within the group to discuss progress, identify challenges, and brainstorm solutions. These meetings happened both during scheduled class times and weekly on Discord calls at everyone’s convenience.   	* TASK DELEGATION: During these meetings, I divided the project into smaller, manageable sections and assigned specific tasks to each group member based on their strengths and areas of expertise. This included software competency, research, design, and validation tasks. As this was the second half of the report, most of the work entailed updating sections to be up to spec with the inclusion of reinforced concrete slabs in our parking complex structure (and making relevant changes in our calculations, design schemes and report formatting to account for that).   	* RESEARCH AND GUIDANCE: I reached out to our convenor, Sorn, for guidance on the relevant Australian Standards and consulted online databases (SAI Global and MQ Multisearch) to gather the necessary information and AS documentation.   	* COMPARATIVE STUDY: To address the uncertainties regarding measurements, I led the team in conducting a comparative study of existing parking lot structures on and off campus. We compared the South 2 Carpark with Bella Vista Metro Station's car park, collected data on average dimensions and layouts (tape measuring and counting spaces), and took photos for future reference.   	* DOCUMENTATION AND COMMUNICATION: I ensured that all findings, assumptions, and decisions were well-documented and communicated clearly within the team. This helped in maintaining a cohesive approach and ensuring that everyone was on the same page.
mid.result: Our proactive and multipronged approach, coupled with effective team leadership, resulted in the successful completion of the design report. We identified all the relevant Australian Standards for our project and made cautious assumptions about the design measurements. The final design was compliant with the necessary standards, and our report was well-received by the convenor. Our team’s ability to navigate through uncertainty and work collaboratively led to a high-quality submission that met all the project requirements.
