mid.situation: This semester was challenging, not only academically but also personally as well. I was attempting to navigate a path to balance my academic journey with my personal commitments to sport and physical wellbeing, alongside working at my job which consisted of erratic shifts due to the casual nature of my role. This presented an opportunity for me to practise the soft skills of analysing my needs to understand what was needed in the short run and how it was different from what is needed in the long run and setting goals using the SMART (Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-Bound) format.
mid.task_action: Before and during the beginning stages of the semester, I used to exercise at least once a week in a gym and then fence on Fridays whilst maintaining a median of 7 hours of sleep. Up till this point, I had a vague idea of what I needed to be doing with my daily life, and I believed that taking things day by day would lead to the optimal outcome. With the introduction of my work as a casual retail assistant, I began to slowly shift my focus on attaining more shifts, with the mindset that I should save more money for future purchases. Along the way, work became more real to me than my role as a student. When I had work on a particular day, after my shift, I would be physically exhausted because I was moving and retrieving and organising the stock at the store during my shift. I rationalised doing nothing else because in my mind, ‘I was done with work for that day’. I lost sight of what my true needs were, true in the sense that working towards my academic goals was for the purpose of investing into my future career. This is understandable, because the future is almost illusory, compacted by the fact that I had not yet learnt of the SMART system of goal setting. Almost all my goals in my life up to this point have been missing some part of SMART. Often it was the catastrophic combination of a lack of specificity and being time-bound, which essentially made it impossible to be measurable. Losing sight of my needs meant that the delicate and fragile way of life was thrown into disarray, this included my gradual weakening of the desire to play the sport I loved, additionally my exercise and sleeping habits began to take a turn for the worse. I had to reevaluate my needs, and set goals for myself that were achievable and measurable.
mid.result: After learning about SMART, I began to reflect on what actionable steps would lead to a more optimal outcome for myself. This process of thinking led to me reflecting on what my needs were, and I came to the conclusion that the choice to work more was not wholly wrong, but my motivations were slightly short-sided. Thus I began attempting to reinvest my efforts into my academic journey, I have begun the process of prioritising my health as well. I have been going to the gym at least once a week, often to stretch and train my mobility.

mid.situation: Within the subject of ENGG2050 we were provided an assignment where I needed to make a consumer a product according to the following wants:      These are their major wants       	* I want an Internet based Tea Maker    	* I want to wake up to tea, just the way I like it    	* I want to come home to tea, just the way I like it.       These are more minor wants       	* I want to share my tea style with my friends    	* I want to have friends    	* I prefer different teas depending on the time of day.    	* I like to experiment with new teas that my friends recommend    	* I want to rate different tea styles
mid.task_action: * _Idea Generation:_   When proceeding with idea generation I took into consideration the consumers needs and wants. I focused on the major wants and began sketching concepts to give myself a standard of the machine I was wanting. In this portion of the innovation process I needed to tick off the consumers minor needs but in some cases avoided them completely. I primarily avoided them as I did not ask the right questions when I was able to.    	* _Preparation:_   Within this phase, I began to develop my sketches more and more. Moving from sketches into rendered design and real world application processes and installation. I struggled within this area as I did not account for the real-world consequences of certain components being next to each other would entail. For example, having Milk next to the hot water system.    	* _Innovation:   _As the task required innovation within the tea making industry, I began to research into differing ways for tea to be made while avoiding contamination of other tea ingredients into different tea types. I opted for a system flush function which would drain the previously used tea out of the extraction pipes and would remove the used leaves or bags. However, coming up with a functioning system which would avoiding: 1. injuring the consumer, 2. detecting when there is no tea cup under the dispenser, and 3. find a way for there to be tea automatically made after the system flush without human input.      I struggled to find a system which would work. I then moved from that segment, due to lack of idea generation for the solution, of the project to the modularity which I wanted the machine to have for easy of used and cleaning purposes. This segment was not difficult and was intentionally designed so that certain segments could be bought after the machine if parts failed.       In completing that segment, I moved back to the system flush and auto tea function. In stepping away from the problem for a while I gave my self a refresh to the idea and opted for a system which would make a tea push the cup to the left using a grated track system and flush the system then insert the next cup on the track system. The grated track system allows for the flushed water/tea to fall into a catching pan which needs to be changed every week.   	* _Testing and Iterations/Monitoring and Evaluation:   _As the project did not require for the product to be produced this segment of the innovation process could not be fulfilled.
mid.result: As previously stated in task/action I struggled to come up with a suitable solution to a problem which I faced. By taking step back from the solution process and moving my mind to another problem I allowed myself to effectively design the product. As such I considered most of the needs and wants of the consumer. HOWEVER, I neglected the friends aspect of the system and only focused on the different teas being made.

mid.situation: As a third-year mechanical engineering student, I worked with an interdisciplinary team to design and build an automated tea maker for our ENGG2050 project. Our team comprised students from various engineering fields, including electrical, software, civil, and mechatronics engineering. While diversity of expertise was a strength, it also presented significant communication and collaboration challenges. Each team member had a unique approach and set of priorities, resulting in frequent miscommunication and disagreements. The project was behind schedule, and the team's morale was low. I felt excitement about our project's innovative potential and frustration with the ongoing conflicts.      Task:      My specific task was contributing to the mechanical design and ensuring it worked seamlessly with the electrical and software components. Although I was not in a leadership position, I needed to help foster better communication and collaboration among team members. This necessitated using critical thinking to navigate interpersonal dynamics and technological challenges.      Action:      To address these challenges, I took several critical steps:             1.       Enhancing Communication:      	* I proposed informal check-ins in addition to our formal meetings (through WhatsApp chats). These provided a comfortable environment for team members to discuss progress and concerns, thereby reducing misunderstandings.   	* I advocated for the use of a collaborative project management tool that tracked tasks, deadlines, and dependencies, allowing everyone to see the big picture and understand how their work fits into the project.      2.       Facilitating Collaboration:      	* During discussions, I actively listened to ensure that everyone's ideas were heard and considered. When disagreements arose, I suggested that we focus on our common goal and hold brainstorming sessions to generate creative solutions.   	* I volunteered to document meeting notes and action items, which helped clarify responsibilities and keep the team on track.      3.       Technical Integration:      	* I worked closely with the electrical engineers to understand their needs and constraints, ensuring that the mechanical design could accommodate the circuitry and components.   	* I collaborated with the software engineers to ensure that the tea maker's mechanical components could be effectively controlled by the automated system they were developing.   	* I provided detailed mechanical drawings and specifications, which helped everyone understand how all the parts would fit and function together.
mid.result: As a result of these actions, there was a noticeable enhancement in the team dynamics and advancement of the project. Improved communication and teamwork resulted in the successful integration of all elements. The automated tea maker not only met but surpassed, the original design requirements.

mid.situation: One of the main transferable skills I developed was taking the initiative during the design of models. For context, during Week 5 (testing / alternatives and their implementation), I had to choose between two different suppliers of COTS (commercial off the shelf) components. The two critical components I came up with were the heating element and the Wi-Fi module.       The task itself required user tests to ensure we meet the TPMs defined and standard testing to ensure compliance with externally mandated rules and regulations. This is a situation where my initiative skill developed, as we are dealing with a hypothetical machine, so I had to think in a new way for how to create the type 2 tests for the customer TPM’s      Task:       I was expected to create a testing plan for the customer TPM’s that included test and evaluation procedures, test site selection, test personnel and training of those personnel, test facilities and resources for those facilities, and equipment needed to perform the test to ensure optimal performance, reliability, and cost-efficiency of the tea maker.      The goals I set were:      	*   Achieve the best balance between performance and cost for the heating element.      	*   Ensure the Wi-Fi module provides reliable and stable connectivity while meeting budget constraints.      	*   Ensure compliance with technical performance measures (TPMs) and regulatory standards.      This task was complex and non-trivial due to the need for careful evaluation of multiple interdependent variables, the integration of components into a cohesive system, and the rigorous testing required to ensure both performance and regulatory compliance. Successfully navigating this challenge required a strong initiative, problem-solving skills and the ability to make informed decisions under uncertainty.      Action:       For the heating element, supplier A offered a heating element with faster heat-up time but higher energy consumption, while supplier B offered a slower heating element that is more energy-efficient. I had to take into account and evaluate the trade-offs between performance (heat-up time) and operational costs (energy consumption). I also had to consider the impact on user experience (waiting time for tea) and long-term cost savings (electricity bills).      For the Wi-Fi module, supplier C provided a module with higher data transfer rates but less reliable connectivity, whereas supplier D offered a module with slower transfer rates but more stable and reliable connectivity. I decided to assign points to different criteria such as data transfer speed, reliability, ease of integration, and cost. I would then pick the highest overall score of each supplier’s module against these criteria.      I encountered unexpected variability in component performance due to differences in supplier specifications. By adapting to these new findings and taking initiative, I demonstrated flexibility and responsiveness to change. This adaptability and initiative allowed me to reassess my component choices, ensuring that the final product met both performance expectations and compliance requirements.
mid.result: After performing a cost-benefit analysis, Supplier B's heating element was chosen because its higher energy efficiency resulted in lower long-term operational costs despite a slightly slower heat-up time. Analysis showed that the energy savings would offset the slower performance over the product's lifecycle, providing better value to users and aligning with sustainability goals. Using the weighted scoring model, Supplier D's Wi-Fi module was selected due to its superior reliability and stability, which were weighted more heavily than data transfer speed. Stable connectivity was crucial for user experience, as it ensured consistent performance and minimised potential user frustrations with connectivity issues. Overall, my ability to take initiative  and reassess component choices based on comprehensive analyses allowed me to make informed decisions that balanced performance, cost, and user experience.      Impactful achievements that came out of this week's task were improved product performance and user satisfaction (as listed above) from suppliers B and D, which were chosen over suppliers A and C. Also, the methodologies and adaptability demonstrated during this project set a precedent for my future projects throughout the weeks, encouraging thorough analysis and flexibility.

mid.situation: During my current Systems Engineering and Design Thinking course, one of the assignments was the A4 infographic. This assignment consisted in the creation of an infographic to explain a key concept from the textbook that I have learnt during this session. I chose Human Factors in Usability Design.
mid.task_action: . My goal was to create an engaging and informative infographic that clearly conveyed the importance of human factors in usability design. I needed to ensure that the infographic structure had three sections:      ·         An abstraction level      ·         An implementation level      ·         Interesting examples      The methods I used to create the infographic were the following:      RESEARCH: I conducted a thorough research to understand the definition of Human Factors in Usability Design and the main methodologies used to achieve it. I listed the definition and the main methodologies used as my _abstraction layer_.      Similarly, I conducted my research for the implementation level. There is a significant number of ways to implement the Human Factors in Usability Design. I have chosen six methodologies which, in my opinion, are the ones most used by commercial entities that provide Usability Design services. These six methodologies formed my _implementation layer_.      SELECTION CRITERIA CREATED AND USED      I found many interesting examples and it was difficult to make choices. I used three selection criteria to ensure my examples were relevant and impactful:      ·         Innovative approach      ·         Impact on Users on global scale      ·         Aesthetic Appeal      Using the above criteria my selection consisted of 13 examples. These examples formed my _interesting examples layer._      BRAINSTORMING: I started by brainstorming different ways to present the information, focusing on clarity and engagement. I explored various themes, color schemes, and layouts.      DESIGN ITERATION: I created multiple drafts, experimenting with different visual elements like location on the page, graphics, and fonts.
mid.result: As a result of these efforts, I submitted the A4 infographic. In my opinion, the infographic effectively communicated complex concepts in an accessible and visually appealing way. Working on the A4 infographic not only enhanced my conceptual understanding of Human Factors in Usability Design but also strengthened my skills in creative problem-solving, visual communication, and user-centered design. It was a valuable exercise in integrating technical knowledge with conceptual design thinking principles.

mid.situation: _      At the start of this semester, I enrolled in a full course load, including several challenging subjects. Each course had demanding assignments, projects, and regular assessments. On top of that, I was involved in extracurricular activities, including being a co-founder of a fashion brand as well as participating in various community programs.      As the semester progressed, I found it increasingly difficult to prioritize my tasks. Assignments from different courses often had overlapping deadlines, and I struggled to decide which tasks needed my immediate attention. The sheer volume of work was overwhelming, and I frequently felt stressed and anxious about meeting deadlines. Despite my best efforts, I was unable to focus effectively on important assignments, often leaving them until the last minute, which affected the quality of my work.      One particularly challenging week, I had two-three major assignments due in the same week, a midterm exam, and a significant event to organize for my brand. I felt paralyzed by the pressure, unable to prioritize which task to tackle first. This situation highlighted my inability to manage my time effectively and underscored the need to develop better prioritization and focus strategies.             _TASK:_      In order to address the overwhelming demands of the semester and improve my academic performance, I identified several key objectives:      1.      DEVELOPING AN EFFECTIVE PRIORITIZATION SYSTEM: My primary task was to create a structured system to prioritize my tasks. This involved distinguishing between urgent and important tasks, and deciding which assignments needed immediate attention versus those that could be scheduled for later.       2.      MANAGING TIME EFFICIENTLY: I needed to allocate my time wisely across various subjects and activities. This required creating a detailed weekly schedule that included specific time slots for studying, completing assignments, attending classes, and participating in extracurricular activities.       3.      BALANCING ACADEMIC AND EXTRACURRICULAR ACTIVITIES: Balancing my responsibilities between academics and extracurricular activities was another important aspect of my task. I needed to ensure that my involvement in student council and community outreach did not detract from my academic performance. This required careful planning and sometimes making difficult decisions about which activities to prioritize.      _ACTION:_      To address this challenge, I implemented several strategies:      	* CREATING A STUDY SCHEDULE: I created a detailed study schedule that allocated specific times for each subject and assignment. This helped me keep track of deadlines and manage my time more effectively.   	* SEEKING HELP: I actively communicated with colleagues and peers to understand better ways to manage my workload and prioritize tasks.   	* UTILIZING TIME MANAGEMENT TOOLS: I used time management tools, such as planners and digital apps, to keep track of deadlines and set reminders for important tasks.   	* EXPERIMENTING WITH DIFFERENT STRATEGIES: I experimented with different prioritization techniques, such as the Pomodoro Technique, to find what worked best for me.      _
mid.result: _      The outcomes of these actions were mixed:      	* INITIAL STRUGGLES: Despite my efforts, I initially struggled with consistency. Some strategies worked better than others, and it took time to find a method that suited my needs. The feeling of being overwhelmed persisted, leading to frustration and anxiety.   	* IMPROVEMENT OVER TIME: Gradually, I began to see improvements in my ability to prioritize and focus. My assignments were completed on time, and the quality of my work improved. I received better marks and I felt relieved and accomplished as I started meeting my deadlines more consistently.   	* ONGOING CHALLENGES: However, the journey was not without challenges. I realized the need for better support systems and resources, such as study groups to maintain focus and prioritize effectively. The process was taxing, but it also motivated me to continue seeking improvement.      _DESCRIBE:_      The soft skill I want to reflect on is prioritization and focus. This skill is crucial in managing academic workloads and ensuring timely and quality submissions. Without effective prioritization, it is easy to become overwhelmed and fall behind, as I experienced initially.             _EXAMINE:_      I demonstrated strength in organizing tasks and seeking help, but I struggled with maintaining consistency, especially when unexpected tasks disrupted my schedule. The constant struggle led to frustration and anxiety, affecting my overall motivation. I noticed that I could improve in anticipating potential delays and being more proactive in addressing them.             _ACT:_      To further enhance this skill, I plan to:             1.      REFINE MY STUDY SCHEDULE: Continuously refine my study schedule to accommodate unexpected tasks and changes.      2.      SEEK FEEDBACK REGULARLY: Regularly seek feedback from instructors and peers to improve my prioritization strategies.      _3.      _DEVELOP A SUPPORT NETWORK: Build a network of support, including study groups and academic advisors, to help manage my focus and priorities.__      _ _      _LEARN:_      From this experience, I learned the critical importance of prioritization and focus in achieving academic success. I now understand that these skills are not static but require continuous improvement and adaptation. Moving forward, I am committed to applying these skills in all aspects of my academic and professional life.

mid.situation: During an ENGG2050 team project, I believe I have improved my soft skills with several situations and challenges I have faced during the time. The purpose of our team project was to build a tea-maker machine. Throughout the process of developing the project, I was responsible for recording and taking notes of our each week’s tutorial work and talk to the group members about their tasks. We were given tutorial tasks each week in order to assist the process of developing our product. I was chosen to record and wrap each week’s work done by everyone. As it was group work my communication skill was to be challenged in order to get everyone’s idea correctly and precisely so that our project runs smoothly.      Task:      At first, it was easy for every member to communicate with each other, but as the project went on, I have noticed some of our members being unhappy about their given tasks. When the time came to discuss and brief about the tasks we had a pretty heated debate on wether or not whose idea was better than the other. Specifically on deciding the design of our product, I had proposed my idea and it was brilliant which indeed was at the time to me. However, two of our members argued that their idea was better than mine. It was ridiculous how much time I have spent on the design and someone says it was “not very good”.Though it took some time for me to listen and understand everyone’s point of view, someone had to take charge and end the argument.       Action:       I stepped in as soon as everyone cooled off after the argument and tried to lighten up the mood by expressing my gratitude on every members contribution, how everyone was involved in the project that they had their own ideas to share. I thought to myself that they wanted the project to be just as much success as I wanted it to be. Also I was part of a team where everyone was doing their best to make it work. I have apologised if I was rude to them by forcing my idea on everyone, and the two of our members have apologized to me for criticizing my work so quickly when I have spent so many time on it. Our group was finally in the mood to actually develop our design ideas, so we have included everyone’s best ideas and finalized our design. I felt so empowered and accomplished though we were not finished with the final product yet. But I had a feeling that it would be good as long as everyone including myself communicate with each other so well that we will be ready to overcome any challenges that we might face.
mid.result: As a result, I have realized how strong communication affects a group’s dynamics and productiveness. I noticed that I struggled with communicating my ideas correctly so that the other person understands it, as well as getting offended my constructive criticism. It was true that my design needed more work, and I couldn’t handle people saying it was not good to me. I have gotten somehow offended by it and flipped out. I could have listened to their points and try improve my design rather than claiming that it was my best work and they shouldn’t judge it. Listening to people and communicating while encouraging everyone else to speak their ideas are the soft skills that I have mostly improved during the project. I am excited to further develop these skills and use it in any team project that I might do in the future. Also, I will be telling this in my job interview how I have strong communication skills that I have been practicing since completing units like this.

mid.situation: In our mechanical engineering design unit, we were tasked with designing a gearbox for a crane, a crucial component that would impact the crane's efficiency and safety. The project required close collaboration among team members, each with varying opinions on the design approach due to differences in technical understanding and priorities.      TASK:      As the team leader, my role was to guide the design process, ensure all technical requirements were met, and maintain team cohesion. This involved managing differing opinions to find the most efficient design solution while ensuring that each team member felt valued and heard.      ACTION:      To address the team's diverse views, I organized structured brainstorming sessions where each member could present their design ideas and the rationale behind them. To promote empathy and deeper understanding, I encouraged team members to critique constructively by first stating what they appreciated in each other's ideas before suggesting improvements. This approach aimed to create a supportive environment that fostered creative collaboration.
mid.result: This empathetic approach to leadership helped the team to merge the best aspects of each proposed design, leading to the development of a more efficient and robust gearbox design. The project was not only completed successfully but also strengthened the team's ability to work effectively under stress and complex technical challenges. This experience highlighted the importance of empathy in leadership, particularly in facilitating effective communication and collaboration in engineering projects.

mid.situation: During the second semester of my mechanical engineering course, we were assigned a project to design a new, efficient mechanism for a conveyor system used in manufacturing. The primary objective was to improve the system's speed and reliability without significantly increasing costs. This task required a high level of creative thinking to balance the technical constraints with innovative solutions.      TASK: My role in this project was to lead the brainstorming session and guide my team in generating and evaluating creative ideas. We needed to come up with multiple design options and then select the most viable solution based on specific criteria such as cost, efficiency, and ease of implementation.      ACTION:      	*   BRAINSTORMING SESSION: I organized and facilitated a brainstorming session where each team member was encouraged to present their ideas. We used the gallery method, where ideas were written on large sheets of paper and displayed around the room. This allowed everyone to see, compare, and build upon each other's suggestions.      	*   ENCOURAGING CREATIVITY: I encouraged my team to think outside the box and consider unconventional solutions. To stimulate creative thinking, I introduced techniques such as mind mapping and reverse brainstorming, where we identified potential failures and worked backward to find solutions.      	*   EVALUATING OPTIONS: After generating a list of potential solutions, we evaluated each one based on predefined criteria: cost-effectiveness, ease of implementation, potential for innovation, and long-term benefits. We ranked the options and discussed their feasibility in detail.      	*   PROTOTYPING AND TESTING: We selected the top three ideas and created low-fidelity prototypes to test their functionality. This hands-on approach helped us visualize the concepts and identify any practical issues early in the process.
mid.result: The result of our creative thinking and collaborative efforts was a novel conveyor mechanism that significantly improved the system's efficiency by 25%. The design combined elements from multiple brainstorming ideas, showcasing the power of collaborative creativity. Our project received positive feedback from our instructors, who highlighted our innovative approach and thorough evaluation process. Additionally, the project was selected for presentation at a university engineering showcase, which further validated our creative efforts.      This experience reinforced the importance of creative thinking in engineering. By fostering an environment where diverse ideas were encouraged and explored, we were able to develop a solution that not only met but exceeded the project requirements. This project also enhanced my leadership and teamwork skills, as I learned to facilitate creative processes and guide my team toward successful outcomes.      Reflecting on this experience has highlighted the significance of creative thinking in my professional development as an engineer. Moving forward, I plan to continue using structured brainstorming techniques and rapid prototyping to tackle complex engineering challenges creatively and effectively.

mid.situation: In our capstone mechanical engineering project, we were tasked with designing an Internet tea maker. The main challenge was to integrate smart technology with traditional tea making, ensuring ease of use, cost-effectiveness, and environmental sustainability. The project required us to not only consider the technical design but also the consumer marketability and operational efficiency.      TASK:      As the project leader, my responsibility was to oversee the design process, ensuring that our product met all specified technical requirements while also appealing to potential users. This involved coordinating with the design, technology, and marketing teams to incorporate their inputs into a cohesive product design.      ACTION:      To tackle the project, we first conducted a needs analysis to identify key features that potential users valued most, such as temperature control, brewing time settings, and energy efficiency. We held multiple brainstorming sessions to discuss possible features and their implications on cost and manufacturability. During these sessions, we used decision matrix tools to evaluate each feature against criteria such as user demand, technical feasibility, and environmental impact. This helped us prioritize features that offered the best trade-offs between functionality, cost, and sustainability. We also consulted with external experts in smart appliance technology to ensure our design choices were viable and innovative.
mid.result: The final design of our Internet tea maker included optimal features that balanced user needs with practical engineering constraints. We incorporated a customizable brewing temperature control, which was highly requested during our market research, and used environmentally friendly materials to enhance the product’s sustainability. The prototype was well-received during initial testing, with particular praise for its user-friendly interface and energy-efficient operations. From a business perspective, the product was positioned competitively in the smart appliance market, leading to potential interest from manufacturing partners.

mid.situation: During a particularly difficult project phase, I, as a leader and engineering student, came into a circumstance where team members were blatantly lacking in empathy.
mid.task_action: Taking the initiative to deal with the matter head-on, I realised how important it was to cultivate empathy within the team. I first planned a team meeting and brought up the issue there, stressing the value of empathy in our joint endeavours while avoiding conflict. I gave examples of how empathy could have enhanced our conversations and results.      I then planned a training on activities that foster empathy by utilising tools related to team dynamics and psychology. We participated in role-playing exercises, perspective-taking exercises, and active listening exercises during the workshop. These exercises developed a more sympathetic atmosphere inside the team and attempted to deepen our comprehension of one another's viewpoints.
mid.result: As a direct result of these efforts, team relationships and collaboration have significantly improved. Team members improved their communication and problem-solving skills as a result of becoming more sensitive to the needs and perspectives of one another. Project efficiency rose as a result, and morale considerably improved. Members of the team also expressed feeling more appreciated and supported, which strengthened the team's sense of unity and togetherness. Overall, cultivating empathy not only improved our project outcomes but also enhanced the overall well-being of the team.

mid.situation: I was required to research the customer segment and product placement of the service HiPages is offering in order to produce a presentation conveying the information I have learnt
mid.task_action: Researching potential competitors to HiPages; posing as a customer to HiPages and its competitors; evaluating the effectiveness of HiPages' marketing strategy and comparing it to the marketing strategies of other competitors. All of these actions helped to find out what HiPages is doing and how it is doing it in terms of marketing.
mid.result: I was able to create and deliver a presentation that conveyed the following points in HiPages' customer segment and product placement: what the service being sold is, how it is being presented to its demographic, the service's archetypal customer and the methods in which HiPages has evolved to customer needs

mid.situation: As a third year engineering student, I am taking the course ENGG3050 as part of the courses to complete my degree. We were tasked with multiple assessments over the semester, alongside the exams and assignments of other units I am taking. I had found that after the end of year break, I had lost my ability to study and complete assessments effectively and even sometimes in a timely manner.      Task:   I decided to change this and task myself with achieving my academic goals to the fullest of my abilities. mid.situation: I am leading a cross-functional team working on a product launch. The team includes engineers, designers, marketers, and customer support representatives. The product launch is critical for the company’s growth, and there are high expectations from stakeholders.
mid.task_action: I start by defining clear objectives for the product launch. These objectives align with the company’s overall strategy and revenue goals, I communicate these objectives to the team, emphasizing their importance and impact. I also recognized the diverse skill sets within the team, and I foster collaboration. Additionally I will organize regular cross-functional meetings where team members share updates, discuss challenges, and brainstorm solutions and encourage open communication and create an environment where everyone feels heard.
mid.result: The results are that the product launches successfully, meeting stakeholder expectations, cross-functional collaboration improves, leading to better outcomes. The team also feels empowered and motivated, fostering a positive work culture. mid.situation: During my civil engineering studies, I found myself leading a team tasked with designing a hypothetical Infrastructure project. Our objective was to adhere to the Australian standards in our calculations and designs while creating this building. However, two weeks before the semester break, we encountered a serious issue. As one of our team members had altered the project contribution percentages in their favor without the team’s knowledge. This manipulation favored certain team members while unfairly reducing the contribution of others, while this issue was closed at the time another issue was brought up because of this event.      TASK: As the team leader, it was my responsibility to ensure a fair assessment of each member’s contribution. The situation required me to rectify the skewed contribution percentages transparently and ensure that each team member received due credit and resolved the eroded trust within the team that would allow us to move forward effectively.      ACTION: I took several steps to manage the situation:      	*   Mediating Trust Issues: I engaged in conversations with Member A, who expressed a loss of trust due to the tampering incident. It was essential to address their concerns and maintain open lines of communication. I emphasized the importance of trust within the team and sought ways to rebuild it or at least minimize or end the issues.      	*   Consult with the Tutor and Convenor: I initiated discussions with the tutor and convenor to explain the issue. Transparency and fairness were my primary concerns. We explored options for adjusting the contribution percentages openly in front of the entire team.      	*   Proposing Dual Submissions: To prevent further tampering and ensure fairness, I proposed a solution: dual submissions. Both Member A and I would submit the project. This approach allowed us to maintain impartiality while acknowledging the existing tensions.      	*   Seeking Advice from the Unit Convenor: I escalated the issue to the Unit Convenor, seeking guidance on managing the team dynamics. We discussed strategies for balancing fairness, trust, and collaboration.
mid.result: The outcome was a compromise that allowed for two submissions, one by myself and one by Member A, to ensure fairness. Although there was residual tension, the team was able to continue working together in class, and the project moved forward.

mid.situation: In my MECH3003  (Mechanical Design II) project, we were required to design & build a gearbox capable of lifting & lowering a heavy weight without breakage or failure, while optimising factors such as mass, cost, and volume. This complex task required accurate numerical analysis and consistent, comprehensive project report format. Initially, I faced challenges with learning and applying advanced Excel functions and report writing skills, which were crucial for staying on track with the project timelines.
mid.task_action: In optimising various performance measures for the final gearbox design, we were required to apply various numerical analysis tools such as Excel’s table formatting, value lookup functions, automatic linear interpolation functions, match, and IF functions. Additionally, I needed to develop my report writing skills, focusing on structuring the report effectively and utilising Word formatting tools such as equations, figure captions, and cross-referencing functions. To address these difficulties with Excel, I did some external LinkedIn learning, teaching me skills such as how to format tables to enhance readability and used value lookup functions to streamline data retrieval. By mastering automatic linear interpolation functions, I could easily & quickly handle given data tables and extract the relevant info. The use of IF functions also allowed implementation of ‘conditional logic’, useful in applying functions within these functions. further enhancing the robustness of my analysis. For the report writing aspect, I invested time in understanding how to structure a technical report logically. Using Word's equation editor to present formulas clearly, figure captions & cross-references as well as tables of contents, figures, tables, equations, graphs, etc. which improved the overall coherence & navigability of the report. These skills are perfect for presenting the calculations in a professional and comprehensible manner.
mid.result: The effort I put into learning & perfecting these skills paid off, successfully completing the numerical analysis enabling me to optimise the gearbox design effectively. It also equipped me with valuable skills of how I can apply this simple tool to real-world engineering problems. The excel tables were well-organised & clearly interpret, enabling simpler decision-making processes, & learning various advanced Excel functions allowed me to handle large datasets & do calculations automatically. The result of the report was my Word doc with a structured format & clear presentation of equations, figures etc. making my submission highly proffessional. The use of cross-referencing allows the markers to navigate through the report seamlessly, & overall the project and report were completed to a highly satisfactory standard. These transferable skills I developed in numerical analysis and report writing not only contributed to the success of this project but also equipped me with valuable tools for future engineering tasks.

mid.situation: This first semester of 2024 has included several group projects from many different classes, which require teamwork and effective communication to ensure a high-quality submission is presented on time. Often, it takes one student to take initiative and assign roles and divide the workload amongst the group.

This role I had taken as the "team leader" required a multitude of skills including not only soft skills such as effectively communicating with my group but also tested my technical knowledge of the project as I was being asked to help with solve problems as required. It was a great test and I enjoyed the role.
mid.result: This experience taught me a lot about being a team leader and made me appreciate the importance of being organized and managing a team. In the end, my group projects were all submitted on time and our group was proud of the efforts we had put into the project. I'm now looking forward to the next opportunity to work on a group project and to embrace being a team leader once again.

mid.situation: Recently, I was appointed as the team leader of a unit I am doing this semester. Our team was assigned to design a controller for a DC motor.      TASK: Our task was to design a controller for a DC Motor that would be part of an elevator. It was our duty to make the elevator system comfortable for customers and keep it within a certain set of preset requirements. Our team consisted of four mechatronics engineering students, including me, and I was appointed as the leader by our unit convenor. As the leader, I was in charge of task delegation and making sure everyone understood their allocated sections. It was also my duty to make sure all work done by the team was documented properly to later be compiled into a project report for submission.      ACTION: Our convenors conducted a session on week 8 for us to get introduced to our teammates and collect kits for simulation. I proactively made sure we all got to know each other well. We talked about topics we found difficult and topics that we found easy. This helped me to make a plan for how I would divide the tasks up. This is because I wanted a team member who had a really good understanding of a concept to work on that concept. This ensures that all tasks are completed to a higher level of perfection and no one is assigned to something they cannot achieve. Starting week 9, everyone proactively started working on their assigned sections. As the team leader, I realised that the amount of time we had would not be enough so I arranged an extra session by taking into account everyone's free times. This session was used to work on the Discretisation of input signals. Being the team leader, I also had to make sure which day worked for everyone for the final presentation. a challenge we faced was not being able to get rid of noise. To fixed that I arranged a group brainstorming session with my teammates and using ideas from that session came up with a solution.
mid.result: After five weeks of tireless working, our team was able to submit a completely working controller. Our convenor praised us for a job well done and we were able to learn how to utilise our theoretical knowledge into practical applications.

mid.situation: The advancement of technology in many areas and industries has been rapid. A few of these advancements are around the use of artificial intelligence and others are automation in environments such as warehouses. The implications of this technology advancement are the replacement of jobs particularly those that consist of many repetitive actions/tasks. In warehouse that are being newly built and old warehouses being retrofitted are automated pick-packing solutions. This means that hundreds of employees that worked in this places don't have a job anymore.
mid.task_action: The actions that are to be taken include for those who have lost their job to automation need to find a way that they can gain employment. Another action is using AI ethically so as not to breach privacy and expose children to explicit content as an example. Both these tasks can be challenging however whilst less man power is required in the day to day operations of a warehouse humans still play an important role at some point along the process and it is important that people keep that in mind. Lastly, as engineers it is important to understand how all parties can be affected by technology as it advances. This creates a third task around understanding impacts to businesses, employees and wider society.
mid.result: The technology advances listed here such as warehouse automation, and AI provide many benefits to most parties. However, some parties are certainly negatively affected, this is typically those who have a job which is repetitive and vulnerable to being completed by automation or AI. The benefits of the technology include increased efficiency, and lower consumer prices. The disadvantages are employment, privacy, and security challenges.      By automating a warehouse the labour costs of distributing and supplying a product are severely reduced. Due to this the consumers of these products can receive a lower price will the business still maintains the same profit margin. Both automation and AI can significantly improve efficiency in many aspects whether that be professional or admin task around the house. Warehouse automation provides a solution which has a lower risk of error when completing tasks that are repetitive and they can also complete these tasks much faster and for a longer period of time than an employee. This increases efficiency.      The disadvantages are the replacement of employees with either automation or AI and this can leave much of the population jobless. It is important to ensure that there is teaching or training that can be provided to these people so that they can find other industries or departments which requires work to be completed. The next challenge is privacy and security. Already, it has become challenging to determine what is factual and what has been created by AI and is incorrect. This is a significant challenge which needs to be addressed. AI is very powerful and has room to become much more powerful and it is important that this is kept in check and AI does not get abused.

mid.task_action: The primary task was to deliver a well-researched and coherent presentation that showcased a deep understanding of Stake Investing's management structure and company culture. mid.situation: I used to work as a part time cashier at a fast food restaurant (KFC). One day during the peak lunch hour rush, I got a customer complaining that their order was missing something important. There had been an increase in customer complaints about order accuracy lately.      Task : My role was to make the dining experience better for customers by reducing customer frustration and improving cash register accuracy/ order accuracy.      Action: I was working with another cashier at the front. I observed the ordering process and discussed this issue with the other cashier. We came up with a few possible causes, such as background noise interfering with order clarity, menu item ambiguity (“with” or “without” gravy), and cashiers in a rush due to higher order volume.       1. Communication : I worked on active listening by requesting customers to repeat their orders, particularly when they were complex or tailored.      Cooperation and teamwork: I suggested a “double-check” system where a second cashier confirms complex orders before they are completed.       Customer focus: I provided a solution (change or refund) as soon as possible and apologized to the customer for the wrong order.
mid.result: This lead into reduced errors from our side meaning orders were delivered with accuracy, and this resulted in higher customer satisfaction rate. By asking the customer to repeat their orders for complex order it gave us the staff more drive to put the order out as quickly as possible as well as efficient as we knew exactly what the customer wanted.

mid.situation: During my mechanical engineering studies, I was elected as the team leader for a capstone project aimed at improving elderly care using AI. This project required us to design and implement a system that used cameras to monitor elderly individuals and detect falls through vision-based detection. The challenge was not only technical but also involved managing a diverse team of peers, each with different skills and schedules.      TASK: As the team leader, my primary task was to guide the project towards its goal while ensuring that all team members were effectively contributing and collaborating. It was crucial to maintain a harmonious team environment and keep the project on schedule.      ACTION: I started by organizing weekly meetings to track progress and address any issues. To ensure that everyone was on the same page, I created a shared document where team members could update their progress and list any challenges they faced. I also arranged for two senior members to mentor the less experienced team members, providing them with guidance on using the necessary tools and algorithms like YOLOv8, CNNs, and LSTMs. Furthermore, I maintained an open line of communication with our faculty advisor to seek advice and ensure that the project aligned with academic standards and expectations.
mid.result: Our project was successful in creating a prototype that could accurately detect falls in real time. The system performed well during the testing phases, showing a high accuracy rate, which was a significant achievement given the complexity of real-time video data processing. The project not only fulfilled its academic requirements but also won recognition in our department for its innovative approach to elderly care. My role as a team leader helped me develop strong leadership and project management skills, which were acknowledged by both my peers and professors.

mid.situation: During a critical project phase, our cross-functional team faced a significant roadblock. We were developing a complex software application for a client, and the deadline was rapidly approaching. The team consisted of engineers, designers, and quality assurance specialists. The project involved integrating multiple APIs, and we encountered unexpected compatibility issues.
mid.task_action: * IDENTIFYING THE PROBLEM: As the team leader, I convened an emergency meeting to assess the situation. We discussed the challenges, and it became clear that the APIs were not playing well together due to version mismatches and conflicting data formats.   	* BRAINSTORMING SOLUTIONS: I encouraged open communication and active participation from all team members. We used a whiteboard to list potential solutions. Each team member contributed ideas, ranging from rewriting parts of the code to finding alternative APIs.   	* PRIORITIZING OPTIONS: We evaluated each solution based on criteria such as feasibility, impact on the project timeline, and long-term maintainability. We ranked the options in order of importance.   	* COLLABORATION AND DECISION-MAKING: Instead of imposing my own solution, I facilitated a discussion. We debated the pros and cons of each approach. Ultimately, we decided to refactor the code to accommodate the APIs better while maintaining backward compatibility.   	* TASK ALLOCATION: I divided the tasks among team members. Some focused on refactoring, others on testing, and a few on documentation. Clear responsibilities were assigned, and deadlines were set.
mid.result: * SUCCESSFUL INTEGRATION: By working collaboratively, we successfully resolved the API compatibility issues. The software now seamlessly communicated with external services.   	* IMPROVED TEAM MORALE: The open dialogue and shared decision-making boosted team morale. Everyone felt heard and valued.   	* ON-TIME DELIVERY: Despite the setback, we met the project deadline. Our client was impressed with the quality of the solution.   	* KNOWLEDGE SHARING: The experience led to knowledge sharing within the team. We documented the lessons learned and established best practices for future projects.

mid.situation: I was working as at Spruson and Ferguson with my client on the patent for a new medical imaging algorithm that would revolutionise the medical industry. Unfortunately, a competitor had also developed a similar software soon after we published ours, raising concerns that they may have potentially infringed on our patent.      Task: My task was to make protect my clients patent by making sure that the competitor’s software did not infringe any intellectual property or patent laws.      Action: I did this by conducting a thorough patent review and analysis. I went through a comprehensive review of what our patent covered, then I used the knowledge I had from being a 3rd year engineering student to understand what parts of our software was covered by our patents.      I then analysed our competitor’s software and found what parts of it infringed on our patents. I then consulted other patent attorneys at my law firm to figure out whether we had a potential case, which we did.      I then filed a cease-and-desist letter to our competitor, telling them the specific parts of their software which infringed our patents.      We then prepared for a potential lawsuit.
mid.result: We were prepared for a lawsuit but fortunately the competitor backed down. We also found other areas that could be patented which allowed us to better protect our medical imaging software. The removal of our competitor’s software gave my company the edge in the medical imaging market, allowing us to gain a solid lead over our competitors. We will continue looking for other potential breaches but there have been few since.

mid.situation:
Describe the situation (location people context) 

A habit is regular tendency which is difficult to give up and can be positive or negative. My situation in relation to my topic of habits is essential to reflect on how effective my habits are in regards to improving my professional and personal achievements in regards to my current education, future careers and personal health.Throughout the past 11 weeks I have been improving my better habits and trying to eliminate my negative have through a personal challenge known as 75 hard in which it is positioned to build mental toughness while also introduce own habits to improve habits in regards education. These habits formed in the past weeks is to gain gratification and to increase personal and professional development.

Task: Explain what your task or role was. What  responsibility did you have? Who gave you the task? What was your relationship with this person?

75 hard is a transformational mental toughness program in which it requires multiple habits created improve discipline and resilience in the mind to help improve self esteem and confidence in its main goal is self evaluation and self improvement. This put me with the responsibility of following with the challenges, of 45 minutes of a workout inside and another outside, strict diet, read 10 pages of a book daily, and drink 4L of water all completed every day for 75 days. The task was brought up by my cousin but the challenge was given myself ready to complete my self improvement plans to create good habits and eliminating bad habits. Along with this challenge it asks you to add your own goals, which I implemented organisation in regards to my university study of starting assessments early to improve my organisation to create a successful habit.

Action: What did you do? What methods, techniques or principles did you use? And most importantly, which competence did you rely on?

The key method to ensure my habits were intact and tried to eliminate my negative habits was to use a journal to write down what happens through my day and used it as a mental check list this helped me to vent what i did well, what i did incorrectly and how to improve my mistakes through using mental techniques such as meditation and resting at the right time, to ensure i had the right habits and removed the incorrect habits apart of my lifestyle.which competence did you rely on?
mid.result:
What was result

Throughout the challenge faced many challenges of giving up and heading back to negative habits but through perseverance and keeping my mindset straight I successfully empowered my mental habits in regards to personal health while also improving my own self driven goals to study extra throughout the day, use organisation tools for my university work. I developed habits effective to my growth while eliminating negative habits.

Was it Expected or unexpected.

Putting myself through this challenge created and ensuring i completed the challenge helped me expect the positives of creating these habits e.g organised uni work, and improving personal health. What was Unexpected was my ability to keep on going for the 75 days, as the expected outcome was known  but the unexpected was can I keep this up for this long can control my mind and build mental toughness to ensure these habits will stick with me even past the challenge.

What feedback did you get

Effective feedback from friends and family was to ensure i keep my body safe and not cause any damage to my health by communicating to me that i required lots of sleep and eat foods that provide the right nutrients while also ensuring I recognised helpful feed back to ensure i did right exercises and didn't hurt myself which helped my personal health. 

How were the results welcomed?

My results were accepted and accepted by all and a lot of people applauded me for this challenge but the results were very positive as the habits i have implemented have stick with me and i can still continue my self discipline habits.

Describe the before and after situation

Before the challenge i was very distracted and a lot of my habits were negative in regards to improve my mental strength, but due to the introduction of the challenge to myself i have improved my discipline to get to work and focus on personal health and university study to work hard and improve my mental strength.

mid.situation: Since this unit is mainly about teamwork, we need all members to be available at all time during the meeting. Furthermore, we need to communicate each other to create our own unique RGM event.
mid.task_action: There will be no guarantee that all member will be free when most of member have their own private life. So, in order to fix it, we have come up with the time schedule and let all members to fill in their free time. This will help us to indicate who is available at what time and date.
mid.result: This help us to have access and communicate with each members without difficult about the unit and RGM event. This also to prevent the confusion of meeting time. Thus, we able to communicate and create our own RGM events quickly before the deadlines

mid.situation:
We needed to find an appropriate sequence for our RGM machine, so that our presentation flows well and so every team member has a 3 second action. Basically, we have to make sure our project meets the requirements in the marking criteria while also ensuring that our plan was feasible and that we had the necessary equipment.

Task/ Action: We started off by deciding on a Soda machine as the theme for our RGM, as well as choosing  the materials available in our households such as cardboard, Jenga blocks, Hotwheel cars and tracks to create our RGM machine. The Soda machine's goal is to pour a can of Soda in a cup via a highly complex succession of events. Our group had six people so we made sure that our final video will contain a 3 second action for each team member, the more team members the harder it is, because we had to come up with more events. My role in the presentation is to push one Jenga block which will trigger a domino effect on the other Jenga blocks behind it making them in succession, which is the first action of the video. The rest presentation involves the movement of a a ball, toilet paper, a Hotwheel car and a seesaw and a toy train which will knock over a can of coke off the table causing it to pour in a cup.
mid.result: We made a successful presentation on Miro as we were well prepared and had all our ideas planned out, so we were able to comprehensively present them. On Miro we created a flow chart to display the flow of each act to the next in our planned video. Moreover, we were able to make nice illustrations to show in our presentation to further explain how our video will work.

mid.situation: Our team needed to form an effectient way to maintain equal portions of workload amongst our team members for whenever we are assigned new tasks relating to our 'Rube Goldberg Machine'. Not only an equal portion of work but, equal effort.
mid.task_action: After discussing various options we first came to an agreement regarding roles team roles. We decided on a team role rotation with a specific order to stick by so everybody could try out each role so nobody had to take the more exhausting tasks everytime, (such as minute taker and leader) whilst others got away with more relaxed roles each time. Alongside this, we agreeded dividing the tasks so that we work on our respective zones seperately whilst checking if the compnents used and the ideas brainstormed worked properly with the adjacent sections in the RGM. By splitting up the work like this, each piece of work labelled under there name so the amount of work each member contributes is visible to the whole team.
mid.result: Work done outside the meeting individually was up to standard for each person from then on, and as all work could be viewed live on google docs or slides, the standard to be met was able to be set passively by the person who contributed the more, encouraging each other to add more detail to their sections. The team rotations worked strong for a bit. Additionally, things not related to our own ideas and the RGM's design and theme such as risk assessments and presentation slides were collectively done with everybody self-assigning contributions were everybody worked at thier own pace.,

mid.situation: I needed fix some time with my group memeber for the development of a presenation regarding our group's Conceptual design
mid.task_action: After the team had developed a brain storm of the Conceptual design, I was suppose to manage some time with another group member to work on the presentation. We eventually came together and worked on the development of the presentation. We first discussed the formatting of the presentation and then discussed the content and aesthetics.
mid.result: The result was a 10 slide powerpoint highlighting the conceptual design of our RGM

mid.situation: In the ENGG1050 unit, we've been tasked to design a Rube Goldberg Machine and to present our final video as a presentation (or any form of media) to the rest of the other groups.

mid.situation:
One of my group members was suppose to present our Reliability Table on the day of presenting. Our whole team thought he was going to be ready to present but when our tutor asked for groups to start presenting he was not present nor was he responding to our messages on the team group chat.

Action:

While everyone in our group was uncertain on how we are going to present. I took initiative and made the decision that I will present our reliability table for our team. I was very uncertain with majority of the content within our table as I had not prepared in advanced due to it not being my task. But I was willing to make that decision so we do not lose marks.
mid.result: The result was our reliability table being presented on time and in a professional manner without our audience suspecting the uncertain mixup. Because of my willingness to take initiative in the last minute audience didn't know we had a mixup, our presentation appears to withhold a  prepared and professional appearance.

mid.situation: One team member went away for work and hadn't completed their tasks or communicated with the team.

mid.result: This created tension in the group as we all took on more workload. But through useful communication and displaying of the deadlines the team was able to look towards the common goal of completing the RGM project.

mid.situation:
CREATE A PRESENTATION OF A DETAILED DESIGN OF OUR MACHINE TO PRESENT IN WEEK 7.

Action:

In this unit of ENGG1050, we are required to make a presentation in week 7 to demonstrate a detailed analysis of what we have come up with as our machine. Our team decided we needed two weeks to prepare as this presentation would be a substantial portion of our mark. We used miro to create diagrams of our modules and google slides to present our design. Due to this large volume of tasks that need to be completed to compile our final presentation, it would take a considerable amount of time. We decided to create 6 meetings in total across week 5 and week 6. We all made it clear to attend these meetings as they had been created at a time that was achievable for all of us.
mid.result: The first group meeting was online which was done online via messenger, and was attended by everyone in the group. The second meeting was created in person, which a few people were unable to attend and let us know via the group chat, prior to the meeting. The third and fourth meeting was online via messenger, with everyone present. The fifth meeting was created as the longest meeting as it was when everything would be compiled together and we all needed to input our opinions. All team members said they would be able to attend prior to the meeting. However, just as the meeting began, a member of our group remembered they had some unfinished work that they needed to complete. We were understanding as a group and told him to check back in with us when he was finished. We finished the presentation without him.

mid.situation: We realized that our presentation slides were too cluttered with text and our graphs were unclear, making it hard for the audience to understand our design.      TASK: We needed to make our presentation clearer and more concise to effectively communicate our design.      ACTION:      	* REDUCING TEXT: We cut down the text on each slide, using bullet points and more visuals.   	* IMPROVING GRAPHS: We redid our graphs to be clearer, using 3-D drawings and breaking complex graphs into simpler steps.   	* VISUALIZING ACTIONS: We created detailed illustrations and animations to show each component and its role.   	* PRACTICING: We practiced our presentation multiple times, learning from each rehearsal to improve our delivery.
mid.result: * BETTER CLARITY: The presentation was clearer and more engaging, making it easier for the audience to follow.   	* EFFECTIVE COMMUNICATION: The illustrations and animations helped explain the design's functionality and sequence.   	* POSITIVE FEEDBACK: Our peers and mentors praised the improved presentation.   	* INCREASED CONFIDENCE: Practicing boosted our confidence, leading to a smooth final presentation.      These actions made our presentation more professional and easier to understand.

mid.situation: We were tasked with making a presentation for our RGM project. Our team consisted of individuals with diverse backgrounds and countries. The goal was to create a unique, sustainable and culturally varied RGM machine.
mid.task_action: Each team member was assigned specific roles and tasks on team meeting. I was responsible for creating the major presentation slides, though some of my team members assisted me in editing them. Moreover, I delivered the main portion of the presentation to the tutors. Rest of my team members presented their own portion of the presentation to the tutors. To ensure accountability within the team, we established clear deadlines for each tasks and conducted frequent check-ins via our messenger group . When difficulties came, we continued to work together and communicate openly, and we addressed them appropriately.
mid.result: As a result of our collective accountability, we successfully presented progress of our RGM. Overall, the experience highlighted the value of individual accountability in accomplishing team goals.

mid.situation: During one of our ENGG1050 projects, our team was tasked with creating a Rube Goldberg Machine using creative ideas and proper implementation. The goal was to solve an engineering problem and use the engineering design process to outline engineering constraints.
mid.task_action: To tackle this challenge, we started with a creative brainstorming session where every team member was encouraged to propose bold and innovative ideas without worrying about feasibility initially. After generating many ideas, we used a mind map to categorize and connect them, identifying potential hybrid solutions that combined the best elements of multiple concepts.      One of the standout ideas was one that I proposed. My idea involved a ball propelled by a hair dryer ascending a ramp to reach a table, then descending another ramp to strike an obstacle that triggered a chain reaction of balls. This created a visually captivating and engaging step in our machine. I meticulously optimized the process by utilizing rubber bands, curved ramps, and lightweight strings. Initially, the ball would bounce off, and the rubber bands were unable to secure it effectively. I spent considerable time experimenting with different materials and configurations to ensure the ball followed the desired path smoothly.      Each team member contributed their own unique events to the machine. I coordinated with others to integrate my segment seamlessly into the overall design, ensuring that each part of the machine transitioned smoothly into the next.
mid.result: Our creative approach and the clear communication of our ideas received significant praise during the project presentation. The tutors appreciated the innovative aspects of our project and provided constructive feedback on our shortcomings. My idea was particularly highlighted for its creativity and the meticulous effort in optimizing the mechanism. This project allowed us to showcase our ability to think creatively and communicate possibilities effectively, and it demonstrated our competence in using the engineering design process to address complex problems.

mid.situation: In High School for one of my subjects I had to do a workplace interview along with many other students in order to get into the actual workplace and gain experience and a set amount of hours for the workplace in order to pass the subject and the HSC requirements.
mid.task_action: The interview process was quite ruthless as they told us that only about 1-5 students out of 50 that were enrolled in the specific company's workplace would actually make it to gain experience and hours. The others who didn't make it would be enrolled in another workplace environment. Knowing this I prepared a plan to give myself the best opportunity to get into the workplace. The plan was to be as professional as possible and to show professional behaviour such as communicating effectively, dressing up responsibly, helping, supporting, giving new ideas, being honest, acting with integrity and being kind. On the day of the interview I was prepared as I knew what to do and what to say and more importantly to be professional in my manners, looks and behaviours.
mid.result: The result was that I completed the Interview and a few weeks later I was selected for the work placement and began my experience and hours. It was also a very smooth and easy interview as being and acting more professional helped to communicate more easily with the interviewer.

mid.situation: I worked at a little fast-food business as a manager. We were ready to launch a new menu item, but we didn't have much money for marketing, so we needed to generate a lot of excitement first. Conventional marketing approaches proved too costly or ineffective for our intended audience. I needed to approach the situation differently to make a good impression on the audience.
mid.task_action: I had to devise a low-budget marketing strategy that would engage our target demographic and spark interest in our recently introduced product. The goal was to keep within our limited budget and garner as much attention and awareness as we could. I convened a team gathering to generate innovative concepts that could be executed within a restricted budget. Secondly, we tried to get customers to create original content on social media as part of a competition we were doing to promote our product. I reached out to several prominent bloggers and influencers, asking them to review our product and spread the word. During the first month of the debut, we tried to give discounts on the item to attract clients. To generate excitement and interest in our product, we also employed marketing techniques like organising pop-up events and flash mobs in crowded areas.
mid.result: We had a large number of participants in our content-creation campaign, which resulted in hundreds of unique social media posts, significantly expanding our online presence. Because of the influencers' and bloggers' promotion, our restaurants experienced a notable increase in foot traffic. Both significant local buzz and media interest were produced by the marketing campaigns. We were getting more orders than we anticipated because of the ongoing offer. Together, these creative strategies produced a very successful product launch in the end. In the weeks after its debut, our campaign generated attention and sales due to the increased visibility it generated; ultimately, we exceeded our initial sales targets by about 25%.

mid.situation: I HAVE BEEN EMPLOYED AT MCDONALD'S, WORKING ALONGSIDE MY CLOSE SCHOOL FRIENDS FOR OVER A YEAR. DESPITE OUR FRIENDSHIP, I MAINTAINED A PROFESSIONAL RELATIONSHIP WITH THEM.
mid.task_action: To maintain professionalism, we ensured that once we clocked in, we focused on our duties. We prioritised serving customers efficiently, fulfilling orders, and keeping the store clean.
mid.result: This approach demonstrated our strong work ethic and maturity to our supervisors, leading to opportunities for more senior positions within the store.

mid.situation: We need to test and record reliability data for our RGM project, then make changes to improve reliability.
