The RGM failed 9 times out of 10. I concluded that this board game was not a good fit for the RGM when it was so inconsistent and so I decided to use my alternative design that I had come up with previously in the event I was unable to make it work.
mid.result: Removing the roller coaster made my section of the RGM work 9 out of 10 times which was a number that I felt confident continuing with.

mid.situation: We came across a challenging difficulty on a team project, several answers were put out, but none of them appeared to go to the root of the problem. It took critical thinking to examine and comprehend the root causes of the issue.
mid.task_action: Finding the source of the issue and creating a thorough plan to deal with it head-on were the tasks at hand. This required employing critical thinking abilities to produce creative ideas, working with team members, and evaluating data. I started by gathering pertinent data and problem-related information. This involved talking with team members, going over project documentation, and getting input from the team. I took the lead in helping the team critically assess each of the many ideas that were put up. We evaluated the viability, efficacy, and possible hazards of putting each proposal into practice. I kept the lines of communication open with my teammates the whole time, asking for their opinions and suggestions at every turn. To make sure that everyone understood their part in putting the idea into practice and felt involved in it, collaboration was essential.
mid.result: Our ability to work together and apply critical thinking skills allowed us to pinpoint the issue's primary source and create a thorough plan of action that successfully dealt with it. Tutors were positively responsive to our approach, which led to quantifiable enhancements in project results. The event also improved group cohesion and encouraged an innovative and critical thinking culture.

mid.situation: For our Week 13 presentation, we needed to ensure that all team members contributed equally and that the final presentation and video was visually cohesive. One team member stopped collaborating on the project early on and didn't communicate, which put our project development at risk.
mid.task_action: During our team meeting we discussed the problem and divided up the missing member's responsibilities among us. Through our shared documents, group chat, and Miro board, we made sure that everyone was always in contact and up to date with where the group was at. This allowed us to keep track of everyone's progress and quickly resolve any problems. Later, through consistent communication we got a hold of the team member and could successfully complete our RGM video and presentation. In general, when someone is absent from class and holds a position of presenter or minute taker someone else is always there to stand up and take on the role. For example, one team member was late for our Week 7 presentation and a team member quickly adjusted their slides to suit them and was ready to present their part. Our RGM video utilised communication and teamwork thoroughly to ensure visual consistency. For example, one team members filmed their video in portrait, so we asked them to re-film it in landscape as everyone else did to ensure a cohesive final product. This collaborative effort demonstrated our commitment to maintaining high standards and working effectively as a team.
mid.result: The result was a successful and cohesive presentation in Week 7 and for our upcoming Week 13 presentation as well. By redistributing tasks, we managed to maintain our project timeline and deliver a comprehensive presentation. Our ability to adapt and communicate effectively ensured that the final product was polished and met all requirements. The structured approach to our meetings and documentation promoted a productive working environment, allowing us to complete our reflections efficiently on a shared Google Document, where we could provide feedback and edit each other's contributions. Therefore, our project demonstrated that effective communication and teamwork are essential for overcoming challenges, ensuring consistency, and achieving successful outcomes.

mid.situation: At the end if each weeks workshop there is a presentation to deliver about our projects progress.
mid.task_action: Organise a system where a new team member would deliver the presentation each week.
mid.result: Each member of the team would present multiple times throughout the semester, which gave each member a chance to practice and improve their delivery method for presentations, and grow their confidence in public speaking.

mid.situation: I found myself often managing a lot of different projects and duties in a fast-paced work atmosphere, that left me feeling overwhelmed and unproductive. Even though I knew what my primary objectives were, I found it difficult to stay focused and manage my time and resources well.
mid.task_action: I used a systematic approach to concentrate and prioritization in order to overcome this difficulty. I started by carefully reviewing my tasks and workload, highlighting important projects and duties that needed to be completed right away. I prioritized and sorted these jobs using tools like project management software and to-do lists. Next, I divided each task into smaller, more manageable subtasks and gave each one specific target and a deadline. This gave me the opportunity to plan out my work and make sure I continued on the right path and advanced steadily in the direction of my objectives. I also worked on time-blocking, which is setting up time limits to concentrate only on important things without interruptions.
mid.result: These steps resulted in a noticeable rise in effectiveness and production. Prioritizing work according to its significance and importance helped me take on important projects more quickly and effectively fulfil deadlines. In addition, time-blocking allowed me to focus attentively on one activity at a time, which decreased delaying and improved my capacity for prolonged concentration on challenging tasks. I felt more satisfied and accomplished at work as a result, and my stress levels also reduced. My ability to manage my time properly has also helped me to make time for self-care and personal growth, which has improved my overall work-life balance. All things considered, putting a prioritization and focus plan into practice boosted my work habits and advanced my career.

mid.situation: In my MECH2003 Mechanical Design 1 course, we were assigned to use CREO (CAD software) to create a detailed model of an object we are passionate about. Given my enthusiasm for motorcycles, I decided to model a motorcycle brake caliper, a critical component that ensures safety and performance.      TASK:   My task was to accurately reproduce the brake caliper in CREO, using the practical CAD skills I had gained throughout the course. This involved taking precise measurements, creating a scaled model, and ensuring that all critical features were accurately represented.      ACTION:   To begin, I took detailed measurements of the brake caliper using calipers and a micrometer to ensure precision. I then started the modeling process in CREO, breaking down the caliper into its fundamental components such as the housing, pistons, and mounting brackets. I applied various CREO features and tools, including extrude, revolve, and pattern, to construct each part. Throughout the process, I continually referenced my measurements to maintain accuracy. I also utilized assembly features to piece together the different components, ensuring they fit together correctly as they would in the real-world object.      During this project, I encountered several challenges, such as modeling complex geometries and ensuring that the assembly constraints were correctly applied. To overcome these, I revisited my course materials, consulted online resources, and sought advice from my tutor. I also iterated on my design, making adjustments and improvements based on feedback and further measurements.
mid.result: The end result was a highly accurate and detailed model of the motorcycle brake caliper, fully assembled and to scale. This project not only reinforced my CAD skills but also deepened my understanding of mechanical design principles. My passion for motorcycles drove me to put in the extra effort, resulting in a model that I was proud of and that received positive feedback from my instructor. This experience highlighted the importance of creative thinking and perseverance in tackling complex design tasks and demonstrated how passion can fuel the learning process and lead to high quality work.

mid.situation: The topic for reflection chosen was experienced during the first moments of my experience in ENGG2050 specifically in the design process of my IOT device, the topic is ‘the design process phasing and approaches’. The daunting situation which I experienced was my own personal inability to form effective design approaches in the engineering task provided, ideas that I thought about pitching towards my fellow student engineers gave me personal doubt in my abilities as I truly believed they were subpar due to my minimal understanding in the other individuals disciplines. This situation would eventually lead to a variety of developments in this design process, both individually and in the project itself.      Task:      The task given to us at the beginning of the unit was to form questions which would be effective in asking our potential clients on the internet of tea device. The discomforting part of this was the clear difference that I as a software engineering student had as I sat next to students excelling in their own majors, ranging from the other various engineering disciplines. This barrier to connect with the other students was a major hinderance to me and impacted my ability to come up with these design process questions and approaches. I had a fear of seeming like I wasn’t contributing when I was desperately attempting to brainstorm independently.      Action:       As time passed and I realized that this was not good for the project or my own well-being I eventually spoke up and attempted to bridge the gap between myself and the other students by asking questions I was confident I knew the answer to, upon doing so and establishing communication with the others the design process suddenly became a lot simpler and overall, much more engaging. The incoming input from the other students in the group environment enabled me to develop new aspects for the internet of tea device that I could implement into my own questions for the client and was an action which I do not regret in the slightest.
mid.result: The result of connecting with these other students made me realize that engineering and the design process was a team effort and should not be done as an individual endeavour, this connection allowed me to form some engaging questions which could be asked towards our client and target audience to gather interesting aspects about their requirements of the IOT device, overall this event was one which was uncomfortable to experience at first but has truly shifted my outlook on the engineering process and any approaches I may take to accomplish tasks in the future.

mid.situation: At an afterschool care facility I work at, I was tasked with creating an emergency evacuation plan to ensure the safety of all children and staff in the event of a natural disaster. This situation was critical as it required immediate and precise action to address potential risks and ensure everyone knew the evacuation procedures. The importance of this task was underscored by the lessons learned from Hurricane Katrina, where lack of preparedness led to catastrophic consequences.             Task:      The main challenge was to develop a comprehensive evacuation plan that accounted for all possible scenarios and ensured swift and safe evacuation of all staff and kids. This task involved identifying potential hazards, determining evacuation routes, and communicating the plan effectively to both staff and parents.             Action:      To tackle this task, I utilised prioritisation and focus. I started by conducting a thorough risk assessment of the facility, identifying the most significant hazards and determining the safest evacuation routes. I organised training sessions for the staff to ensure everyone was familiar with the plan and their specific roles during an emergency. Additionally, I conducted evacuation drills to test the plan's effectiveness and make necessary adjustments. My focus was on clear communication and efficient coordination among all stakeholders.
mid.result: The evacuation plan was successfully implemented and tested through drills, significantly improving the facility's preparedness for emergencies. Staff members felt more confident in their roles, and parents were reassured by the thoroughness of the plan. The focused and prioritised approach ensured that critical safety measures were addressed first, creating a safer environment for the children and staff.      DEAL framework:      Describe:      Prioritisation and focus were crucial skills in developing an effective emergency evacuation plan for the afterschool care facility. These skills ensured that the most critical aspects of the plan were addressed first, leading to a more organised and efficient emergency response.             Examine:      Applying these skills, I recognised my strengths in analytical thinking, clear communication, and leadership. However, challenges included balancing thoroughness with the need for swift action and ensuring all staff were equally informed and prepared. Emotionally, the experience was demanding, given the responsibility for the safety of many children and staff.             Act:      To further enhance these skills, I can work on delegating tasks more effectively and developing more detailed emergency plans. Before this experience, I might have struggled with maintaining focus under pressure. Now, I understand the importance of clear priorities and streamlined communication in managing complex situations.             Learn:      This experience taught me the critical impact of prioritisation and focus in emergency planning. These skills have positively influenced my professional approach, enabling me to handle high pressure situations more effectively. I will continue to improve these skills in future situations, ensuring that I can make strategic decisions quickly and efficiently, regardless of the challenges.

mid.situation: During this engineering course, I was tasked with completing a challenging case study assignment that required a comprehensive analysis of a real-world engineering problem. This assignment was vital for my success in this unit, as it represented a significant portion of our final grade. However, I struggled to manage my time effectively due to other academic and personal commitments, which ultimately led to me delaying my work on the project.      TASK:   The task involved analysing a specific engineering situation and implementing the engineering strategies I had learned in class. This included discussing various critical aspects such as ethics, safe engineering practices, the importance of thorough testing, and detailed planning. Furthermore, the assignment was not trivial; it demanded a deep understanding of complex engineering principles and their application in real-world scenarios. The goal was to produce a comprehensive report that demonstrated my ability to integrate these principles and articulate their importance clearly and effectively.      ACTION:   Initially, I underestimated the amount of work required and delayed starting the assignment, thinking I could catch up later. When the deadline approached, I found myself overwhelmed, struggling to compile my thoughts and research into a coherent report. Realizing the gravity of the situation, I took full responsibility for my lapse in time management. In turn, I dedicated several late nights to completing the assignment, pushing myself to meet the deadline. Despite my efforts, the rushed nature of my work led to a report that lacked depth and cohesion. Although I had attempted to rectify the time I wasted by not working on the assignment, I ultimately had to submit sub-par responses to the questions.
mid.result: The outcome was a disappointing grade that reflected the disjointed and incomplete nature of my report. This experience taught me a valuable lesson in accountability, by not managing my time effectively and failing to start the assignment early, I compromised the quality of my work. Despite my last-minute efforts, the quality of my analysis and discussion suffered.      From this experience, I understood that accountability is crucial in professional settings, particularly when working independently on complex tasks. It is essential to take responsibility for one's tasks, manage time effectively, and seek help or feedback when needed. This skill is vital as it ensures that projects are completed efficiently and to a high standard, and I will be sure to capitalise on the time I'm given on future projects.

mid.situation: DURING MY FIRST YEAR AT UNIVERSITY, I WAS ASSIGNED TO A GROUP PROJECT IN MY SOFTWARE ENGINEERING COURSE. OUR TEAM CONSISTED OF SIX MEMBERS. FROM THE START, THREE MEMBERS WERE FREQUENTLY ABSENT FROM MEETINGS AND FAILED TO CONTRIBUTE TO THE PROJECT. THE REMAINING THREE OF US HAD DIFFERING IDEAS ON HOW TO APPROACH THE PROJECT, LEADING TO FREQUENT CONFLICTS AND A LACK OF PROGRESS. FOR INSTANCE, ONE MEMBER WANTED TO FOCUS ON BACK-END DEVELOPMENT WHILE ANOTHER PREFERRED FRONT-END TASKS, WHICH LED TO DISAGREEMENTS ON PRIORITIZING OUR EFFORTS.      Task:      As the project deadline approached, it became clear that we needed to resolve these conflicts and find a way to work together effectively. My task was to mediate between the differing viewpoints, ensure everyone’s ideas were heard, and come up with a compromise that would allow us to complete the project on time. This involved not only addressing absenteeism but also finding a unified approach to our project.      Action:      I initiated a meeting with the present team members and laid out the urgency of the situation. I suggested we follow a structured approach to resolve our conflicts:      	*   Identify Key Issues: I facilitated a session where each member listed their main concerns and ideas for the project. This was documented in a shared online document. We found that our main disagreements were on task prioritization and workload distribution.      	*   Prioritize and Compromise: We collectively prioritized the tasks that were most crucial for project completion, such as developing the core functionalities first. I encouraged each member to be open to compromise, emphasizing the importance of collaboration. For instance, we agreed to split the project into smaller, manageable parts that addressed both front-end and back-end development simultaneously.      	*   Set Clear Roles and Responsibilities: Based on each member’s strengths and preferences, we divided the tasks accordingly. I assigned myself to oversee the integration of all parts, ensuring consistency. We established a schedule for regular check-ins to monitor progress and address any issues promptly.      	*   Communication: To ensure transparency and accountability, I set up a shared document where we could all track our progress and leave any comments or concerns. This document also served as a log for any decisions made during our meetings.
mid.result: By implementing this structured approach, we were able to resolve our conflicts and agree on a unified strategy for completing the project. The team members who were initially absent became more involved after understanding the critical situation and seeing a clear plan in place. We successfully completed the project on time, receiving positive feedback from our professor for our collaboration and problem-solving skills. The final product was a robust software application that met all the project requirements. This experience taught me valuable lessons in negotiation, compromise, and conflict resolution, which I have since applied in other group projects and settings.

mid.situation: DURING MY FIRST YEAR IN THE UNIVERSITY, A SOFTWARE ENGINEERING GROUP ASSIGNMENT WAS A MAJOR ASSIGNMENT I WAS GIVEN. OUR TEAM COMPRISED OF SIX MEMBERS. IN THE FIRST STAGES OF THE PROJECT, THE THREE MEMBERS OF THE GROUP WERE OFTEN MISSING FROM THE MEETING AND NEVER TRIED ANY EFFORT IN OUR PROJECT. THE OTHER THREE OF US OFTEN DISAGREED TO EACH OTHER'S CHOICE OF APPROACH TO THE PROJECT, THUS HAD FREQUENT CONFLICTS WITH EACH OTHER AND MADE NO PROGRESS. FOR INSTANCE, ONE WANTED TO WORK ON THE BACK END BUT ONE WANTED TO DO THE FRONT END, SO WE KEPT FIGHTING OVER IT.      TASK:      WITH THE PROJECT DEADLINE CLOSING IN, IT BECAME PRETTY APPARENT THAT WE NEEDED TO IRON OUT THESE DIFFERENCES AMONGST OURSELVES AND WORK IN A CONCERTED MANNER. MY TASK WAS TO MEDIATE BETWEEN THE DIFFERING VIEWPOINTS, ENSURE THAT ALL VIEWS WERE TAKEN IN GOOD SPIRIT, AND ARRIVE AT A COMPROMISE TO COMPLETE THE PROJECT ON TIME. THIS CALLED NOT ONLY FOR THE ADDRESSING OF ABSENTEEISM BUT ALSO FOR A UNIFIED APPROACH TOWARD OUR PROJECT.      ACTION:      I CALLED A MEETING WITH THE TEAM MEMBERS AND EMPHASIZED THE URGENCY OF THE SITUATION. I PROPOSED A SYSTEMATIC WAY TO EFFECTIVELY RESOLVE OUR CONFLICTS:      Identify Key Issues: I facilitated a session where each member described the key issues and thoughts in the project. It was recorded on the google document. We discovered our primary differences lay on task prioritization and workload distribution.      Negotiate and Compromise: We collectively negotiated the tasks most important for project completion, such as the core functionalities to be developed first. I encouraged all members to be open to compromise and really stressed that this was a very important collaboration. For example, we agreed to break down the project into subtasks, which could be developed both in the front end and the back end.      Set Clear Roles and Responsibilities: Based on each member's strengths and preferences, we divided the tasks accordingly. I assigned myself to oversee the integration of all parts, ensuring consistency. We established regular check-in meetings to assess progress and iron out issues on time.      Communication: For transparency and accountability, I had a shared document where the progress of all parties could be tracked, and I could leave comments or any pressing concerns. This document was used also to keep a log of decisions arrived at during our meetings.
mid.result: It allowed us to resolve our conflicts and reach agreement on how to implement the project. Team members who were initially absent really became involved after understanding the critical situation and a clear plan. We managed to finalize the project in time, and our professor highly praised our collaboration and problem-solving skills. The final product was a software application that completely met all the requirements of the project. This is a teaching experience in negotiations, compromises, and conflict resolution that has been applied to other group projects and other cases.

mid.situation: During the first year of engineering, I was given a mandate as a group leader, whereby I had a co-leader, in the project to design and build a Rube Goldberg machine. The point of doing this was to ensure that the group members all worked in synchrony to make all the components of the machine work as one. It was apparent that some of the group members had not been doing their jobs properly or even on the timelines that had been set. This played a part in a certain number of delays and hence the group's poor performance in general.             TASK: As group leaders, our job was to identify the problems at the root and implement solutions through which each task could be done perfectly and on time. We tried to improve the performance of the team in the direction of ensuring the successful completion of the RGM project.             ACTION: To make matters right, we organized a meeting as a group, where we openly talked about the issues that were stalling us. We encouraged everyone to share their perspectives and any challenges they faced. During the meeting, we identified that there were various issues: unclear task assignments, lack of understanding concerning some of the tasks, and some members who felt overwhelmed by their workloads.             To resolve these issues, we did the following:      1. Clear Task Assignments: We re-visited the task list and outlined clearly the responsibility of every member. We made it clear to all members what is required of them and within what timeframe.      2. Provided Support and Resources: We provided more support and more resources to those who were lagging behind with their tasks, be it by pairing with members who are more experienced, tutorials, or reference materials.      3. Revised Work Distribution: We redistributed tasks to accommodate a work balance for all members so that no one member was overburdened and all the work was manageable.      4. Assigned Regular Check-ins: We scheduled regular check-in meetings to monitor your progress, address any new issues, and provide continued support and feedback.
mid.result: Due to these practices, our team performed well, with an increased rate of task completion and better quality in performance. Consistent check-ins maintained the track of the whole procedure and allowed us to address any problem in time. We were able to complete the Rube Goldberg machine in time and with all quality work. The project was appreciated by our instructors with feedback on having good teamwork and strong problem-solving abilities. The experience not only helped us to pull off the project but also enhanced our leadership and collaboration skills.

mid.situation: I was given the solo task to create an advanced tea system through many engineering properties for a certain customer with a desire.      Task:       Our goal was to create an internet based tea maker, with the satisfaction of the customers desires, through vigorous designing, testing, modeling and much more engineering applications. Through this individual project, I noticed that I could improve in multitasking, as the roles and responsibilities of engineering seemed very daunting and fast paced at times. There were many roles to cover such as designing, testing and even modeling, in which all had to be of engineering standard and individually done, which made the task even harder than I expected at first. Through this, I now understand the importance of the roles and responsibilities of engineers no matter the field.      Action:      Since the task was of heavy load with high persistence and detail in every task, I took the initiative and intuition to apply many soft skills which helped me through the task both mentally and physically. One of these soft skills that I used was time management. I personally was poor in managing time, so through time management, I was able to complete certain tasks at certain times, with deadlines for each, making me push myself and reach my goals efficiently. Another such skill was creativity. Without creativity skills, this task was near impossible to complete, as the task was to create an advanced tea system with many functions and features with a satisfactory design. Through creative thinking, I was able to complete the task effectively and efficiently. Developing these soft skills through this project inspired me to develop more of these skills, and I would be thrilled to see what the development of other skills can do for me in the future.
mid.result: Through the soft skills I obtained and utilized, I was able to prioritize tasks, manage tasks and also made me think more critically and made me more aware of deadlines I had to reach, which ultimately led me to the successful completion of my tea maker project. The impact of the skills I obtained while completing this project, made me realise there is room for so much more improvement in future projects, and applying them effectively was just as useful.

mid.situation: I PERFORMED WELL ON SEVERAL OF THE QUIZZES IN MY ENGINEERING SYSTEM AND DESIGN THINKING UNIT BECAUSE I CONSISTENTLY WENT THROUGH THE MATERIAL AND WAS ON TRACK. HOWEVER, A 15% QUIZ HAD TO BE COMPLETED AFTERWARD THE MIDTERM BREAK AND I WAS RELAXED THROUGHOUT THE MIDTERM BREAK. WE WERE GIVEN A SAMPLE QUIZ TO ENSURE WE WERE WELL-PREPARED. THIS WAS DONE TO SEE HOW WELL WE UNDERSTOOD THE UNIT'S ENGINEERING THEMES AND TO HELP US PREPARE FOR THE FINAL QUIZ. IT WAS UPSETTING BECAUSE EVEN THOUGH OUR CONVEYNOR TOOK ALL THE REQUIRED ACTIONS, I DID POORLY—MUCH BELOW MY AVERAGE. THIS RESULT WAS HUMBLING. IT BROUGHT TO LIGHT MY LACK OF KNOWLEDGE OF THE ESSENTIAL MATHEMATICAL CONCEPTS REQUIRED IN ENGINEERING.       TASK: I FOUND MYSELF SEEKING WAYS TO IMPROVE MY UNDERSTANDING OF THE CONCEPTS AFTER I DID HORRIBLY ON MY SAMPLE QUIZ. I WAS DETERMINED TO WORK ON MY WEAKNESSES AND PREPARE MYSELF FOR THE ACTUAL QUIZ.       ACTION: I DECIDED TO PROACTIVELY ADDRESS MY INADEQUATE PERFORMANCE. I ACTIVELY TALKED WITH MY FRIENDS TO ORGANIZE A STUDY GROUP WHERE WE COULD HELP EACH OTHER LEARN AFTER THE LECTURE WHERE WE COMPLETED OUR SAMPLE QUIZ. I WOULD SAY IT WOULDN’T BE SO EASY IF IT WASN'T FOR OUR TEAMWORK. ADDITIONALLY, I USED MY ABILITY TO SOLVE ISSUES TO PINPOINT THE PRECISE AREAS IN WHICH I WAS WEAK, SUCH AS THE CRITERION PROBLEMS. I STARTED READING THE CHAPTERS MORE THOROUGHLY IN AN EFFORT TO FULLY UNDERSTAND THE IDEA. EVEN THOUGH IT REQUIRED A SUBSTANTIAL AMOUNT OF EFFORT, THE EFFORTS I PUT IN RESULTED IN A MORE PRODUCTIVE AND SUCCESSFUL STUDY PROCESS, WHICH ENHANCED MY CONFIDENCE AND IMPROVED MY CAPACITY TO SOLVE CRITERION QUESTIONS FLAWLESSLY.
mid.result: AS A RESULT, I PASSED THE ACTUAL QUIZ. THIS MEANT A LOT TO ME BECAUSE I FIRMLY BELIEVED IF YOU WORKED HARD ENOUGH, YOU COULD ACHIEVE ANYTHING. I ACCEPT ACCOUNTABILITY FOR THE FACT THAT MY LAID-BACK ATTITUDE DURING THE MID-SESSION BREAK AND THEREAFTER WAS THE PRIMARY REASON I PERFORMED POORLY ON THE SAMPLE QUIZ. FINALLY, I FELT RELIEVED AND PROUD OF MY ACCOMPLISHMENT. MY ATTITUDE OF INITIATIVE CONTRIBUTED TO A MORE EFFICIENT STUDY EXPERIENCE. TO FURTHER EXPAND THIS SKILL, I WILL CONTINUE TO SEEK ASSISTANCE FROM PEERS TO IDENTIFY AREAS FOR GROWTH AND APPLY WAYS TO FURTHER STRENGTHEN MY ABILITIES.

mid.situation: I performed well on several of the quizzes in my Engineering System and Design Thinking unit because I consistently went through the material and was on track. However, a 15% quiz had to be completed afterward the midterm break and I was relaxed throughout the midterm break. We were given a sample quiz to ensure we were well-prepared. This was done to see how well we understood the unit's engineering themes and to help us prepare for the final quiz. It was upsetting because even though our conveynor took all the required actions, I did poorly—much below my average. This result was humbling. It brought to light my lack of knowledge of the essential mathematical concepts required in engineering.       Task: I found myself seeking ways to improve my understanding of the concepts after I did horribly on my sample quiz. I was determined to work on my weaknesses and prepare myself for the actual quiz.       Action: I decided to proactively address my inadequate performance. I actively talked with my friends to organize a study group where we could help each other learn after the lecture where we completed our sample quiz. I would say it wouldn’t be so easy if it wasn't for our teamwork. Additionally, I used my ability to solve issues to pinpoint the precise areas in which I was weak, such as the criterion problems. I started reading the chapters more thoroughly in an effort to fully understand the idea. Even though it required a substantial amount of effort, the efforts I put in resulted in a more productive and successful study process, which enhanced my confidence and improved my capacity to solve criterion questions flawlessly.
mid.result: As a result, I passed the actual quiz. This meant a lot to me because I firmly believed if you worked hard enough, you could achieve anything. I accept accountability for the fact that my laid-back attitude during the mid-session break and thereafter was the primary reason I performed poorly on the sample quiz. Finally, I felt relieved and proud of my accomplishment. My attitude of initiative contributed to a more efficient study experience. To further expand this skill, I will continue to seek assistance from peers to identify areas for growth and apply ways to further strengthen my abilities.

mid.situation: In my second-year engineering course, ENGG2050, I was assigned a project to create an infographic explaining a key concept from our textbook. The objective was to make the infographic highly graphical with minimal text, formatted as a 297 × 840 mm portrait. The infographic needed to effectively communicate the concept across three sections: abstraction level, implementation level, and examples and tools. This task underscored the role of engineers in simplifying complex information and presenting it in a clear and accessible manner.      TASK: My specific task was to design and develop the infographic. The challenge was to distill the complex information into a visually appealing and easily understandable format while ensuring that all necessary details were accurately represented. The task required not only technical understanding but also skills in graphic design and communication.      ACTION: To address this challenge, I first broke down the key concept into its core components. I sketched initial layouts and then used graphic design software to create various iterations of the infographic. Each iteration involved refining the design to enhance clarity and visual appeal. I ensured that the visual elements were clear and intuitive, with minimal text to avoid overwhelming the viewer. I also sought feedback from peers and the professor, incorporating their suggestions to improve the final product.
mid.result: The final infographic was well-received, earning positive feedback for its clarity, visual appeal, and effectiveness in conveying the concept. It successfully met the project requirements and demonstrated the ability to communicate complex information in a straightforward and engaging manner. This project highlighted the critical role of engineers in not only understanding and solving technical problems but also in effectively communicating solutions to diverse audiences.      DEAL FRAMEWORK      DESCRIBE: I led the design and creation of an infographic for my ENGG2050 course, focusing on a key concept from our textbook. The project required a highly graphical 297 × 840 mm portrait with minimal text, divided into three sections: abstraction level, implementation level, and examples and tools.      EVALUATE: This experience underscored the importance of communication and individual initiative in engineering. While the technical understanding of the concept was crucial, the ability to present it clearly and engagingly was equally important. Incorporating feedback from others was essential to the project's success.      ARTICULATE LEARNING: From this experience, I learned that effective communication is a vital responsibility of engineers. It's not enough to have technical knowledge; the ability to convey that knowledge clearly and concisely is crucial. In the future, I will focus more on honing my communication skills and seek feedback regularly to ensure clarity and effectiveness in my presentations and designs.      LEARNING FROM THE EXPERIENCE: This project taught me that the role of an engineer extends beyond solving technical problems to include effective communication and individual responsibility. Moving forward, I will prioritize these skills in my professional development, understanding their impact on the success of engineering projects and the broader community.

mid.situation: I was introduced to a class where I didn’t know many people. I had to step out of my comfort zone to interact with others, share my thoughts and solutions, and learn to be open to constructive criticism while relaying our ideas to each other. Though being introduced to a class full of new people was difficult and the thought of it was unpleasant, I knew it was necessary for the development of my professional skills.
mid.task_action: I was required to learn how to meet new people and learn how to work successfully and communicate with the new people I met. In particular, two of my peers and I worked together on our IoT Tea Maker every week together. As the weeks went by, I noticed that we were starting to open up more and become more comfortable sharing our ideas, even if we weren’t very confident in them.      I feel as though the major breakthrough was in week 4 when we needed to accomplish a detailed design for the hypothetical Tea Machine. Two of us were not able to attend the class, so we set up an online call on Discord to go over the tasks for the lesson. We bounced ideas off each other about how the IoT Tea Maker would work, from the systems within the actual Tea Machine to the phone connectivity feature, we drew up a couple of designs, whilst tweaking parts of the drawing.
mid.result: The result of this online call was that not only did we build a great detailed design of our Tea Maker, but we also connected with each other outside of ENGG2050. This allowed for open discussion of future weekly tasks in ENGG2050 and other classes where collaboration was needed. I knew that this task would test my communication abilities and I am thrilled to have proven that I can communicate to new people.      Though I am not usually a fan of group work, because it often ends with someone doing more work than the others, this group was different, everyone had a fair amount of input, and suggestions were taken seriously.      Ultimately, I enhanced my communication skills by meeting new people, which will motivate me to be more involved in future group projects.

mid.situation: We were tasked with designing an internet of tea maker which through the SGTAs I was able to develop my communication of different ideas to classmates. Thinking creatively to amalgamate these ideas into a unique design was supported as there was limited guidance in the unit as to what the tea maker should be.
mid.task_action: With each SGTA we needed to work towards a unique and completed IoT design. However, we were given free rein to decide what our IoT concept would be, as long as it met the client’s requirements. We were allowed to work with others in our SGTAs to communicate design possibilities and the features we wanted to include, which allowed me to draw inspiration from others. Additionally, we could actively receive input from others on how to solve problems we came across such as the materials that would be most suitable to build a component and meeting other TPMs. Further, we were required to have multiple designs, where in class I used the time to compare the alternatives and look at which design was more suitable and effective by talking with classmates.
mid.result: Through my active participation each week, I was able to develop my communication skills in the context of attempting to create a successful design. By communicating different possibilities I bounced my ideas off others and overcame challenges such as the logistical processes of the tea being made and the different mechanical units and software needed. By hearing other’s ideas and their creative features, I improved many components of my design and opened my mind to aspects that I had not thought of. Thus, this collaborative atmosphere had a positive impact on my learning and allowed me to practice the skills that I will use in the future.

mid.situation: In our Advanced Mechanical Design course, our team was assigned the task of developing a cost-effective and efficient gearbox for the crane prototype. As the project progressed, we encountered a significant challenge when our initial gearbox design did not meet the performance criteria during testing, and the deadline for the final submission was only a few weeks away.      TASK: As the team leader, I was responsible for steering the project back on track. The critical task was to quickly come up with an alternative design that met the performance requirements without exceeding our limited budget and remaining timeline. The uncertainty regarding the availability of materials and the feasibility of new design modifications added to the complexity of the task.      ACTION: I initiated an emergency team meeting to discuss our options and brainstorm new ideas. Recognizing the urgency of the situation, I encouraged a proactive and creative approach from all team members. We utilized a combination of round-robin and gallery methods in our brainstorming sessions to ensure every idea was heard and considered. After generating several alternative designs, we evaluated each based on criteria such as cost, manufacturability, and performance efficiency. We selected the most promising design and quickly prototyped it to validate its feasibility.
mid.result: The redesigned gearbox not only met the required performance criteria but also proved to be more cost-effective, reducing the overall production cost by 25%. The team successfully met the project deadline, and our gearbox design was commended during the course review for its innovation and practical applicability. This experience significantly enhanced my ability to lead under pressure, make decisive choices in uncertain conditions, and foster a collaborative team environment. It underscored the importance of adaptability and rapid problem-solving in engineering, skills that are critical to my future career development.

mid.situation: In the ENGG2050 course, I undertook a project to create an Internet based tea machine. This project was complex, including several phases of system engineering, ranging from the initial conceptual design to the precise detailed design, and finishing in testing and logistical planning.      TASK: My primary goal was to ensure efficient communication among all individuals involved in the project. I was responsible as a student engineer to convert the client's requirements into comprehensive technical specifications and ensure that both the engineer and the client had a clear understanding of them. Thorough documentation was necessary at every stage of the project to record the changing design choices and ensure they were in line with the client's vision and technical performance metrics (TPMs).      ACTION: I made sure to address numerous important points to enhance the communication of our project:      	*   REQUIREMENT GATHERING: Engaged in comprehensive discussions with the client to extract specific and quantifiable requirements. This required asking them detailed questions to get to the bottom of what they expected and wanted.      	*   DOCUMENT CREATION: Created many types of documents, such as conceptual design documents, detailed design documents, test plans, maintenance, production and logistic. These documents were customized to guarantee clarity and precision in conveying technical information.      	*   ITERATIVE INPUT: Consistently sought input from peers and tutors on written materials to improve and verify the accuracy of the information presented.      	*   STRATEGY FOR SELECTING SUPPLIERS AND TESTING: Utilized risk analysis in decision-making to choose suppliers and devised comprehensive testing techniques including test site, test requirements and procedures  to verify the product complied with all defined TPMs, trade offs and regulatory standards.      	*   LOGISTICS AND RELIABILITY PLANNING: Developed plans for the efficient distribution, maintenance, and reliability of the system. This included addressing different usage scenarios, selecting the most efficient packaging and assembly methods to guarantee reliable transportation, designing easily accessible repair procedures, and picking reliable suppliers to save both downtime and maintenance expenses.
mid.result: The project is currently in its concluding phases, the preparation and strategic written communication have already resulted in numerous favorable outcomes:      	*   ENHANCED STAKEHOLDER ENGAGEMENT: The comprehensive and clear documentation contributed to a significant degree of involvement and satisfaction among stakeholders, including the client.      	*   ENHANCED PROJECT EFFICIENCY: The collective's workflow and efficiency have been optimized through effective written communication, which has reduced misunderstandings and the need for modifications.      	*   EXPECTED TECHNICAL SUCCESS: The extensive testing methods and thorough documentation indicate that the machine will operate as planned, fulfilling all specified TPM(s).      	*   OPERATIONAL SUSTAINABILITY: The development of logistical and maintenance frameworks aims to improve the machine's reliability and decrease lifetime costs by enabling straightforward installation, routine maintenance, and efficient serviceability.      The thorough process of documenting and written communication has ensured that all stakeholders are in common ground with the objectives and requirements, thereby preparing the successful completion of the project.

mid.situation: At the beginning of the ENGG2050 unit this semester, we were tasked with creating an Internet of Things (IoT) project by following Systems Engineering and Design principles. The project spanned over the whole semester, and while it was intended to be an individual task, we were encouraged to work and consult with our peers as a team. Given this opportunity, I started connecting with peers at the same table from the first week. We worked as a group of 4 where two of them were from Mechanical Engineering and the other one was my friend who was pursuing Software Engineering. We were required to use systems engineering principles to design our project product.      TASK: The name of the IOT was the Internet of Tea. My primary objective was to design and develop the Internet of Tea project, which involved creating a smart system to monitor and manage tea brewing processes according to the client’s needs. I also wanted to develop my interpersonal and communication skills, strengthen my professional connections, and advance my technical expertise.      ACTION: In the first week, I actively participated and communicated with my peers to discuss the client’s requirements. We discussed the end product and the possible designs. By the week, we progressed and our project was coming together. We attended weekly SGTAs to discuss the project's scope and design. Each member contributed ideas that helped us refine our system design and develop a robust final product. I assumed responsibility for promoting communication among team members, guaranteeing that all members agreed. This included planning agendas, scheduling meetings, and keeping track of our advancement. One of the core functional units of the machine was called the "Smart Brew Module." I concentrated on designing the Smart Brew Module's functional features, like temperature control, brewing time, and user interface. I worked with teammates to make sure our design was workable and efficient. Outside of academics, I joined weekly football practices with one of my teammates. This has allowed me to meet new people from different ages and backgrounds which helped me develop my social skills. Acknowledging my early social shortcomings, I made a conscious effort to work on my English and gain confidence. I actively engaged in class discussions and looked for chances to socialize with my classmates.
mid.result: The collaborative effort led to the successful conceptualization of our Internet of Tea project, which received positive feedback from our SGTA instructors. This experience yielded several significant outcomes. I enhanced my understanding of system design and the principles of IoT, focusing particularly on the hypothetical functionality of the Smart Brew Module. Participating in extracurricular activities and having regular interactions with my peers helped me become more confident and proficient in English. I now feel more at ease speaking in front of groups of people and in professional settings. I developed close relationships with my teammates and fellow students, forming a network of support that grew outside of the classroom. I have no doubt that the skills and confidence I have acquired will help me in my future career pursuits.

mid.situation: Our group needed to design a tea maker that was based on an IoT system and I was given the task of designing a subsystem of a loose-leaf tea dispenser. It was stated that the customer needs storage for four different types of tea in the machine and tea should be dispensed automatically.
mid.task_action: When the tasks were equally divided within the team, I started to plan for my part. We needed to communicate our progress and design with our team so that it wouldn't mismatch the whole machine at the end. I used the design process phase      (NEEDS--->CONCEPTUAL DESIGN--->PRELIMINARY DESIGN--->DETAIL DESIGN) graph as my reference to track my progress. Initially, I reviewed the customer's requirements to inform my design approach. I organized my workload over six weeks: dedicating the first three weeks to the conceptual design, the next two weeks to the preliminary design phase, and the final week to the detailed design.      Our team analyzed the NEEDS of the customers and made a list of features(sub-systems) that can be included in the machine. We distributed the sub-systems to each person on the team to ease the process of building the machine.      In the CONCEPTUAL DESIGN phase, I generated multiple conceptual designs based on the requirements. I conducted extensive research on various dispensing systems analogous to our project, which involved dispensing tea leaves. Through a week of brainstorming and reviewing different mechanisms, I expedited this phase to two weeks by maintaining regular communication with my team. I illustrated six conceptual ideas on paper and presented them for team discussion. After a detailed review, we refined and eliminated infeasible ideas, ultimately narrowing down to three preliminary designs.       During the PRELIMINARY DESIGN phase, which spanned two weeks, we focused on material selection and refining our chosen design. We established key parameters such as cost, reliability, and sustainability. The designs were ranked based on these criteria, and the top-ranked design was selected for further development. Additionally, I developed the Technical Performance Measures (TPMs) and the House of Quality for this sub-system.      In the DETAILED DESIGN phase, I translated the selected preliminary ideas into a CAD model and constructed a small prototype to verify that the actual test parameters aligned with our assumptions. Finally, I completed the final design, which the team agreed would seamlessly integrate into our tea dispensing machine.
mid.result: As a result, I completed my task beforehand with much more efficiency and meeting the requirements of the client. The efficiency was primarily achieved by dividing the machine into sub-systems and allowing me to work on one sub-system while maintaining ongoing communication about their progress within the team. My design was reliable within the parameters and was meeting the client's requirements. This caused the seamless integration of my sub-system to integrate perfectly into our final machine design. Finally, the use of the design process phase graph allowed me to effectively track my progress. This facilitated the timely completion of each phase, ensuring that the project stayed on schedule.

TIME ALLOCATION: I allocated specific time slots in my schedule for each step. By setting clear deadlines for each part, I maintained steady progress. I allocated specific time slots in my schedule to focus solely on each step. By setting clear deadlines for each part of the process, I maintained steady progress. mid.situation: During week 4 we were asked to implement a detailed design for the Internet based Tea maker machine. Our objective was to transition from preliminary design and conceptual design to a detailed design that met all client requirements.      TASK: After finalizing our initial draft, I conducted a thorough analysis and discovered that our design unintentionally violated a minor client requirement. This was a challenge as the design needed modification without compromising other established requirements and the existing design.      ACTION: I informed my team about the issue and provided a clear explanation of how the design failed from the client's specifications. We all looked at the design together to find the mistake and then came up with ideas on how to fix it. mid.result: The team accepted and implemented the modifications, and we developed a new, improved design that adhered to all requirements.
