mid.situation: During the semester, our class was assigned to design and manufacture an Internet-based tea maker tailored to meet the specific wants and needs of a customer. The customer expressed major desires such as waking up and coming home to tea just the way they like it, and minor preferences like sharing tea styles with friends and experimenting with new teas.      TASK: My task was to critically think through the design and functionality of the tea maker to ensure it met both major and minor customer needs. This involved integrating internet capabilities, personalized settings, and social sharing features into the tea maker.      ACTION: To address the customer’s wants, I started by conducting research on existing smart appliances and how they integrate with the internet. I focused on understanding the mechanics of IoT (Internet of Things) and how it can be applied to a tea maker. I then brainstormed potential features and functionalities, such as programmable brewing schedules, remote control via a smartphone app, and user profiles to save personalized tea preferences.      Next, I created a prototype design incorporating these features. For instance, I designed an app interface that allowed users to set their preferred brewing times and tea strength. Additionally, I implemented a feature where users could share their tea recipes and ratings with friends through the app. I also considered the customer’s preference for different teas at different times of the day and included an option to schedule different types of tea based on the time.
mid.result: The final design of the tea maker met the customer’s major and minor wants effectively. The integration of internet capabilities allowed the customer to wake up to their preferred tea and come home to a freshly brewed cup. The social sharing feature enabled users to connect and share tea styles with friends, enhancing the user experience. This project not only satisfied the customer's requirements but also expanded my understanding and application of IoT in everyday appliances.

mid.situation: In the first week of the SGTA course, we focused on the importance of written communication. The content of this lesson is that as an engineer, you must make a list of questions to ask customers to confirm their needs for the product.      Task:      We were asked to simulate a situation on a project where a client (played by a teacher) was sent a list of questions. This list is based on the customer's original need for the product, asking questions to the customer to further determine the customer's specific needs. Our goal is to describe the problem clearly and concisely and to ask clear questions so that customers can quickly understand and provide effective feedback.      Action:      To complete this task, I first carefully read the customer's initial requirements. On this basis, I wrote down the questions I had during the reading. First, I raised questions about the customer's requirements document. Some of the descriptions were vague and confusing, and I had to ask the customer questions to avoid misunderstandings. The vague part is that the customer said they want hot tea when they wake up and when they get home, so I added a query to the list of questions asking if the customer needs hot tea immediately and if they mind using a smart accessory like a sports watch to do so. Get better service (through GPS and sleep detection, make tea before detecting that the customer is on the way home or about to wake up). Secondly, I asked about the price because the customer did not mention this aspect in the original demand. After identifying my problem, I discussed it with members of the team and got some inspiration, then I added another question about the size of the machine and whether the customer is willing to pay more for better automation service (something like Standard and advanced versions)
mid.result: Unfortunately, I did not send any questions to the ilearn forum in the end, because other students had already submitted similar questions one step ahead. And I also got a satisfactory answer from the teacher's answer.

mid.situation: During high school, I was part of a group working on a project. Our goal was to renovate a local park that had fallen into disrepair. As the project progressed, I noticed some members of the group were cutting corners and not adhering to the safety guidelines we had established. This not only compromised the quality of the work but also posed potential risks to the volunteers and future park visitors.      TASK: As one of the project leaders, it was my responsibility to ensure that the project was completed with high standards of ethics and integrity, and that the safety of all participants was prioritised. I needed to address the issue with the team, reinforce the importance of following the guidelines, and find a way to motivate everyone to adhere to our standards.      ACTION: I called for a team meeting and openly discussed the concerns I had observed. I emphasised the importance of integrity in our work and how cutting corners could negatively impact the community we aimed to serve. To make sure everyone understood the safety guidelines, I organised a refresher training session. I also set up a system where team members could anonymously report any safety or ethical concerns. Additionally, I worked closely with the team to reassign tasks, ensuring that everyone was clear on their responsibilities and the standards we needed to maintain.
mid.result: As a result of these actions, the team's adherence to safety guidelines and ethical standards improved significantly. The quality of the renovation work increased, and we were able to complete the project without any safety incidents. The park was successfully renovated and reopened to the community, receiving positive feedback from local residents. This experience reinforced the importance of ethics, integrity, and social responsibility in my personal and professional life, and it highlighted the impact that a committed and conscientious approach can have on the community.

mid.situation: DESCRIBE THE SPECIFIC SITUATION OR CONTEXT IN WHICH YOU UTILISED OR DEVELOPED THE SOFT SKILL. IT COULD BE A WORK-RELATED PROJECT, A TEAM COLLABORATION, OR ANY PERSONAL EXPERIENCE WHERE THE SOFT SKILL CAME INTO PLAY. DESCRIBE THE CONTEXT AND RELEVANCY/CHALLENGE THE TASK HAS. CONTEXT!    For the infographic assessment I was forced upon the obstacle of needing to remain within the limitations of creativity & practicality. With endless amounts of ideas &  online layouts, I was ready to create a masterpiece, however I usually am not to be considered creative. I understood that the infographic needed to require the necessary information to explain the topic with captivating graphics, yet after x amount of years in school just writing words on paper & not exploring my creative side, I realised the challenge was going to be a learning curve for me. The time I spent on my infographic, I knew I had to embrace my inner creative thinking, so I took to google and explored in search for inspiration into what captured my attention & what I thought would be half professional, & half captivating enough to keep a reader interested.      T - TASK: CLEARLY OUTLINE THE TASK OR CHALLENGE YOU FACED DURING THAT SITUATION. WHAT WAS EXPECTED OF YOU, AND WHAT GOALS WERE YOU TRYING TO ACHIEVE? DESCRIBE IN DETAIL AS MUCH AS POSSIBLE HOW THE TASK IS NOT TRIVIAL.       To keep my infographic short and sweet, I needed to embrace a level of creative thinking as I had not previously required it whilst in school. For the infographic assignment, I was expected to provide a summarised topic from the textbook in a graphic & captivating manner, Throughout this, I attempted to achieve and gain a sense of creative thinking by introducing a less 1+1 logic & a more "go with the flow" creative state. Through this task, I have discovered that it is far from trivial due to understanding a crucial aspect of engineering, this being design. An engineer is challenged to think outside of the box on a day to day basis to engineer their way around problems. Without a degree of creative thinking, one would be stuck within what I like to call, "the logic box", & be unable to perceive challenges from as many angles as they should.      A - ACTION: DESCRIBE YOUR ACTIONS TO ADDRESS THE TASK OR CHALLENGE. HIGHLIGHT THE SPECIFIC SOFT SKILL YOU EMPLOYED DURING THE PROCESS. THIS COULD INVOLVE COMMUNICATION, LEADERSHIP, PROBLEM-SOLVING, ADAPTABILITY, OR OTHER RELEVANT SKILLS. THE ACTION CAN ALSO BE EVALUATED BASED ON HOW NON-CONVENTIONAL IT MIGHT BE. AGAIN GIVING YOUR ACTIONS CONTEXT.       Overtime I addressed my previous lack of creativity & spent a day & a half introducing myself to different ideas/themes/templates to understand how I could implement my logical thinking into the infographic. Of all places, I found Pinterest to be my best friend. Pinterest had let me see all ideas/themes I was looking for & topics similar through simple searches. I had chosen to do an elimination method of which theme I liked best, unsurprisingly I found the theme 'minimalism' to be my leading advocate. I used the elimination method as it gave me an opportunity to weigh in the pros and cons of each theme/template. I discovered that although an explosion of graphics is captivating to the eye, it can be overwhelming and allow for valuable space meant for information to be taken away.      R -
mid.result: EXPLAIN THE OUTCOME OF YOUR ACTIONS AND HOW THE SOFT SKILL YOU APPLIED INFLUENCED THE RESULTS POSITIVELY. WHAT DID YOU ACHIEVE, AND HOW DID IT IMPACT THE SITUATION AND OTHERS INVOLVED? EXPLAIN NOT JUST THE DEVELOPMENT BUT THE IMPACT OF THE EFFECT ON THE BROADER CONTEXT.      The final outcome of my infographic assisted by the development of creative thinking allowed me to create a piece of 'educational art' with maintaining the balance I desired, half info, half graphics. Embracing this soft skill has allowed me to have a more open minded perspective into how I can tackle problems outside of my infographic & introduce the skill into the real world.

mid.situation: During the week 5 SGTA when my group and I were tasked with developing a testing plan for our machine, multiple disagreements occurred within our group while brainstorming and designing tests for our system. This was due to many misunderstandings and conflicting opinions that my team members and I had on what would constitute an effective test. This was when I applied my negotiation and conflict-resolution skills and actively communicated with team members so our group could reach a compromise.
mid.task_action: The primary challenge I was faced with was helping my team to reach a compromise, my goal was to ensure a productive SGTA and contribute to creating a healthy team chemistry. The task of conflict resolution was not trivial as it required being careful with my words to not further stir up conflict within my group. Another challenge for me was to resolve the conflict without leaving any group member out of the conversation, as it is important for everyone to contribute and have their contributions acknowledged. I addressed these challenges by employing my conflict resolution skills, by helping us collectively decide to select multiple test ideas and seek external feedback from tutors and other groups to aid in solidifying our selection of tests for our machine.
mid.result: The outcome was an overall more productive team effort and a healthier work environment in which everyone was comfortable communicating and working as a team. This made reaching project milestones a less daunting task than otherwise, had we not taken the steps necessary to ensure good communication among team members.

mid.situation: In our SGTA classes, we were assigned to design an innovative internet-tea maker that had specific needs that the customer would need in their tea maker. This project was a blast to design because it combined the challenge of technical thinking and creative innovations to bring comfort and joy to the customer. The goal was to create a design using our creative thinking to upgrade an existing appliance into something innovative, convenient, reliable and engaging for the user.      TASK:      During our SGTA sessions,  I faced the challenge of designing the internet tea maker to the customer's wants, and so that the TPM's will satisfy. The “major wants” of the internet tea maker was that it was connected to the internet, it would perfectly make the tea to the users preference at specific times. There was also a need for innovation as the customer wanted some minor wants which inspired me to make creative solutions. The “minor wants” was to share her tea style with her friends, she wants to try different teas, wants to experiment with teas and to make friends. I was determined to design a solution to the customers' wants, which made me go beyond conventional engineering solutions and required me to think creatively and empathetically. In order to design the internet tea maker to satisfy the customer.      ACTION:      To nurture creative thinking, I suggested to the group we should brainstorm our ideas. This got us all thinking and I also encouraged everyone to share all ideas. My actions contributed to a more efficient mapping of our ideas, which we could easily explore more various innovative features and how they could be integrated into the tea maker. Some brainstorming ideas that our group came up with was that we needed the internet tea maker to be controlled by a smartphone in addition to a smartphone app. Our group decided that the smartphone and app should be intuitive and user friendly, with programs that let you adjust settings, pre-made settings for tea, programmable scheduling, social media integration, friend recommendations and a tea discovery page. We, as a group, successfully compiled a brainstorm of creative and innovative solutions to the customer's needs. I encountered a situation where communication, teamwork, problem solving and creative thinking was essential in engineering. This process of everyone in our group taking an initiative to give their opinion has allowed us to explore diverse inspirations, to create an amazing brainstorm of ideas to solve a design problem.
mid.result: Our creative approach to designing the internet-tea maker yielded a positive outcome. My group and I were able to brainstorm our ideas and were able to pick solutions. Our goal was to communicate with each other and use creative thinking to solve problems involving the design. I was responsible for motivating everyone in the group to share ideas. Instead of an individual approach we were able to accomplish features of the design that will also satisfy TPM’s. I now understand the importance of creative thinking to solve problems in the design process. I also encountered a problem where one person isn't contributing, so I decided to talk to him and he explained he was unsure of what to do. Using communication skills I proceeded to tell him that we were creatively thinking of solutions. I now understand that the soft skill of communication is essential to creative thinking as if we have an innovative and creative idea but we are able to communicate it will contribute less to our design.       In summary, prioritising the user needs and thinking creatively to solve their needs, helped us create a design that is innovative. Soft skills such as communication and team building helped us achieve our goal.

mid.situation: At the beginning of the semester, we had a quiz due, but I missed it without even realising it. This situation left me feeling overwhelmed and challenged, making me understand the importance of time management to avoid missing future assessments and quizzes.      Task: I had multiple assessments and quizzes throughout the semester. My goal was to be on time with them and establish a way to track all my tasks so I wouldn't miss any deadlines.      Action: To manage my time better, I downloaded Google Calendar and added all my assessment dates. I set notifications for one week before each due date and allocated a maximum of three days to complete each task. This approach ensured I wouldn't fall behind and allowed me to seek help if I didn't understand something instead of leaving it to the last minute. I felt inspired and motivated to improve my organisational skills.
mid.result: As a result, I managed my time perfectly, which significantly enhanced my sense of accomplishment. I felt proud and fulfilled for not missing any assessments or quizzes and for staying organised and less stressed. This experience left me feeling empowered and enthusiastic about my ability to handle future tasks efficiently. I was also relieved and thrilled to see the positive impact of my improved time management skills.

mid.situation: THROUGHOUT THE SESSION IN ENGG2050, I EMBARKED ON A PROJECT GIVEN TO ME THAT REQUIRED ME TO TAKE CUSTOMER WANTS INTO A MACHINE, SPECIFICALLY A TEA DISPENSING MACHINE. THE CONVENORS ALLOWED US TO MAKE THIS PROJECT IN ANY WAY SHAPE OR FORM. AS I AM A SOFTWARE ENGINEERING STUDENT, I DECIDED TO DELVE MORE INTO THE APP SIDE OF THIS. THIS REQUIRED ME TO TRANSLATE CUSTOMER WANTS THAT WERE GIVEN TO ME INTO SPECIFIC TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS, TPMS (TECHNICAL PROGRAM MEASURES) TO MAKE SURE ALL THAT WAS REQUIRED FOR THE APP WAS MET.      Task: My task was to primarily make a plan for the development of the app, and decide how the TPM’s would be met with what features, essentially making a complete and coherent plan before the development stage. This involved analysing customer needs to create technical features/specifications for the app, and thus checking to see if these new features aligned with the customer wants.      Action: I employed by problem-solving and critical thinking skills through the following steps:      	*   Firstly I started off by having a look at the customer wants and listed the ones by grouping the common ones together, the ones that align under possibly the same feature, this allowed me to quantise them in a way so that I could easily make these wants into TPM’s.      	*   Secondly, I used these groupings and used my critical thinking skills and converted these user wants to TPM’s. An example of this is that there was a desire for making sure there was preset times for tea to be dispensed. The relevant TPM was to create a plan for scheduling and the accuracy for when there is a request for tea to be dispensed at a certain time.      	*   Using these TPM’s allowed me to create problems that I was going to solve with the app. Using my problem-solving skills, I grouped these TPM’s like before, together where those TPM’s could belong to that feature. From then , I devised features that would solve this, ranging from Tea Dispense Time Accuracy, User Settings Adherence for Tea Dispensing and more.      	*   Moving onwards, to create a more comprehensive plan, I created functions of each feature, in order to have a more systematic approach to the development process, this further employed my problem-solving skills to tackle the problem of creating a feature.
mid.result: The end of this planning phase concluded with a detailed and comprehensive plan on each feature that addresses its respective TPM’s. This included customer wants that changed to TPM’s and provided developers with a systematic approach to developing the app, going from function to feature to the complete app. This proves to be a foundation for developers to us in the next phase of the development process.

mid.situation: While pursuing my diploma, I juggled my studies with a part-time job at Domino's Pizza. The coursework was demanding, and I found it increasingly difficult to keep up with my studies.  The coursework was challenging, and I struggled to keep up with my studies due to the demanding schedule. This left me with insufficient time to thoroughly understand the material, leading to constant stress about my academic performance. I recognized the need for more study time to fully grasp the concepts and succeed in my course.      Task:      The main problem was deciding whether to continue working at Domino's Pizza or to take a break from my job to concentrate on my studies. This decision involved a thorough assessment of the effects on my finances, and academic performance.      Action:      To address this situation, I utilized critical thinking to weigh the advantages and disadvantages of continuing my part-time job or focusing on my studies. I evaluated the impact of a reduced income, the potential improvements in my academic performance, and the practicality of resuming work or finding a new job once my studies were back on track.      After careful consideration, I concluded that pausing my work and focusing on study was the best option. I spoke with my manager to explain my situation, detailing how my studies were suffering and my need for more time to focus on my diploma. I requested a temporary pause from work, assuring him that I would return if he still wanted me once my academic situation improved or that I would seek a new job that better suited my schedule.
mid.result: As a result of my decision, I was able to allocate more time to my studies, which led to a significant improvement in my academic performance. I experienced less stress and became more engaged in my coursework, which positively impacted my grades. Unfortunately, after my academic situation improved, my manager found someone to fill my position, so I sought a new job that offered a better balance between work and study. This ensured that I could maintain both without compromising either. This decision not only helped me excel in my diploma course but also strengthened my ability to think critically and make informed decisions in challenging situations.

mid.situation: We were tasked to design a Internet of Tea machine where we have to meet a few requirements. The problem was not that it would be hard to meet those requirements, but it was the possible outcome. For the lack of a better word, I was struggling to come up with 1 GOOD design, quality vs quantity kind of situation. I was trying to justify if adding a component that is not specified or was not required and it was hard to do.      ACTION:      Instead of trying to come out with new ideas and be innovative, I stuck to the basics and looked at the requirements, customer's needs and wants. From this I made a brain storm of all the components that I think is required and some extra one that was not. The brainstorm includes functionality such as,  auto reorder the low pods, tracking systems, cup warmer and so on. I try to weigh out the pros and cons, therefore I was able to eliminate the extra functionalities/components in order to priorities on making a GOOD design and not many OKAY design.
mid.result: I was able to come up with a coherent design that makes up all the customers needs and wants such as waking up to tea, rating some teas and sharing teas with friends.

mid.situation: A customer requested the design and manufacture of an Internet-based tea maker that could prepare tea at specific times, tailored to their preferences. They wanted the device to be user-friendly and capable of being controlled remotely.      TASK: The task was to conceptualize, design, and develop a smart tea maker that met the customer's requirements. This involved integrating internet connectivity, creating a user-friendly interface, and ensuring the machine could brew various types of tea according to the user's specifications.      ACTION:      	*   CONCEPTUALIZATION:      	* ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: Engineers were responsible for brainstorming and defining the technical requirements of the project. This included determining the necessary features, connectivity options, and user interface design.   	* PROCESS: Held brainstorming sessions to gather ideas and define the features needed for the tea maker. We decided the tea maker should have a mobile app for remote control, a schedule feature to brew tea at specified times, and an option to customize brewing preferences.      	*   DESIGN:      	* ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: Design engineers were tasked with creating detailed CAD models and ensuring that the design was practical and manufacturable. They were responsible for the overall aesthetic and functional design of the tea maker.   	* PROCESS: Formed a small team to develop the initial design. We used CAD software to create a detailed model of the tea maker, ensuring it included compartments for different tea types, a water reservoir, and heating elements.      	*   DEVELOPMENT:      	* ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: Software engineers developed the mobile app, while hardware engineers worked on the integration of sensors and electronic components. They ensured that all systems functioned seamlessly together.   	* PROCESS: Collaborated with software developers to create the mobile app. The app was designed to allow users to set brewing schedules, select tea types, and control the machine remotely. We also integrated sensors to monitor water levels and temperature.      	*   TESTING:      	* ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: Quality assurance engineers were responsible for testing the device to ensure it met all specifications and performed reliably. They provided feedback for improvements and oversaw the final testing phase.   	* PROCESS: Conducted multiple testing phases to ensure the machine worked as intended. We tested the scheduling feature, remote control functionality, and customization options. We gathered feedback from potential users and made necessary adjustments.
mid.result: * OUTCOME: Successfully developed a prototype of the Internet-based tea maker that met all major customer requirements.   	* IMPACT: The tea maker could be controlled via a mobile app, allowing users to schedule and customize their tea brewing. This showcased the engineers' ability to integrate hardware and software effectively.   	* FEEDBACK: Positive feedback from initial testers highlighted the convenience and user-friendliness of the device.   	* COLLABORATION: The project demonstrated effective collaboration between hardware and software teams, resulting in a functional and innovative product.   	* NEXT STEPS: The successful prototype led to further discussions with potential investors for mass production, highlighting the engineers' role in bringing a product from concept to market readiness.

mid.situation: Through the development of my Internet of Teas maker I have had the opportunity to actively develop and strengthen my critical thinking skills. Utilising this skill has proven incredibly useful in the progression of my project, from simply deriving ideas to developing TPMs and further developing concepts and testing for my project. To be able to develop my project, I was required to be able to apply creative thinking with means of real-world application to produce viable and effective ideas.
mid.task_action: Developing my understanding of TPMs was fundamental to my ability to progress in my project development. Investing time into reading the prescribed chapters of the ‘Systems Engineering and Analysis’ textbook aided my understanding of what a TPM is and how they shape the basic testing/requirements for a new project and/or existing project. Investing in the textbook also provided the comprehensive and detailed steps of system design.      I completed a critical thinking course on LinkedIn learning which detailed the importance of critical thinking, developing the way of thinking and the application of such a mindset.      I then committed to honing in on the customers functional requirements and how these could be incorporated into an inclusive and effective product, by being specific but not overly ‘over-the-top’; e.g. real-world applications we can already see in automatic coffee makers and how this could be adapted to make tea instead.      Furthermore, applying the steps of preliminary design, conceptual design, testing etc. to my own project allowed me to actively practice critical thinking in development as well as troubleshooting.
mid.result: Conclusively, I have been able to design and conceptually develop a concept which addresses consumer requirements. Through my use of critical thinking, I have been able to adapt to inconsistencies that were presented through each step within the design process. Working around potential flaws and devising alternative approaches are all a product of my further developed critical thinking skills.      Had I not engaged in such growth, design and development of my independent project may have been stunted by inability to devise/innovate ideas and/or address flaws whilst also working to mitigate inconsistencies within the system.

mid.situation: During my internship at an engineering firm, we were tasked with developing a new industrial process that aimed to increase production efficiency. However, the proposed process involved the use of chemicals that could potentially harm the environment if not managed properly. This situation raised significant ethical, integrity, and social responsibility concerns, as we had to balance the efficiency gains with the potential environmental impact.      TASK: My responsibility was to lead a team in assessing the environmental risks associated with the new process and to develop a plan that would mitigate these risks while still achieving the efficiency goals. This involved conducting a thorough environmental impact assessment and exploring alternative methods or technologies that could reduce potential harm.      ACTION: I initiated a comprehensive review of the proposed process, collaborating with environmental experts to analyze the potential risks and impacts. We conducted simulations and modelled various scenarios to understand the extent of environmental damage that could occur. Based on this analysis, we developed a set of recommendations that included changes to the process, investment in cleaner technologies, and the implementation of strict waste management and pollution control measures. I presented these findings and recommendations to the senior management and stakeholders, advocating for the necessary changes to uphold our ethical and social responsibilities.
mid.result: The outcome of this initiative was the adoption of an environmentally responsible process that minimized the negative impacts while still improving efficiency. The firm implemented the recommended cleaner technologies and waste management practices, which resulted in a significant reduction in potential environmental damage. Additionally, this approach strengthened the company's commitment to corporate social responsibility and ethical practices, enhancing its reputation in the industry. My role in this process not only contributed to a sustainable engineering solution but also underscored the importance of integrating ethics and social responsibility into the core of engineering decision-making.

mid.situation: In a project I was involved in, our team was tasked with optimizing the cooling system of an industrial machine. We observed that the machine was overheating frequently, leading to shutdowns and decreased productivity. The existing cooling system was not effectively dissipating heat, primarily due to inadequate fluid dynamics within the coolant circuit.      TASK: My responsibility was to lead the analysis and redesign of the cooling system to improve its efficiency and prevent the machine from overheating. This required a thorough understanding of fluid dynamics principles to ensure optimal flow and heat transfer within the system.      ACTION: I started by conducting a detailed analysis of the existing system, including flow rate measurements and temperature profiling to identify the bottlenecks in the coolant circuit. Using computational fluid dynamics (CFD) software, I simulated various modifications to the system's design, such as altering pipe diameters, adjusting flow rates, and incorporating more efficient heat exchangers. Based on these simulations, I proposed a redesigned cooling system with optimized fluid paths and improved heat exchanger performance. My team and I then implemented the new design, carefully monitoring the installation and initial operation to ensure that it performed as expected.
mid.result: The redesigned cooling system significantly enhanced the machine's performance. The overheating issue was resolved, leading to a 30% reduction in downtime. Additionally, the improved efficiency of the cooling process resulted in a 15% decrease in energy consumption, saving costs for the company. The success of this project not only demonstrated the importance of applying fluid dynamics knowledge in practical engineering solutions but also contributed to increased productivity and operational savings for the client.

mid.situation: For our ENGG3050 class, we had to come up with pain points that we received from other students. This meant we needed to find out what was troubling our peers, how bad it is and how frequently they encounter it, and probe them to discover the root cause. We were interviewing multiple people so that we would have many opinions on what is hurting university students at the moment.
mid.task_action: The way we obtained all of these pain points was by surveying random students and asking them questions. We would keep delving deeper and deeper into why they were feeling certain ways and why things were happening so that we could get them to realise the true reason for this problem. We put them all on iLearn so that we could compare everyone's pain points and what the causes were, meaning we had the chance to see how similarly everyone felt and what parts of university life needed to be restructured to accommodate for these issues.      Later on, we had a new task to dig even deeper into a specific category of pain point by coming up with numerous strategies (in different categories such as administrative or software solutions) that students could employ to make their situation better. We drew on our own experiences, comparing ideas and feelings about the pain points we found so that we could come up with the best strategies, while also using them for ourselves.
mid.result: As a result of this exercise and our approach to it, we ended up with an incredibly extensive list on iLearn of what other people in the same situations as us have been feeling bad about. We also were given an Excel sheet of many strategies from our peers of how to deal with the groupings of pain points. This not only connected the student body among engineering students because we have all of this information on how everyone is feeling, but we are also more connected with people outside of our discipline as we got to talk to them and have nice conversations that we relate to.

mid.situation: As part of a group assessment, I was assigned with answering whether certification was necessary for cyber experts or job training would suffice.
mid.task_action: As I collected information regarding the topic, I thought back to one of the Udemy courses I completed, which described presenting your data with a storyline. As such, I searched through the information to find a storyline. This led me to look deeper into the question, with an investigation into both the content of certifications that can currently be achieved, the total number of internet users, and the total cybercrime incidents that have occurred over time. This created additional data that I looked into, leading me to the discovery that both certification and job training is necessary to stay focused and up-to-date with future cybercrime. I then correlated this information to better create a storyline for my information.
mid.result: I was able to present my findings in a way that began with a look into what certifications provide, then what job training achieves. I then asked the question of which was more necessary, using my data and graphs on cybercrime and total users as the climax before finishing the presentation with my findings. This resulted in a satisfying presentation for myself, while seeing the engagement from my peers as I presented.

mid.situation: The members of our team at ENG3000 came from a variety of backgrounds, including different genders, ethnicities, and professional experiences.
mid.task_action: It was important to me that all members of my team would have an equal voice on the important decisions we needed to take. So, the first thing I did was to facilitate an open discussion with all team members present. In the discussion, each person would share her or his thoughts about the campaign and their suggestions/ideas on the content and channel strategy. I made sure that everyone would have an equal opportunity to speak by paying close attention to what was being shared while refraining from adding on more thoughts/ideas. The other part of my tactic was to introduce a rotation structure whereby various team members took turns to facilitate a brainstorming session in each weekly team gathering, as well as greater decision-making. This would help us to tap on the diversity of expertise in the team and create a sense of inclusion and ownership for all. Another tactic I used was to delegate tasks based on the specific skills/interests of each person, rather than their background or status in the organisation (such as university rank/hierarchy).
mid.result: Our team created a completely functional cube as a consequence of our inclusive strategy. We were able to combine many ideas and points of view to create innovative approaches. Our combined efforts also increased team cohesion and morale, which increased productivity and increased team members' job satisfaction. When everything was said and done, the environment in which we worked and the quality of our work were enhanced by our commitment to diversity and justice.

mid.situation: I ONCE WORKED AS A PART-TIME RETAIL CONSULTANT, ONE WEEKEND SHIFT I HAD I SPENT A SIGNIFICANT PORTION OF THE DAY ALONE AT THE REGISTERS. ONE CUSTOMER ATTEMPTED TO RETURN OR EXCHANGE THINGS THAT DIDN'T BELONG IN OUR STORE AT THE BUSIEST TIME OF DAY IN THE HOPES OF USING THE MONEY TO PAY FOR OTHER ITEMS THEY WANTED TO BUY. THOUGH THESE THINGS WERE PURPORTEDLY PURCHASED THREE WEEKS AGO, THE CLIENT HAS OBVIOUSLY DONE THIS KIND OF THING BEFORE, AS SEEN BY THE TAGS AND RECEIPTS REMAINING ATTACHED.
mid.task_action: Fortunately, I had expertise with this kind of thing, so I was able to recognize that something did not match up, even if the items appeared to most associates to be properly tagged and genuine objects that belonged to us. Since I had no evidence to support my claim that this was fraud of any type, I initially handled this as though it were a legitimate refund. I looked up the items using the receipt to confirm that they were, in fact, the ones we sold and that their descriptions matched what I was looking at. From that point on, it was clear that the products were fake since they were marked as being much too cheap for what they were, the descriptions in our system did not correspond at all with what I had in front of me, and I had taken the actual items off the floor and put them in front of the customer to demonstrate this. I told the customer that I could not complete the transaction on their behalf because it would be unethical and I would lose my job, and that these products were not ours to return. They attempted to explain to me, in a very hostile and combative way, that they had experienced a similar event in another store, but that time they had received a refund, so if they could make it happen there, they should be able to accomplish it here as well. I made sure to conduct myself in a kind and professional manner during the entire exchange because I didn't want to upset a customer in any way, especially during such a busy time of the week. Because I was merely informing them in the most courteous manner possible that I was unable to reimburse those products, it became more and more difficult for them to work with me. This only served to enrage them. It had escalated much too much by then, so it was obvious that I should have approached my management for assistance much earlier. When I phoned my store manager, they effectively told me that the customer was acting unethically and that they should leave, threatening to call centre security due to their actions against me.
mid.result: When my manager arrived, I had to leave the situation rather than allow it to emotionally impact me since, even though I had followed all the procedures up until then, they would not listen to me. I just continued working from that point on, going down to the next register and tending to the next person in line.

mid.situation: I was working on my race motorbike, in my garage. I was completing a routine, but last minute service, before a big event when I noticed something concerning. The fuel line leading to the injector seemed a little loose, and upon closer inspection, a small crack in the fuel line was visible. Eventually this could potentially cause engine problems or even a fire hazard, but regardless, was certainly not ready to be riding in race conditions the next day.       TASK: Replacing the fuel line seemed straightforward enough. However, a quick online search revealed that the part will be expensive, and not something I could source in time for the event.      ACTION:  I rummaged through my dad's old toolbox, hoping to find a spare fuel line or something close. I found a length of fuel hose that looked similar but slightly larger in diameter. Adapting to the Challenge: Knowing a larger hose wouldn't fit the injector nozzle directly, I looked at the internet again. My research led me to discover a solution a special kind of fuel line clamps. These clamps could potentially secure the larger hose to the existing injector nozzle. I ventured to a supercheap auto,  with the measurements of the hose and nozzle. After some trial and error I found a perfect match.
mid.result: The process took longer than a simple fuel line swap, but the outcome was a success. Using the larger hose and the appropriate clamps, I managed to create a secure and leak-proof connection between the fuel line and the injector nozzle. The fix worked. After a thorough inspection for any leaks, I was stoked to finally hear the engine come back to life, and the joy of a successful repair, fueled by perseverance, was unmatched.

mid.situation: One of our customers called up informing us a delivery he ordered had been incorrectly packed. As I was the one who answered the phone i had to rectify this problem and send out the correct products which the customer required.
mid.task_action: The end goal of an employee at my company is to ensure everything we do creates a positive experience for our customers. By quickly solving the problem at hand we can maximise our chances that the customer will remain happy and continue shopping with us. This is especially important with many competing suppliers creating a situation in which the customer does not come back.      Before i delt with this pain point i immediately placed myself in a position of empathy to the customer while on the phone with him. This gave the appearance that i cared about the customer's problem and i understand the dilemma he is facing.      As soon as i got off the phone with the customer i opened our sales screen and organised a delivery to the address provided. I found the product which was missing from the order and keyed it into the system. I then went straight to our delivery coordinator and asked him if we could get a delivery sent straight out. He obliged.       Our delivery coordinator then spoke with our driver who retrieved the item and expedited the delivery to the customer's location.
mid.result: The customer called up a few hours after the delivery saying thank you for the speedy process in getting the situation rectified. He is still a regular customer of ours and we've actually seen an increase in sales from him in recent times. I believe this may be due to us appearing more trustworthy.       By dealing with the pain point quickly and efficiently it gave the customer confidence in our company and showed that we will do what we can to solve any potential problems our customers may face.

mid.situation: In a project competition for autonomous droid racing in one of my societies, I encountered a critical decision point where we needed to choose between 3 design/manufacturing options pertaining to the chassis as it would affect the placement of batteries and other hardware.       Task: The task at hand was to analyze the available options thoroughly, considering the potential impact on project timelines, paint points, and success. Given the uncertainty in the narrowed down pool of choices, it was essential to take the initiative and make a well-informed choice that would lead us to achieve the project.      Action: To address the situation, I took the initiative to lead a short analysis of all options, gathering input from team members while keeping the requirements of the competition in mind. I facilitated discussions to explore the pros and cons of each design choice and in the end chose the option which would be the most doable in shortest amount of time and without bias for our collective "favorite idea" or any one person's idea so that we can consider improvements or other options if the solution doesn't work. The best course of action was to go with the option that we could quickly prototype during that same day as opposed to fleshing out design options over the week, requiring more resources and delaying the project further.
mid.result: The decision-making process enabled us to move forward on a design option with clarity and purpose, mitigating indecision and potential delays. By taking decisive action in the face of uncertainty helped maintain momentum forward in our project timeline. Furthermore, the chosen design option proved to be successful while still leaving room for improvement. Overall, the experience highlighted the value of initiative and the willingness to make decisions, even when faced with uncertainty, in driving project success.

mid.situation: I had a case study due for ENGG3050 which I was not able to complete on the day it was due, hence I received an extension until Tuesday 26th of March.
mid.task_action: The goal of the case study was to answer questions for the which the information was provided in 3 articles. All three articles were about the General Electric company, each being focused on a different topic such the “evolution of strategic planning at GE”, “Jack Welch’s creative revolution of GE”, and the “successful implementation of Six Sigma at GE”. There were 3 main questions with multiple parts, and they specified which document to extract the required information from for each question.      Looking back, I had greatly underestimated the amount of time and effort it would take to complete this task and I had proceeded to focus on complete other assessment tasks. I had initially presumed it would take me one day to complete the task, which lead me to waiting until Sunday (the due date) to start working on the assignment whilst I completed an assessment for my MTRN4066 unit. As I was getting closer to the due date, my fatigued and tired self was unable to properly start the case study and I was forced to ask for an extension.
mid.result: The result of me asking for extension, which was until Tuesday night (2 days), changed my university and life schedule for following days. I skipped all my lecture classes because I was worried and anxious about completing the assessment on time and I ended up spending far more time in front of the computer than was healthy. I did manage to complete the assignment in time, however I missed a few questions where I did not understand (because of my compounded stress) or I did not care to complete because I had other questions to complete (there was one question with multiple parts, and the entire question was worth 1 mark).

mid.situation: In this reflection, I hope to go over the the task assigned to us in the MECH3003 Gearbox Project where the teams were asked to design a robust and efficient gearbox for a space crane intended to lift up to 28kg to operate on the moon. The project was complex and involved about 4 subsystems which included the gears, shafts, bearings and the casing. We were a group of 3 team members with my subsystem being gears. Early in the project, multiple challenges were faced in coordinating all our efforts and analysis in order to make sure everyone was on the same page.
mid.task_action: Although not assigned specifically as a team leader, I stood up to challenge to ensure effective collaboration amongst each other and streamline the design process and achieve the required goals within the set timeline. And for this, quick and effective communication, eagerness to jump in and help needed and regular updates was an important part. There was a clear tasks allocation, where each team member specifically worked on the analysis of their subsystem and used shared drives and messenger to have constant communication and updated files. I also made sure that all the design decisions and progress were documented which allowed for better traceability and therefor also allowed everyone to have a clear idea of what is required of them in the tasks. Moreover, these documents allowed me to provide the required feedback and helped others make a more informed design decision and optimizations which reduced cost but increase performance of our gearbox.
mid.result: The use of these techniques created a positive team dynamic where open communication and regular feedback not only from me but from each other allowed the team to be self sustaining where I could step back and still have tasks completed. This also enhanced our collaboration where everyone felt they had made an impact through their contributions and increased the quality of work which resulted in a gearbox that exceeded the requirements and passed the testing phase by meeting the required TPMs. This experience reinforced the importance of effective team leadership in managing complex engineering projects. By fostering a collaborative environment, clearly defining roles and responsibilities, and facilitating open communication, I was able to guide my team to successfully achieve our project objectives.

mid.situation: We needed to plan actions for each group member in order to make the RGM complete.
mid.task_action: Each group member was free to select the activities they wanted to employ for their part by exercising critical thinking and effective communication. This involves making plans for the tools they'll use and how they'll use them. For instance, an action was that I wanted to tie a string to a nerf gun so that when it fired, it would hit a ball, which would then topple dominoes. We had to work together to determine the angle of the nerf gun, the length of the string, the distance, and other details so that we could accomplish this flawlessly for our video. Safety was also planned, for example, the possibility that a nerf gun bullet can unintentionally strike someone's eye and so on.
mid.result: I think that with all of this preparation and the application of critical thinking, we were able to recognize potential problems and determine how to lessen them if they do arise. So, once the action is prepared, I will be able to complete it without difficulty.

mid.situation: We were assigned to design and create a marble machine with various mechanisms within it. The main challenge was to elevate the marble to an exit hole located on the top side of the box.
mid.task_action: We initially began to creatively think of ideas since various requirements needed to be met to ensure that our marble machine worked appropriately. We began to think of creative ideas since we were required to elevate the marble upwards since the existing hole was on the top side of the box.  We noted down each of the ideas said by each member of the group on paper and listed the pros and cons of each idea. We branched out the ideas in a mindmap and ranked each of the ideas from the most applicable to the least applicable. Further, we invited all the other groups for their input and had conversations with the structural group about the various designs and their production.
mid.result: The result was being able to produce a creative idea that could solve our issue of elevating the marble to the exit hole. Our chat with the structure team about our creative designs allowed them to formulate a mechanism that could meet the requirements and transport multiple marbles at once.

mid.situation: While working on the development of a cutting-edge project management software, our team discovered a number of features that had the capacity to significantly transform user engagement and enhance efficiency. Given the distinctiveness of these characteristics, we have taken into account the importance of obtaining intellectual property rights in order to safeguard our ideas and provide a competitive advantage. Nevertheless, our team was deficient in knowledge and experience regarding intellectual property and patent law. Additionally, we were uncertain about the current patents in this field and the appropriate steps to take in the patenting procedure.
mid.task_action: Our main objective was to conduct a comprehensive investigation of existing patents and ascertain the patentability of our software features. In order to tackle this issue, I took charge of a specialised sub-team assigned with carrying out an extensive patent search. We employed databases such as Google Patents, the United States Patent and Trademark Office (USPTO), and the World Intellectual Property Organisation (WIPO) to detect any preexisting patents that may coincide with our characteristics. Upon collecting and recording our discoveries, we sought the guidance of a patent attorney to assess our study, offer specialised counsel, and aid in the creation and submission of patent applications. In addition, we diligently created comprehensive documentation and technical illustrations of our groundbreaking features to bolster our patent applications, guaranteeing that all components of our work were fully elucidated and safeguarded.      Outcome: Our dedicated endeavours yielded multiple favourable results. The patent search indicated that although there were existing patents pertaining to project management software, our particular features were innovative and had not been patented. The patent attorney verified that our innovations satisfied the essential requirements for being granted a patent. As a result, we were able to effectively submit provisional patent applications for three crucial aspects of our programme. By taking this move, we ensured that our filing date was protected and we were given a one-year period to improve our invention and finalise the overall patent application. Furthermore, obtaining these temporary patents greatly improved the legitimacy of our project, making it more appealing to potential investors and partners who appreciated the additional safeguarding of our intellectual property. This experience enhanced our team's comprehension of intellectual property rights and the process of obtaining patents, arming us with essential knowledge to safeguard future inventions.

mid.situation: Our team was tasked with preparing a presentation on a newly founded company called Hypersonix.
mid.task_action: Despite facing a challenge in finding extensive information about Hypersonix due to its recent establishment, we approached the task with a positive attitude and saw it as an opportunity for learning. We initiated by conducting thorough research using all available resources, including online databases, news articles, and the company's official website. However, the limited information posed a challenge in crafting a comprehensive presentation.      To tackle this, we organised brainstorming sessions where team members shared their insights and perspectives on the available information. We encouraged open dialogue and creativity to explore potential angles and themes for the presentation. Additionally, one of our team members attended a networking event where they had the opportunity to meet the Chief Financial Officer (CFO) of Hypersonix. This encounter proved invaluable as they engaged in a fruitful conversation, gaining valuable insights and information directly from a key figure within the company.
mid.result: Despite the initial struggle to gather sufficient data, our positive attitude and collaborative effort paid off. We were able to piece together enough information, including the insights gained from the networking event, to create a compelling presentation on Hypersonix. Our approach of leveraging each team member's strengths and perspectives, combined with the firsthand information obtained from the CFO, led to a well-rounded analysis of the company's potential, despite the limited data available. Furthermore, the process of navigating through the challenge enhanced our research and analytical skills, underscoring the value of maintaining a positive attitude and embracing learning opportunities in the face of obstacles.

mid.situation: In the week 7 SGTA I had to answer questions based on intellectual property and patent law.
mid.task_action: There were a variety of questions to answer, on the topics:       	* Commercial Pathways in need of protection   	* Trademarking, Copyrighting, Patenting, Industrial Designs, Trade Secrets   	* Advantages, Disadvantages and Limitations of different IP protection methods   	* IP laws in multiple jurisdictions   	* Patent validity and working around claims      Additionally, I had to think about how I would take a hypothetical product to market in terms of IP protection.
mid.result: By researching and completing the questions, I was able to gain a thorough understanding and provide high quality information relating to the topics mentioned above. In the terms of my product, I was able to provide a suitable market approach for an iPhone application involving trademarking slogans and logos and patenting a gaming system.

mid.situation: In my second year at university, one of our assessments for the engineering design course required us to create a Rube Goldberg machine (RGM). The objective was to design a complex, multi-step machine that performed a simple task, such as turning off a light switch, in an overly complicated and creative manner. This project aimed to test our understanding of various mechanical principles while fostering teamwork and creative thinking.      TASK: Our goal was to design and construct an RGM that could successfully turn off a light switch using at least ten different steps. My role was to lead the brainstorming sessions, coordinate the construction efforts, and ensure that each step of the machine was innovative and functional.      ACTION: To start the project, I organised a brainstorming session with my team. We used the "mind mapping" technique to generate a wide range of ideas for each step of the machine. This allowed us to visualise the overall process and think creatively about how to link different mechanical actions. Each team member contributed unique ideas, and we encouraged free-flowing discussion to explore all possible options. We identified and selected the most promising ideas by considering their feasibility, complexity, and how effectively they could be integrated into the overall machine. After compiling our ideas, we created a detailed plan for the sequence of steps. Each team member was responsible for constructing specific components, such as a domino cascade, a marble run, and a pulley system. Throughout the construction phase, we conducted regular testing and refinement to ensure each step worked seamlessly. We made several adjustments to improve reliability and ensure the machine's smooth operation from start to finish.
mid.result: The result was a successful Rube Goldberg machine that turned off the light switch through a series of ten intricate and interconnected steps. Our machine included a combination of mechanical actions, such as a marble triggering a series of dominoes, a toy car rolling down a ramp, and a pulley system that ultimately pulled the switch. The project not only improved our technical and problem-solving skills but also demonstrated the importance of teamwork and creative thinking in engineering. Reflecting on this experience, I learned the value of collaboration and open-mindedness in tackling complex challenges. It reinforced the importance of creative thinking in engineering projects and encouraged me to continue seeking innovative solutions in my future endeavours. This project also highlighted the joy and satisfaction of turning imaginative ideas into functional, tangible results.

mid.situation: I currently working on a construction project with site team of 8 people and we're currently doing a refurbishment and fit out to all all interiors of a hotel in the CBD.
mid.task_action: One of the many things the client has tasked us with is to update/replace all existing guestroom evacuation plan signage to ensure the hotel signage is compliant with todays signage standards upon handover of all floors to the client. So I was tasked with finding a suitable subcontractor which specialises in the trade and meets our given budget for the trade. Once this was completed and the contract was awarded to them, shop drawing development for the guestroom signage was underway. I was sure to send through all relevant floor plans and other drawings required as well as dimensions of existing signage and peepholes as the sign was to be installed on the back of the entry door and to be fit so that the back of the peephole could sit over the top of it. Once this was done I was then sent the first iteration of shop drawings for the sign which I reviewed and provided comments to areas which required amendments. The second revision of shop drawings was then sent through and approved by myself, this meant that production of signs could begin. One week later the subcontractor was on site installing the first batch of signs to the first two floors which were to be handed over to the client, four weeks from then.
mid.result: Once signage installation was complete my team and I conducted a defect walk to both floors to ensure a smooth transition into hand over. During which time I discovered that the signs they had installed were from the first revision of drawings and not of the agreed upon revision of drawings. Unfortunately at this time subcontractor handover was a day away so time was running out very quickly. So almost immediately once realised I conducted various discussions of negotiation with the subcontractor where it was later agreed on that they had made a mistake and as a result would have to reprint the signs to match the correct shop drawings at their own cost as soon as possible. Unfortunately whilst this dilemma of having to potentially double costs on signage for these two floors was resolved there was still the issue of meeting the clients hand over date. Luckily my team and I were running ahead of schedule and were forecasted to handover these two floors earlier than agreed on but unfortunately this information was already disclosed with the client. Thankfully after very thorough negotiation and compromise the client agreed to allow for a slightly longer defect period to rectify all issues we experienced which meant that the new signs were installed at no additional cost and still in line with the original agreed upon handover date.

mid.situation: As a Quality Assurance (QA) engineer at Zeiss, a company specializing in ophthalmology, we encountered a significant issue with one of our new ophthalmic devices. The device was failing certain critical quality tests, leading to delays in its approval and potential release. This issue risked not only the timeline of the product launch but also the company's reputation for high-quality medical devices.      TASK: My task was to identify the root cause of the quality failures, implement corrective actions, and ensure the device met all regulatory standards and quality benchmarks. This required a detailed analysis of the device's production and testing processes, followed by strategic adjustments to address the identified problems.      ACTION: I began by organizing a cross-functional team meeting with engineers, production staff, and quality control personnel to discuss the failures and gather insights. We used the Fishbone Diagram (Ishikawa) method to systematically identify potential causes of the quality issues, such as materials, methods, machines, and human factors.      Once potential causes were identified, I led the team in conducting a thorough root cause analysis using the 5 Whys technique. This helped us pinpoint the primary cause of the failures: a specific calibration issue with the testing equipment that was causing inaccurate readings.      To address this, we took several actions:   Calibration protocoL: We revised the calibration protocol for the testing equipment, ensuring it met stricter standards and was calibrated more frequently.   Training: I organized additional meetings with FSE to ensure they were well-versed in the new calibration procedures and aware of the critical checkpoints.   Process audit: We conducted a comprehensive audit of the production and testing processes to identify any other potential issues and ensure all procedures were followed correctly.   Implementation of control procedure: To monitor the quality of the devices in real-time, we implemented SPC tools. This allowed for early detection of deviations and immediate corrective actions.
mid.result: The changes led to a significant improvement in the quality of the ophthalmic devices. The failure rates in critical tests dropped by 85%, and we were able to meet all regulatory standards well ahead of the revised schedule. The improved calibration protocol and process controls also ensured consistent device quality in subsequent production batches.      The successful resolution of this issue not only allowed us to proceed with the product launch as planned but also reinforced our commitment to quality, earning positive feedback from regulatory bodies and enhancing our reputation in the ophthalmology industry.

mid.situation: AT MY RETAIL JOB DURING THE BUSY HOLIDAY SEASON, OUR STORE'S CUSTOMER SATISFACTION RATINGS WERE DROPPING. CUSTOMERS WERE COMPLAINING ABOUT NOT GETTING ENOUGH HELP AND FEELING IGNORED BY THE STAFF. OUR MANAGER WAS CONCERNED AND ASKED EVERYONE TO IMPROVE OUR CUSTOMER SERVICE.      TASK:      As a team member, my task was to help improve customer satisfaction by being more helpful and positive with customers, learning more about our products, and encouraging my teammates to do the same.      ACTION:       To address this, I took the following actions:      	*   Set an Example: I made sure to greet every customer warmly and offer help right away. I wanted to show my teammates how we should be treating customers.      	*   Shared Tips: During breaks, simple tips were shared between my co-workers and I about how to engage with customers better. We encouraged each other to be more friendly and attentive.      	*   Learned About Products: I spent extra time learning about the products we sold. I shared this knowledge with my teammates so we could all give better advice to customers.
mid.result: The results of these actions were clear:      	*   Better Attitudes: My teammates and I started being more positive and proactive with customers. We all worked together to improve our service.      	*   Happier Customers: Customers were happier with the help they received. We got more compliments and fewer complaints.      	*   Increased Confidence: We felt more confident about the products we were selling. This made it easier to help customers find what they needed.      	*   Improved Ratings: Our customer satisfaction ratings went up. The manager received positive feedback about the friendly and helpful service.      	*   Stronger Teamwork: Our team became more supportive and cooperative. We learned from each other and worked better together.      By improving our attitudes, learning more about our products, and working as a team, we were able to make customers happier and improve our store's performance.

mid.situation: In our group project presentation, each team member was responsible for presenting a different topic on the same company. As the presentation deadline approached, we couldn’t decide which topic to assign to each member due to overlapping interests and expertise. We needed to negotiate and reach a compromise to allocate topics effectively while ensuring that each member had an opportunity to contribute meaningfully.      TASK: We had to overcome the difficulty of dividing up the presentation topics among the team members while taking into account their individual preferences and strong points. To resolve any disputes and guarantee a fair result, effective negotiation, compromise, and conflict resolution were crucial.      ACTION:      I initiated a team meeting to discuss the allocation of topics for the presentation. I encouraged open dialogue and invited each team member to share their preferences and areas of expertise.      I actively listened to my team members' preferences regarding the topics they were most interested in presenting. By understanding their individual strengths and interests, we could align topic assignments accordingly.      We engaged in negotiations to allocate topics based on a combination of individual preferences, expertise, and the need for a balanced distribution of workload. We were willing to compromise and adjust our initial preferences to ensure fairness and effectiveness in the presentation.

mid.situation: During my first semester as an international engineering student, I faced significant challenges in managing my time and resources. I was enrolled in four units which comprised of lectures, compulsory practical and SGTA classes.The combination of academic pressure and adjusting to life in a new country led to me frequently missing assignment deadlines. This not only impacted my grades but also increased my stress levels and made it difficult to keep up with the course material.             TASK: My task was to develop and implement a strategy to manage my time and resources more effectively. This included ensuring I could meet all my academic deadlines and commitments while also adapting to the new environment and its demands.             ACTION: To address this issue, I started by assessing all my commitments and deadlines, ranking tasks based on urgency and importance. I created a detailed weekly schedule using Google Calendar, allocating specific times for studying, attending classes, and completing assignments, along with personal time. I took short breaks of at least 20 minutes in between my study session. I also joined study groups, visited numeracy centre frequently for my most challenging courses to benefit from collective knowledge and reduce the time spent struggling with difficult concepts alone. Additionally, I set weekly reviews to track my progress and make necessary adjustments to my schedule and priorities to stay on track.
mid.result: These actions led to a significant improvement in my academic performance. I was able to submit assignments on time and adequately prepare for exams. My study load became more manageable, and I felt less overwhelmed by my responsibilities. This reduced my stress levels and enhanced my productivity, allowing me to feel more in control of my time and better prepared for future challenges in my engineering studies.

mid.situation: Our Rube Goldberg Machine (RGM) required precise adjustments during a number of intricate phases of our project to ensure its proper functioning. The original design was not able to function as anticipated due to frequent failures in some areas. It became apparent that we needed to figure out which stages to modify and how to adjust them in order to improve our project.   TASK:      As a team, we needed to identify the problematic steps and determine the exact changes required to enhance the machine's performance. My role was to lead the effort in collecting and analyzing data to guide these decisions, ensuring that any modifications were based on factual evidence rather than guesswork.   ACTION:      I initiated a comprehensive data collection process, meticulously recording the performance of each step over multiple trials. We measured variables such as timings, angles, and the required force for each step. Using this data, I created a detailed spreadsheet to identify the steps that were deviating from our plans. Next, I collaborated with my team to brainstorm potential modifications for the identified steps. We conducted controlled experiments, adjusting one variable at a time and recording the corresponding results. This approach enabled us to isolate the impact of each change and make data-driven decisions on the optimal modifications.
mid.result: We were able to make exact adjustments that considerably improved the performance of our RGM by relying on valid data. The machine performed well at every stage, and the improvements resulted in a 75% decrease in failure rates. The successful execution of a data-driven approach not only contributed to the success of our project but also emphasized the significance of evidence-based decision-making in complex projects.

Aside from conducting reviews of current rules, we took the initiative to conduct proactive research on developing trends and best practices in regulatory compliance specific to the engineering business. mid.result: Due to our proactive strategy in dealing with the regulation of engineering in society, our project team successfully maneuvered through the intricate regulatory environment with assurance and precision. We effectively detected and resolved any compliance issues before their potential impact on project schedules or financial resources. Our meticulous documentation and strict adherence to industry regulations have bolstered our project's image among both industry professionals and regulatory authorities, ultimately leading to its successful implementation and long-term viability.

mid.situation: I mentor a high school FRC robotics team and we needed to prototype a wheeled intake mechanism to pick up rings off of the floor for a large-scale robotics project
mid.task_action: Firstly we started brainstorming and developing ideas based on how to approach the design, looking at existing solutions that other teams had already developed as well as real work solutions to alike problems. From there we identified common occurrences between designs and focused our attention on those aspects. In our case, I highlighted that the distance between the wheels and the distance from the floor were key dimensions that we had to tune perfectly. By identifying this, I then got the students to design modular testing rigs to test different heights from the floor, and different distances between wheels (for different compression) to obtain the most efficient and effective intake behaviour.  The modular test rigs also allowed the students to explore multiple options for different wheels to see which wheels had a better time griping the rings as well as any other positive or negative behaviour. By doing so, the students were able to notice and document that the rings failed to pick up with too small or too large distances between wheels and the floor, and it extremely succeeded when there was slight compression on the ring due to a slightly smaller distance between the wheels than the ring's outer thickness. Furthermore, the students noticed that with the same compression, rigid wheels worked relatively well, however, softer squishy compliant wheels worked exceptionally well as once the wheels touched the ring nothing was stopping it from being acquired by the intake mechanism. The students also noticed that the compliant wheels could vector the ring laterally toward the centre of the mechanism if the ring was caught on the outer edge of the intake mechanism. This would ultimately prove extremely powerful for the final design as it could reduce the time taken to pick up a ring due to misalignment of the mechanism.
mid.result: The result was that we tested all of our wheels and identified the most optimal ones to design the mechanism around, as well as measured and documented the appropriate key dimensions that would assist in designing the final mechanism design.

mid.situation: In the SGTAs we discussed and brainstormed various pain points that fellow students have experienced on Macquarie University’s Campus. These questions would involve things like timetabling, study spaces, studying habits, work life balance or availability and access to a variety of facilities.
mid.task_action: After discussing the many pain points that students would experience on campus, we had to formulate our own questions in order to gather data about the pain points. Hence we were tasked to interview and record responses from other students with our formulated questions. We asked students a variety of related questions about how they felt about a certain pain point and ask them on scale of 1 to 5 on how painful it was and how often it occurred. In addition we recorded a detailed description of the pain paint and it’s root cause. After interviewing and gathering responses from these students we had to consolidate our findings into a database.
mid.result: During the following week we discussed our findings to the class and talked about our chosen pain paint and how we approach asking students about it. We would then come up with 10 solutions that would resolve or alleviate the pain point. We had to come up with solutions within 3 categories which were software, administrative and hardware. Software solutions were typically used to provide innovative solutions, for example a study space booking system can be implemented to ensure that students who want to study on campus can reserve a space at a specific time. Administrative solutions would include extending operating hours in study spaces during peak times such as exam time. Hardware solutions would typically provide physical improvements to the environment, for example implementing sturdy soundproof rooms in libraries and study spaces or outdoor study areas.
