mid.situation: During the third workshop in my COMP2250 course, we were given a dataset on statistical patterns and alloted to conduct speculation testing. This circumstance was basic in understanding how to utilise measurable strategies to approve our discoveries and guard them against examination. The workshop was expecting to recreate real-world information examination scenarios in which data-driven choices are basic.
mid.task_action: The task was to develop a hypothesis about the dataset and test it with appropriate statistical methods. I specifically hypothesised that there is a significant difference in average income between two demographic groups in the dataset. The goal was to use hypothesis testing techniques to confirm or reject this hypothesis, ensuring that our analysis was rigorous and statistically sound.      To begin this task, I conducted an exploratory data analysis (EDA) to become acquainted with the dataset and identify key variables and potential outliers. I then developed both the null hypothesis (no significant difference in average income) and the alternative hypothesis (significant difference in average income). Using Python, I used statistical libraries like pandas for data manipulation and sciPy to perform a t-test to compare the two groups' means. Throughout the process, I meticulously documented my steps and ensured that my code was repeatable and simple to understand. I also consulted with peers and instructors to confirm my approach and interpretations.
mid.result: The analysis revealed a statistically significant difference in average incomes between the two demographic groups, resulting in the rejection of the null hypothesis. This result confirmed my hypothesis and provided useful information about potential socioeconomic disparities. The successful application of hypothesis testing not only validated our methodological approach, but also demonstrated the value of statistical analysis in drawing meaningful conclusions from data. The results were well received during the workshop presentation, demonstrating the practical value of rigorous data analysis and hypothesis testing in real-world scenarios.

mid.situation: During the Internet Tea Maker design process, my team and I tried to incorporate everyone's unique ideas into our machine. However, that diverted us from one of the main goals of the final product: Affordability and staying within the budget.
mid.task_action: The groundwork and brainstorming were the most exciting parts of planning the design for the machine. It was exhilarating to exchange unique ideas among ourselves! We made it our ultimate goal to have one of the distinctive tea makers and integrate a unique functionality from each person, for instance: Smart Water Filter, Interactive Recipe Sharing, Tea Sommelier Mode, Multi-Compartment Infuser etc. We found it difficult to rule out any of these amazing features, so we decided to incorporate everything.       This made the designing process complex and lengthy. As we progressed into Test and Alternatives segment of the decision-making process, we realized our budget had skyrocketed, making it impossible to accommodate all those features with the budget constraint.
mid.result: As a result, we had to make alternative arrangements and rule out features that were too expensive. This was a significant setback and prolonged our process. Firstly, we decided to cross-check and understand the key features expected of the final product. We made a list of goals and labelled the priority levels. Secondly, we explored alternative approached where we searched for options from different suppliers and focused on cost efficiency. Finally, we updated the whole design.

mid.situation: This semester, I was assigned a project to design an internet-based tea maker with a group of strangers in my engineering class. The client had a few requests: they wanted an internet-controlled tea maker to ensure that they could prepare tea the way they preferred it when they woke up or arrived home. They desired features like the ability to score various tea types, try out new teas, and share tea styles with friends. We weren't really familiar with one another, however we engaged fully in brainstorming meetings. The broad scope and vague requirements created a challenging and overwhelming situation that required robust critical thinking skills to navigate.
mid.task_action: During a team project last month, the specific task that proved most challenging was defining the system's conceptual design while balancing the diverse and sometimes conflicting customer requirements. This involved developing system operational requirements, conducting exploratory studies, prioritizing technical performance measures (TPMs), and performing a system-level functional analysis.       Initially, there were conflicts among the group members. One team member argued that we should integrate the social elements first to satisfy the customer's minor desires, but I suggested concentrating on the key functionality to make sure we satisfied the major needs first. This disagreement created a tense and challenging atmosphere as we struggled to find common ground.      To resolve disagreements and ensure progress, I took the initiative to facilitate conversations inside the group and kept in constant contact with team members. Although there was a lot of energy in the room, I led brainstorming sessions where we evaluated the viability of each suggested addition. Despite the stress, we divided the work into two sub-teams, one of which was working on the user interface and internet connectivity and the other on integrating social elements. Sustaining alignment and subsystem integration with this technique necessitated ongoing supervision.       We collaborated to deal with the complexity utilising critical thinking despite our initial disagreement. I utilized my problem-solving skills to document our progress, ensuring every team member was on the same page and encouraging open communication to foster collaboration. We had made significant improvements in our conceptual design at the end of the session, which enabled the group to enhance its brainstorming and problem-solving techniques for subsequent meetings.
mid.result: We came up with a thorough conceptual design for the tea maker at the conclusion of that session. In order to avoid problems with integration, we thoroughly analysed each functional unit's interfaces and determined which design philosophy each one should adhere to. To provide assurance that the finished product would live up to client expectations, we made sure that every TPM could be tested successfully. The critical thinking applied throughout the process helped us navigate the complexities and conflicting requirements effectively.

mid.situation: We had to develop critical thinking skills to assist in our design of the different functions of the tea maker.
mid.task_action: Used a group collaborative effort to combine different disciplines' critical thinking approaches. The combination of different backgrounds and engineering disciplines allowed for a streamlined approach to the design phase; different people allowed for an overall more cohesive design and solved conflicting design choices.
mid.result: The result was the utilization of different approaches to critical thinking, allowing for better overall design choices and decisions. We further combined our different critical thinking approaches to create our own individual tea maker design.

mid.situation: During the semester, we were tasked with designing an IoT tea maker. Initially I created a preliminary design based on general assumptions about consumer needs. However, after a survey was conducted to gather specific consumer requirements, it became clear that the initial design did not fully meet these new expectations. This required a significant revision of the design to incorporate the updated consumer needs.
mid.task_action: The challenge was to quickly adapt and revise the existing design to align with the newly identified consumer requirements. This needed to be done without losing my initial unique idea but still ensuring that the final product would meet the consumer requirements.      I dedicated extra hours, outside of the STGA to rework the design, staying late to ensure every requirement was considered and integrated correctly. Despite feeling overwhelmed at times, I persevered to meet the new requirements.      I maintained a study schedule, setting personal deadlines for different parts of the project, including the consideration of the consumer requirements, to ensure a calm and steady progress to the overall project timeline.      I utilized various resources such as previous innovations of this kind and online forums to gather insights and solutions for the new design challenge. Determined to utilizes as many resources to my advantage to get the design finalized before the coming weeks.      I adapted my design approach to stay flexible and incorporate the new requirements, being open to pivoting from my initial ideas and experimenting with different design until the optimal solution was found.
mid.result: The revised design has continued throughout the semester to successfully met the consumer requirements. The final product will now hopefully be better received by markers after the enhanced functionality and user-centric features of the IoT tea maker. I felt a profound sense of accomplishment and pride in overcoming the challenges and being able to adapt within an engineering environment. This experience not only improved the projects outcome but also demonstrated my ability to handle significant changes under pressure.

mid.situation: We were expected to independently analyse real-world engineering problems and provide workable solutions for the ENGG2050 unit. This was a big change from the coursework we usually did, which consisted mostly of group projects or so not complicated scenarios. The assignment required the application of theoretical knowledge to real world situations as well as a thorough awareness of limits and defects in real world scenarios.      Task:   It was quite complicated to find answers to the questions about the real life engineering scenarios at first as finding the data and information and doing extensive study on it was quite challenging and to fully analise and find possible answers. Not only did this endeavour had technical competence, but it also required the ability to handle complicated real-world variables including societal repercussions, regulatory obligations, and economic restrictions and need to be taken as factors while answering the questions.      Action:      To manage this task effectively and critically and came up with multiple strategies such as data gathering, critical thinking, creativity and etc. I began by carrying out a thorough investigation to obtain relevant data in order to guarantee the accuracy and reliability of the material gathered to answer the questions. I took care to use reliable sources and mostly data provided to us. I used critical thinking throughout the procedure to assess the data critically and come to reasonable outcomes. Understanding the value of creative thinking, I used creative problem solving strategies to create distinctive and successful solutions. I considered both standard and unusual tactics while brainstorming for ideas. By using these strategies I was able to overcome the problems and come up with solutions.
mid.result: THE EXPERIENCE WAS BOTH DIFFICULT AND ENJOYABLE AS I WAS ABLE TO IMPROVE IN MANY OF MY SKILLS WHICH WAS DATA GATHERING, THINKING CRITICALLY AND CREATIVELY AND BY DOING SO I WAS ALSO ABLE TO HANDLE THE PROBLEM AND WAS ABLE TO PROVIDE REASONABLE ANSWERS.

mid.result: By taking these steps, I could complete my infographic on time and make it visually appealing whilst also having enough information to satisfy the assignment requirements.

This however would not have been needed if adequate preparations were made before undertaking the quiz.
mid.result: In a challenging environment ingenuity and determination allowed me to correctly answer a question I did not know how to do prior. This led me to achieve a High Distinction on the quiz and recieve full marks.

The objective given to students was to create a smart device capable of brewing tea with numerous integrated features such as precise temperature and steeping time, controlled via a mobile app while also considering the needs of customers.
mid.task_action: From the first lesson, I committed to professional behaviour because I knew the task ahead could prove difficult without it. I researched smart kitchen devices and IoT technologies so we could first gather a good idea of the task we were undertaking and how to develop an effective and quality product. It was also important to approach the needs of customers with a professional attitude as in a real-world situation would be critical. I also collaborated effectively with my peers who worked alongside me, and together we communicated our goals clearly in a professional manner so that there was better structure and planning for all ideas shared. I organized a simple project timeline and set milestones to keep myself on track for the class to make sure all peers in the class knew when items were due and were able to still discuss professionally about our tea challenge all while maintaining a positive attitude to further enforce professional behaviour.
mid.result: My peers and my own Professionalism allowed me to excel in the research and development of the project and practice professional behaviour when dealing with customer needs further creating a closer professional relationship with my peers, while also effectively completing designated work in the given timeframe. Overall, the Professional approach to this class has benefited me greatly in the sense that I have developed numerous transferable skills but specifically grown my skills in professional behaviour

mid.situation: As a student of ENGG2050, I was given many tasks to complete such as various quizzes, case studies, and an infographic. However, I was also given tasks from other units, resulting in a heavy workload and consequently, lots of stress.

mid.result: Although completing these tasks required sacrificing weekends of enjoyment and fun, the strong feeling of urgency and will to deliver supported me throughout the completion of each task. mid.situation: During the mid term time i.e week 7, I had to prioritise my tasks and make sure that i focus on what tasks i needed to complete one at a time.

mid.situation: I had to do a case study on Firestone 500.
mid.task_action: Where i used creative thinking was when it said to use the textbook to identify the different types of testing that occurred, and the effects of each type of testing. mid.situation: Our goal was to brainstorm functional designs for our heating and cooling components in the Internet Tea Maker creatively.
mid.task_action: During our brainstorming session, our team all shared each of our ideas.       We followed the 6-3-5 approach, starting off with concepts for heating, then cooling, then a design combining both. We collected 36 designs.       At the end of each round, we actively communicated on the flaws and strengths of each of our designs. I suggested creating designs with differing cooling components for the different ingredients (according to ADA, milk and water are required to be stored below 4 °C, while honey is ideally stored at 18 °C). A group member proposed we imitate designs from coffee machines (specifically those small domestic coffee machines), and integrated these suggestions into our designs.      We then sketched out a Decision Matrix, judging our ideas with criteria like cost, safety, efficiency, and discussed our individual rating for each design. It greatly aided in narrowing down our ideas more concisely, and helped us understand what factors we individually gave more focus to. We selected 3 designs, out of the 36, that had the highest overall rating.      Before we could narrow it down even further, the SGTA was coming to an end. My group agreed to finish this creative process next week.
mid.result: We succeeded in developing and critiquing our creative designs. The methods we used narrowed the designs from 36 to 3 designs within 2 hours, which exceeded our expectation.

mid.situation: At the very beginning of the unit, students were individually tasked with designing an internet based tea maker. Due to the individual nature of the project, I was responsible for every aspect - from initial concepts to final designs and implementation. I would have to prioritise and effectively allocate my focus to each aspect of the project.
mid.task_action: The task of creating an internet based tea maker contained many aspects and variables. I needed to prioritise elements such as safety, durability, reliability, sustainability, ergonomics and ease of use all while staying within the budget constraint of $1000. My approach to this challenge was to employ the skill of prioritisation and focus. I focussed on prioritising elements of the tea maker based on their prevalence to the customer’s experience. For example, I prioritised the scheduling and storage aspects of the maker highly, whilst the social aspects of the maker such as experimenting with teas recommended by friends were not prioritised highly.
mid.result: By approaching this project with the skill of prioritisation and focus in mind, I was able to effectively work through various design iterations and achieve high quality final designs that are inline with the customer’s wants and needs. Furthermore, by staying focussed on the essential elements of the project, I was able to complete it within the allocated budget, demonstrating the effectiveness of prioritisation and focus.

mid.situation: During my first year at university, my team and I were assigned a group project in our Engineering course. We were tasked with developing a student housing building where students would be able to study in peace and enjoy relaxing areas. This project was a significant part of our grade, and we had a strict deadline to meet.
mid.task_action: Knowing how important it was to stay professional, I stepped up as the team leader. The first thing I did was organize a meeting to discuss what we needed to do, set clear goals, and assign roles based on each person's strengths and interests. We put together a detailed project plan with specific milestones and deadlines to keep us on track. To keep communication smooth, I set up a shared online workspace where we could upload documents, track our progress, and easily communicate. I scheduled regular check-in meetings to talk about our progress, tackle any problems, and adjust our plan as needed. I made sure everyone felt comfortable sharing their ideas and concerns by encouraging open communication. I also kept in close contact with our course instructor, giving regular updates on our progress and asking questions to get feedback to make sure we were on the right track.
mid.result: Our professional approach paid off. We finished the web application on time, including all the required features and making it user-friendly. During our class presentation, the application received positive feedback from both our instructor and peers. The regular updates and transparent communication helped us quickly resolve any issues, and the collaborative environment we created allowed us to make the most of each team member's strengths. Our team's professionalism not only resulted in a high-quality project but also improved our teamwork skills and earned us a high grade for the assignment.

mid.situation: In our engineering design course, we were charged with creating a new tea maker that could not only brew tea but also add honey and butter to the cup once it was ready. The objective was to create a dependable mechanism that could correctly distribute honey and butter into the cup without interfering with the overall performance of the tea maker.
mid.task_action: The purpose of the brainstorming session was to come up with a practical design for this dispensing mechanism. The primary considerations were the differences in viscosity and melting points between honey and butter, as well as ensuring that the mechanism was simple to clean and maintain. Our goal was to create a design for this device that blended perfectly with our main tea maker design while meeting all safety and usability regulations. We employed the gallery style of brainstorming, in which each team member separately generated multiple design ideas. We then placed all of the suggestions on a whiteboard for everyone to see. We assessed each offered solution using particular criteria such as weight, complexity, manufacturability, ease of cleaning, and cost. We ranked the proposals according to their feasibility and efficacy. This helped us reduce the list down to six potential options.
mid.result: I successfully chose a design solution for my design from among the six choices generated during the brainstorming session. The preferred design included a dual-chamber system with a heating element for butter and a precision pump for honey.

mid.situation: In the early stages of the automated tea machine maker project, I came to the realisation that I needed to establish clear project goals and have a thorough understanding of demands. Without a thorough grasp there was a chance that a design would be created that fell short of expectations.      TASK: It was a job to carry out in-depth research to comprehend wants and establish reasonable, doable benchmarks for the tea machine's  design. Making ensuring that design will fulfil expectations and be well received was the goal.      ACTION: Took the initiative after gathering feedback from friends and mates. Then thought of some measures based on their feedbacks. For example, one measure was regarding the temperature of tea.
mid.result: This helped to look at this design formation with a greater view and validated my approach.

mid.situation: I worked at a modest fast-food business as a manager. We had a very limited marketing budget, so in order to successfully launch our new menu item, we needed to generate a lot of hype. Traditional marketing methods were either too costly or too ineffective for our target market. To make a good impression on the consumer, I had to adopt a new strategy.      Task: I had to devise a low-budget marketing strategy that would engage our target demographic and spark interest in our recently introduced product. The goal was to keep inside our limited budget and attract as much attention and awareness as we could.      Action: I convened a team gathering to generate innovative concepts that could be executed within a restricted budget. Secondly, we tried to get consumers to create original material on social media as part of a competition we were doing to promote our product. I reached out to many prominent bloggers and influencers, asking them to review our product and spread the word. During the first month of the debut, we tried to give discounts on the item to attract clients. In order to generate excitement and interest in our product, we also employed marketing techniques like organising pop-up events and flash mobs in crowded areas.
mid.result: A large number of individuals took part in our content-creation effort, resulting in hundreds of unique social media posts that significantly increased the visibility of our website. The influencers' and bloggers' marketing resulted in a notable increase in traffic to our eateries. Significant media attention and a great deal of local buzz were produced by the marketing campaigns. We weren't expecting as many orders as we were getting because of the continuous deal. In the end, these creative strategies produced a highly successful product launch. In the weeks after debut, our campaign generated awareness that sparked interest and purchases, and we ultimately surpassed our initial sales objectives by about 37%.

mid.situation: Our team was required to create an energy-efficient, economical, sustainable, and advanced tea maker, which is a step-up from the current tea maker on the internet. This is bound to identify the ways to reduce energy consumption.
mid.task_action: In the first step, we could only have the existing tea maker designs, and then we looked into the current tea makers, keeping in mind improvement in energy efficiency and improvement in design to provide the new features. To get a holistic view: an interdisciplinary team was formulated and it was consisting of engineers, product designers, and sustainability experts. We had brainstorming sessions to produce more and more new out of the box ideas which may lead to an advancement in a tea maker's design. We made a feasibility analysis based on the potential, the cost, energy savings, and advanced features that should be available in an advanced prototype that is automatic brewing with temperature control. The available options were streamlined after which a detailed analysis was conducted to compare the top three designs in short-term and long-term benefits. We used critical thinking for judging the benefit and drawback of each design, which helped in the identification of risks and also helped to develop a mitigation strategy for those risks.
mid.result: A result of the critical evaluation was the development of the prototype of tea maker, in which an energy-efficient heating element was fitted, and an automatic brewing system had been initialized to help customize the settings. Thus, it shall save 30% of energy as compared to the existing models that are available on the internet sites and additionally offer advanced settings that can enhance user convenience. It is not that only the success of the project made it possible to have a more sustainable product, but it also set a whole new benchmark for innovation in the product line of the company.

mid.situation: At one point in the semester, I was faced with a situation in which I had several assessment tasks for a variety of subjects due within a short period of time, all of which had to be completed within rapid succession.
mid.task_action: To address the number of assessment tasks that I had to complete in this short duration, I began a written plan of the assessments, detailing all actions that needed to be taken to get these assessment completed to a high standard. This included breaking down larger tasks into smaller and more manageable ones, as well as learning to prioritise which tasks were most valuable, based on weighting and their imminence.
mid.result: As a result, I was able to complete all of the tasks that were required of me, to a standard that I was happy with, and was able to be more effective with my time to maintain a healthy work-life balance with my time.

mid.situation: In this unit, i had multiple assignments and weekly quizzes to complete; with that, I was usually left confused about how to meet the requirements to achieve distinction-level standards.
mid.task_action: As said previously My main objective was to complete the weekly quizzes and the assignments to the best of my ability, aiming for that distinction-level mark. In order to achieve this goal, I was required to gain a thorough understanding of the course material and rubric expectations.      In order to address my confusion and to make sure that I was on the right track, I made efforts to communicate with fellow peers and my convener. I also spent a significant amount of scrolling through the discussion forums and the iLearn page, asking questions and participating in discussions. Additionally, I attended all the weekly lectures and took every opportunity to seek clarification on any problems I encountered. All of these solutions helped me significantly as it allowed me to clear up the issues that I was facing at the time.
mid.result: Through these consistent communication efforts I was able to gain a sound understanding which enabled me to perform to the best of my abilities in this course. I was then able to complete my assignments and quizzes to a high standard, ultimately achieving credit-distinction level marks. This experience showed the importance of dedicated communication and the effectiveness of collaboration. This is a skill I am now confident to use and will utilize in other units and my future career.

mid.situation: During the semester, as a group we were required to produce a design for a tea machine, whereby we were given a series of specific customer wants and needs, of which we had to incorporate into our machine. Some of these conditions could be conflicting, so we had to ensure that we chose the extent to which each condition would be included and how it would effect the machine.
mid.task_action: Each individual member of the group designed their own machine and incorporated the components in their own way. After the completion of each members machine design, we then got together and discussed the pros and cons of each machine, and any notable ideas that should be incorporated into the final product. This communication process was important as it would ensure that the final product would have as many of the best ideas that we each came up with, and as few of the bad ideas. We also noticed that many of our ideas were the same, which reinforced our reasoning as to why that specific idea/component should be included.
mid.result: The result of this communication collaboration was that we were able to produce and incorporate the best ideas that we could think of across the group, meaning that our final product would be at a greater quality than if we had never contributed our own ideas to the group. Since the tea machine assignment is an individual assignment, we now all have a greater expansion of ideas that we have gained from each other, compared to if we had only used our own individual designs.

mid.situation: Our engineering group needed to create a final design for the IoT Tea Maker using several differing ideas.
mid.task_action: We had put together several ideas together in terms of different components into a list of features that the machine was going to have, communication about how the machine would work proved to be difficult. Using these features we had designed a visual representation of the machine in terms of it's logical layout and physical appearance respectively.
mid.result: Using this visual model of the Tea Maker we were able to clearly communicate how the machine would work logically and give input on where to adjust the machine to improve  on the design.             -----------------------             Describe: Communication is essential to any project that is conducted between several people, a project can only progress through clear communication of the required actions necessary to develop the final product. Communication may be conducted in many forms such as visually, audibly, verbally or written, depending on what is needed to communicate and who you are communicating it to, each form of communication has it's advantages and disadvantages. It is essential to know what type of communication to use and to execute that given communication form properly to ensure your vision is seen properly.             Examine: Many types of communication were used in order to realise the final design of the tea maker. Verbal communication was used when brainstorming initial ideas for how to address the given requests of the client, and furthermore these ideas were written and everyone in our group had access to this list of ideas. However, while communicating these ideas, not everyone in the group understood how each concept worked, for example how the mechanism for the tea leaf dispenser worked and how the tea leaves were being cleaned out. It was mildly frustrating not being able to communicate these ideas clearly but in the end with the right form of communication we were able to fully explain the device properly.             Act: Based on the communication issues our group had in the beginning, it was difficult explaining the structure of the machine to everyone else and when we came up with a final design, it felt like not everyone had input into the design. In terms of improving on this, it would be better to discuss the requirements as a team first and come up with solutions for each individual requirement one step at a time instead of assigning different members a requirement and then communicating said solution while encouraging questions from everyone where confusion arises. This way every member contributes and there is less confusion over the solution.             Learn: By evaluating the importance of communication i have found that this is not only applicable in the engineering workforce but in any given situation at all. It is always important to fully understand a given situation/problem before starting to approach how to deal with it, and this can only be done through communication with others. I have learned that communication is key to my personal development both professionally and personally and I will strive to improve on this skill in order to more efficiently reach my goals and help my future teammates with completing projects.

mid.situation: In a class in semester 1, Team collaboration was a requirement for assessment. Communication is a very important and key factor in whether group work will succeed or fail. The situation was challenging as in the group the ability to work well with everyone was important but that is hard when members of the group don't meet the standard that is needed. therefore, good communication is very vital in voicing what needs to be done or how someone feels within a certain situation.
mid.task_action: The task required us to write a programme in four words. To create a high-quality programme, we discussed and presented our ideas with the goal of developing a high-quality mini-game. Following a productive discussion, we reached an agreement on our project. We then divided the various portions among team members to improve efficiency. Over the next few weeks, some team members failed to meet expectations, while others worked hard. Recognising this as a possible problem, we communicated calmly to find a solution and guarantee that we could go forward efficiently as a team.
mid.result: Our effective communication resulted in major improvements in team relationships and project quality. As a group, we were able to assist those who found specific duties difficult, ensuring that everyone contributed significantly. By openly addressing our challenges and needs, we created a collaborative environment that improved our capacity to create a high-quality mini-game. This event demonstrated the value of communication and teamwork, giving us significant lessons for future group initiatives.

mid.situation: Defining requirements for a tea maker connected to the internet from a small list of customer wants and from interacting with the customer.
mid.task_action: In newly formed groups, we analysed the original list of customer requirements and split them into requirements that could be fulfilled and requirements on which we needed more clarification from the customer. We then went through the customer correspondence with other groups, updated our requirements based on the answers, and asked additional questions. Once we felt we had a baseline understanding of the customer requirements (little confusion) we began brainstorming ideas based on these requirements.
mid.result: The result was a well-defined list of requirements that reflected the customer's wants including TPM's which was used for future brainstorming and design stages.

mid.situation: My team at the software startup where I interned was tasked with creating a new feature for our product that would increase user engagement. However, user feedback revealed that the feature was falling short of expectations due to its excessive complexity and lack of user-friendliness following the initial development period.      TASK:      I was given the responsibility of managing a small team to reevaluate and rethink the functionality as a junior developer. Simplifying the functionality without sacrificing its ability to achieve the initial goal of raising user engagement was a challenge.      ACTION:      To fully comprehend the criticism, I started a series of critical thinking meetings with all the stakeholders. We used a variety of critical thinking techniques, including brainstorming sessions to come up with solutions and root cause analysis to determine why the feature was not user-friendly. Every meeting sought to disprove our presumptions and objectively consider every option. We painstakingly recorded every meeting, emphasise important ideas and workable solutions.
mid.result: These sessions of critical thinking produced a redesigned functionality that was more user-friendly and in line with user expectations. We saw a 40% boost in user engagement after relaunching the feature, and the response we received thereafter was very favourable. This experience strengthened my capacity to use critical thinking successfully in software development, in addition to improving the final product.      This reflection shows how using critical thinking to address problems in the real world in an organised and efficient way can result in quantifiable gains and individual growth in problem-solving abilities.

mid.situation: I was assigned the task of designing an internet tea-making machine. This project included the complete design process, from developing the initial concept to validating the finished product. I went through all of the design stages, conceptual, preliminary, and detailed, to ensure that my design performed well and was sustainable. This challenge required me to manage each phase on my own effectively.      TASK: My task was to work on the entire design process. This involved defining the machine's requirements, generating multiple design concepts, selecting the most viable option, selecting the best materials both cost-effective and good in quality, and refining it through detailed design and validation. I needed to ensure that my design was innovative, sustainable, and met all the specified performance standards required by the customer.      ACTION: My task was to work on the full design process. This included defining the machine's needs, creating numerous design concepts, selecting the most viable option, selecting the best materials that were both cost-effective and high-quality, and improving it through extensive design and validation. I needed to make sure my design was original, sustainable, and met all of the customer's performance requirements.
mid.result: The structured approach to the design process and the great guidance of lectures and tutors resulted in a thoroughly planned and innovative Internet tea-making machine. The final design was both efficient and sustainable, with an energy-saving mode due to the materials used, a range of tea alternatives that could be made, functionality based on app instructions, and customer ease. This experience significantly enhanced my ability to manage a complex design project from start to finish independently.

mid.situation: Throughout the semester, we had to design an IoT tea machine that could cater to user preferences via a smartphone app. The designing of this project required close collaboration of our multidisciplinary team, which included three civil engineers  and three software engineers, working hand in hand.
mid.task_action: We had to ensure that all components of the IoT tea machine worked harmoniously together. The civil engineers were responsible for the physical design of the machine, while the software engineers developed the app design and integrated the machine’s functionalities. We worked towards our shared objective by distributing every role and responsibilities according to our discipline, setting up regular meetings, clarifying requirements, and addressing any emerging issues promptly.       Importantly, we didn't work in isolation; the software and the civil engineers cross-checked and supported each other's work to ensure that the system was feasible as a whole. For instance, when the civil engineers encountered a challenge with the machine’s water heating mechanism, the software engineers also brainstormed together and explored a potential solution: a dispenser that could heat up the required amount of water on demand without compromising the machine's structural integrity. Therefore, by leveraging our collective expertise, we devised a solution that integrated the heat-up-on the-go dispensers seamlessly into the design.
mid.result: As a result, we achieved our targets by designing an efficient tea machine that fits exactly to the customer's requirements; allowing users to customise the tea preferences via the app and share the tea recipe within a friend's group. This successful outcome was a testament to the power of effective teamwork and the combined efforts of engineers from varying disciplines.

mid.situation: In my ENGG 2050 course, the cohort and I were tasked with designing an automatic tea maker.      TASK: Our task was to conduct research and formulate a hypothesis on what tea drinkers would find beneficial to reduce tedious waiting time. The goal was to create a basis for further research in subsequent semesters. My group and I identified optimal brewing times, adequate drinking temperature, and a user-friendly mechanism.      ACTION: I took the initiative by initiating conversations with my fellow group members. Our aim was to understand consumer needs and set goals while creating a well-designed blueprint. I organised focus groups and brainstorming sessions using techniques from ENGG 1050, such as the Six Thinking Hats method. Based on these sessions, we collectively decided to distribute surveys to gather information from tea connoisseurs. This proved pivotal as it taught us the value of attentiveness and understanding consumer needs. We then collectively set our goals and turned them into actions.
mid.result: As a result of my actions and my experience as a licensed concreter, I bridged the gap between young novices and seasoned veterans. By understanding customer needs and setting goals, we achieved a breakthrough and revolutionary idea: a programmable tea maker that allows consumers to set precise temperatures and steeping times for different types of tea from a mobile device. Another innovation was automatically lifting the tea bag once the steeping time is complete.

mid.situation: We are given a task to design an internet tea maker based on client's demands.
mid.task_action: Small group was formed in our table and lists of questions were gathered from each group members related to client's needs to get better visualization about the client's product concept. Questions were reassessed and reconstructed to prevent misconception and miscommunication between us and client. Furthermore, we prepared lists of possible solutions to be presented to client during consultation, in order to get better insights and further clarify the requirements or criteria.
mid.result: More detailed and specific questions were constructed to achieve a better understanding regarding client's preferences or needs. We had rough ideas on how to approach the design of internet tea maker.

mid.situation: I was presented a challenge where I along with the people in my table had to brainstorm a couple of ideas for an automatic tea maker that could enhance and simplify the tea making process. The goal of this brainstorming task was to brainstorm a couple of ideas that would offer the user convenience and consistency while also making sure that it still was able to achieve its main function of making a nice warm cup of tea for the user. Key considerations during this brainstorming section included user simplicity, brewing precision, safety, etc.
mid.task_action: The Primary task was to develop a design plan that covered some of the phases from conceptualisation with the people in our table. This involved understanding the user needs in which this was done by asking our questions for the consumer (REX) on iLearn. Using the responses from iLearn we were able to understand that there were many more user requirements such as the fact that Rex wanted the Automatic Tea Maker to deliver the tea after it was made. Implementing the responses from Rex into our brainstorming session, it led to several innovative concepts. We used sketches to visualise these ideas and we would evaluate each other's opinion and provide feedback suggesting why/why not it was not appropriate for the tea maker.
mid.result: The result from this brainstorming section was that we were able to come up with a couple of ideas that were not initially included in our tea design. Key features such as temperature control were added into the design process however this also made us adjust a couple of our initial ideas since they were clashing.

mid.situation: In my engineering design course, my team was tasked with designing an innovative tea maker. The goal was to design a tea-making automation tool that would be simple to use and produce excellent tea. We had a lot of ideas from our first brainstorming meetings, but we had trouble coming up with a concept that was simple, functional, and feasible to produce.
mid.task_action: Recognizing the need for structured creative thinking, We formed a small working group and clearly articulated the problem statement: design a tea maker that automates brewing, offers customizable options for different types of tea, and is easy to clean and use. Each team member was given the task of sketching their ideas on paper and displaying them around the room.       During this process, I facilitated the discussion to ensure everyone’s ideas were heard and evaluated fairly. We used criteria such as user-friendliness, ease of cleaning, brewing customization options, and potential manufacturing challenges to assess each concept. This structured approach allowed us to combine the best features from different designs into a cohesive solution.
mid.result: By the end, we had generated six viable design options. We ranked these based on the established criteria and selected a final design that incorporated the most beneficial aspects of each idea. This final design featured an intuitive interface for customizing brewing parameters, a detachable infuser for easy cleaning, and a compact form factor suitable for various kitchen sizes.

mid.situation: During the second week of the semester when I got all the requirements of the machine then I had a task to make conceptual design for my Internet of Tea maker machine for that I need to communicate with my peers.      TASKS/ACTIONS: To get started for the project, I used the requirement documentation along with the SGTA worksheet and got involved in brainstorming with my group members about how we all could possibly achieve a good conceptual design. We begin by critically thinking and dividing the whole system into different subsystems and categorizing those subsystems into deeper small subsystems and components after that we had to design TPM (technical performance Measures) for them. For that we started by doing research on different websites, use different YouTube videos, having  long discussion which helped me  to create a list of nearly 5 TPM’s for each subsystem. After that  we also made an online  chat group so that we could help each other on different phases of design level.
mid.result: By sitting in a group and discussing different possibilities and getting to know different points of view of different members helped in designing a better conceptual design.

mid.situation: Throughout the semester we have had the opportunity to complete various assignments, quizzes and attend SGTA’s.
mid.task_action: For most of the units I have studied at university I have not had as many quizzes and assignments as this unit has had. It has made a significant difference to my experience at university. With all the university work that I have each week to complete as well as my new hobbies I have taken up; playing tennis and going to the gym, it has been a challenge to keep up each week with what I am tasked to do for this unit. Although all the tasks have not been particularly large ones, the shorter intervals between each task gave rise to a challenge for something I struggle with already – my prioritization and focus.
mid.result: The way my assignments and quizzes turned out this semester were satisfactory in my opinion. Even with my job and my new hobbies I was still able to keep university toward the top of my priority list and complete all assignments and quizzes on time, even though sometimes they were just on time.

mid.situation: I was employed as a manager at a small fast-food restaurant. Our new menu item was about to be introduced, but we had a very tight marketing budget, so we needed to create a lot of buzz in order to pull it off. For our target population, traditional marketing techniques were either too expensive or too inefficient. I had to take a different approach in order to leave a positive impression on the audience.      TASK : My job was to come up with a low-cost marketing plan that would generate interest in our newly launched item as well as interact with our intended audience. The idea was to attract as much attention and publicity as possible while staying within our tight budget.      ACTION: To come up with original ideas that might be implemented on a limited budget, I called for a meeting with the team. Secondly, We made an attempt to launch a competition to promote our product by inviting customers to post unique content on social media. I made contact with a few well-known bloggers and influencers and invited them to provide feedback and promote our product. In order to get customers' attention, we attempted to offer deals on the item during the first month of the launch. Additionally, we used marketing strategies to create buzz and pique interest in our item, such as planning pop-up events and flash mobs in busy places.
mid.result: A great number of people participated in our content-creation campaign, which produced hundreds of original postings on social media sites, greatly boosting our online platform. Our restaurants saw a significant rise in traffic due to the promotion of the influencers and bloggers. The marketing initiatives generated a lot of local buzz and substantial media interest. Due to the ongoing offer, we were receiving more orders than we had expected. These innovative tactics came together to provide a very successful product launch in the end. Our campaign produced awareness that drove interest and sales in the weeks after launch, and we ended up exceeding our first sales targets by almost 25%.

mid.situation: I was tasked to design a tea-making machine with IoT capabilities.
mid.task_action: Although the video for the assignment is individually marked, a few of my classmates and I formed a group so we could collaborate and inspire each other with ideas for the machine. Early on in the design process, we each came up with different suggestions. We made a list of all of our ideas and began choosing the best and most feasible ones until we reached a design we were happy with. When we had similar ideas, we would debate which one would be better. In some cases, two similar designs were merged into one, both building on the other to create something better.
mid.result: As the weeks went by, our combined efforts led to a creative and well-rounded approach to designing the tea maker. Due to our close collaboration, everyone in the group had a good picture of the machine's final and alternative designs and what tests were/were not applicable to it.

mid.situation: I had an assignment where I had to design an infographic to explain any one of the topics which have been discussed in this unit.       TASK: The infographic I had to design required explaining the concepts learnt in this unit creatively by using graphics mostly, the core concepts covered were theory dense, and I had to explain them using the graphics in a simple manner, making them easy to understand.      ACTION: The concepts I selected to explain were based on the queuing system, and I had to explain those concepts using the graphics, at first nothing came to the mind, but then I broke down the concepts into simpler concepts and brainstormed how those simple concepts can be turned into graphics. I used multiple designing tools for my graphics and experimented with different templates, designs, figures, and pictures to get the right set of visuals, background and aesthetics, from changing the color themes, to text size and font. All of this helped in making the most suitable design for my infographic.
mid.result: The process of creative thinking helped in designing the infographic, all the core concepts related to the queuing system were explained using the graphics in a comprehensive manner.

mid.situation: While working on one of my software design units, our team was tasked with designing a complex game using Processing and managing the project on GitHub where we had faced some major conflicts. Our team consisted of four members with diverse skills and backgrounds. Midway through the development, disagreements arose over the game's core mechanics and aesthetic direction. These disputes led to fragmented code contributions, inconsistent design elements, and a stalled project timeline.      Task:      I had become the team mediator with my primary task being resolving conflicts, negotiating and conciliating everyone's views to a single point, and ensuring a cohesive and efficient development progress. It was crucial to balance different creative perspectives while keeping the project on track and maintaining a collaborative atmosphere.      Action :       To address the conflicts within the team, I facilitated open communication by organizing a meeting where everyone could voice their opinions and concerns. We redefined the game's core objectives and documented them in a shared project roadmap on GitHub. I introduced a voting system for major design decisions to ensure a democratic process and set up coding standards to maintain code quality and consistency. We utilized GitHub Issues and Projects boards to enhance task management and established a conflict resolution protocol with regular check-ins and a designated mediator.
mid.result: These actions led to a unified vision for the game, with improved coherence in mechanics and design. The implementation of coding standards and regular reviews streamlined development, resulting in higher quality code. Enhanced collaboration through GitHub tools increased productivity and accountability. The team successfully completed the game within the revised timeline, with improved morale and cohesion due to transparent conflict resolution and democratic decision-making processes.

mid.situation: In ENGG2050, students were divided into small interactive classes to work on an engineering design project every week. We were to discuss in groups to come up with a design for a smart tea maker that meets the customer's needs.
mid.task_action: There were a set of activities and topics for discussions assigned for each week in order to assist with the design process. We formed our own working group where the team members discussed their different ideas and opinions regarding the project. For each given task, we communicated the different ways to approach the problem within the group and each member got to utilise their own strengths to contribute and share their insights in the discussion. Consequently, the remaining members gave feedbacks and further suggestions for improvements on the particular topic.
mid.result: Through this task, we were able to establish connections with other engineering students and gained networking experiences. Furthermore, we were each able to come up with our own tea maker design with the help of others.

mid.situation: During an SGTA around the start of the semester, we were tasked with creating technical performance measure's (TPM) for our tea making machine project. There was one major issue. None of us knew what a TPM was and how to structure it. Nobody was on the same page with it, our interpretations of it were different and it was clear we needed to get on the same page. That is where the soft skill of life long learning and educating came into play. With everybody in disarray, I felt a desire to bring everybody on the same page and take initiative to learn the concept and be able to explain it so that everyone would understand and that we can all work together on the task at hand. It was important that everyone understood the topic and could be able to contribute to the task so that we could progress with our tea making project.
mid.task_action: The first thing I did was to actively research and gain a deep understanding of TPMs so that I had the knowledge to explain and respond to questions about it from the group. This looked like re watching relevant parts of the lecture, looking at the textbook and asking my tutor on behalf of the group what was expected of us for this task. The second thing I did was to format the TPM structure from my understanding in our group document so that our team members could understand. Thirdly was sharing with to the group with confidence my understanding of the topic and leading them in the direction of completing the task by responding to questions about the topic and organizing what we were going to contribute to the task.
mid.result: The result of using the skill of life long learning and educating was that everybody was on the same page and feeling the confidence they needed to get on with the task because they we able to get clear direction from someone who took the time to look at the topic from different angles and provide a direction and pointers on how best to approach the task. This led to more group camaraderie as we could have easily all did a poor job of the task by simply not engaging due to feeling confusion and lack of direction had someone not taken the time to actively learn the material well and educate on how to approach the task based on this understanding.

mid.situation: During our task to develop a sustainable IoT device, we faced a challenge in designing an energy-efficient power management system that was also cost-effective.      Task: We needed to create an innovative solution that balanced both cost and energy efficiency while meeting all design specifications.      Action: I initiated a brainstorm using the SCAMPER technique. We generated and evaluated multiple ideas, considering alternative materials, renewable energy sources, and optimised circuitry. We used a decision matrix to assess the feasibility, cost, and sustainability of each idea.
mid.result: We developed a hybrid power management system combining solar energy with low-power design. This solution met our efficiency goals and reduced production costs by an estimated 15%, becoming a key feature of our IoT device.      DEAL:      Describe: The soft skill I want to reflect on is creative thinking, which was essential for designing a cost-effective and energy-efficient power management system for our IoT device.      Examine: I utilised creative thinking effectively during the brainstorming session using the SCAMPER technique, generating diverse and innovative ideas. However, I need to improve time management to ensure all ideas are evaluated efficiently. I felt inspired and motivated but occasionally overwhelmed by the volume of ideas.      Act: To improve, I plan to practise more structured brainstorming techniques and seek feedback from my team. In future situations, I will balance exploring creative ideas with practical time management.   LEARN: This experience taught me the value of structured innovation methods. It boosted my confidence in leading creative problem-solving sessions and encouraged me to continue leveraging creative thinking to tackle engineering challenges.

mid.situation: Gained knowledge about Engineering failure  consequences.      Task: To research firestone 500 and Hurricane Katrina in New Orleans(case study A3 and A5).      Action :       The firestone Case study gave me insight into the tire failure which was due to blowouts. A blowout is the sudden loss of air pressure in any inflatable tire. These occurred because the radials on the tire  carried a set of reinforcing belts beneath the tread made of fiberglass or steel and encircled the tires' carcasses, enhancing the rigidity of their structure. But, the firestone-built radials had issues causing heat to build up within the tire, causing the tread to separate from the steel-belted inner layer. When this separation occurred, the worst possible result was a blowout.       I also learned that the  engineering failure caused by the hurricane were as a result of  Difficulties associated with tremendous geologic complexity and challenges involved in construction of flood protection, Challenges involved in the complex and often dysfunctional interactions between federal government, federal agencies being responsible for design and construction oversight,outsourced engineering and construction, and local government and local oversight agencies with overlapping responsibilities and jurisdictions.
mid.result: These case studies helped me realize that engineering projects are intrinsically risky due to a variety of factors, including budgetary limits, technical difficulties, and problems with regulatory compliance. As an engineering student, looking back at previous mistakes made it easier for me to understand how important it is to carry out in-depth risk assessments and put in place suitable mitigation techniques. By doing this, engineers can reduce the possibility of failure and its possible consequences.

mid.situation: During the semester of our ENGG2050, we faced significant issues with our projects when some groups stopped participating and attending the STGA. This unexpected drop in attendance dramatically increased our workload and caused a setback in our project timeline. The situation was stressful as we fell behind by a few weeks, impacting our overall productivity and progress.      TASK: Complete a comprehensive plan to build a coffee machine that requires consistent collaboration and contribution from all team members. Despite the reduced number of active members, the intent was to produce the highest quality work within the set deadlines.      ACTION: I took the initiative to contact the missing members by exchanging details about the start of the semester. I wanted to understand their reasons for not showing up and encourage their participation. I Communicated with team members to redistribute the workload, with the remaining team members taking on additional responsibilities to cover the unfinished tasks. After a thorough discussion, we collectively agreed on a plan involving the remaining team members taking on additional responsibilities to complete all group tasks. Leveraging my problem-solving skills, I worked closely with the team to address any challenges during this process. Through effective communication and collaborative problem-solving, we successfully re-integrated the absent members into the project, ensuring everyone was onboard and engaged.
mid.result: Our efforts enabled us to catch up on the delayed work and complete the project on time. Teamwork and collaboration significantly improved once the group members rejoined, and we produced a high-quality project that met the course requirements. This experience taught us the importance of perseverance, communication, and teamwork in overcoming challenges.

mid.situation: During the ENGG2050 course, our team was tasked with designing a smart tea maker. The goal was to create a device that could brew various types of tea perfectly by controlling factors such as water temperature, steeping time, and tea type selection. This project required not only technical skills but also significant critical thinking to solve the numerous challenges that arose during the design process.      TASK: Our main challenge was to develop a user-friendly, efficient, and reliable tea maker that could cater to different user preferences. The task required us to integrate multiple systems, including temperature sensors, timers, and a user interface, while ensuring the product was cost-effective and easy to manufacture. The non-trivial nature of this task lay in balancing functionality with user experience and production constraints.      ACTION: To address this challenge, I employed critical thinking by breaking down the project into smaller, manageable tasks. I initiated brainstorming sessions to identify potential issues and solutions, encouraged team members to think creatively, and facilitated discussions to evaluate different design options. For instance, we debated whether to use a digital or analog interface and considered various heating mechanisms. My role involved questioning assumptions, analyzing data from prototypes, and synthesizing diverse viewpoints to guide the team toward the best solution.
mid.result: As a result of applying critical thinking, we developed a prototype that met the project’s requirements. The smart tea maker could accurately control water temperature and steeping time based on the selected tea type. Our critical approach led to a design that was both functional and user-friendly. The project culminated in a successful demonstration, receiving positive feedback from peers and instructors for its innovation and practicality.

mid.situation: During the semester we were tasked with designing an Internet based Tea Maker. The project required me to think of creative conceptual ideas and designs that would include innovative features such as user customisation options, sharing options and having the ability to make multiple types of teas. Given these interesting and complex requirements, creative thinking was essential to developing a unique and functional product that met the customers specifications.
mid.task_action: The main challenge was to design a concept that would make the tea maker practical and also user friendly. In order to create such a machine brainstorming was required, assessing the feasibility and integrating them into the design was also a big task at hand. To foster creative thinking, I asked for help from people around me and at home, I also researched existing team makers and some smart home appliances to see what features could be used for my tea maker.
mid.result: The creative approach led to the development of a tea maker design that featured a range of innovative functionalities, including tea profiles, scheduled tea brewing and tea profile sharing capabilities. In the end it seems as if the design for the tea maker covers all the wants of the customer without being too complex to operate and still being practical and functional.

mid.situation: During my first year as a software engineering student, our team project involved creating a Rube Goldberg machine that resulted in dropping a ball onto a computer's space bar to automatically play a lecture video. This was meant to demonstrate the integration of physical components with software components. However, we realized that the video included in the demonstration was a copyrighted lecture from another institution, which we hadn't obtained permission to use publicly in our class presentation.
mid.task_action: As the project leader, I knew the importance of addressing this copyright issue to be at par with ethical standards in our project. I organized a meeting with the team to discuss the potential copyright infringement and its implications. We decided to contact the copyright holder for permission to use the video or find an alternative that was freely available. I took responsibility for communicating with the copyright holder while also leading the team in researching open-source educational videos that could be used as a substitute.
mid.result: The copyright holder did not grant permission to use their video, so we opted for an open-source alternative that fit the educational theme of our project. This switch not only resolved the copyright issue but also emphasized the importance of ethical considerations in the use of digital content. Our project was successfully presented with the new video, and it was well-received by our peers and professors. This experience reinforced the importance of adhering to ethical and legal standards in software engineering and highlighted the necessity of proactive problem-solving and ethical awareness in academic and professional settings.

mid.situation: Within the unit ENGG2050, we needed to develop an Internet-based Tea Maker that can make a cup of tea at a set time under a set of preferences for a set amount of people and implement the ability to create a community of friends with a shared enthusiasm for tea.      TASK / ACTION:   Throughout the process of designing, testing, and refining into a final and reliable product that stratified the requirements of our consumers, it is key to understand the importance of taking accountability. This includes the development of a Preliminary Design that recounts possible elements and materials to be used whilst setting goals and a realistic timeline in which these milestones can be met; a Final Design created by applying adjustments and improvements to the prior design and having the ability to receive constructive feedback as well as implement inspirations for probable challenges faced along the way to the final product; Reliability Data produced through initializing and evaluating the success of each component as well as the overall machine and being transparent with the customer and/or other team members along the design process. During revisions, it was key to view this project with the needs of the customer at the forefront of each design decision made. Additionally, this product was completed by forming a small peer group in which ideas could be constructed, produced, and evaluated to ultimately benefit each individual.
mid.result: By completing these practices at a satisfactory level, the product could be completed whilst eliminating the time and effort spent on distracting activities and other unproductive behavior. Additionally, through being accountable throughout this design process, an engineer can review their progress and improve within areas such as communication, advocacy in self, and creative thinking for the next project.

mid.situation: Tasked with designing an innovative tea maker that is tailored to specific customer needs that ranged from temperature, types of tea, types of condiments (sugar, milk, honey) and brewing time.             Task: Our objective was to create a machine that could preform all the tasks mentioned above whilst also having an easy to use, friendly user-interface that allowed for customization. Our vision was to create a personalized tea experience that fully catered to the individuals taste.             Action: To achieve customer satisfaction, researching different design mechanisms to make the tea, along with conversing with other students, I was responsible for using creative thinking to come up with the idea of having a coffee-machine like system that could hold condiments on top for customization. The idea was to have a conveyor belt like system on top so it is easily interchangeable. Along with a innovative design idea, a simple and elegant user interface allows the individual to alter the tea to their preferences on a mobile app.
mid.result: The result was a conceptual idea of a tea maker that could “make tea on demand” using a simple user interface. Along with that, the final product was able to make multiple types of tea ranging from herbal tea to milk teas, all of which can be adjusted and fine tuned towards customers preferences. mid.situation: During the course of this unit I have learnt about the various roles and responsibilities of engineers. Specifically, during both case studies, I have been required to consider what these roles and responsibilities are, and perhaps more importantly the consequences that follow when these are not fulfilled.      T - TASK: My challenge was to complete the case studies and to build on my own understanding of all the different requirements and constraints that engineers face when carrying out their roles.       A - ACTION: To do this I referred to the textbook to gain a better understanding about designing for different factors such as affordability, reliability and sustainability. I also analysed the case studies. For example in the second case study about New Orleans Flood Protection System, I needed to apply critical thinking to evaluate the pros and cons of different choices that were made when designing the system. I then had to evaluate the consequences of these results and what it meant in regard to an engineer's responsibilities. It was often the situation in this case study that system costs were reduced at the expense of human safety. From my point of view safety should be prioritised above money, so I needed to consider the many different responsibilities placed on engineers to understand how the failures that occurred in this case study were allowed to happen.      R -
mid.result: As a result of this evaluation around the meaning of an engineer's roles and responsibilities, I was able to complete the case study with what I feel is a high level of understanding. I was also able to greatly develop my critical thinking skills, and understanding of what it means to be an engineer in this modern age with so many varying and often contrasting responsibilities. Lastly I believe this deeper understanding will reflect in my professional career and make me a better engineer.

mid.situation: I was attempting to build a DM Screen for Dungeons and Dragons with a friend of mine. We were attempting to build this screen with a lot of special requirements, such as magnetic back panels to attach notes and a large screen out of the front to display images/information.
mid.task_action: Me and my friend underwent a brainstorming session; where we designed how each panel would sit and work. We created detailed design plans, but had an argument about how these panels should connect. I wanted these panels to connect via magnets, allowing for the ability for these panels to connect to a large tower or another panel instead. The large tower would either be a storage tower, or a dice tower. My friend wanted the panels to be in fixed positions, using hooks instead to connect the panels just to each other in a fixed order.
mid.result: We worked out why each of us wanted this, and discussed the pros and cons of each method. We drew up two different designs, before settling on a design in the middle, where we would use a board with magnets on to allow the DM screen to connect to the magnets. Which screens/boards were placed on the board would be modular, while the positions of those screens/boards would be fixed.

mid.situation: The task we were undertaking was developing the design for the Internet of Teas System for a customer who had very rigorous and specific demands for the way they wanted the system to behave. With such extreme and somewhat confusing demands, as a team, and independently, we had to think critically in order to meet the customer's demands.       Tasks: It was our responsibility to design a system that met all of the customer's requirements even when they were confusing and sometimes very hard to cater to. For example, the customer wanted the tea maker to have tea prepared every morning when he woke up, but did not want an alarm clock system as they 'don't have an alarm clock' and 'didn't want to be spied upon,' however, they also liked the idea of customising their tea 'when setting their alarm'. This was very difficult as in order to have a system that worked according to a schedule, it would generally use an alarm clock system and it seemed the customer themself was unsure of their requirement.       Task: In order to overcome such demanding requirements, we had to think critically. With so many precise demands, we had to break down each requirement, come up with solutions and discard them based on the other demands. Critical thinking also led us to ask critical questions, and think of solutions that may not be conventional in order to satisfy the customer.
mid.result: Thinking critically, we asked questions regarding the scheduling of the tea maker, like whether the customer would want to set their tea each time, to which they answered that they can use the app to say when they will be getting home to set the time for tea. This seems to be an unconventional demand as it adds extra responsibility on the customer, however, it does allow us to think critically to offer solutions, despite how unique it may be. This also taught me that thinking critically is important in gathering requirements from the customer because often they themselves may not know what kind of system they want. So it is the responsibility of the engineer to ask critical questions that help the customer and developer create a clear outline of a functioning system.
