mid.situation: During discussions about the Internet of Tea machine, our group had two major opposing ideas of how the tea brewing aspect of the machine will be implemented. Half of the team was leaning towards a simpler design involving heating the tea in water and later separating the leaves, whereas the other team favoured a more complex but versatile brewing method that involved multiple processes for different types of tea (cold brew, hot tea, etc.).      Task:   As this divided the group into two, we needed to come up with a solution that satisfied both groups and prioritise the customer requirements.      Action:   I recommended a pros and cons list for both ideas. This helped highlight our common goals and identify a feasible solution that is within the project requirements and timeline. After detailed discussions, we agreed on a combined system that uses both these methods. The tea will be brewed in water, but included other features such as temperature control and separated brewing with a permeable and movable tea leaves container to help control brew time.
mid.result: This compromise resulted in a balanced solution, that provided flexibility for the user to brew different types of tea without requiring the more advanced subsystems that would increase complexity, reduce reliability and increase design efforts and required time.

mid.situation: In my engineering design course, I was assigned to create an innovative tea-making machine that could automate the entire process from boiling water to serving tea.      TASK: The task was to design a machine that efficiently brewed tea, considering factors like temperature control, brewing time, and user interface simplicity.      ACTION: To tackle this, I first defined the key criteria for a successful design: precise temperature control, optimal brewing time, and user-friendly operation. I then brainstormed multiple design concepts, using sketches to visualize different mechanisms. After evaluating each idea against my criteria, I selected a design featuring separate compartments for water heating, brewing, and serving. I built a prototype, integrating temperature sensors and a simple control panel to ensure ease of use.
mid.result: The final prototype was an automated tea-making machine that accurately controlled water temperature and brewing time, resulting in consistently perfect tea. The machine reduced brewing time by 20% compared to manual methods and received positive feedback for its innovative design and functionality. This project enhanced my skills in creative problem-solving and engineering design.

mid.situation: For the completion of this unit, we have looked in detail at 2 Case studies, The Firestone 500 and The Hurricane Katrina Flood Protection System. Both historical situations that have led to deaths and property damage could have been prevented by Engineers, Companies and Government Representatives taking on the Responsibility of the project or by taking Corrective measures once a fault is found, however in both cases it is found that engineers have been “Cutting Corners” to complete jobs.               Task:      The unit requirement for these activities is to research each Case study and then complete the specific questions for each study.             Action:      The overwhelming Costs and loss of life has caused an unease to settle about the processes through which jobs are completed today. I took initiative to look for more recent examples of Engineering Failures, closer to home, and reconsider the role and responsibilities an engineer undertakes and how this could affect the people closest to me. New south Whales alone has seen 3 major projects have had disastrous failures with potential life-threatening repercussions. Below is a short recap of the problems faced on each site.         1.      Cracks throughout the Opal tower foundations have caused the evacuation of all residents on Christmas Eve. These cracks or faults in the foundations have been contributed to Shortcomings in the design of the building and construction engineers Cutting Corners during development.      2.      Snowy Hydro 2.0 in the later stages of 2023 caused a sinkhole only 150m into drilling. During the recover process workers were lied to about toxic gas collecting throughout the tunnel.      3.      Most recent the M6 motor way has also faced a sinkhole incident, forcing workers to flee fall debris and the isolation an office building in Rockdale, Sydney.
mid.result: The Result of completing These case studies and further research have broadened and challenged my perspective of the importance for engineers to understand the Role and Responsibility while undertaking projects. The Role of a decision maker to ensure all work is Adequate and sustainable for the safety of all those involved and anyone current or future who will interact with the project. Also, the Responsibility to accept possible failure and take immediate action to fix the issue (Opposite response compared to Firestone 500) but also the ability/responsibility to stand up for the officials/superiors and push for the correct measures to be in place for all aspects of the job (lessons Learned from Hurricane Katrina Investigation). I am now more determined than before to ensure my current and future projects and completed with a focus on safety and Accountability to the Role and Responsibilities an Engineer must uphold.

mid.situation: Our situation was to create and brainstorm a design and product for a "Tea Making Product" using the internet. The task involves using Creative Thinking and team collaboration to create a cohesive and functional product that were up to the standards of the customer.      Task:   The task of creating a product/design for an "Internet Tea Maker" involved many steps and specifications, both needing creative thinking and team collaboration. Specifications involved specific standards and safety precautions that come with designing a product and also specifications that were particularly asked for by the "Client". The client asked for a product that could make them tea using the internet and through further questioning, we were able to find some specific functions that they wanted. Such as the tea being already made at certain times, in certain ways and at a certain temperature; all through the internet. As well as including a way to share tea recipes, ratings and recommendations through social media with their friends. Furthermore, as a team we needed to use our creative thinking and collaborative efforts to make sure that these specific requirements are met but are also up to standards with safety and regulations.      Action:   To increase efficiency and make use of creative thinking, a few brainstorming sessions offered many different ideas over the course of the first few weeks. Eventually coming up with a conceptual design with a rough idea of its functions and requirements. This conceptual design for the "Internet Tea Maker" was then put through its "Technical Performance Measures" to further grasp what actions and requirements were needed to create a product. Through the use of creative thinking, our design was split into many different systems. This included; the app system which was in charge of communication with the product through the internet, steeping system/ boiling system which monitored and controlled the boiling and steeping of the tea, the storage system that handled the storage of both the tea, milk and water for machine, the power management system which controlled and monitored the power required for the product as a whole, the pouring and dispensing system that controlled and monitored how the tea/water/milk is dispensed into the receiving mug/cup and finally the mug/cup placement system which was responsible for making sure that there is a receiving cup/mug for the tea once it is ready. Through the use of creative thinking and team work, we systematically and cooperatively went through each step and using the TPM's we were able to come up with a product that is reliable, sustainable, up to standards and also within the expectations of the client.
mid.result: The result being that we were able to split up the work between our group members, each selecting a sub system of the product and creatively thinking of ways to make sure that the product will be up to standard and also follow the clients wishes. My sub system being the storage system for the product. I was able to find a reliable, sustainable and also up to standard solutions for the manufacturing and choice of material. This task has challenged both me and my groups creative thinking, often having to think outside of the box to find solutions for the complex problems that occur when creating a product.

mid.situation: During a team project last month in my engineering class, I was faced with the challenge of managing my academic workload alongside a part-time job. The demands of both my studies and my job were overwhelming, and I needed to find a way to balance them effectively.      Task: Our goal was to complete a comprehensive engineering design project while ensuring that I could meet my work commitments and maintain my academic performance. I was responsible for coordinating my schedule to accommodate both my studies and my job.      Action: I took the initiative to create a detailed schedule that prioritized my tasks based on deadlines and importance. I actively communicated with team members to align our project milestones with my availability. I utilized my problem-solving skills to identify potential conflicts and find solutions that allowed me to stay on track. Additionally, I coordinated with my colleagues to ensure we could meet outside of my working hours, and I set aside specific times for focused study and project work.
mid.result: As a result, we exceeded our targets by completing the project ahead of schedule. My actions contributed to a more efficient process, as my clear communication and structured approach kept the team organized and motivated. The outcome was a positive impact on both my academic performance and my job, as I was able to fulfill my responsibilities in both areas successfully.

mid.situation: I was appointed as the coordinator for a large-scale volunteering project aimed at setting up a weekend learning camp for underprivileged children. The challenge was not only logistical in coordinating with multiple NGOs, volunteers, and local sponsors but also in ensuring educational materials were suitable and engaging for children from diverse backgrounds.      TASK: My responsibility was to oversee the entire project from planning to execution, including resource allocation, volunteer training, and program scheduling. The task was particularly challenging because it required balancing the educational needs of the children with the resources available, all while keeping the volunteers motivated and the sponsors engaged.      ACTION: I led the project by first creating a detailed plan that included timelines, roles for each volunteer, and a curriculum tailored to the children’s educational levels. I emphasized communication, holding regular meetings to ensure all volunteers were aligned with the project’s goals and comfortable with their responsibilities. I also initiated a feedback mechanism with continuous monitoring, allowing for adjustments to be made swiftly, ensuring the project adapted to real-time needs and feedback.
mid.result: The learning camp was a tremendous success, with over 200 children benefiting from the weekend program. We achieved a 95% satisfaction rate among volunteers and sponsors, and follow-up assessments showed improved literacy rates among the participating children. This project not only enhanced the educational prospects of these children but also strengthened community ties and showcased the impact of well-coordinated volunteer efforts.      DESCRIBE: In this reflection, I focus on my leadership and strategic communication skills, which were crucial for managing a diverse team and ensuring the project's success.      EXAMINE: During this project, I experienced significant emotional growth, particularly in handling pressure and expectations from different stakeholders. There were moments of doubt and stress, especially when facing logistical hurdles. These situations taught me the importance of resilience and the need for proactive problem-solving while maintaining empathy and support for the team.      ACT: To further develop my leadership skills, I plan to engage in more complex volunteer management roles and seek mentorship from experienced leaders in the nonprofit sector. I intend to take specialized courses in nonprofit management to better understand the nuances of this field and to enhance my ability to lead diverse teams effectively.      LEARN: This experience profoundly influenced my personal and professional development, highlighting the transformative role of effective leadership in volunteer settings. It reinforced the value of empathy, proactive communication, and strategic planning in achieving meaningful outcomes. I've learned how these skills can significantly impact community-based initiatives, and I am motivated to apply this knowledge to future projects, aiming for even greater success and community impact.

mid.situation: A systematic computational approach to monitoring and controlling production processes to improve quality and reduce variability.
mid.task_action: Implement statistical process control (SPC) charts to monitor critical production processes. Use this to understand the sources of variability, set appropriate control ranges, and ensure that all processes operate within these ranges. This will help proactively identify and resolve problems, improve product quality, and increase customer satisfaction.
mid.result: The implementation of SPC charts has improved the manufacturing process. This improves product quality and reduces rework and scrap costs. SPC charts can play a role in quality control and can more objectively describe the impact on product quality.

mid.situation: This semester, I had four units to complete, which put an obstacle on how well I managed to balance multiple tasks. Every unit had a unique set of difficulties, such as difficult homework and strict deadlines. Even though I knew how important it was to prioritize and stay focused, I still had trouble finding a balance.

I had to do more study sessions and reviews because the material was so complex, which reduced the amount of time I could dedicate to the ENGG2050 unit.  Consequently, my contributions to ENGG2050 discussions and group work began to diminish, as I struggled to balance the competing demands of all four units.
mid.result: My prioritization's consequences were becoming more and more obvious. Although my efforts in the MECH and MATH units resulted in some understanding, the ENGG2050 unit was left out, which had serious consequences. Trying to keep up with lectures and assignments was making it difficult for me to understand concepts that I had previously understood. My struggles were made worse by not participating in the ENGG2050 activities, which made it harder for me to understand the subject matter completely.

mid.situation: I would like to recount about the time in this semester where I had to submit the A3 Case study 1 for the unit ENGG2050. I had fallen sick just that week and had many assignments due towards the end of the week.I had been studying for my quizzes, due to which I had left my assignments for the last moment, which became very stressful for me as I was overwhelmed and struggled to manage all the assignments as their due date neared. I was not able to focus on my work as I had not properly planned and prioritized the assignments, which were more important as the deadlines were approaching.
mid.task_action: I immediately rearranged my schedule, giving the assignments a higher priority depending on their relevance and deadlines. Due to its substantial weight in the course grade, A3 Case Study 1 was ranked first on my list. Using my time management skills, I made an extensive timetable by dividing every task into smaller, more doable tasks. Considering my health condition, I set aside certain time windows to rest to prevent burnout. To increase my focus, I stayed away from all distractions. I tend to waste a lot of time watching TV; therefore, I disconnected my TV and kept it locked away so I did not break my focus and get the work done on time.
mid.result: I was able to successfully finish all of my assignments, including my case study, on time by using these steps. Even though the process was challenging and frustrating, I gained important insight into the significance of setting priorities and managing my time in a coordinated manner. Despite my illness and initial disorganization, I was able to submit quality work and maintain my performance across all units.

mid.situation: We were tasked with creating a visual infographic as a part of our ENGG2050 coursework. mid.situation: In one of my core mechanical engineering courses, our class was faced with the challenge of designing a cost-effective and efficient water filtration system for rural communities as part of our final year project. The primary goal was to create a system that could be easily manufactured with locally available materials and require minimal maintenance.
mid.task_action: My role in this project was to lead the research and design phase. I conducted extensive research on existing water filtration technologies and their adaptability to rural settings. Collaboratively, our team decided to focus on a gravity-fed system using sand and charcoal as natural filters, which are readily available and effective in removing impurities. I was responsible for the prototype design, ensuring it was simple yet effective. We built and tested multiple prototypes, adjusting the proportions of sand and charcoal and experimenting with different container materials to find the most cost-effective solution.
mid.result: The final design we developed was both affordable and efficient, successfully removing over 98% of contaminants from the water during testing phases. We presented our project at the university's engineering showcase, where it received positive feedback for its practical application and sustainability. The project was not only a success in meeting its design goals but also in contributing valuable knowledge to my field of study. It enhanced my skills in design and testing, and more importantly, it strengthened my ability to work effectively in a team-oriented environment toward a common goal.

mid.situation: We needed to brainstorm and make the finalised decision of selecting a logistical fluids mixing system (LFM) for the IOT machine, but we were in a stalemate situation with no progression in the project due to no unanimous decision has been made in selecting a LFM.
mid.task_action: Have advocated to team members to brainstorm alternative LFM systems, including myself. In the subsequent week's SGTA, with the intention of coming to an unanimous decision, I have taken the initiative in showcasing the option analysis method to team members, which is a approximated quantitative decision making framework that critically analyses critical factors that aligns with the objective of the project (in this project these critical factors are cost, engineering design proficiency and potential risk).
mid.result: With the provided framework, we have analysed each of the design alternatives and we were able to select the most optimal design based on a ballpark quantitative figure.

mid.situation: I was working in a group project wherein work needed to be divided and with four people in a group but only 3 sections of work to complete.
mid.task_action: Our group was unsure how to split the workload so initially we had decided to split the marks based on how many points the individual sections were worth, however, someone in the group pointed out that typically the last 20% of marks are the hardest to achieve so if someone has the last few sections of work they would end up being the hardest. This thus, led us to split the work based on difficulty which is hard to gauge accurately thus a compromise was needed.
mid.result: In the end we had a discussion about a fair split of work and decided that two people would take the first two sections and two people would take the last section. Then those two subgroups were able to split work between them as their work was more closely related. This allowed a fair compromise in terms of workload and difficulty.

mid.result: By researching soft robotics target market and technology edge we have learnt the importance of understanding customer needs and conducting market research as they have created a new market for supplying automation tools for the food industry. Soft robotics research of the market showed how the food industry was late to adopt automation and by having meetings with large food supplies determined the need for such machinery. This skill of understanding your customer needs and conducting market research is transferable and is a valuable lesson for finding new markets or expanding in an existing market.

mid.situation: I was working with one other student (semi-random) and a lecturer to research how adding tariffs to the electric chargers at Macquarie Uni has changed consumer behaviours. This had to include customer feedback, data analysis and suggested solutions, compiled into a research paper.
mid.task_action: We obtained the data (how much power the chargers need, who charged their car and for how long, etc.) from the website that holds information on each charger you have installed for a particular party. We also created a survey for the drivers to use but we didn't get many responses at first because it was released during the holidays. This led us to extending how long it would be open for and we doubled the responses in the following month. We also had to combine our brainstormed ideas and suggestions from the public, in the survey, to come up with solutions for problems that the consumers had revealed.
mid.result: As a result, we had a heap of compiled data on how people are using these chargers (grouping things like car models, how much revenue we are making and which locations they use to charge the most often), as well as what issues people have with the current chargers (such as the one perpetually broken charger, sun heat rendering all of them useless in the middle of the day and far away charger locations). We were then able to find new solutions and now have changed companies to people who actually help us fix the chargers and added new chargers on the other side of campus (near 4RPD/the hospital) and have potential other locations, which will hopefully please the people using them.

mid.situation: The project was part of a course assignment where we were tasked with developing a web application that could serve as a resource management tool for small businesses. mid.situation: We needed to undergo an innovative process in the creation of the automated safe.

After assembly, the safe was tested multiple times in various environments and scenarios to ensure its full effectiveness.

mid.situation: Working at a engineering firm, we were tasked with developing an innovative transportation solution that would reduce carbon emissions and improve urban mobility. The project was difficult, involving cutting edge technology and a significant investment from stakeholders and clients.      TASK: My role as the Intern Cadet Engineer was to oversee the project’s development from concept to completion. This involved coordinating with multiple teams, managing resources, and ensuring that the project follows strict environmental and safety standards.      ACTION: I helped a series of collaborative workshops with engineers, urban planners, and environmental scientists to align our goals and create a roadmap for the project. We adopted a specific engineering approach to optimize resource usage and reduce waste. I also communicated with stakeholders and clients to keep them informed and involved throughout the decision making process.
mid.result: The outcome was the successful launch of a zero emissions transportation system that was well received by the public and lauded for its innovation. The project came in under budget and was completed ahead of schedule, setting a new industry standard for sustainable engineering practices.

mid.situation: In my ENGG2000 university course, my motions team struggled to maintain individual accountability after the success of our Scoping Document.
mid.task_action: When starting the next document, the Design document, my team allocated different sections for individuals to complete. However, people lost motivation to complete the document, and to a low standard, when other assignments and classes required more attention. However the Design Document, being worth a large percentage of the units final mark, also required attention. Thus, both myself and a few other members of my team tried to step up to make up for the shortfall of other members' contributions, however we quickly achieved burnout when the workload became too much in addition to other classes and commitments. With being granted an extension over the break, myself and a few devoted members were able to complete the document by the due date, albeit rushed with many late nights.
mid.result: The motions team was able to submit a well laid out and detailed Design Document on time.

mid.situation: When working in a project group, there was many times where individuals were not held responsible for their own section of work.
mid.task_action: To solve this issue, expectations of what work should be done by each member and the consequences were laid out. Individuals that were found to not contribute to the tasks at hand were recorded with evidence. The individual was talked to multiple times and at the end, this evidence was later submitted to hold the individual accountable.
mid.result: The team held accountability practices and help individuals accountable for any tasks that were required to be done.

mid.situation: Creating and Designing a gear box within MECH3005, In this we needed a communication structure in order to ensure that the project ran smoothly and efficiently as we had tons of documents that each of us would require from one another, thus a way to collect and index these would be needed.
mid.task_action: To solve this we created a discord server that contained all of our documents and links to google drives that contained CAD, along with having a master sheet in google sheets that contained every single one of our calculations within, Further more within this discord server we have made sure to have all our contact information along with best times of communication in order to make sure that we can talk and work through the project.      Results: Overall, so far with the project it has been going efficiently and smoothly. The team is able to effectively communicate about our ideas and what we need from the others to continue with the project. This has lead us to being ahead of the schedule within our project and we should have a decent projected mark.

mid.situation: We needed to brainstorm methods where we can meet the design criteria for the marble machine.
mid.task_action: For the agreed upon grade, we were aiming for, HD, we analyzed the requirements for the design. with the other groups we brainstormed possible methods where we can achieve these actions. The brainstormed ideas were then gathered and discussed upon, their feasibility, cost, complexity and function taken into account. these later refined our choices and which lead to us decide our final combinations of internal processes.
mid.result: we were able to design a feasible solution to our design problem. these solutions also went through several iterations, improving on the last.

mid.situation: During a team project, we faced a notable setback in finishing our designated task of creating a prototype for a new software feature.
mid.task_action: Understanding the significance of being accountable, I proactively took steps to address the delay. First, a team meeting was organised to discuss the reasons for the delay and to identify any obstacles that may be impeding progress. During the meeting, we carefully examined our project timeline, resource allocation, and individual responsibilities. We discovered that there were issues with communication and task assignments that were causing the delay. In order to address this issue, we made the decision to review our project plan, provide clear definitions of each team member's role and responsibilities, and implement regular check-ins to closely monitor our progress. In addition, we established a communication protocol to promptly address any issues or challenges that arose.
mid.result: Due to these measures, we witnessed substantial enhancements in the efficiency and productivity of our team. By implementing efficient task assignments and enhancing communication, we successfully optimised our workflow and proactively addressed any potential obstacles. As a civil engineer, we were able to efficiently finish the prototype within the updated timeline and provide a top-notch product that fully satisfied the project requirements. In addition, the team's ability to address the accountability issue improved our unity and bolstered our problem-solving abilities, establishing a favourable example for future partnerships.

mid.situation: During Engg3050, we brainstormed the needs and wants of university students to understand what products can be created to solve a problem.
mid.task_action: We brainstormed various problems associated with daily life and university. Assessing the problems and what the similarities between each other. This could be small issues or just inconveniences. The study was expanded to various students around the university with questions about the biggest struggles of university.      Results: The results varied between individuals with university workload to finding a study space. Although, the most common problem included some sort of traffic and transport.  This led me to reducing transport times or increasing the enjoyment of traveling to university.

mid.situation: We must assemble and prepare the finished product for shipment by the due date.       Task:       the problem is to work together in assembling a wind turbine under a time constraint, while trying to complete other tasks, giving everyone in the team a specific part to prepare in advance, with a time attached, holding them accountable for completing everything in time. also adding the person's name to a record list to make sure everyone who has worked on the product can be held accountable for what they have made.
mid.result: Everyone works promptly, making sure that everyone is done to the expected perfection, as they are held accountable by other names being on the product identification.

mid.situation: we needed to brainstorm and  come up with creative ways to improve document control and communication to do a assessment .
mid.task_action: During week 5 we were tasked to create a presentation by working in a group . after getting assigned  in a group we started brainstorming . at first it was going so bad since one of our team members was not getting any information on that topic . then we helped that team member and made sure that she catches everything . Then  we started defining the problems and set the rules and then started generation ideas . we marked down the problems and and started solving them . we also created group chat to keep up with the ideas of each members of our group . we noted the important information and set out goal . we also wrote down all the important tasks.
mid.result: We were able to find out all the solutions of our problems . and every one got the proper idea of the topic that we are working on for our presentation .

mid.situation: AS A TEAM LEADER IN A GROUP PROJECT, OUR TASK WAS TO DESIGN A SUSTAINABLE SOLUTION FOR WATER CONSERVATION IN URBAN AREAS.   TASK: I ORGANIZED TEAM MEETINGS TO BRAINSTORM IDEAS, DELEGATED SPECIFIC RESPONSIBILITIES BASED ON TEAM MEMBERS' STRENGTHS, AND CONDUCTED THOROUGH RESEARCH ON EXISTING WATER CONSERVATION TECHNOLOGIES.   ACTION: I FACILITATED OPEN COMMUNICATION WITHIN THE TEAM, ENCOURAGED CREATIVE THINKING, AND COORDINATED THE INTEGRATION OF VARIOUS DESIGN CONCEPTS INTO A COMPREHENSIVE SOLUTION PROPOSAL.
mid.result: THE TEAM SUCCESSFULLY PRESENTED OUR INNOVATIVE WATER CONSERVATION DESIGN TO INDUSTRY EXPERTS, RECEIVING POSITIVE FEEDBACK FOR ITS FEASIBILITY AND SUSTAINABILITY ASPECTS.

mid.situation: I was doing a Comp unit in which there was a group assignment and in that group assignment there were international students in the group that i was apart of.       Task: We were told to work together in a group and communicate with them and divide the assignment amongst ourselves, however there wasn't a way in which we could all talk on one platform if we needed anything from each other. For instance some People have WhatsApp and some people didn't, some had Facebook while others didn't. So that made it difficult for us as a group to work together as there wasn't an effective way for us to communicate between each other.      Action: I proposed that why don't we download an app that we can all use to communicate for this assignment and that way we can help one another and keep on up to date with how this assignment is going. My group member suggested us to download discord as its not too personal and an effective way to talk among group members and share files/ documents easily.
mid.result: We all downloaded discord and kept up to date with our assignment. We were able to have effective communication on how we should handle this assignment and we ended up doing good together in the assignment both academically and socially.

mid.situation: We were required to select a startup for our presentations but the members of our group were initially unable to reach an agreement on what startup would be best.
mid.task_action: By creating a shortlist of each startup the members of our group wished to research we were able to go through and evaluate each option as a group we were able to arrive at a choice that the entire group agreed upon.
mid.result: Our group was able to select a startup to research that we all agreed upon in a timely and efficient manner.

mid.situation: We had to work on a laboratory report of forces on a plastic truss.
mid.task_action: We used software to determine the force on a plastic truss. Someone had assigned themself as the group leader, but did not follow through on their leadership responsibility. The group members recognized the issue of unequal contribution and its impact on grading fairness. We had a thorough discussion with the teaching staff about the unfair nature of the grades, as some members failed to do their assigned tasks.       RESULTS:      After discussion with the teaching staff, the grades were scaled to more effectively reflect the level of effort from each group member. When the 2nd part of the assignment needed to be submitted, the members who had poorer scaling and subsequently had lower grades worked harder to contribute in the group.

mid.situation: During the ENGG3000 project, I along with another student, were group leaders and were responsible for dividing up workload and documentation writing.
mid.task_action: During the first half of the semester we all began to work on the project. This included documentation like the scoping document as well as design documents. Overall the group was performing well, with the quality of work being fairly similar amongst the group, with everyone delivering what was expected of them. There was one student whose work output was significantly worse than anyone else. This often meant that another group member would have to proofread  their work and improve both the length and quality of what was written. This was due to language barriers as English was not their first language. We (the other group lead and I) were unaware of the impact that this had on them. Moving forward we would meet with them privately after our group stand-ups to really dissect their workload and tasks into sections they understood.
mid.result: The result was that the work output of this student improved significantly in the second half of the semester. We were able to rely on them to deliver quality work on time. That contributed to the overall flow of the document.

mid.situation: During one of my engineering units, our group at the time was tasked with delivering an oral presentation to the whole cohort consisting of students and teachers from diverse educational backgrounds. The topic was our engineering specialization.
mid.task_action: To achieve our task, we had to thoroughly research the topic. We not only had to understand the content, but also know it indepth in such a way that we can explain it to someone that had no experience or prior knowledge on the topic. After the research phase, we divided the workload onto ourselves to create slides for the presentation with the key points and had mock presentations, while also taking feedback from our peers into consideration, to make sure we were ready.
mid.result: When the day of the presentation arrived, we had proceeded like we practiced and our efforts had paid off. We were able to provide the audience a concise but engaging presentation. Moreover, the feedback from the audience let us know that the content was easy to understand and their overall knowledge on the topic had improved.

mid.situation: Several months ago, my workplace underwent a hiring process to onboard six additional casual workers. However, the training approach for these new hires merely involved shadowing longer-tenured colleagues. Despite recognizing the limitations of this method, I remained silent and allowed the process to proceed unquestioned.      As time passed, frustrations emerged among my coworkers, including myself, regarding one new hire's lack of initiative and performance. While gossip circulated about his shortcomings, I refrained from participating, understanding its futility in fostering improvement.
mid.task_action: To address the issue constructively, I devised a communication strategy aimed at effectively conveying concerns without provoking defensiveness. I scheduled a meeting with the individual to discuss the matter candidly. My approach involved presenting factual evidence of his performance and articulating how it was perceived by others. I then offered constructive feedback and clarified expectations to facilitate a mutual understanding.
mid.result: The outcome of this intervention was a notable improvement in the individual's performance once expectations were clearly understood. Additionally, delving deeper into the underlying reasons for his struggles provided valuable insights that were shared with the team. This enhanced understanding fostered a supportive environment and contributed to overall workplace satisfaction.

mid.situation: In ENGG2000 during our submission of our project, we discovered some bugs in the codes. We were the software team, so we were responsible for all the coding of the marble project.
mid.task_action: We tried to fix as many bugs as possible.      Here are the actions we took,       1) Ask the tutors for which areas needed help.      2) Team work - we each took time to look at the code and tried to work as a team.      3) Testing - checked the codes again and asked the tutor for feedbacks.
mid.result: The end code was sufficiently better and took lesser time to run.

mid.situation: Within the ENGG4001 unit we needed to demonstrate a hypothetical solution for a net zero carbon emission export to replace coal within Australia. To this end we had to visually relay and defend our solution to the review and criticism of our peers.
mid.task_action: A series of diagrams, plans and models were devised to be included within the defense presentation; flow charts were created to relay the proposed timeline of production, diagrams to visualize the area in question and 3D models to show the relationship between building elements and integration into the area. Parameters such as time, dimension and cost were also relayed visually through graphs .
mid.result: An engaging and clear presentation of our solution could be given. By not inundating the presentation with words our audience were able to quickly grasp the information given in the oral component of the presentation without being confused or bored.

mid.situation: We needed a rework on our visual communication stratergies due to low interaction and engagement on social media

mid.situation: We needed to design and construct a gearbox that would lift a certain weight the required height
mid.task_action: As a group we discussed further details on the chosen design. We clarified certain aspects of the design that group members didn't understand or had some problems with. The design was then delegated to each person in the group to do their work and to be accountable for the area. It was put on their shoulders to finish the design and draw it up as well as CAD it to provide a proper model of the gearbox.
mid.result: A fully designed gearbox was designed and constructed due to everyone taking accountability for their parts and completing their respective sections on time and within the agreed requirements.

mid.situation: We needed to find a way around producing the spiral in ENGG2000/3000 due to excessive build failures, the build was simply too much to build at once.
mid.task_action: After gathering in a group to discuss possible options I helped push forward the idea of segmenting the printer into smaller prints to then tessellate and attach them together via screws. From this it would be possible to not only complete the print but prevent the print from being a complete loss if a part fails.  We also utilised how far certain changes could be made with segmented prints.
mid.result: Because of our new approach to how we printed we created a new solution concept that made the implementation process as lead times went down by 40% due to how the segmented approach was added to our manufacturing process.

mid.situation: In my role as a civil engineer working for Abergeldie Complex Infrastructure, i was on a project for TfNSW conducting a road upgrade at eastern creek Archbold Rd Upgrade. There was one aspect I was looking at where the "Trench Drain" clashed with the water main, and sewer main.       Task: The task was to conduct investigation on what would be the easiest way to action this issue. Brainstorming all options with not just myself but with senior engineers and also supervisors we decided to get the surveyor to model up our option to see if there would be any more clashes throughout the project regarding this issue.       Action: To Begin, I sat down with my senior engineers, surveyor and supervisor to conduct a model that would ultimately work with no clashes with any utilities / services. Once we came up with the best way of conducting this I submitted this as a part of my RFI to TfNSW to seek there approval / advise.
mid.result: The result was that TfNSW accepted the RFI and our model and we continued the work throughout the project.

This process involved brainstorming multiple concepts and weighing the benefits and drawbacks of both. This involved discussion of the various options and how we should tackle the project in its entirety, with discussion about its level of modularity being key.
mid.result: This discussion and brainstorming allowed us to narrow down the solutions to a single option we decided was worth pursuing and this further allowed us to progress in the project.

mid.situation: The project throughout the ENGG2050 unit was to make a tea maker. This was inspired by reviewing the ethics, integrity and social responsibility while creating the tea maker for the customer.      TASK/ ACTION: Every week, there are weekly SGTAs and lectures to tackle the decision of the requirements on the tea maker while adhering to the customer needs and making sure that the tea maker's manufacturing process follow the the ethical standards for the customer to avoid environmentally harmful materials and a socially responsible tea maker that is good for the environment. This was challenging because of the customer needs are required to meet while also practicing the ethics, integrity and social responsibility that is required in the engineering environment.      Ethically sourced materials from the supplier for our tea leaves was one of the main priority where we source it from the Fairtrade tea company where their tea leaves are ethically sourced to fit the standards. Honesty in the project was also considered as one of the main requirements where we are transparent with the customer, letting them know where all the materials are sourced from to create a sustainable and about 10% of commission of the tea maker will go into charity for world vision. For social responsibility, the tea maker is equipped with 'power saving mode' to make it environmentally sustainable and to also make the tea maker portable and last longer.
mid.result: The result was the creation of the tea maker that matches the customer's needs and wants while also understanding the crucial practice of ethics, integrity and social responsibility that was incorporated as a transferable skill into the workforce.

mid.situation: We needed to come up with creative ideas for what we, as a group, wanted to include in our RGM video and we needed to brainstorm a storyboard for the video as well.

mid.situation: We needed to productively work together on our shared purpose to produce a good RGM presentation.

The meeting resulted in the decision-making in which our videos would be re-shot in landscape and include objects moving from 'left to right', to improve consistency and use transition objects between member videos for flow.

mid.situation: we needed to design a machine that makes beverage

mid.situation: My group and I decided on designing a confetti-popping machine for our Rube-Goldberg Machine project. We needed to brainstorm ideas to complete the task on hand.

mid.situation: We need group discussion to plan and build a model.
mid.task_action: Divide a task into manageable pieces and assign one to each group member. when a member encounters a problem with their task, they can raise questions, and we then discuss the problem with each person contributing their own arguments in support of or opposition to the viewpoint.
mid.result: We can get a solution that is relatively acceptable to everyone, and that results in relatively satisfactory results.

mid.situation: I had four assessments due in the same week.
mid.task_action: The way I approached trying to complete everything was firstly, writing everything down two weeks prior to everything due in a list and put down when everything was due. I then proceeded to list everything in priority order and what needed the most amount of time, when it was due and difficulty. Afterwards, I worked on all of them a little each day with the most time spent on what was the top priority, second, third and fourth.
mid.result: The result was that I was able to finish all my assessments before the deadlines because I used all my time wisely even though I had other commitments too.

mid.situation: I needed to present 1000 letters UN report on Sustainable Design Goal based on Zero Hunger for a presentation in under 5 minutes and it was my first time presenting.
mid.task_action: The first thing i did was to read the report thoroughly and found important things to be mentioned in the presentation. Once i prepared my presentation, I practised my presentation skills in front of a mirror at home while recording it so to remove my anxiety and to see through recorded video where i am going wrong.
mid.result: The result was in under 3 attempts of practising for presentation at my home I figured out my mistakes and during the presentation I was able to present in under 5 minutes.

mid.situation: We needed to divide workload equally among peers for an important assessment due.
mid.task_action: In order to divide the work clearly, first we had to examine the group and communicate with how we can work effectively together, and how to communicate with each other on or off campus. After figuring out how to work cooperatively we then had to create an agenda of everything that needed to be done to get this assessment done. Subsequently, we evaluated the rubric and notification and deciphered what was needed in total, then decided to take it among ourselves to assign ourselves what we thought was a manageable but equal load.
mid.result: The result was a workload that was divided into an equal load ensuring that all of us were fair and equal to one another.

mid.situation: Establishing a solid foundation and developing transferable skills to thrive and excel as individuals in the engineering field, thereby advancing our career portfolio's further.
mid.task_action: Attitudes, thinking, and learning are essential employability skills that one must value. By attending classes, reading assigned texts, and completing tasks, students gain valuable skills that can propel their careers. Additionally, engaging in conversations with fellow students and building a social network are often overlooked yet crucial areas for personal and professional growth.
mid.result: Acquiring knowledge and participating in these activities has strengthened student's engineering foundations, enabling them to grow and flourish.

mid.situation: As the team leader for a group project in our Mech unit, we had a serious setback when one of our members became unwell just before a big presentation deadline.
mid.task_action: I called an emergency team meeting as soon as I realised how serious the situation was in order to evaluate the effects of our team member's absence and come up with some remedies. We talked about splitting up the work among the surviving team members so that all the necessary parts of the presentation could still be finished on schedule. We divided up the team's work and changed deadlines after assessing each member's strengths and areas of competence. I also got in touch with our lecturer to let her know about the circumstances and ask for a short postponement of the impacted team member's assignment.

mid.situation: As an undergraduate engineer working for the Central Coast Council, my team and I were tasked with assisting with upgrading a water mains infrastructure, which is a common job required for most projects. Traditional methods required a complete water shutdown to install new water mains or relocate them, causing significant disruption to the community. For this particular job, traditional methods did not seem applicable due to the time required for traditional methods to be done, and we were on tight deadlines. A new technology known as a S-gate valve, was proposed for trial. This valve could be installed on live water mains, eliminating the need for a full water shutdown.      Task:       My task was to assess the feasibility and potential benefits of implementing the S-gate valve technology. This included planning the trial installation, coordinating with my supervisors, and evaluating the performance and impact of the new technology. I was tasked with writing an article on the technology to inform the managers about the potential use of this new technology.      Action:      	*   Research and Analysis: I began by researching the S-gate valve technology, understanding its design, functionality, and previous implementations in other regions.       	*   Stakeholder Coordination: I organised meetings with my managers, including the council's senior engineers. These meetings aimed to discuss the potential benefits, address any concerns, and ensure all parties were informed about the trial process.      	*   Planning the Trial: I developed a detailed plan for the trial installation. This included scheduling the installation to minimise disruption and organising all necessary equipment and personnel.      	*   Monitoring and Evaluation: During the installation, I helped to monitor the process and evaluate the outcome of the trial by writing an article about the installation.
mid.result: The trial of the S-gate valve technology was successful and had several positive outcomes:      	*   Minimised Disruption: The installation of the S-gate valve was completed without requiring a full water shutdown, significantly reducing inconvenience for the local community. This demonstrated the technology’s potential to streamline future projects.      	*   Cost and Time Savings: The trial showed that using the S-gate valve could save considerable time and costs associated with traditional shutdown methods. Reduced labour hours and minimised service interruptions both reduced the cost of the project.      	*   Improved Efficiency: The new technology improved the efficiency to our water mains maintenance procedures and was a lot easier job compared to traditional methods.      	*   Positive Feedback: Feedback from the community and internal managers was positive. The community valued the reduced disruption, and the engineering team appreciated the innovative approach to the problem.

mid.situation: In Warriewood, a local public park needed an improvement due to a lack of amenities and poor accessibility. The Northern Beaches Council decided to re-energise the park to encourage community engagement and provide a welcoming recreational area for residents of all ages.      Task: As the lead undergraduate civil engineer, my responsibility was to design and implement improvements to the park. The primary objectives were to enhance accessibility, introduce new recreational facilities, and ensure the project adhered to principles of environmental sustainability while staying within a tight budget.      Action: To kick off the project, I organised community meetings to gather input from residents. The feedback highlighted a need for features like a modern playground, walking paths, and picnic areas. I designed paved walking paths with energy-efficient lighting, inclusive playground equipment and picnic areas with tables and barbecues. To promote sustainability, i included native plants for landscaping and incorporated an irrigation system and solar-powered lights. I coordinated closely with contractors to ensure the project stayed on schedule and within budget, conducting regular site visits to address issues promptly.
mid.result: The re-energised park was completed on time and within budget, quickly becoming a popular destination for families and individuals. The new amenities significantly increased the park's usage, providing diverse recreational opportunities and enhancing the community's quality of life. The project received positive feedback from residents and set a positive example for future public projects.

mid.situation: WE WERE EXPECTED TO MAKE A RUBE GOLDBERG MACHINE BY FOLLOWING A FIVE STAGE MODEL OF DESIGN PROCESSES WHICH ARE PROBLEM DEFINITION, CONCEPTUAL DESIGN, PRELIMINARY DESIGN, DETAILED DESIGN AND FINAL DESIGN COMMUNICATION. mid.result: By following the engineering design process we were able to successfully assemble our RGM machine. We then further start discussion on making and editing our video which was about our RGM machine.

As a first-year engineering student at Macquarie University, I was assigned a challenging task to design and build a Rube Goldberg Machine as part of our engineering design course. This project was designed to put not only our technical skills to the test, but also our ability to work collaboratively and professionally with our peers. The assignment was to design a complex, chain-reaction contraption capable of performing a simple task, such as turning on a light switch, through a series of intricate mechanical steps.      TASK:   The project necessitated the collaboration of a diverse group of students, each with their own set of ideas and backgrounds. The main task was to design and build a Rube Goldberg Machine that not only met the project criteria but also demonstrated professionalism in our teamwork and communication with each other and the audiences to whom we presented.      ACTION:   To face the task while remaining professional throughout the project thus far, I have taken the following steps:      Team Communication: Recognising the importance of clear and open communication, I established regular team meetings. These meetings ensured that everyone was on the same page when it came to our objectives, timeline, and individual responsibilities. To avoid misunderstandings and conflicts, effective communication is required.      Active Listening: I made a conscious effort to actively listen to my teammates' ideas and concerns during our team discussions. I fostered a collaborative environment in which everyone felt heard and valued by encouraging open dialogue and respecting diverse viewpoints.      Time Management: I realised that time management was critical to our project's success. I made a detailed project schedule that included milestones and deadlines. This allowed us to allocate our time more efficiently, ensuring that we meet project deadlines without having to rush at the last minute.      Task Delegation: Recognising our team's strengths and weaknesses, I collaborated with my teammates to assign tasks that capitalised on each member's expertise. This approach fostered a sense of team ownership and motivation.      Conflict Resolution: Because of differing opinions and approaches, conflicts have inevitably risen during the project. However, I dealt with these conflicts quickly and professionally, encouraging compromise and assisting in the mediation of discussions to reach mutually acceptable solutions. This helped to keep our project on track.
low.result: Our efforts to maintain professional behaviour throughout the project so far have had several positive outcomes:      High Team Morale: By encouraging effective communication and professionally resolving conflicts, our team morale remained consistently high throughout the project. This optimistic atmosphere contributed to a more enjoyable and productive working environment.      Collaboration Skills: I improved my collaboration skills, which included active listening, conflict resolution, and task delegation. These are highly transferrable skills that will undoubtedly help me in future engineering projects and in my professional career.      The experience of designing and building a Rube Goldberg Machine thus far has highlighted the critical importance of professional behaviour in engineering projects. Effective communication, collaboration, and conflict resolution skills are not only necessary for project success, but they are also beneficial for personal and professional development. This project served as a stark reminder of the vital importance of professionalism in the ever-changing field of engineering.

low.situation: During a major RGM (Risk and Growth Management) project, our team faced the challenge of identifying and mitigating various risks that could impact the project's success and growth.
low.task_action: I was in charge of making sure our RGM (Risk and Growth Management) machine project ran smoothly and safely. The machine I had a setup where a car moved down a slope, went through a cardboard tunnel, and then triggered a bucket attached to a pulley. This bucket released balls that hit a clock. To make sure everything worked without problems, I started by doing research and started taking advice from my teammates. Then, I looked at past data and used tools to figure out any potential issues. Based on what I found, I made a plan to solve these problems and asked for feedback from others. I also held meetings to explain the plan to the team and set up a system to keep an eye on how well it was working.
low.result: My efforts paid off, as the RGM machine project operated more smoothly, safely, and with fewer unforeseen issues. As a result of the team's increased comprehension and application of risk reduction techniques, stakeholder satisfaction and project performance both increased.      -------------------------

low.situation: We were tasked with creating a video centred around designing a Rube Goldberg machine and the time management to present the video over an allotted time frame.
low.task_action: To tackle this challenge we embarked on a meticulous planning process. Firstly, we convened as a group to conceptualise the overarching storyline of the video, ensuring it showcased the intricacies of a Rube Goldberg machine while meeting marking criteria. With the narrative framework established, we divided the storyline into distinct sections, assigning each member responsibility for crafting one segment with two action tasks within.      Next, we delved into the technical aspects of the project. Collaboratively, we brainstormed the various components and mechanisms that would comprise our Rube Goldberg machine, ensuring each section seamlessly transitioned to the next. Through iterative discussions and prototyping, we refined our ideas, prioritizing coherence and visual appeal.      With the project scope defined, we turned our attention to resource management. Conducting an inventory of available equipment, we identified the tools necessary for realizing our vision. Recognizing potential constraints, we devised contingency plans and sought alternative solutions to mitigate risks.
low.result: The meticulous planning and coordination resulted in a presentation meeting time frame expectations, while adhering to marking criteria. Each section seamlessly transitioned to the next, maintaining the narrative flow decided by the group within the planning stages. By optimizing resource allocation, we maximized productivity and minimized unnecessary expenditures.      Moreover, our strategic approach to time management facilitated adherence to deadlines, preventing delays and ensuring timely completion. As a result, we delivered a compelling Rube Goldberg machine video that not only met the project requirements but also exceeded expectations. The project's success underscored the effectiveness of our time and resource management strategies, demonstrating our ability to execute complex multimedia projects with precision and creativity.

low.situation: During my third year of engineering studies, I unexpectedly sustained a significant injury that left me unable to attend university for three weeks. This unforeseen situation created challenges as I had to balance my recovery with staying on top of my coursework, group projects, and other academic responsibilities.      Task:      My task was to find a way to keep up with my studies despite being physically absent from campus. This required coordinating with my professors and classmates to catch up on missed lectures and assignments, as well as managing my recovery effectively.      Action:      To address the situation, I took the following actions:      - I reached out to my professors and informed them of my situation, requesting extensions or accommodations as needed.      - I communicated with my classmates and group project partners to keep up with the material and contribute remotely to group work.      - I adjusted my study schedule to accommodate my recovery, focusing on self-care while still dedicating time to studying.      - I used online resources and lecture recordings to keep up with the coursework and stay engaged with the material.
low.result: As a result of my actions, I was able to stay on track with my studies and return to campus without falling too far behind. My professors were understanding and granted me the necessary extensions, while my classmates supported me by sharing notes and updating me on group projects. Ultimately, I was able to maintain my academic performance despite the setback.
